result.stance: CUT. Parametric generics/polymorphism should be excluded from the core language. AI-side source-level monomorphization — every specialization written out as concrete code, bound to a declared template by a mandatory, build-gating conformance CHECK — carries the load.
result.coreArgument: The corpus's own record convicts parametric generics of being a code generator wearing a type system's clothes. The round-1 dispatch-generics leading candidate is 'guaranteed static specialization as the core's ONLY implicit path' — i.e., the sanctioned end state of generics is exactly the concrete per-instantiation code D001 documents Rust producing by monomorphization ('runtime behavior like concrete code'). In an all-AI-written language, the writer can emit that end state directly; the generic layer contributes no runtime artifact the alternative lacks, while charging the trusted, verifiable, extreme-performance compiler for four things. (1) An inference/bound-solving/instantiation subsystem whose design the debate leaves entirely open: where the specialization decision lives (frontend vs the F009 Swift-SIL 'fourth model', which drags generics INTO the IR — witness tables, generic ABI, resilience domains, all uncarded), cross-unit instantiation dedup and codegen-unit/LTO sensitivity (D004), and generic-ABI/separate-compilation questions listed as unexamined. Cutting generics deletes this entire open-question cluster from the core. (2) The topic's single decisive open soundness check — whether shared/erased generic bodies can discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and at what precision loss — exists ONLY because generics make shared bodies possible. Concrete-only code attaches the corpus's strongest-attested optimization facts (F001–F003 non-interference) per instantiation, with no discharge question, no precision loss, and no erased type-dependent alias facts. (3) The generics×dispatch interaction taxes: D003 dyn-compatibility must exclude generic dispatchable methods, producing the composition hole the critic landed on Advocate 1 (no sanctioned path for heterogeneous generic APIs, predicted hand-rolled void* erasure). With no core generics, interfaces carry only concrete methods; the D003 restriction class is moot by construction, and heterogeneity is written as explicit, checkable source the optimizer sees as ordinary code. (4) The one carded benefit unique to in-core generics — D005 bounded sharing — was structurally damaged in debate: JIT-only precedent with no AOT transfer story, uncarded dictionary/type-handle costs, D002's cost class on the shared path, an F003 tax never priced, and reflection metadata that D005's own negative scope says constrains closed-world assumptions. The charter asymmetry the judge credited — source-level guarantees over conditional, revertible recovery (D007 negative scope, D008, F007, misleading-comparisons note 3) — reaches its maximum in the alternative: what the optimizer ingests is literally what the writer wrote, with no instantiation step whose output must be imagined. J001/J002 corroborate the endpoint: every high-performance runtime converges on specialization to concrete types; the only question is the channel, and written-out source is the channel with zero guard, deopt, or instantiation machinery. The admissible machine-writer objection — consistency drift across near-duplicates — is answered by the alternative's own CHECK mechanism, which is architecturally superior on the trust axis verification-first design demands: a template-conformance checker does substitution matching, not inference or solving, and sits OUTSIDE the optimization trust path — a checker bug yields detected-late drift, never miscompilation, whereas a bug in a core generics engine miscompiles silently at scale. Finally, the burden of proof: every load-bearing dispatch card (D001–D007) is a provisional, unverified direct extraction (caveats.md standing rule); the corpus contains zero quantitative evidence — runtime, code size, i-cache, or compile time — that in-core generics wins on any measured axis (round-1 evidence gaps; caveats: documentation does not quantify); and the N/A/C/J series were largely absent from the debate. The null hypothesis stands. The feature has not met its burden. Cut it.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Rust generics compile by monomorphization into concrete type-specific code with 'runtime behavior like concrete code' — proving the feature's sanctioned end state is exactly what AI-side monomorphization emits directly; its negative scope (unquantified code bloat, compile time, cross-crate limits) attaches the only carded costs to the in-core mechanism the prosecution wants removed.

Dynamic dispatch has optimizer-visible representation and cost and 'should be explicit when used' — under the alternative everything is explicit source; also supplies the cost class that damages the shared-body path of D005-style in-core sharing.

Dyn-compatibility must exclude generic dispatchable methods, associated constants, opaque returns — a restriction cluster generated by generics interacting with dispatch. With no core generics, interfaces contain only concrete methods and this entire restriction class plus A1's composition hole vanish by construction.

Even monomorphized generic code's optimization is codegen-unit/LTO-option dependent — compiler-side instantiation scatters instances across units and forces share-generics/dedup machinery into the core; AI-side monomorphization makes instantiation placement a visible, deliberate source decision.

The only carded benefit unique to in-core generics (hybrid value-specialize/reference-share) is a JIT precedent with lazy instantiation; its negative scope disclaims proof of JIT optimization quality and warns reflection metadata constrains closed-world assumptions — the benefit does not transfer on evidence to an AOT extreme-performance target.

Devirtualization/recovery machinery is conditional, speculative, revertible, and option-dependent — establishing the guarantee-by-construction vs recovery-by-optimization asymmetry that maximally favors fully written-out concrete source.

ABI/linking semantics (interposition) can silently block optimizer assumptions even over statically-known-looking code — more evidence that guarantees must live in visible source-level facts, which the alternative maximizes.

Julia shows implicit compiler-driven specialization is heuristic, workload-dependent, and opaque, with over-specialization costing compile time and cache pressure — costs paid under both designs, so generics adds mechanism without removing them; also documents Function-typed fields defeating optimizers, a polymorphism-induced defeat class.

Dynamic multiple dispatch requires world-age and unlimited-case management — the far end of the polymorphism spectrum, showing that polymorphism mechanisms in the language create standing optimizer-assumption maintenance burdens in the trusted core.

LLVM noalias/alias.scope facts carry UB-grade obligations; the debate's decisive open check — can a shared/erased generic body discharge them, at what precision loss — exists only because generics permit shared bodies. Concrete-only code attaches these strongest-attested facts per instantiation with no open question.

Language-level non-interference is the corpus's best-attested optimizer enabler; per-instantiation concrete code lets every alias/exclusivity fact be stated and checked against concrete types directly, with no generic-context indirection.

Managed-runtime recovery (escape analysis) is conditional elimination, not a guarantee — with misleading-comparisons note 3, this cards the qualitative asymmetry: conditional recovery is inferior to source-level guarantees for a transparency-first performance core.

The strongest rival model (Swift SIL generics preserved into the IR) shows in-core generics does not stop at the frontend — it metastasizes into IR design, generic ABI, and witness-table machinery, all uncarded and open; cutting the feature deletes that commitment from the verifiable core.

CPython/V8/PyPy all converge on specialization to observed concrete types — the information endpoint of every performance strategy is concrete types; written-out source delivers that endpoint through the cheapest possible channel.
Speculation requires guard/deopt/invalidation machinery — the cost side of letting any mechanism other than literal source decide specialization; the alternative needs none of it.

machineWriterCosts: Token/generation cost multiplies: N specializations x body length must be generated and re-read instead of one generic body; context-window pressure grows in modules with many instantiations of many templates. Magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED - no corpus card quantifies it.

Edit amplification: a template-level bug must be fixed once and then regenerated N times; until regeneration completes, copies are stale. The conformance CHECK must gate builds, otherwise this becomes silent drift.

Larger source text plausibly raises parse/lex time in the AI iteration loop, even though instance-level semantic checking work is comparable to compiler-side monomorphization (the same instance set is checked either way). Unquantified - the corpus has zero compile-time measurements (D001 negative scope, round-1 evidence gaps).

Diagnostic fan-out: one template-level defect surfaces as N per-copy diagnostics; tooling must aggregate by template identity or the AI wastes context triaging duplicates. Machine-readable 'copy X diverges from template T at node Y' diffs are the mitigation, but their repair-loop value is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

New language surface: the template-declaration/instantiation-binding mechanism must itself be used correctly; binding a copy to the wrong template, or declaring a template that never matches, is a new error class the writer must manage.

Heterogeneous/registry-style APIs (the D003 composition-hole workloads) require the writer to hand-emit erasure or closed-world dispatch tables as explicit source - more tokens, and a site where hand-rolled patterns could be less optimizer-legible than a vetted core construct would have been (the critic's warning against A1 transfers here in weakened form).
consistencyRisks: Drift between copies and template if the conformance check is optional or advisory: the entire anti-hallucination case for the alternative collapses unless CHECK is mandatory at build time and failure blocks compilation.

Legitimate-divergence pressure: real extreme-performance specializations sometimes must differ beyond pure substitution (type-specific SIMD widths, layout-specific loop shapes, per-type constants). The check either forbids valid divergence or grows declared escape hatches - and every hatch is a region of unchecked near-duplicate code where hallucination drift returns.

Mechanism regrowth: the template language risks accreting bounds, inference, and conditionals until it is a parametric generic type system by another name living inside the checker. It must be constitutionally limited to substitution matching (no inference, no solving) or the prosecution's simplicity claim is forfeit.
Duplicate/competing templates: two modules declaring near-identical templates drift from each other; template identity, ownership, and deduplication need a designed story or drift moves up one level.

Stale instantiation inventory: with no compiler-driven instantiation, the set of needed specializations is writer-maintained; a missing specialization must surface as a hard resolution error and a wrong-template binding must be impossible to satisfy accidentally - otherwise a call site silently uses a semantically wrong copy.

Cross-copy invariants outside the template's scope (e.g., a shared table all copies must index consistently, or a protocol all copies must uphold) are not covered by per-copy conformance checking and need separate contract machinery.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence of any kind. Specifically: the actual error/drift rate when an AI emits N near-duplicate specializations, with and without a conformance checker.
Token/context cost magnitude of fully expanded monomorphized source vs a single generic body in real AI iteration loops (generation, re-reading, and repair).
Whether AI writers reliably regenerate all bound copies after a template edit, i.e., whether the edit-template/regenerate/recheck workflow is dependable at scale.

Compile-time delta between parsing+checking N written-out copies and compiler-side instantiation of the same instance set - the corpus has zero quantitative compile-time cards (D001 negative scope; caveats.md: documentation does not quantify magnitude).

Code-size and i-cache outcomes: both designs monomorphize to the same nominal endpoint, but round-1 lists mono-vs-shared-vs-dictionary as the decisive quantitatively unarbitrated axis; whether written-out copies plus linker-level dedup (ICF/outlining - itself uncarded) bounds growth as well as D005-style shared bodies is unmeasured.
Whether substitution-only template checking covers the specialization patterns extreme-performance code actually needs, and how often checked-divergence escape hatches would be used in practice.
Whether machine-readable template-divergence diagnostics measurably shorten AI repair loops compared to generic-instantiation error traces.

The load-bearing dispatch cards themselves (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007) are provisional direct extractions not yet adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule), and the F003 shared-body discharge check the debate flagged as decisive has not been run - the prosecution's case inherits these evidentiary limits.

result.stance: CUT. Dynamic dispatch — including the restricted, D003-style trait-object form in the round-1 leading candidate — should be excluded from the core language. Exhaustiveness-checked switch-over-variants (closed sets) plus explicit typed function-pointer tables (genuinely open sets), both written out by the AI, carry the load.
result.coreArgument: The corpus prices dynamic dispatch as a direct, carded performance defect and offers only conditional, revertible recovery — the wrong side of the one asymmetry this corpus actually establishes. D002 cards the cost: a dyn call is an indirect lookup that "prevents inlining of the method body, blocking some optimizations," and inlining is the gateway to the corpus's best-attested optimization class, the F001/F002/F003 non-interference machinery (F007's negative scope confirms downstream optimization is inlining- and context-dependent). The only carded counter-mechanism is D007-class devirtualization, which is explicitly compiler- and option-dependent, speculative, revertible, and paid for in LTO/IPA compile time and memory — an admissible cost that lands squarely in the AI iteration loop (D004 compounds it; D008 shows linking semantics can void it). LLVM's actual WPD machinery has no card at all (round-1 evidence gap). The round-1 leading candidate already concedes the decisive point by requiring that the dyn construct's "stated cost model does not rely on devirtualization." But a dyn construct that promises only an indirect call IS a (data-pointer, fn-table) pair — exactly what the alternative writes explicitly — while charging the core compiler for: (a) an object-safety sub-language (D003) of type rules and multi-cause diagnostics whose own negative scope admits it is "not direct proof of optimization benefit"; (b) a vtable ABI and metadata pathway that must survive lowering (misleading-comparisons #5: IR metadata is only a conditional carrier); and (c) a standing expectation of devirtualization passes to make it fast. Worse, the round-1 critic showed restricted dyn cannot even cover its own use case: D003 excludes generic dispatchable methods, so heterogeneous generic APIs fall back to hand-rolled fn-pointer erasure anyway — keeping the feature buys two dispatch mechanisms, one opaque. Cutting it buys one uniform, fully visible mechanism. Switch-over-variants puts every callee syntactically in the program: direct calls, per-arm inlining, per-arm alias facts, guarantee-by-construction on the favorable side of the D001/D007/F007 asymmetry — and exhaustiveness checking converts variant-set membership into a compile-checked, machine-readable obligation at every site, which matters because the writer is fallible at scale: dyn defers binding to runtime data flow, where writer drift produces silent misdispatch instead of a diagnostic. Verbosity is inadmissible as a defense; verification and compile time are admissible, and both favor the cut. Under the null hypothesis and the caveats.md standing rule — every load-bearing D-card provisional, zero quantitative evidence of any dyn benefit anywhere in the corpus — the feature has a carded performance cost, no carded performance case, and a large spec-and-verifier surface. Cut it.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The direct indictment: trait objects are a data pointer plus vtable, and dyn calls add lookup cost and prevent inlining, blocking optimizations. Its positive scope endorses making dynamic dispatch explicit when used — the alternative is maximal explicitness. Its positive scope also names the exact representation (fn table + data pointer) the AI can write out by hand, showing the cut loses no machine-level capability.

Restricted dyn requires an object-safety sub-language (no Self:Sized, no associated consts, no generic dispatchable methods, receiver restrictions) — pure spec/type-checker/diagnostic surface whose own negative scope says it is not proof of optimization benefit. It also proves the composition hole: restricted dyn excludes generic dispatchable methods, so heterogeneous generic APIs need hand-rolled fn-pointer erasure even if dyn is kept.

The only carded devirtualization machinery is conditional, speculative, revertible, and option-dependent, and LTO/IPA cost compile time and memory — recovery cannot be promised, and its compile-time cost is an admissible tax on the AI iteration loop.

Semantic interposition shows the recovery path is additionally fragile at module/ABI boundaries: whole-program assumptions needed to devirtualize can be blocked by linking semantics the language does not control.
Codegen-unit/LTO boundaries already stress compile time vs optimization; a dyn feature whose performance depends on LTO-scale visibility worsens the compile-time budget of every AI iteration.

Carded template for the governing asymmetry: managed-runtime recovery is conditional (inlining-, profile-, implementation-dependent), unlike source-level guarantees (misleading-comparisons #3). Dyn-plus-hoped-devirtualization is structurally the conditional-recovery side; explicit switch dispatch is the guarantee-by-construction side.

The corpus's best-attested optimization value is proving non-interference around writes (register promotion, read/write elimination, reordering via alias/mod-ref analysis). An opaque indirect callee at a dyn call site forces conservative mod/ref assumptions [inference from F002, flagged]; inlined direct switch arms let this machinery apply per arm.

Noalias/alias.scope facts are the flagship optimizer currency and are only sound if frontend obligations survive lowering — machinery that applies inside inlined, concrete code, i.e., what dyn call boundaries suppress and switch arms preserve.

Language-level non-interference is the central carded optimizer enabler; the core should spend its complexity budget on constructs that feed this machinery, not on a dispatch construct that interrupts it.

The core's leading generics path is already guaranteed static specialization with concrete-code runtime behavior; switch-over-variants extends the identical guarantee-by-construction philosophy to dispatch, keeping one architectural principle instead of two.

Cuts both ways and I cite it honestly: Function-typed fields hide call targets even in closed programs — indirect dispatch is an optimizer defeat in general, which indicts dyn; it equally warns that the alternative's fn-pointer-table half retains the defeat, so the alternative's performance case rests on the switch half.
Negative scope: runtime dispatch/reflection metadata constrains closed-world assumptions unless restricted — supporting a core with no runtime dispatch metadata at all.
Even sealed/finality information is carded only as semantic information with no claimed devirtualization benefit — a second warning against keeping dispatch machinery on the promise of recovery.

Acknowledged adverse-adjacent card: it favors preserving high-level semantics into the IR, but it cards IR visibility of semantics, not any benefit of runtime dispatch; an exhaustive switch keeps the callee set structurally visible to the optimizer with no vtable and no new IR construct.

machineWriterCosts: Token/context inflation (borne by the alternative): N dispatch sites x M variants of explicit switch arms or table definitions instead of one dyn call site; generating and re-reading these consumes context budget on every AI pass over the code.

Fan-out edits (borne by the alternative): adding a variant requires touching every switch site; each edit is a hallucination opportunity. Exhaustiveness diagnostics bound this to presence (a missed site is a compile error) but not to semantic correctness of the new arm.

Consistency drift among near-duplicate arm bodies (borne by the alternative): exhaustiveness checks catch missing/extra arms, not wrong arm bodies; semantically drifted copies of similar arms compile clean. This is the strongest surviving defense argument and it is real.

Table-coherence obligation (borne by the alternative): fn-pointer tables check per-slot signatures, but 'right function in right slot' is an unchecked writer obligation unless a table-coherence check is added outside dyn; dyn/D002's compile-time trait checking for heterogeneity is genuinely lost.

Dyn-compatibility rule burden (borne by keeping dyn): D003's object-safety rules are a knowledge/prompt burden and a multi-cause diagnostic family the writer must parse and react to; exhaustiveness has one crisp machine-readable diagnostic shape (missing-variant list).

Compile time in the AI loop (borne by keeping dyn): making dyn fast requires D007/D004-class LTO/IPA/devirtualization passes that cost compile time and memory per iteration; making it slow-but-honest makes the feature a worse-instrumented fn-pointer table. Either branch charges the writer loop or the run time.

Opaque cost model (borne by keeping dyn): with devirtualization conditional and revertible (D007), the writer cannot predict performance from source text; the switch form's cost is syntactically visible, which is what an optimizing writer needs to iterate against.
consistencyRisks: Contradicts the round-1 dispatch-generics leading candidate, which retains dyn as an explicit restricted construct; adopting the cut means reopening that verdict — defensible since its outcome was design_candidate_later (explicitly not a decision) and the critic already exposed the construct's composition hole (D003 excludes generic dispatchable methods).

Indirect calls do not vanish with the cut: the alternative itself requires first-class function pointers, and D009 shows function-typed fields hide call targets even in closed programs. The backend, the F002 mod/ref story, and the round-1 gap on closures/first-class-function dispatch must be solved regardless; the cut removes vtable ABI and object-safety machinery, not indirect-call analysis.

Exhaustiveness-checked switches presuppose closed variant sets — consistent with the A2 closed-world/no-interposition amendment (D008) but in tension with the interop dossier's dynamic-linking targets; genuinely open sets fall back to fn-pointer tables, exactly where the verification story is weakest.

Repeated near-duplicate switch sites are the precise AI-consistency-drift surface the design brief warns about; absent a core dispatch abstraction, out-of-core codegen/macro tooling will likely be invented to stamp out switches, and that layer's drift is unverified by the core compiler.

IR-strategy coupling (round-1 ir-strategy direction: a verifier-checked IR carrying facts structurally): a switch carries its callee set structurally into the IR for free, but fn-pointer tables carry nothing — callee-set fact attenuation for the table half is undesigned, and per misleading-comparisons #5 any metadata patch is only a conditional carrier.

Loss of a typed heterogeneity interface (D002 positive scope: trait objects give compile-time trait checking) may generate pressure to later reintroduce a checked record-of-fn-pointers construct — dyn by another name; the design should pre-commit to whether table-coherence checking is in scope for the alternative to prevent that regression.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: that AI writers can generate and maintain N-sites-x-M-variants explicit dispatch within realistic token/context budgets — the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence at all.
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: that exhaustiveness diagnostics are sufficient in-loop feedback for an AI writer to repair fan-out edits quickly and convergently.
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the rate and severity of AI semantic drift across near-duplicate switch arms (asserted here as a real cost of the alternative, but unmeasured in either direction).
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: whether AI writers handle D003 dyn-compatibility diagnostics better or worse than exhaustiveness diagnostics; the 'one crisp diagnostic shape' advantage is asserted, not measured.
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the defense's presumed benefit — that dyn call sites meaningfully reduce token/context cost in real AI-written codebases — is equally unmeasured.

Corpus-flagged: all load-bearing dispatch cards (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule; round-1 follow-up #1), and the N/A/C/J card series named in the debate ground rules were absent from the debate files.

Corpus-flagged: no quantitative comparison exists anywhere in the corpus of vtable dispatch vs switch-over-variants vs fn-pointer tables on runtime, code size, i-cache behavior, or compile time — the decisive measurement for this verdict is missing; the claim that exhaustive switches are strictly more optimizable rests on D002's inlining logic plus F002's non-interference machinery, not on benchmarks (e.g., branch-predictor vs indirect-branch-predictor behavior on branchy workloads is unquantified).

Corpus-flagged: whether the compiler may silently convert (devirtualize/densify) an explicit dispatch construct it proves unique is the unresolved antecedent charter question from round 1; the prosecution's cost-transparency argument assumes 'predictable cost model' wins that question.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP, restricted — matching the round-1 leading candidate (design_candidate_later, /Users/bytedance/Dev/xlang/optimizer-language-research/debates/round1-dispatch-generics.md): no implicit dynamic dispatch anywhere; guaranteed static specialization stays the only implicit path (D001); dynamic dispatch exists solely as an explicit, D003-restricted trait-object construct whose stated cost model is an honest indirect call (no reliance on devirtualization, per D007's negative scope). I do NOT defend dyn for closed, locally-owned variant sets — there, compiler-checked exhaustive switch-over-variants is legitimately competitive or superior, and the language should have it. The defense is that cutting the dyn construct entirely forces the alternative's second half — hand-rolled function-pointer tables over erased payloads — for every open-set, cross-module, heterogeneous-container, and function-value use, and that half loses compiler facts (D009, F003), destroys checkable invariants (D003-class coherence), and imposes O(sites × variants) regeneration costs on the machine writer. Crucially, keeping dyn forfeits nothing the switch alternative offers: under closed-world module semantics (D008 amendment) the compiler can derive the switch/direct-call lowering from a dyn call site; nothing can derive dyn's semantic facts back out of a hand-written table.
result.coreArgument: The dyn construct is a semantic carrier, not a convenience, and the defense rests on the three admissible legs. (1) OPTIMIZER NECESSITY: an explicit dyn call preserves into the compiler the fact "this is a dispatch over the closed set of checked impls of interface I, through a compiler-generated constant table paired with a payload of known bound." That fact is exactly what D007-class machinery consumes — devirtualization to direct calls, speculative guarded devirtualization, indirect inlining once targets become known — and what closed-world lowering needs to convert a dyn call into the very switch or guarded direct call the alternative would hand-write, chosen per call site by cost model rather than frozen at generation time. The information flow is one-directional: from the high-level construct the compiler can derive any lowering (F009's workflow-confirmed lesson: keep performance-critical semantics visible to optimizer passes, decide lowering in the compiler); from a hand-rolled fn-pointer table it can derive almost nothing — that representation is literally the corpus's documented defeat mode (D009: Function-typed fields hide call targets and defeat optimization even inside fully closed programs), and its void*-erased payload blocks the frontend from emitting the F003 noalias/alias.scope and type-derived dereferenceable/alignment facts that are the corpus's best-attested optimization enablers (F001–F003). The round-1 record already names this failure: A1's own risk list predicts hand-rolled void*/fn-pointer erasure "that the optimizer and F003 machinery handle worse." (2) VERIFICATION NECESSITY: the language construct packages three invariants the compiler checks and the alternative demotes to conventions: (a) dyn-compatibility of the interface so the runtime representation is coherent (D003 — precedented restriction); (b) coherence — the type-to-method-table binding is compiler-generated from exactly one checked impl, so a wrong-slot binding is impossible by construction; (c) the (data-pointer, table) pairing is established at a checked coercion, so "this erased pointer really is a T matching this table" is a type-system invariant. Under fn-pointer tables, a slot swap between two same-signature methods type-checks and misdispatches silently, table layout consistency across construction sites is unchecked, and payload/table provenance is locally unverifiable. The proposed exhaustiveness check catches MISSING switch arms only — it cannot catch a wrong function bound in a present arm. Under this charter — a writer fallible at scale that cannot be trusted with unchecked invariants — verification necessity weighs heavier than in human languages, and the alternative deletes precisely the checks that convert wrong-binding bugs from runtime mysteries into impl-site compile errors. (3) MACHINE-WRITER FAILURE MODES OF THE ALTERNATIVE: switch-over-variants requires a globally closed enumeration; adding variant M+1 is a whole-program edit touching every dispatch site (or every per-method dispatcher), regenerating O(sites × variants) near-duplicate arms — the canonical consistency-drift setting — and triggering a recompilation cascade across every unit containing a switch (D004 cost class) inside the AI iteration loop; with dyn, the same change is one new impl block, checked once, surfacing one machine-readable diagnostic at one location. The alternative also puts the writer in the loop for what is a compiler cost decision: choosing switch vs table vs guarded call per site, and re-choosing when profiles change, means regenerating source instead of recompiling (against the F009 lesson and the round-1 static-vs-profile direction of profile-data-for-cost-decisions). Honest concessions: the dyn cost model must not promise devirtualization (D007 documents conditional, revertible, option-dependent recovery only; LLVM WPD is uncarded); D003 restrictions leave the generic-methods-on-dyn composition hole open (a documented round-1 follow-up, kept as a known limitation); enum-based dispatch remains the sanctioned and preferred tool for closed sets; and heterogeneous storage via enums pays max-variant-size padding where trait objects pay uniform pointer+table (D002's representation), an uncarded but arithmetic representation fact. No quantitative dispatch measurements exist anywhere in the corpus (caveats.md), so every magnitude claim here is a cost-class claim, not a measured one.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Positive scope explicitly supports making dynamic dispatch explicit-when-used with an optimizer-visible representation and cost — the exact restricted construct being defended; its cost honesty (lookup cost, blocked inlining) is why the keep is explicit-only with an indirect-call cost model.

Attests that a language can restrict dyn-compatible interfaces to keep the runtime dispatch representation coherent — the verification-necessity leg: these compiler-checked representation/coherence invariants are what the fn-pointer-table alternative demotes to unchecked conventions. Also sources the conceded composition hole (no generic dispatchable methods).

The strongest card against the alternative's open-set half: Function-typed fields hide call targets and cause dynamic-dispatch/indirect-storage defeats even in fully closed programs — hand-rolled fn-pointer tables ARE Function-typed fields, so the alternative mandates the documented optimizer-defeat representation.

Devirtualization, speculative guarded devirtualization, and indirect inlining exist for compiler-recognized virtual calls — i.e., the dyn construct is what makes this machinery applicable; its negative scope (conditional, revertible, option-dependent) simultaneously disciplines the keep: recovery is upside, never the promised cost model.

Frontend-emitted noalias/alias.scope facts are the corpus's best-attested optimization enablers and are sound only if the frontend knows the semantics; void*-erased payloads in hand-rolled tables block that emission at every dispatch boundary — the round-1 record itself flags hand-rolled erasure as something 'the optimizer and F003 machinery handle worse.'

Workflow-confirmed (highest-confidence class in corpus): performance-critical semantics should stay visible to optimizer passes so lowering is decided in the compiler — dispatch-over-interface is such a semantic; the alternative bakes one lowering into generated source and destroys the fact.

Interposition/linking semantics can block IPA even for statically-known code — supports the condition under which the compiler can derive the switch lowering from dyn (no-interposition, closed-world modules, the A2 amendment already attached to the round-1 leading candidate).

Compilation-unit boundaries trade compile time against optimization — grounds the cost class of the alternative's recompilation cascade: every variant addition invalidates every unit containing a regenerated exhaustive switch, inside the AI iteration loop.

Monomorphization's code-size/compile-time negative scope grounds the cost class the switch alternative inherits and amplifies: exhaustive per-site enumeration is manual defunctionalization with the same code-growth exposure, unquantified.

Documents guard/invalidation machinery as the price paid when dispatch facts are not static — supporting evidence that keeping the target-set fact as a checked language construct is the cheap channel for the same information dynamic runtimes reconstruct expensively.

machineWriterCosts: Token blowup of the alternative: O(dispatch sites × variants) switch arms (or O(interfaces × methods × variants) table slots) generated, and re-read on every context load; the dyn construct costs O(impls) + O(call sites). Adding one variant rewrites every dispatch site instead of adding one impl block. Asymptotics are arithmetic; magnitudes EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Compile-time in the AI iteration loop: variant addition under the alternative invalidates every translation unit containing a regenerated switch or table (D004 cost class), and whole-program exhaustiveness must be re-checked per edit; under dyn, one impl unit recompiles. No corpus quantification exists.

Diagnostics asymmetry: with dyn + coherence, 'type T lacks method m of interface I' is one machine-readable error at the impl site; with the alternative, missing-arm errors surface at N scattered sites, and wrong-arm bindings (correct signature, wrong function) surface never at compile time — only as runtime misbehavior the AI must debug without a diagnostic anchor.

Writer-in-the-loop for compiler decisions: the alternative freezes the dispatch lowering (switch vs table vs guarded call) into source at generation time; re-optimizing after profile data or workload change requires regenerating source across all sites rather than recompiling — the writer becomes the optimizer's actuator (against F009's lesson and the static-vs-profile round-1 direction).

Global closure requirement: switch-over-variants forces a single whole-program enum per interface, making every extension a cross-module edit and serializing what dyn keeps as independent, separately-checkable impl additions.
consistencyRisks: Alternative (drift): N near-duplicate exhaustive switches over the same variant set must be kept identical as the set evolves; exhaustiveness checking catches missing arms but NOT a hallucinated wrong-function binding in a present arm — silent misdispatch. Rate of such drift in AI writers: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Alternative (drift): parallel fn-pointer tables must keep slot order and slot-function bindings consistent across every construction site; slot swaps between same-signature functions type-check, so drift between table copies is invisible to the checker by construction.

Alternative (drift): the global closed enum definition is replicated knowledge across every dispatching module's context window; partial regeneration risks divergent copies of the variant list. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Keep-side risk: AI writers may overuse the explicit dyn construct where monomorphization was intended, silently trading performance for brevity — mitigation is syntactic explicitness plus a machine-readable cost diagnostic at every dyn call site, but the mitigation's effectiveness on AI writers is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Keep-side risk: the aliasing story must be explicitly specified at dyn boundaries (what F001/F003 facts the erased payload retains), or the language's flagship non-interference guarantees become inconsistent exactly at dispatch points — this is the round-1 decisive follow-up check, not yet run.

Keep-side risk: the unresolved charter question (predictable cost model vs guaranteed-optimization floor — may the compiler silently devirtualize a provably-unique dyn call?) leaves generated code and optimizer behavior potentially inconsistent until answered; it is antecedent per the round-1 record.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI-writer consistency-drift rates across near-duplicate switch arms, table constructions, or replicated enum definitions — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence.
The claim that AI writers will fall back to hand-rolled void*/fn-pointer erasure when no sanctioned dyn construct exists (predicted in round-1 A1's risk list, but as inference, not measurement).
Magnitude of token/context cost differences between O(sites × variants) and O(impls) representations on real workloads.

Whether dyn-boundary erasure measurably loses F003 noalias/alias.scope precision versus per-variant code, and at what cost — the construction-level check the round-1 critic identified as decisive; not yet performed.

Whether the compiler-derived closed-world lowering (dyn → switch/direct calls) actually materializes on an LLVM target: LLVM whole-program devirtualization (!type metadata, -fwhole-program-vtables) has no evidence card; D007 is GCC-only and conditional per its negative scope.
All load-bearing dispatch cards (D001, D002, D003, D004, D007, D008, D009) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule).
No quantitative measurements anywhere in the corpus of dispatch strategies on runtime, code size, i-cache, or compile time — every cost comparison above is a cost-class argument, not a measured one.
Whether the alternative's recompilation cascade is material in practice for an AI iteration loop (D004 gives the cost class only).
The max-variant-size padding cost of enum-based heterogeneous storage versus pointer+table trait objects is representation arithmetic but uncarded and unmeasured.

result.stance: KEEP — parametric generics remain in the core language, as generic templates type-checked once against declared bounds and expanded compiler-side (round-1's leading candidate: guaranteed static specialization as the only implicit path, explicit restricted dyn separate). The checked-copy alternative is strictly dominated: its own CHECK mechanism already contains parametric generics, so cutting the feature does not achieve the null hypothesis — it only moves the expansion step from the compiler to the fallible writer.
result.coreArgument: The defense is a dilemma inside the alternative plus two foreclosure arguments, all admissible under optimizer necessity, verification necessity, and machine-writer failure modes. (1) THE CHECK IS GENERICS. To 'declare and CHECK that copies instantiate a common declared template/intent,' the language must contain a template sub-language with type parameters, generic type-checking of the template against bounds (otherwise well-typedness-for-all-instantiations is unverifiable and errors surface per-copy, post-expansion), and a computable instantiation relation so the checker can verify copy == template[T]. But if the compiler can compute template[T] to check a copy, it can emit template[T] directly — compiler-side monomorphization is a strict subset of the checker's own work. D001 attests compiler monomorphization already yields runtime behavior identical to hand-written concrete code, so AI-written copies add ZERO optimizer information: identical machine code from strictly more source. The alternative retains essentially all of generics' language complexity and discards only the deterministic expansion step — the one part machines do perfectly and writers do redundantly. (2) THE DILEMMA. Either the check is full substitution-equality — then every copy is pure redundancy (O(instantiation-closure) token mass, per-copy parse/typecheck/verify compile time inside the AI iteration loop, regeneration cascade on every template edit, no new facts), or the check is weaker (an 'intent' contract) — then copies can diverge behaviorally inside the contract's blind spots, institutionalizing an unverifiable drift surface in a charter where verification matters MORE because the writer is fallible at scale. Contracts strong enough to pin full behavioral equivalence to a template ARE the template plus substitution. The alternative's own inclusion of a CHECK concedes drift is expected; this dilemma does not depend on measuring drift rates. (3) OPTIMIZER NECESSITY. The fact lost is not per-instance information (concrete copies carry MORE local facts — conceded) but cross-copy identity and deferral of the specialize/share decision. With language generics the optimizer knows N instantiations are one body scheme: it can deduplicate across codegen units (D004 context; rustc share-generics is a named round-1 follow-up), share bodies where representation permits (D005, the corpus's only carded bounded-code-growth generics strategy), instantiate lazily on demand (D005, D010), and throttle the over-specialization compile-time/cache pressure D009's negative scope documents. Source-level pre-expansion destroys generic structure before the IR exists: F009 — the only workflow-confirmed dispatch-adjacent card — supports preserving high-level semantics into IR so the optimizer decides, and is the basis of the Swift-style 'fourth model' (annotated/bounded specialization) that round-1's judge explicitly held open pending measurement, ruling that 'if bloat proves severe, the leader should shift toward a bounded-sharing or Swift-style hybrid.' AI-side monomorphization forecloses that entire held-open axis irreversibly at the source level: there is no compiler knob to un-expand; remediation means regenerating the whole expanded corpus. (4) VERIFICATION NECESSITY. A template checked generically once yields a universally quantified proof covering every future instantiation; per-template obligations (F003-class noalias/alias.scope discharge, purity, contract obligations from the violation-semantics track) transfer to instantiations by substitution soundness. Under checked copies every proof re-runs per copy, scaling verification time with the instantiation closure — and any weakening of the check to make this tractable reopens (2). Note also that round-1's decisive follow-up (can a shared body discharge F003 obligations?) presupposes IR-visible generic bodies; the alternative deletes the object of the core's own open verification program. (5) WRITER-SIDE WHOLE-PROGRAM ANALYSIS. Which copies must exist is a transitive fixpoint over the call graph (generic calling generic at the caller's parameters). Compilers compute this during compilation via on-demand collection (D001/D005/D010 precedents). The alternative shifts this whole-program analysis onto the writer at generation time: misses are late build errors; adding one new type triggers regeneration of expansions in every downstream module — compile-time and token cost in the AI loop, both charter-admissible. HONEST LIMITS: no quantitative card measures mono-vs-sharing anywhere in the corpus (round-1 evidence gap #1); all load-bearing D-cards are provisional per the caveats standing rule; every claim about AI drift rates and token economics is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED. STRONGEST PARTIAL KEEP if the tribunal narrows: monomorphization-only generics — templates checked pre-instantiation, expansion always compiler-side, polymorphic recursion and higher-rank parameters excluded so the instantiation closure stays decidable and compile time bounded, dyn dispatch remaining a separate explicit restricted construct (D002/D003). That preserves round-1's design_candidate_later skeleton while conceding every contested extension.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Rust monomorphization yields runtime behavior identical to concrete type-specific code — so compiler-side expansion already delivers everything AI-side expansion could deliver at the machine-code level, making the copies optimizer-redundant; its negative scope (unquantified code-size/compile-time tradeoffs) flags exactly the axis that maximal source-level expansion worsens with no compiler throttle.

.NET's hybrid (specialize value types, share one body across reference types, lazy instantiation) is the only carded bounded-code-growth generics strategy; it requires the compiler to know instantiations are one template — a fact textual AI-written copies destroy, foreclosing the sharing option round-1 explicitly held open pending measurement.

Julia evidence that over-specialization causes compile-time and cache pressure; AI-side full expansion commits the maximal point on this axis at the source level and removes the compiler's ability to throttle it.

Julia compiles specializations on demand for concrete argument tuples — instantiation demand is discovered during compilation, i.e., it is a whole-program analysis; the alternative shifts that fixpoint computation onto the AI writer at generation time.

Optimization depends on codegen-unit/LTO boundaries; expanded copies inflate every unit, and cross-unit instantiation deduplication (rustc share-generics, a named round-1 follow-up for bounding mono cost) requires compiler-known cross-copy identity that near-duplicate textual copies lose — structural merging needs identity that AI drift breaks.

The corpus's only workflow-confirmed dispatch-adjacent card: Swift SIL preserves high-level semantic information so optimization decisions can be made that LLVM could not otherwise see — direct support for keeping generic structure alive to the optimizer, and the basis of the fourth model (IR-level generics, optimizer- or annotation-decided specialization) that source-level pre-expansion forecloses irreversibly.

LLVM noalias/alias.scope facts are sound only if frontend obligations are discharged; with a generic template, discharge arguments are established once and transfer per-instantiation by substitution, whereas per-copy checking re-runs the obligation N times — and round-1's decisive follow-up (shared-body F003 discharge) presupposes IR-visible generic bodies that the alternative deletes.

Attests that a restricted, dyn-compatible explicit dynamic-dispatch construct is precedented — the shape of the partial keep: generics stay as the sole implicit path (round-1 leading candidate), dyn stays separate and explicit; cutting generics would gut one of only two topics that reached design_candidate_later status.

Dynamic dispatch has optimizer-visible representation and cost and should be explicit when used — supporting the kept architecture in which static specialization via generics is the default and the only implicit path, rather than pushing heterogeneous needs into hand-rolled erasure.

machineWriterCosts: Token/context blowup: source mass scales with the transitive instantiation closure (templates x type arguments x call-graph depth), all of which must be generated, stored, and re-read into context on every edit; the generic template is O(1) in the number of instantiations. Magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, direction forced by construction.

Regeneration cascade: any template/intent edit obligates rewriting every copy in every downstream module before the build can pass; each rewrite is a fresh hallucination opportunity, and each caught divergence adds a full compile-check-regenerate iteration to the AI loop.

Writer-side whole-program analysis: deciding which copies must exist is a call-graph fixpoint that compilers perform internally via on-demand collection (D001/D005/D010); the writer must either re-implement that collector as external tooling (the feature moves, not disappears) or guess — undershoot surfaces as late build errors, overshoot as dead expanded code.

Compile-time in the loop: the frontend must parse, typecheck, and verify every expanded copy plus run template-conformance equivalence checks on all of them every build, versus typechecking one template and instantiating on demand with caching; F003-class verification obligations re-run per copy, so verification time scales with the closure.

Diagnostic noise: one root-cause error at the template level manifests as N near-duplicate per-copy divergence reports, degrading the machine-readable diagnostics channel the charter treats as a first-class cost.

Irreversibility: if code-size/i-cache costs (D001/D009 negative scopes) prove severe in measurement, a generics language flips a compiler strategy (share/dedup/outline); the expanded-source language must regenerate its entire code corpus — the round-1 judge's contingency plan becomes structurally unavailable.
consistencyRisks: Weak-check drift: if conformance checking is anything less than full substitution-equality, copies can diverge behaviorally inside the check's blind spots — silent, unverifiable drift in exactly the regime where the charter says verification matters more because the writer is fallible at scale.
Stale-but-valid copies: after a template edit, copies that were missed during regeneration may still pass a weak intent check, persisting semantic skew across builds indefinitely.

Cross-module duplicate instantiations (two modules independently needing the same specialization) drift apart textually, defeating structural deduplication and link-time merging that require bit-identical bodies; recovering identity then demands semantic equivalence checking, undecidable in general.

Mid-edit version skew: every multi-step AI edit opens a window where template and copies disagree; only the compile-time check closes it, converting each drift event into extra iteration-loop churn rather than preventing it.

The self-defeating fix: adopting full substitution-equality checking eliminates drift only by making every copy a deterministic, compiler-recomputable artifact — i.e., the consistency risk is removed exactly by collapsing the alternative back into parametric generics plus a redundant echo of the compiler's own output.
empiricalUntested: AI consistency-drift rates across near-duplicate specializations — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence; the alternative's CHECK presumes drift exists but no card measures it.
Token/context-cost scaling of maintaining expanded instantiation sets versus single generic templates in real AI generation/editing workflows.
Whether AI writers can reliably compute whole-program instantiation closures (including transitive generic-calls-generic demands) without compiler tooling.

Whether AI hand-tuning of individual specializations could ever outperform compiler-side specialization — the alternative's only conceivable performance upside, which its own consistency CHECK forbids anyway.

Quantitative deltas of monomorphization vs shared-body vs dictionary-passing on runtime, code size, i-cache, and compile time — round-1's number-one evidence gap; no quantitative cards exist, so all code-bloat claims (mine and the prosecution's) are directional only.
Whether single-definition generic code actually reduces AI generation error rates relative to repeated expansion — the pro-generics behavioral claim is exactly as untested as the anti-generics one.

All load-bearing dispatch cards (D001-D010) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule); the F-cards cited are workflow-confirmed but do not address generics implementation strategy directly.

result.stance: CUT. Closures and higher-order functions should be excluded from the core language. Defunctionalization — top-level functions, explicit environment structs, and compile-time (static, monomorphized) function parameters, with no capture semantics anywhere in the language — carries the load.
result.coreArgument: Five counts, each anchored in the corpus. COUNT 1 — Closures are an implicit dynamic-dispatch channel that contradicts the project's own leading design. Round-1 dispatch-generics reached design_candidate_later on 'guaranteed static specialization as the core's only implicit path, with dynamic dispatch available solely through an explicit, restricted construct' (round1-dispatch-generics.md). A first-class closure is an anonymous function pointer plus a hidden environment whose call sites are indirect: D002 documents that indirect dispatch adds lookup cost and prevents inlining, and D009 documents that Function-typed fields hide representation and call targets and force dynamic dispatch even inside a fully closed program — the dispatch debate's critic used exactly this card to refute closed-world optimism, and the debate's own evidence-gap list concedes 'dispatch through first-class functions and closures is unaddressed by every position.' Admitting closures into the core smuggles back, implicitly and at every call site, the construct the leading candidate only permits explicitly and restrictedly. Defunctionalization keeps every call first-order: either a direct call to a named top-level function, a D001-style monomorphized static function parameter (guaranteed specialization, the corpus's best-carded generics mechanism), or an explicit exhaustive dispatch over a closed enum of behaviors — finite, optimizer-visible target sets, matching D003's precedent that restricting dispatch interfaces keeps runtime representation coherent. COUNT 2 — Closures attack the corpus's only adversarially verified payload: non-interference around writes (F001, F002, F003). A call through an opaque captured function value has unknown mod-ref against everything reachable from its invisible environment; misleading-comparisons note 2 explicitly names callbacks among the things that break ownership-to-noalias lowering, and the aliasing-model verdict records that 'no position has a carded story for higher-order functions/callbacks.' The implementation capability map's 'closure capture' row is 100% TBD — no verified lowering exists from capture semantics to any IR fact. Shipping closures in the core means shipping a feature with no verified story for the one optimization class the corpus proves matters, while an explicit env struct is an ordinary struct that inherits the aliasing-model design candidate (checked ownership/exclusive mutation) for free. COUNT 3 — Implicit capture forces the compiler into conditional recovery, the corpus's documented losing channel. Captured environments must be allocated somewhere; recovering that cost requires escape analysis, and the verified cards F007 and J004 establish that escape-analysis recovery is conditional elimination and scalar replacement — never guaranteed stack allocation — while J001/J002 show that facts not stated statically must be reconstructed at runtime with guards and invalidation machinery. Explicit environment structs make allocation, lifetime, and ownership writer-stated and checker-verified: guarantee-by-construction, the channel whose qualitative superiority over recovery-by-analysis is the most consistent asymmetry in the whole corpus (D007 negative scope, D008, F007, misleading-comparisons note 3). COUNT 4 — Verification economics, which this charter weights above everything. The writer is fallible at scale, so every implicit inference the compiler performs — capture set, capture mode (by-value/by-ref/by-move), environment layout, Send/Sync-like sharing capabilities of an invisible struct (C004), lifetimes of hidden captured references — is an invariant the writer never states and the verifier must reconstruct and then explain back through diagnostics about a struct that appears nowhere in the source. Defunctionalization converts all of it into ordinary declared facts: the capture list IS the struct definition; the ownership checker checks it with machinery already committed to; diagnostics are ordinary field/ownership/exhaustiveness errors in a vocabulary the toolchain must support anyway. The corpus's repeated meta-finding is that restriction buys checkability (F006 SPARK — which also requires side-effect-free function calls, an in-corpus precedent of a verification-first language severely restricting function values; A008 Futhark; F001 Rust exclusivity), and the arrays-loops rationale states outright that every genuinely checked precedent achieved checkability by restricting the model. Cutting capture semantics also deletes an entire compiler subsystem (capture inference, closure conversion, per-lambda anonymous types, an Fn/FnMut/FnOnce-like trait hierarchy, core-language escape analysis) from the AI iteration loop's compile path and from the verifier's soundness surface. COUNT 5 — The workload does not need the feature. The dominant consumer of higher-order code is iteration (map/filter/reduce), and the arrays-loops leading candidate already carries it with first-order structured constructs: A002 (iteration spaces, indexing maps, iterator kinds as first-class ops), A003 (affine restriction making dependence analysis tractable), A010 (independence-encoding loop forms). Generic parameterization by behavior is carried by static function parameters under D001 monomorphization. The residue — genuinely runtime behavior selection — is precisely where D009 documents the optimizer defeat, so it should cost explicit syntax (env struct + closed enum dispatch), not be an ambient core feature. The only classic argument left for closures is human ergonomics, which is inadmissible under this charter. The null hypothesis stands unrebutted by any verified card: nothing in the corpus supports paying for capture semantics in the core, three separate corpus artifacts (dispatch verdict, aliasing verdict, capability map) independently flag closures as an unsolved hole, and the coverage matrix rates the closures/captures evidence low-to-medium confidence resting on a single unverified card. Cut it; make the machine write the structs it would have made the compiler infer, and make the checker check them.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's most direct closure card: Julia guidance documents Function-typed fields hiding representation and call targets, causing dynamic dispatch/indirect storage — closures as function values are an optimizer defeat even in fully closed programs. Cited by the dispatch-generics critic against closed-world claims; the verdict's gap list says no position handles closure/function-value dispatch.

Indirect (vtable/function-pointer) dispatch adds lookup cost and prevents inlining, blocking downstream optimization; its positive scope explicitly supports making dynamic dispatch EXPLICIT when used — closures make it implicit and pervasive, defunctionalized enum dispatch makes it explicit and restricted.

Monomorphization gives concrete-code runtime behavior — the mechanism that lets static (compile-time) function parameters replace most higher-order use with guaranteed specialization, so the alternative's load-bearing path is the corpus's best-carded generics mechanism.

Precedent that restricting which interfaces may be dynamically dispatched keeps runtime representation coherent — supports a closed, explicit, enum-based behavior-dispatch construct instead of open first-class function values.

Verified 3-0: HotSpot escape analysis yields conditional allocation elimination/scalar replacement, never guaranteed stack allocation — the exact analysis regime implicit closure-capture allocation forces a compiler into; explicit env structs avoid it by construction.
Primary-vendor sharpening of F007: escape analysis explicitly does not convert heap to stack allocation — runtime/analysis recovery of closure-environment costs is conditional, not a guarantee.

What is not expressed statically must be recovered by profiling and guards; an opaque closure's call target is exactly the fact dynamic runtimes burn inline-cache machinery to reconstruct — keeping the language first-order keeps the channel static.

Speculative recovery requires guard/deoptimization machinery — the price of hiding facts (like call targets behind function values) rather than stating them; a simple extreme-performance AOT compiler should not be architected to need it.

The flagship verified payload is language-level non-interference; hidden captured references are aliases inside an invisible struct, threatening exactly this machinery, while explicit env structs participate in ordinary exclusivity rules.

Alias value is proving non-interference around writes; a call through an opaque function value has unknown mod-ref against everything reachable from its environment, forcing conservative treatment of all optimizations F002 enumerates (register caching, read/write elimination, reordering).

LLVM's noalias/alias.scope carriers require sound frontend-discharged obligations; the capability map's 'closure capture' row is entirely TBD — no verified path exists from capture semantics to these carriers, whereas struct fields and direct calls have the carded path.

SPARK, the corpus's verification-first precedent, buys checkability by restriction — including requiring expressions and function calls to be side-effect free — in-corpus precedent that a verification-oriented language can and does ship with severely restricted function-value semantics.

Send/Sync-style type-level sharing capabilities (the concurrency leading candidate) must be computed for every captured environment — an invisible struct the writer never declared; explicit env structs make sharing capabilities ordinary declared-type facts.

MLIR Linalg shows the optimizer-friendly encoding of 'loop body over data' is structured first-order ops with iteration spaces, indexing maps, and iterator kinds — not opaque lambdas passed to combinators; the iteration workload closures usually serve is carried without them.
Affine restriction makes dependence analysis efficient and reliable — first-class restricted loop constructs, not higher-order combinators, are the carded path for the map/filter/reduce workload.
Fortran DO CONCURRENT: loop forms that encode independence intent and restrict effects — further precedent that iteration structure belongs in first-order constructs the optimizer can read.

Devirtualization/indirect-call recovery is conditional, speculative, revertible, and option-dependent — the backstop a closure-heavy design would lean on is exactly the recovery channel the corpus rates below source-level guarantees.

machineWriterCosts: Token expansion per behavior-passing site: a lambda is one expression; the defunctionalized form is an env struct declaration, a top-level function, a construction site, and (for runtime selection) an enum variant plus dispatch arm. Generation and re-read context cost per callback site goes up, and the writer must re-read the struct definition to use it correctly. Magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Global namespace pressure: every former lambda needs a stable top-level name and an env-struct name; the symbol inventory the writer must keep consistent across a large codebase grows. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED whether this measurably raises AI error rates.

Central edit points: closed apply/dispatch enums mean adding a behavior touches a central definition plus the dispatch site, increasing the cross-file context window needed per edit and serializing parallel AI generation on hot enums (merge-conflict surface). Partially mitigable by sharding dispatch enums per subsystem; mitigation efficacy EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Compile-time in the iteration loop is a trade, not a pure win: aggressive monomorphization of static function parameters has uncarded code-size/compile-time costs (D001 and D009 negative scopes) — though a closure-capable compiler pays capture inference, closure conversion, per-lambda anonymous types, and trait-hierarchy solving instead; the net direction is unmeasured.

Offsetting credit the defense must not double-count: diagnostics improve for a machine reader — capture-mode/hidden-lifetime errors about invisible compiler-generated structs are replaced by ordinary field/ownership/exhaustiveness errors over structures that literally appear in the source, which is a smaller machine-readable diagnostic vocabulary. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
consistencyRisks: Near-duplicate environment structs proliferate (many callbacks capture similar state); AI drift across near-copies — hallucinated field names, transposed same-typed fields, stale cloned structs — is the classic failure mode. Type-checking catches renames but NOT semantically-wrong same-type wiring; needs a lint/canonical-schema rule (e.g., required construction-by-field-name, no positional init).

Two sanctioned encodings for 'parameterize by behavior' (compile-time static function parameter vs runtime env-struct + enum dispatch) invite inconsistent selection across AI-generated modules; without one enforced canonical decision rule (static unless runtime selection is proven needed), the codebase drifts into mixed idioms that defeat whole-program analysis assumptions.

Env-struct/behavior-function contract drift: the invariant 'this env struct is only ever passed to this function family' is a convention, not a language rule, once capture semantics are cut; an AI writer can construct a well-typed env and pass it to the wrong apply. Mitigation: newtype-per-behavior or sealed association enforced by the checker — which quietly re-adds a small language feature and should be priced honestly.

Central dispatch enums become contention points under concurrent AI edits (conflicting variant additions, unreachable stale arms). Exhaustiveness checking catches missing arms — a genuine verification win — but does not catch duplicated or dead behaviors.

If the standard library or codegen templates ever regrow an ad-hoc 'function value' convention (void*-style env + fn-pointer pairs) without language blessing, the optimizer faces the worst of both worlds — the dispatch verdict's own warning about hand-rolled erasure that F003 machinery handles worse. The cut only wins if the defunctionalization pattern is a first-class, verifier-checked convention, not folklore.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI-writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence: token-cost deltas of lambdas vs defunctionalized boilerplate, drift/hallucination rates on near-duplicate env structs, error rates on central enum edits, and machine consumption of capture-checker vs struct/ownership diagnostics are all unmeasured.

No quantitative performance evidence anywhere in the corpus (caveats.md): the runtime delta between indirect closure calls, defunctionalized enum dispatch, and direct/monomorphized calls is unquantified; D002/D007/D009 are qualitative and D-cards are provisional direct extractions awaiting adversarial verification.

D009 itself — the prosecution's most direct card — is rated low-to-medium confidence in feature-evidence-coverage.csv and needs adversarial verification; it is also Julia-specific (a dynamic-dispatch JIT), so its transfer to a static AOT language is an inference.

Whether closure capture COULD be soundly lowered to F003 noalias/alias.scope facts is untested in either direction — the capability-map row is TBD; the defense could in principle close this hole with a Rust-like capture-borrowing design (Rust does exactly this), so the prosecution's Count 2 rests on absence of verified evidence, not proof of impossibility.

The workload fraction of higher-order usage NOT covered by structured loops (A002/A003/A010) plus static function parameters (D001) is unknown; if callbacks/async/event-driven code dominates real AI-generated workloads, the defunctionalization tax is paid far more often than this case assumes.

SPARK's restriction of function values (F006 side-effect-free calls) demonstrates viability for a verification-first niche language, not for a general extreme-performance language with async/concurrent workloads — the transfer is untested.

Compile-time direction is unmeasured: whether deleting capture inference/closure conversion/anonymous types beats the added monomorphization and boilerplate-volume costs in the AI iteration loop has no card.

result.stance: KEEP, restricted form. Keep statically-typed, unboxed closures with unique anonymous types and compiler-managed capture — but with explicit, compiler-VERIFIED capture lists (declared per-variable mode checked against the body's free-variable set) instead of silent inference, and with no implicit erasure to function pointers or dyn-callable types: type-erased/stored function values are available only through the dispatch-generics verdict's explicit restricted dynamic construct (D002/D003 pattern). Cut the parts of the classic feature that are ergonomic sugar: capture inference opacity, implicit boxing, and implicit closure-to-function-pointer coercion.
result.coreArgument: A closure in a monomorphizing, ownership-checked language IS defunctionalization — an environment struct plus a code pointer with a unique type — performed by the compiler. So the alternative does not remove the pattern; it transfers the lowering from a verified compiler transformation to an unenforceable multi-site convention executed by a writer the charter itself declares fallible at scale. Three admissible losses follow. (1) Optimizer necessity: unique anonymous closure types make every higher-order call site monomorphize to a statically known direct call (D001), the only implicit path the dispatch-generics leading candidate permits. Under the alternative the language cannot force the writer to use the disciplined struct+callable-trait encoding; the path of least resistance is function pointers or tag-dispatch, which is exactly D009's documented optimizer defeat (Function-typed values hiding call targets inside fully closed programs) paying D002-class indirect-call/no-inlining costs, with recovery only via D007-class devirtualization that the corpus records as conditional, revertible, and option-dependent — a conditional-recovery-vs-source-guarantee asymmetry the corpus explicitly warns against treating as equivalent (misleading-comparisons #3, J001/J002). Anonymity is itself a fact: closure types are unnameable and unexportable by construction, so D008-class ABI/interposition surfaces cannot form around them, whereas named env structs can leak into module boundaries. (2) Verification necessity: with compiler-managed capture, three invariants are checked or true by construction — the env/code pairing (type-level), the correspondence between each environment field and the enclosing-scope variable it snapshots (no re-encoding step exists to get wrong), and capture-mode facts (by-ref/by-move/exclusive) that feed the ownership core and discharge F003 noalias-style obligations soundly (F001/F002). Under the alternative, packing is ordinary field assignment: transposed fields with coincident types (`env.lo = hi`), stale packing before a final update, and mismatched (env, fn) pairs all type-check silently; no checker can flag them because the intent is never expressed. The charter's premise — unchecked invariants will be violated at scale — makes this decisive: the alternative's BEST case is byte-identical to the closure lowering, but the language cannot verify the writer used it. (3) Machine-writer failure modes: the alternative amplifies every body edit into 3+ coordinated distant edits (struct, packing sites, body) in near-duplicate boilerplate — the canonical consistency-drift zone — and multiplies token/context cost per higher-order use; this tax compounds because the arrays-loops leading candidate (structured-arrays-first, A002/A006) parameterizes essentially every array operation by a computation body, so cutting closures taxes another topic's leading design. The proposed restriction (checked explicit capture lists) concedes the alternative's one real virtue — explicitness — while keeping the capture declaration a compiler-verified redundancy rather than either silent inference or unchecked convention, matching the violation-semantics direction that compiler-proven contracts are the assumable kind. I flag honestly: compile-time is not a winning axis for closures (monomorphization costs are unquantified, D001 negative scope), and Send/Sync-style concurrency checking survives the alternative at rough parity (C004 auto-traits work on plain structs).

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Documents Function-typed fields hiding call targets as an optimizer defeat inside fully closed programs — exactly where defunctionalized code lands when the writer stores raw function pointers; the round1-dispatch-generics judge separately flags closure/function-value dispatch as an unaddressed gap affecting iterators, callbacks, and async in every candidate design.

Monomorphization compiles generics to concrete specialized code; unique closure types are the enabling fact that lets higher-order functions monomorphize into guaranteed direct calls with zero indirect dispatch — the fact the compiler loses when call targets arrive as writer-chosen function pointers.

Establishes the cost class of the alternative's fallback path: indirect dispatch through erased function values adds lookup cost and blocks inlining of the callee body, defeating downstream optimization.

Its negative scope shows recovery from indirect calls (devirtualization, indirect inlining) is conditional, revertible, and option-dependent — so writer-written indirect calls are not reliably converted back to the direct calls closures guarantee by construction.

Provides the precedent for the partial-keep shape: a deliberately restricted, explicit construct for type-erased callables (dyn-compatible pattern), quarantining the D009 defeat instead of letting it be the default encoding.

Language-level non-interference is the central optimizer enabler; compiler-managed capture modes tie closure environments into exactly this machinery (exclusive capture = checked exclusive borrow), keeping closure bodies inside the aliasing core rather than outside it.

Register promotion, read/write elimination, and reordering depend on proving non-modification through other paths; inferred-and-checked capture modes give the compiler those facts about environment fields, whereas writer-chosen sharing (defensive clones/copies to satisfy the checker) silently degrades them.

LLVM noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-bearing promises that are only sound if the frontend's obligations are true; compiler-derived capture facts are a sound discharge source, while hand-packed environment structs make the discharge depend on writer-asserted field types at every site.

The SIL lesson: performance-critical semantics should stay visible to optimizer passes rather than be pre-lowered. Source-level hand defunctionalization erases the fact 'this is a closure with this capture set' before the compiler ever sees it; a language-level construct lets the compiler own representation choice (unboxing, SROA, specialization) per instance.

Linalg-style structured array ops are parameterized by a computation body; the arrays-loops leading candidate therefore presupposes cheap, typed body-passing — cutting closures multiplies defunctionalization boilerplate across every structured-array combinator use.
Halide's algorithm/schedule separation likewise assumes computations are first-class values to be scheduled; the same dependency of another topic's leading design on higher-order body-passing.

Exported symbols and semantic interposition restrict interprocedural analysis; anonymous closure types cannot be named or exported so this boundary cannot form around them, while writer-named env structs can leak into public ABI surfaces.

Cited honestly for near-parity: Send/Sync auto-traits work on plain env structs too, so concurrency race-checking largely survives the alternative; the residual closure advantage is only minimal inferred capture sets avoiding over-capture rejections.

When the static channel for call-target/shape facts is absent, runtimes must reconstruct them with profiling and guards; supports the general principle that losing a statically guaranteed fact converts it into a conditional, guarded recovery problem.
Documents the invalidation machinery and cost side of speculative recovery — the price paid when facts like call targets are observed rather than guaranteed.

machineWriterCosts: Edit amplification: adding one variable to a computation body under defunctionalization requires coordinated edits at 3+ distant sites (env struct definition, every packing site, the top-level body); with capture, the body edit IS the whole edit. Partial edits type-check whenever field types coincide, so drift is silent.

Transposition/staleness hallucinations in packing code: env field assignment is an unchecked re-encoding of variable identity, so swapped same-typed fields (env.lo = hi) and packing-before-final-update bugs compile cleanly; near-duplicate packing blocks across many call sites are the canonical near-duplicate drift zone.
