Dyn-compatibility restrictions show a language can constrain which interfaces are dynamically dispatchable so the runtime representation stays coherent — this restriction mechanism only exists if an interface construct exists; it is the carded escape-hatch design in the round1 leading candidate.

Devirtualization/indirect-inlining/IPA consume structured dispatch and finality facts, and its negative scope shows recovery is conditional, revertible, and option-dependent — so dispatch structure discarded at the source (as under the alternative) is not reliably reconstructed by the backend; source-level structure is the guaranteed side of the asymmetry.

Monomorphization compiles one generic definition into concrete type-specific versions with concrete-code runtime behavior — nullifying the only performance rationale for AI hand-duplication and grounding the claim that generics-over-interfaces cost zero runtime performance versus N hand copies; its negative scope is my conceded unquantified code-size/compile-time cost.

.NET shows a shipped hybrid (specialize value types, share reference-type bodies) that requires generic/interface metadata to exist; hand-duplicated copies destroy the instantiation identity that makes any such bounded-code-growth strategy — or cross-copy deduplication — available to the compiler.

Codegen-unit/LTO boundaries already stress code-growth and optimization-visibility tradeoffs; keeping instantiations tied to one generic definition is what lets share-generics/dedup strategies (a named round1 follow-up) bound that cost — N independent source copies forfeit this.

LLVM noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-if-violated contracts that are only sound if frontend obligations are true — establishing that annotation drift under the alternative is not a correctness bug but a miscompilation vector, which is why minimizing independently-trusted annotation sites is a verification requirement, not ergonomics.

Language-level non-interference rules are the corpus's central optimizer enabler; the pipeline that discharges these facts in safe code presupposes race-freedom (round1 index: non-interference proofs quantify over race-free executions), tying the flagship optimization to the capability mechanism in C004.

Send/Sync auto traits plus exclusive borrowing — the leading round1 concurrency candidate — are type-level capability facts with structural derivation and per-type (possibly unsafe/asserted) impls; the alternative's function-level law annotations cannot express per-type, field-compositional capabilities, making the leading concurrency design literally unwritable without a trait-like construct.

Swift SIL evidences preserving source-level semantic structure into the IR so the optimizer decides specialization; the round1 follow-up names this witness-table 'fourth model' as the strongest unexamined option — cutting interfaces from the source forecloses it permanently, since there is no interface semantics left to preserve.

Dynamic runtimes converge on recovering type/shape facts via profiling and guards, showing the information demand is constant and only the channel differs; an AOT optimizer-first language that also deletes the static channel for dispatch structure (interfaces) is left with neither channel.

machineWriterCosts: ALTERNATIVE — token/context blowup: every polymorphic algorithm must be generated, re-read, and maintained once per concrete type; D001 shows compiler monomorphization already yields concrete-code runtime behavior from one definition, so the duplication buys zero performance. Magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

ALTERNATIVE — UB-grade consistency drift: the drifting near-duplicates include optimizer-law annotations (pure/associative/commutative) that feed F003-class UB-if-false assumptions; a drifted copy is silently miscompiled, not merely incorrect, and no shared contract exists for a checker to flag the divergence. Drift frequency EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

ALTERNATIVE — iteration-loop multiplication: one semantic change requires regenerating N copies, N re-verifications, and produces N duplicate diagnostics with no shared contract identity, degrading machine-readable root-cause repair; a missed copy is a silent semantic fork.

ALTERNATIVE — ad-hoc dispatch conventions: with no dyn construct, the AI must invent per-site erasure encodings (fn-pointer records, tagged unions); these drift structurally across sites and produce exactly the D009-class optimizer defeat, with no D003-style restriction the verifier can enforce.

FEATURE (conceded) — trait solving and coherence checking add frontend compile time to the AI iteration loop; monomorphization code-size/compile-time costs are real and unquantified (D001/D009 negative scopes, D004); resolving which impl a call binds to adds an indirection the AI must re-read context to follow. None of these are quantified in the corpus.
consistencyRisks: Alternative: types-times-operations independent law annotations with no shared contract identity — nothing ties merge_i32 and merge_f64 to one associativity statement, so per-copy annotation drift is undetectable by construction.

Alternative: per-site hand-rolled erasure/dispatch conventions diverge across the codebase (different record layouts, calling conventions), so no single verifier rule or optimizer pattern covers them.

Feature: if coherence is loose (overlapping/blanket impls), which impl — and therefore which facts and laws — a call binds to becomes ambiguous; the keep requires strict single-impl-per-type-per-interface coherence or the fact channel itself drifts.
Feature: default method bodies invite near-duplicate override drift between the default and specialized copies — which is why default bodies are excluded from the defended core.

Feature: laws declared once on an interface become a single point of unsound truth if impl obligations are not mechanically discharged; the keep is only sound with per-impl check-or-prove obligations, consistent with the round1 violation-semantics direction (compiler-proven contracts assumable; asserted ones quarantined).
empiricalUntested: That AI writers actually exhibit consistency drift across near-duplicate code copies at rates that matter — the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence; this is the load-bearing behavioral premise of the machine-writer case and is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
Token/context cost magnitude of N-copy generation and re-reading versus one generic definition plus instantiation requests — EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
That AI-invented ad-hoc erasure is handled worse by the optimizer/verifier than a language dyn construct in practice (D009/round1 support the mechanism qualitatively; no measurement exists).

Quantitative monomorphization vs shared-body vs hand-copy comparisons on runtime, code size, i-cache, and compile time — round1-dispatch-generics names this the decisive unmeasured axis; caveats.md confirms documentation evidence quantifies nothing.

Whether shared/erased generic bodies can soundly discharge F003 noalias obligations and at what precision loss — the round1 construction-level check that determines where the specialize/share boundary of this keep should sit.
Whether per-impl algebraic/effect law obligations (associativity, purity) are mechanically checkable at scale — the round1 index explicitly lists 'no purity-check evidence' as a corpus gap.
All load-bearing dispatch cards (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats standing rule); the defense inherits that provisionality.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP. I concede syntactic/token/procedural AST-rewriting macros to the alternative — they are indefensible under this charter. I defend a restricted staged core: (1) parametric templates/generics with compiler-owned guaranteed specialization, (2) pure, terminating-bounded compile-time evaluation (CTFE) whose semantics are spec-identical to runtime, (3) the schedule-layer metaprogram confined to the pure fragment. Zero staging is not a live option: it contradicts two round-1 leading candidates already on the books.
result.coreArgument: The alternative ("the AI is the macro processor; zero staging") does not eliminate metaprogramming — it relocates the expansion stage outside the verified artifact, which is exactly the move the round1-arrays critic condemned in every position: relocating rather than eliminating the trust boundary. Three admissible lines. OPTIMIZER NECESSITY: when the AI pre-expands a parameterized algorithm into N specialized copies, the compiler loses the single fact that they are one body. That fact is precisely what the round-1 dispatch-generics leading candidate (guaranteed static specialization as the only implicit path, D001) consumes, and what the explicitly-held-open D005 option (bounded sharing of instantiation bodies) and the rustc share-generics/deduplication follow-up require: instantiation identity is structural only if the compiler performs the instantiation. Pre-expansion forecloses sharing, dedup, and lazy instantiation permanently and at the worst layer — the source — converting the corpus's one carded specialization cost axis (D001/D009 negative scopes: code size, compile time, cache pressure) from compiler-controlled to writer-fixed. It also inverts the corpus's clearest architectural lesson: F009 (Swift SIL keeps generic/high-level semantics into the IR so specialization is decided where cost is visible) and A002/A003 (keep structured form; do not lower to scalar loops early). Writer-side pre-expansion is premature lowering performed by the least cost-informed agent in the pipeline. The same holds for the arrays-loops leading candidate's schedule layer (A006): a schedule is a program that transforms a pure algorithm — if the AI emits pre-scheduled loop nests, the compiler must re-prove from raw loops the legality facts (iteration space, indexing maps, parallel/reduction roles) that the staged form carries by construction. VERIFICATION NECESSITY: with in-language staging, one template is one proof obligation, and expansion is semantics-preserving by construction — the F006 pattern (checked, not promised) that the arrays-loops rationale identifies as the only precedent class that ever made optimizer facts trustworthy. Under pre-expansion, each copy is an independent obligation AND the meta-invariant "these N copies implement the same algorithm" becomes formally uncheckable, because the template exists only in the AI's latent state, outside the artifact. This matters most where copies carry UB-bearing contracts: F003 noalias/alias.scope, N002 nsw/nuw, N004 range — a drifted near-duplicate that mis-copies one contract is silent unsoundness with no cross-check, the exact fallible-writer-at-scale hazard the charter elevates. Second verification argument: cross-stage semantic consistency. Compile-time-computed artifacts (lookup tables, sizes, layout constants) produced by an in-language CTFE defined to have spec-identical semantics provably agree with runtime semantics; the same values computed by the AI or an external script may silently use different overflow/FP semantics (N001/N002 establish these are contracts, not conventions), and no verifier can check agreement with a stage absent from the artifact. The steelman — deterministic external generator scripts — rebuilds a macro system outside the language: unverified generator-to-intent relation, non-language evaluation semantics, no instantiation provenance. If you harden the external generator until it is verified and semantics-identical, you have reimplemented comptime and should put it where the verifier and optimizer can see stage structure. MACHINE-WRITER FAILURE MODES of the alternative: token cost is O(K·T) for K pre-expanded instantiations of a T-token body versus O(T + K·args) staged — arithmetic, not conjecture; every re-read of the expanded region pays it again. One algorithm change becomes N coordinated edits (consistency-drift surface) and N changed compilation units (D004: unit boundaries already stress the compile-time/optimization tradeoff), versus one template edit with compiler-tracked reinstantiation. Diagnostics lose provenance: N unlinked errors in generated copies versus one machine-readable instantiation backtrace naming the single fix site. Compile time is not saved either — the backend compiles N bodies in both worlds, but only compiler-owned instantiation can dedup or share (D005 precedent). WHAT I CONCEDE: arbitrary syntactic macros make program meaning depend on executing writer-supplied code, so the verifier and every re-reading AI must run them to know what the program even is — for pure syntax sugar with no compiler-consumed facts, AI-side expansion strictly dominates, and the null hypothesis wins. Comptime must also be restricted to pure, effect-free, resource-bounded evaluation (F006's side-effect-free expression discipline) or it imports its own verification and compile-time-loop hazards. The defended core is thus narrow: staging constructs whose expansion the compiler performs, whose stage-0 code is checked once by the same typechecker, and whose outputs carry provenance — everything else expands before the source exists, as the alternative proposes.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Monomorphization is compiler-performed staged expansion of one parameterized body; its negative scope (unquantified code bloat/compile time) names the exact cost axis only compiler-owned expansion can manage via dedup/sharing/laziness — writer pre-expansion fixes the tradeoff in source, unmanageably.

Swift SIL preserves high-level generic/ownership semantics into the IR so optimization decisions are made where cost is visible; AI pre-expansion destroys generic structure before the compiler sees it — the writer performing the lowering the corpus says to defer. Also the round1-dispatch 'fourth model' (IR-level generics, optimizer-decided specialization) is only reachable if instantiation structure survives into the compiler.

.NET's bounded sharing of reference-type generic bodies is a shipped code-size-control strategy; round1-dispatch explicitly held this option open pending measurement. It is structurally available only when the compiler knows N uses are one body — pre-expanded near-duplicates foreclose it permanently.

Codegen-unit/incremental/LTO boundaries trade compile time against optimization; one template edit under staging is one changed unit, under pre-expansion it is N changed units — a compile-time and optimization-boundary cost in the AI iteration loop.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-on-violation contracts. Per-copy contract emission in AI-written near-duplicates means one drifted copy is silent unsoundness; single-source instantiation emits and checks the contract pattern once. Verification necessity, on the corpus's strongest-attested fact class (F001/F002 payload).

nsw/nuw overflow facts are optimization contracts producing poison; same drift-to-unsoundness argument as F003, and the basis of the cross-stage consistency claim: comptime arithmetic must provably use the same overflow semantics as runtime, checkable only if the stage is in-language.

range metadata makes out-of-range values poison — compile-time-computed tables/constants that feed range facts must be produced under certified semantics; an out-of-language expansion stage cannot be held to this.

LLVM's undefinedness lattice is an explicit contract regime; any expansion stage that computes values entering this regime must share the language's defined semantics — the formal reason 'AI computed it before source existed' is unverifiable.

SPARK's verification route — restrict the model so facts are checked, not promised — is the template for the defended core: pure, effect-free comptime evaluation, checked stage-0 code, no side-effecting expansion. Also the discipline my concession applies to unrestricted procedural macros.

Halide's schedule layer is in-language metaprogramming (programs transforming a pure algorithm), and it sits inside the round1-arrays leading composed architecture ('optional schedule layer confined to the pure fragment'); zero staging deletes a layer another verdict already carries forward.

Linalg keeps iteration spaces, indexing maps, and iterator roles as structure so tiling/fusion legality is checkable; AI-emitted pre-scheduled loop nests force the compiler to re-derive or trust exactly these facts — optimizer necessity for keeping the pre-expansion form alive into the compiler.

Affine restriction shows the pattern: constrain the form to make analysis reliable. The staged pure-algorithm form is the analyzable form; writer-side expansion replaces it with post-transformation code that is strictly harder to analyze.

Julia's over-specialization warning (compile-time and cache pressure) shows specialization degree must be a cost decision; a writer that pre-expands has made the decision blind, and per the static-vs-profile candidate, cost decisions belong with (firewalled) profile-informed compilation, not in frozen source.

Dynamic runtimes reconstruct specialization facts at runtime because their sources lack them — evidence that the information demand of specialization is invariant; the only choice is the channel. Pre-expanded source is a third channel that provides the specialized code but withholds the relating fact, the worst of both.

Restricting a construct (dyn-compatibility) to keep its representation coherent is precedent for my restriction move: keep staging but confine it to typed, compiler-executed forms rather than arbitrary rewriting.

machineWriterCosts: Token blowup under zero staging: K pre-expanded instantiations of a T-token body cost O(K*T) tokens to generate and to re-read on every later edit, versus O(T + K*args) staged; this is arithmetic on the artifact, though its downstream effect on AI edit accuracy is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Consistency drift under zero staging: one algorithm change becomes N coordinated edits across near-duplicates; where copies carry UB-bearing contracts (F003/N002/N004 class) a single drifted copy is silent unsoundness with no cross-check, because the relating template exists only outside the artifact.

Compile-time in the AI iteration loop under zero staging: the backend compiles N bodies either way, but loses dedup/lazy-instantiation/sharing options (D005), and each template-equivalent edit dirties N incremental units instead of one (D004); additionally the AI itself must re-run its 'expansion' on every iteration, serially, at generation speed.

Diagnostics under zero staging: N unlinked errors in generated copies with no instantiation provenance, versus one machine-readable instantiation backtrace naming a single fix site; repair-rate impact EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED but the provenance loss is structural.

Honest costs of KEEPING the feature (why the keep is restricted): unrestricted comptime/procedural macros make program meaning depend on executing code, so every re-reading AI must expand mentally or by tooling to know what the program is — an admissible machine-reader cost that motivates conceding syntactic macros entirely; and unbounded compile-time evaluation puts unpredictable latency inside the AI iteration loop, so CTFE must be pure and resource-bounded (F006 discipline), with expansion results cacheable and dumpable in machine-readable form.
consistencyRisks: Zero staging contradicts the round1 dispatch-generics leading candidate: 'guaranteed static specialization as the core's only implicit path' (D001) IS a staging mechanism — cutting all staging forces AI-side monomorphization and re-litigates a design_candidate_later verdict.
Zero staging contradicts the round1 arrays-loops leading composed architecture, which explicitly carries 'an optional schedule layer confined to the pure fragment' (A006) — a metaprogramming layer.

Zero staging also forecloses the dispatch verdict's explicitly-held-open D005 bounded-sharing option and the F009-implied fourth model (IR-level generics, optimizer-decided specialization) before the mandated measurement program runs — it decides the specialization-vs-code-growth axis by artifact format instead of by evidence.

Static-vs-profile candidate (firewalled profile data restricted to cost decisions): specialization degree is a cost decision; writer-frozen pre-expansion cannot respond to profile updates without regenerating source, moving a cost decision outside the sanctioned channel.

Risk in the keep direction: comptime with side effects would violate the F006-style side-effect-free expression discipline the aliasing/violation candidates lean on — the kept CTFE must be pure and terminating-bounded, or it undermines those verdicts.

Risk in the keep direction: compile-time reflection over program structure conflicts with the closed-world/no-interposition module amendments (D005 negative scope, D008) if it exposes open-world metadata; the kept core should exclude open-world reflection.

If the mandated F003-discharge check shows shared/erased bodies discharge alias obligations cheaply, heavy specialization weakens — but that strengthens, not weakens, this defense: only compiler-owned instantiation can choose sharing; pre-expanded source cannot.
empiricalUntested: That AI writers actually drift between near-duplicate expansions at rates that matter — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence; the drift failure mode is charter-admitted but unmeasured (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).
That pre-expanded token volume degrades AI edit/repair accuracy or meaningfully consumes context budget in practice (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).
Relative iteration-loop latency: AI re-expansion per edit versus compiler macro/template expansion per build — no measurement exists either way (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

Magnitude of monomorphization code-size/compile-time costs — D001 and D009 negative scopes explicitly say unquantified; the corpus's own dispatch verdict calls this axis 'empirically unarbitrable today'.
Whether instantiation-provenance diagnostics improve machine repair rates — no card covers diagnostics consumption by any writer, human or AI (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

The strongest steelman of the alternative — AI plus deterministic external generator scripts maintaining the single-source invariant outside the language — is untested; my rebuttal (semantic-consistency gap per N001/N002, unverified generator-to-intent relation) is an argument from the contract model, not from measurement.

Whether pure CTFE compile times are acceptable in an AI iteration loop — C++ template-metaprogramming folklore is inadmissible; no card covers compile-time evaluation cost, and the backlog lists C++ templates as missing_primary_source.

The entire macro/comptime topic has no direct evidence cards: the missing-research-backlog explicitly flags dispatch_generics primary sources (C++ templates, Rust monomorphization, Swift witness tables) as missing_primary_source — this defense is built on adjacent staged-expansion evidence (D001, D005, F009, A006) and should be re-weighed when those cards land.

result.stance: CUT. Rich error handling (unwinding, panics-as-exceptions, drop-during-unwind) must not be admitted to the core language. The alternative — Result values as ordinary typed dataflow, traps that abort the process, zero unwinding machinery anywhere in the compiler, IR, or ABI — carries the load.
result.coreArgument:
The null hypothesis wins on the corpus's own admission rules, and it is not close. Start with the evidentiary posture. The corpus contains exactly one adversarially verified card about exception machinery, F008, and it is a pure cost card: EH representation choice affects optimization, Windows-style funclets forbid register allocation of any value live into or out of the funclet, and even LLVM's preferred landingpad model is only 'better for optimization' relative to funclets — a comparative statement between two costly representations, not evidence the machinery is cheap. Meanwhile the only two claims favorable to EH that entered adversarial verification were both refuted 0-3 — 'LLVM's EH design uses a zero-cost model that keeps EH machinery off the normal execution path' and the invoke-mechanics claim (notes/refuted-and-unverified.jsonl, lines 1-2). These are the ONLY refuted claims in the original F-series verification run. caveats.md gives a standing order: do not silently discard these refutations, store them 'so similar overbroad claims are not reintroduced later.' Any defense of this feature that leans on zero-cost-exceptions folklore is literally reintroducing the corpus's refuted claim. And the one research area that would be needed to underwrite unwinding in the core — 'ABI, unwinding boundaries, GC statepoints, stack maps' — is carded in missing-research-backlog.jsonl as missing_primary_source, priority high, explicitly 'blocks moving this area from research_only to design_candidate.' Under the standing rule that every proposal must cite exact evidence cards, rich EH cannot even reach the floor: carded costs, refuted benefits, missing prerequisites. That alone sustains the cut; everything below is aggravation.

Performance case. Extreme performance in this corpus means one thing above all: proving non-interference around writes so the optimizer can cache, reorder, eliminate, and promote (F002). Unwinding is a machine for destroying exactly that. If any call can unwind, every call site grows an implicit exceptional successor on which local state is observable — by cleanup code, by drops, by the handler — so stores cannot be dead, values must be reconstructible, and F008 shows the concrete endpoint of that logic: values live into cleanup regions lose register allocation on a major ABI the language does not control. N003 shows the optimizer already refuses to speculate across a single guarded trap point (udiv cannot be hoisted above its zero-guard); a rich-EH language repeats that barrier structure at essentially every call in the program. Drop-during-unwind is the worst multiplier: it makes every scope holding a destructor-bearing value into an implicit exceptional exit with live state, and it injects those edges precisely into the loop bodies that A005 says must keep clean control-flow shape for vectorization legality. If checked contracts (bounds, overflow — the violation-semantics leading candidate's checked tier) throw catchable panics rather than trap-abort, every hot inner loop acquires exceptional exits; if they trap-abort, the failure path is a noreturn cold call with no cleanup obligations, no live-out state, and no unwind table. F009's verified design lesson — performance-critical semantics should be visible IR structure, not opaque runtime effects — indicts unwinding by name-shape: personality functions, unwind tables, and runtime drop execution are the paradigm case of semantics buried in opaque runtime effects, while a Result is an ordinary SSA value whose error path is an explicit branch the optimizer already optimizes under F002-class reasoning. J002 is the cautionary parallel: speculation machinery (deopt, invalidation) is real, permanent infrastructure cost; unwinding is the AOT twin — permanent whole-program metadata and control-flow machinery to service events the design intends to be rare.

Verifiability case — decisive under this language's ground rules, because the writer is fallible at scale and the compiler is the only trustworthy component. The ir-strategy verdict's leading candidate is a verifier-checked IR carrying ownership/non-interference/effect facts, and violation-semantics lists contract-survival-through-lowering as a wholly unmeasured gap (F003/F009 negative scopes). Every unwind edge is additional CFG on which the verifier must re-establish every ownership and effect fact; drop-during-unwind additionally requires drop flags, partial-move tracking, and exception-safety of every &mut invariant across edges that appear nowhere in the source text — none of which has a single card. The round1 judges already warned that violation-semantics, concurrency, and aliasing cannot be finalized independently; rich EH welds a fourth coupled dimension onto all three (unwinding through &mut invariants per F001's exclusivity model, unwinding across threads per C002/C004 — poisoning policy entirely uncarded, panic-during-panic policy uncarded). The EH documentation domain is also, empirically, where adversarial verification found the most overclaiming in this entire corpus — a domain whose basic folklore fails source-checking is the last place to anchor a simple, verifiable core compiler.

The alternative is not a degraded fallback; it is what the corpus's own leading candidates already assume. The violation-semantics leading candidate's only carded observability precedent is Go's race-detection halt (C002) — trap-with-report, i.e., abort semantics, not unwinding. F006 shows the corpus's only verification-oriented precedent (SPARK) gets optimizer-friendly semantics by restriction — side-effect-free expressions, no hidden control flow — not by adding runtime machinery. Result values give machine-readable diagnostics by construction: the error is a typed value, and a mis-propagated error is a type error the compiler reports mechanically, whereas an omitted handler or non-exception-safe cleanup in AI-generated code produces no diagnostic at all — the invisible-control-flow failure mode is unchecked by exactly the checker we are relying on to compensate for a fallible writer. I concede the honest costs: token-inflation from explicit propagation (boundable by a mechanical propagation form that is pure sugar over Result, adding no unwinding), and the loss of in-process recovery — traps kill the process, so fault isolation must move to process/task supervision boundaries, an architectural consequence with no corpus card either way. Per misleading-comparisons #4, I do not argue 'exceptions are always slow'; I argue the corpus shows representation-dependent costs (F008), refuted zero-cost folklore, an explicitly missing research prerequisite, zero carded benefit, and a large uncarded verification surface. Under a null hypothesis, extreme-performance goals, and a verification-first compiler, the feature fails admission on every axis it can be judged on today.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's only verified EH card, and it is a cost card: EH representation affects optimization; Windows funclets forbid register allocation of values live into/out of the funclet; landingpad is merely 'better than funclets', not free. Its negative scope also records that two overbroad LLVM EH claims were refuted.

The claim that LLVM EH is a 'zero-cost model keeping machinery off the normal execution path' was refuted 0-3 by adversarial verification — the defense's strongest folklore argument is the corpus's canonical refuted claim, and caveats.md forbids reintroducing it.

Even the basic invoke-mechanics claim about LLVM EH failed verification 0-3 as worded — evidence that the EH domain is where folklore most reliably fails source-checking, a dangerous foundation for a core feature in a simple verifiable compiler.

'ABI, unwinding boundaries, GC statepoints, stack maps' is carded as missing_primary_source, priority high, and explicitly blocks promotion from research_only to design_candidate — the prerequisite evidence for admitting unwinding does not exist.

LLVM refuses to speculate (hoist udiv) across a guarded trap point — concrete verified evidence that potential-failure points are code-motion barriers; making every call a potential unwind point replicates this barrier at program scale, while trap-abort confines it to explicit check sites.

The verified core of optimization value is proving non-interference around writes (register promotion, dead-store elimination, reordering); implicit exceptional edges make local state observable on paths the source never wrote, defeating exactly these optimizations — F008's register-allocation constraint is the carded instance.

Verified design lesson: performance-critical semantics should be visible optimizer-facing structure, not opaque runtime effects. Unwinding (personality routines, unwind tables, runtime-driven drops) is the paradigm opaque runtime effect; a Result is visible SSA dataflow.

Vectorization legality depends on control-flow shape among other facts; exceptional exits from checked operations inside loop bodies break the loop shapes the arrays-loops candidate depends on, whereas noreturn trap-abort keeps the cold path out of the loop structure.

Go's race-detection halt is the violation-semantics debate's only carded post-violation observability precedent — and it is trap-with-report/abort semantics, not unwinding. The corpus's own leading candidate already assumes the alternative, not the feature.

SPARK, the corpus's verification-oriented precedent, achieves optimizer-friendly, mathematically treatable semantics by restricting hidden effects and control flow — the restriction route, not the rich-runtime-machinery route, is the carded path to verifiability.

The core aliasing candidate rests on &mut-exclusivity-style invariants; drop-during-unwind requires those invariants to hold across implicit exceptional edges (exception safety), an obligation with zero corpus cards, expanding the uncarded verification surface of the design's centerpiece.

Carded evidence that speculative/rare-path machinery (deopt, invalidation) is real permanent infrastructure cost; unwinding is the AOT analogue — whole-program tables and CFG machinery maintained to service intentionally-rare events.

Guards the prosecution against its own overclaim: 'exceptions are always slow' is flagged misleading; the case is therefore built strictly on representation-dependent carded costs, refuted zero-cost claims, and absence of carded benefit — which the preferred wording permits.

The corpus's violation-semantics vocabulary (immediate UB > poison > undef > freeze > concrete) plus trap covers every failure-response need of the leading candidates without any unwinding construct; no lattice point requires resumable or unwinding semantics.

machineWriterCosts: Costs the feature imposes on machine writers: exception paths are invisible at call sites, so a fallible AI writer gets zero compiler diagnostics for an omitted handler, a wrong catch granularity, or cleanup code that is not exception-safe — the failure is silent until runtime, which is the worst case for a writer that cannot be trusted to keep unchecked invariants (behavioral component EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the checkability asymmetry itself is structural).

Costs the feature imposes on the AI iteration loop: unwind-table emission, dual-successor CFG construction, cleanup-region codegen, and EH-aware pass legality all add compile time and compiler-implementation surface; no corpus card quantifies this (flagged as unquantified per caveats.md discipline), but the machinery is strictly additive over the Result-only compiler.

Costs the feature imposes on re-reading/context: to reason about any function's behavior an AI reader must reconstruct the invisible exceptional CFG (what can throw, what drops run, in what order) — information not present in the tokens it reads; Result-only code carries the complete error CFG in the visible text.

Conceded cost of the alternative — token inflation: explicit Result propagation at every fallible call site generates more tokens to write and re-read than implicit exceptions; boundable by a mechanical propagation form that is pure local sugar over Result (desugars to a branch, adds no unwinding machinery), but the residual overhead is real and admissible.

Conceded cost of the alternative — loss of in-process recovery: traps abort the process, so isolating one failed subtask (e.g., one request in a server) cannot be done by catching a panic; fault isolation must move to process/task supervision boundaries, which the AI must generate as explicit architecture. No corpus card either way.

Conceded cost of the alternative — abort-time resource externalities: process death reclaims memory but can strand cross-process resources (lock files, shared-memory locks, half-written external state); mitigation must be written explicitly (journaling, robust-lock protocols), adding generated-code surface.
consistencyRisks: Propagation-boilerplate drift: with Result-only, error propagation is repeated at every call site, and near-duplicate copies are a known AI drift surface — but every drift instance is a type error or a visible wrong branch, i.e., machine-checkable; the drift risk is real, the escape-detection story is strong (detection claim structural, writer-behavior component EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

The symmetric risk under the feature is worse in kind: exception-safety invariants (state consistency at every implicit unwind edge, drop-order assumptions) are repeated implicitly across near-duplicate code and are checked by nothing; drift there is silent. Cutting the feature converts an unchecked consistency surface into a checked one.

Error-type proliferation drift: AI writers may mint inconsistent error types/variants across modules, fragmenting the Result vocabulary; needs a fixed core error-carrying convention (a diagnostics-schema decision, not unwinding machinery).

Build-mode divergence risk inherited from the violation-semantics candidate: if checked builds trap-with-report and release builds assume, behavior differs across modes; trap-abort keeps that divergence to 'dies vs assumes' — adding unwinding would widen it to 'runs arbitrary recovery code vs assumes', a strictly larger semantic gap between tested and shipped binaries.

Cross-decision consistency: the round1 leading candidates (verifier-checked IR carrying ownership facts; checked-ownership aliasing core; trap-with-report observability per C002) are all consistent with trap-abort and none requires unwinding; admitting rich EH later would force re-opening aliasing (exception safety of &mut invariants), concurrency (unwind-across-threads/poisoning), and violation-semantics (throwing checks) simultaneously — the exact coupling the round1 judges warned about.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence. Specifically untested: whether AI writers produce fewer/more escaped defects with explicit Result propagation vs invisible exception paths; whether AI writers reliably maintain exception-safety invariants; whether propagation boilerplate measurably increases hallucination-drift across near-duplicates.
Token/context overhead of Result-only style vs exception style for AI generation and re-reading: unmeasured; no card.

Happy-path runtime cost of unwinding-based EH in a modern AOT pipeline: the zero-cost claim is refuted as worded (0-3), but the actual magnitude in either direction is unquantified — consistent with the corpus-wide gap that no contract regime has quantified performance deltas (caveats.md, F002 negative scope).

Compile-time cost of EH machinery (unwind tables, dual-edge CFG, cleanup codegen) in an AI iteration loop: no card; asserted here only as strictly-additive implementation surface, not as a measured delta.

Whether trap-abort plus process-level supervision matches in-process catch-and-recover for the reliability patterns AI-generated systems actually need (server request isolation, task cancellation): no corpus evidence; this is the alternative's largest unproven load-bearing element.

Verifier cost of exceptional CFG edges: the claim that unwind edges materially enlarge the IR-verifier obligation surface is structural extrapolation from the ir-strategy candidate and F003/F009 negative scopes (contract survival through lowering is itself carded as unmeasured); no direct measurement exists.

Trap/check lowering mechanics and costs are uncarded even for the alternative (round1-violation-semantics evidence gaps: no trap-cost or checked-lowering cards) — the alternative's trap tier needs the same benchmark artifact the violation-semantics verdict already demands.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP — the full feature is indefensible on admissible grounds. I concede notation-flexibility, formatting freedom, semantic elision, implicit conversions, and user-defined operators to the alternative. I defend (1) compiler-expanded composite constructs (compound assignment, structured loops/iterators/slices) as verification-necessary and fact-generating regardless of notation, and (2) a narrow, fixed, non-extensible infix arithmetic layer for pure expressions only, stored via mandatory canonicalization — defended solely on machine-writer failure modes of the alternative, all EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
result.coreArgument: First, honesty about the null hypothesis: the corpus contains zero surface-syntax evidence — A001's negative scope states outright that the carded facts 'do not decide source syntax' — and desugaring is a semantics-preserving AST transformation, so I cannot claim the optimizer loses any fact when infix notation is swapped for canonical notation; the parse trees are isomorphic and F009's lesson (preserve high-level semantics into the IR) is about constructs, not glyphs. The defense therefore splits the feature into three parts. (1) DEFENDED AS CONSTRUCTS, NOT NOTATION: what the cut-framing sweeps away as 'sugar' includes compiler-expanded composite constructs that are fact generators and drift preventers. Compound assignment (a[i][j] += x) encodes by construction the invariant that the read and write target the identical location; its hand-expanded form duplicates the index expression in load and store position — a within-statement consistency-drift site where the two copies can diverge under generation, and where the same-location RMW fact (feeding exactly the write-centric non-interference payoffs F001/F002 identify as the valuable payload) must otherwise be re-derived. Structured loop/iterator/slice constructs generate in-bounds, induction, and reduction facts by construction — the facts N005's inbounds contract and A005's vectorizer legality checks consume, in the restricted shape A003 shows makes dependence analysis tractable — whereas hand-expanded index arithmetic forces re-derivation or runtime checks. These must survive as first-class canonical AST nodes even if every token is an S-expression; this is verification necessity and optimizer fact-generation, not ergonomics. (2) DEFENDED ON MACHINE-WRITER ECONOMICS ONLY: the sole defensible remnant of infix/precedence itself is a tiny, fixed arithmetic layer, justified by two concrete failure modes of the alternative: (a) token blowup — fully-parenthesized prefix/JSON forms plausibly cost 1.3-2.5x tokens on expression-dense kernels, and context pressure is not merely cost: less code visible per window when re-reading plausibly increases cross-file consistency drift, partially defeating the alternative's own diffability virtue (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED); (b) transcription distance — performance kernels are transcribed from specs and papers whose formulas are infix; a prefix-only target relocates precedence application from a deterministic parser into the stochastic writer at every formula, where regrouping slips yield well-formed, silently different programs that uniform syntax has no redundancy to reject (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED). This layer is only defensible with corpus-backed guardrails: expressions are effect-free in SPARK's manner (F006) so a grouping error can never reorder effects; the precedence table is minimal and fixed with mandatory parentheses across precedence tiers; numeric modes are never elided or implicitly converted (N002 — overflow flags are optimizer contracts, and round 1's numeric verdict wants named, truthfully-lowered modes); and every accepted parse is echoed back in canonical form for a writer/checker cross-check, adopting N006's lesson that the optimization pipeline converges on canonical forms anyway. (3) CONCEDED: flexible formatting (one canonical formatting wins — diffability has no admissible counterargument), elision of performance-relevant types/modes (hides contracts the optimizer must see per N002/F009), and user-defined operators/precedence (makes parsing name-resolution-dependent; pure cost, no fact gained). If the tribunal rejects the write-infix/store-canonical compromise as two sources of truth, I concede the notation entirely and part (1) — composite constructs as canonical AST nodes — is the surviving keep.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Negative scope explicitly states the evidence 'does not decide source syntax' — establishes the corpus is silent on notation, so no optimizer-necessity claim for infix is available; simultaneously its in-bounds/no-alias contracts are the facts structured indexing/slice constructs can emit by construction.

Non-interference rules are the central optimizer enabler; the same-location RMW fact encoded by compound-assignment constructs feeds this fact class, and hand-expansion destroys it into two textually-duplicated access paths.
Write-centric non-interference (register promotion, read/write elimination, reordering) is the payoff class that by-construction facts from composite constructs deliver without re-derivation.

Inbounds pointer arithmetic is an optimizer contract with poison consequences; loop/slice constructs can emit inbounds facts by construction, whereas hand-written index arithmetic in a minimal core requires proof or runtime checks before the fact is sound to emit.

Restricting index/loop forms to affine shapes makes dependence analysis tractable — the argument that restricted structured surface constructs (not notation) are how programs enter the analyzable fragment.
Vectorizer legality depends on reductions, inductions, and loop shape — facts a structured loop construct carries intrinsically and a hand-expanded jump/index form forces the compiler to re-derive.

SPARK's effect-free expression rule is the guardrail that makes a retained infix layer safe: if expressions cannot have side effects, a writer/parser grouping disagreement can corrupt a value but can never reorder effects, bounding the blast radius of silent misparse.

Overflow flags are contracts, not annotations — grounds for conceding semantic elision: integer widths and overflow/FP modes must be explicit in the surface form, never inferred or implicitly converted.

InstCombine's canonical-form discipline (canonical forms must not vary; transforms backed by Alive2 proofs) is precedent for storing one canonical form and for requiring proof obligations on desugaring rules; supports adopting the alternative's single-canonical-formatting property.

Optimization power comes from preserving high-level semantic constructs into the IR (Swift SIL) — supports keeping fact-bearing constructs as first-class nodes while conceding that surface notation per se carries no optimizer value.

IR fact soundness is conditional on frontend obligations being true — the basis of the consistency risk that a buggy desugaring or canonicalizer injects false facts the optimizer then exploits as UB, so kept sugar demands verified expansion rules.

machineWriterCosts: Token blowup of the canonical alternative: fully-parenthesized prefix or JSON-like forms with named operators plausibly cost 1.3-2.5x tokens versus minimal infix on expression-dense numeric kernels under current BPE tokenizers (JSON-like worst, due to quotes/keys) — inflating generation cost, every re-read of code into context, and every diagnostic quote. Magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED but measurable today.

Context-pressure-to-drift coupling: at higher tokens per construct, fewer definitions fit in the writer's window during edits; the writer sees less of the surrounding codebase, plausibly increasing cross-file consistency drift — the alternative's verbosity works against its own diffability/consistency selling point. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Transcription-distance hazard: performance code is transcribed from mathematical specs, papers, and reference implementations that are overwhelmingly infix; a prefix-only target forces the stochastic writer to perform mental shunting-yard at every formula, and each regrouping slip produces a well-formed, silently different program that uniform syntax has no redundancy to reject. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Forced hand-expansion of composites (if 'sugar' is cut as constructs rather than only as notation): read-modify-write statements duplicate the lvalue expression in load and store position, creating within-statement drift sites; hand-written index arithmetic destroys by-construction facts (same-location RMW, inbounds, induction/reduction shape per N005/A005/A003), pushing the compiler to re-derivation or runtime checks and the writer to more tokens per invariant.

Silent-misparse symmetry: the alternative does not eliminate silent structural errors — a misplaced paren in uniform S-expression syntax frequently yields another syntactically valid program; the error-detecting redundancy of a mixed keyword/delimiter grammar is lost, so structural writer slips shift from parse errors toward semantic errors. Relative rates in each direction EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
consistencyRisks: Precedence misparse class (the 'a & b == c' bug): writer-intended grouping differs from grammar-derived grouping with no error raised; mitigated by a tiny fixed precedence table, mandatory parentheses across precedence tiers, effect-free expressions (F006), and canonical echo-back — but residual rate is unmeasured.

Dual-form skew: write-infix/store-canonical creates two representations of the same program; a canonicalizer bug or version drift silently diverges stored form from written intent — the canonicalizer needs a verified round-trip property (parse∘print = identity) or it becomes a new unverified trust boundary.

Desugaring soundness: every kept construct's expansion is compiler-injected semantics; per F003, emitted facts are only sound if frontend obligations hold, so a desugaring bug injects false no-alias/inbounds facts the optimizer exploits as UB — expansion rules need N006-style proof discipline (Alive2-like src/tgt obligations).

Mixed-form near-duplicates: if both a sugared construct and its hand-expanded equivalent are legal surface programs, identical logic appears in two textual forms, defeating textual dedup/diff tooling and enabling drift between copies — the formatter must force the sugared canonical form wherever it applies.

Diagnostic provenance: machine-readable diagnostics computed on desugared/canonicalized nodes must carry span provenance back to the written form, or automated fix loops will mis-locate edits; this mapping layer is itself a consistency surface that must be tested.
empiricalUntested: The actual BPE token multiplier of S-expression/JSON-like canonical syntax versus minimal infix on representative optimizer-language kernels (no AI-codegen evidence exists anywhere in the corpus; this is cheaply measurable with current tokenizers).
Relative silent-error rates of AI writers across syntaxes: paren-nesting/level errors in uniform syntax versus precedence-misparse and grouping errors in infix — no evidence in either direction.

Whether a canonical echo-back verification loop (parser returns canonical form; writer or checker model compares against intent) actually catches regrouping errors at a useful rate, and its added iteration-loop latency/token cost.
The causal link from context-window pressure (more tokens per construct) to cross-file consistency drift in AI-maintained codebases.
Transcription fidelity of AI writers converting infix mathematical specifications into prefix/canonical form, versus into infix code — the core of the transcription-distance argument.

Compile-time deltas: parsing/desugaring cost of the richer grammar in the AI iteration loop (expected negligible against typechecking/optimization, but unmeasured), and the typechecking cost avoided by conceding inference-based elision.

Whether fine-tuning models on a bespoke canonical syntax closes the out-of-distribution generation gap — if it does, most of the remaining defense of infix notation (though not of fact-bearing composite constructs) erodes.

Whether facts emitted by by-construction sugar (inbounds, same-location RMW, reduction shape) measurably survive lowering and change optimizer decisions — the same unproven-lowering gap round 1 flagged for all fact channels applies to the sugar-as-fact-generator defense.

result.stance: CUT — module system / separate compilation / stable internal ABI should NOT be in the core language. The alternative (whole-program single compilation unit, closed world, no internal ABI stability, one explicit FFI boundary) carries the load. The null hypothesis stands unrebutted: no evidence card in the corpus attests any performance or verification benefit of internal compilation boundaries, while multiple confirmed and provisional cards attest their costs.
result.coreArgument: The feature on trial is, on this corpus's own evidence, a machine for destroying the exact facts this language exists to preserve. Three counts. COUNT 1 — Separate compilation is a documented optimization inhibitor that performance builds must then pay to undo. D004 states the tradeoff directly: more separate units may speed compilation but inhibit optimization, and whole-program analysis is recovered only by LTO at longer link time. D007 shows the payoff machinery (devirtualization, indirect inlining, IPA propagation) is gated on cross-file visibility, and its negative scope shows recovery is conditional, speculative, and revertible — the same 'conditional recovery vs source-level guarantee' asymmetry that the dispatch-generics judge scored decisively against (F007/J002-class conditional mechanisms vs guarantees). A performance-first pipeline therefore converges on whole-program visibility regardless; the module system merely inserts a boundary that a mandatory LTO-like pass must re-dissolve. The alternative deletes the round trip. COUNT 2 — Stable internal ABI is interposition semantics by another name. D008 is the corpus's sharpest card here: ABI/dynamic-linking promises restrict interprocedural analysis, inlining, and propagation even when code is statically known. Every internally-ABI-stable symbol is a standing promise not to specialize, re-layout, or inline — a barrier the optimizer must honor forever. The round-1 dispatch-generics verdict (the corpus's most decided topic, design_candidate_later) already carries 'no-interposition/finality module semantics' — i.e., the negation of this feature's ABI half — as an amendment to the leading candidate, and D005's negative scope adds that runtime metadata/reflection 'may constrain closed-world assumptions unless restricted.' Worse, the leading generics candidate is guaranteed monomorphization (D001); a stable generic ABI across separate units forces either cross-unit instantiation patchwork (D001's own negative scope flags unquantified cross-crate optimization limits) or Swift-style resilience/witness-table indirection — a model the corpus has not even carded (it sits in missing-research-backlog). The feature is in direct tension with the only near-decided design candidates we have. COUNT 3 — Verification: boundaries convert checked facts into trusted assertions. The ir-strategy verdict's kernel is a verifier-checked IR carrying ownership/non-interference/effect facts (F009), emitted late as UB-bearing LLVM contracts that are sound only if frontend obligations are discharged (F003/F004 negative scopes), and it names boundary multiplication as a fact-loss and miscompile surface. A module ABI is a permanent internal boundary class: F001/F003-class facts crossing it must be serialized into frozen interface summaries and trusted, not re-derived — precisely the 'programmer-trusted promise' class F001's negative scope flags, except here the fallible promiser is an AI writer operating at scale. In a closed world the compiler re-checks every call site and instantiation against the one definition; nothing is trusted across an internal seam. And freezing an internal ABI now would fossilize a fact vocabulary the caveats file says is incomplete and aliasing-heavy by selection. Finally, compiler simplicity: the ABI dossier's topic sprawl (mangling, layout freezing, generics ABI, versioning, interposition rules) is an entire unresearched subsystem — the ffi_abi_runtime backlog entry is 'missing_primary_source' — and under the standing rule (every proposal must cite cards) a feature with zero supporting cards and three adverse ones cannot enter the core. Cut it; keep one explicit, honestly-attenuated FFI boundary where D008-class fact weakening is designed once, deliberately, instead of implicitly at every internal seam.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Direct documentation that separate compilation units inhibit optimization and that whole-program performance requires LTO to undo the boundaries — the feature's core mechanism is an attested performance cost that performance builds must pay to reverse. Also shows codegen units are build artifacts, not language semantics, so incremental compilation for the AI loop can be a compiler-internal detail rather than a core language feature.

ABI/linking-level stability promises (semantic interposition) restrict IPA, inlining, and propagation even for statically known code — the closest card to 'stable internal ABI' and it is purely adverse: a stable ABI is a standing optimization barrier. Its positive use in the dispatch debate was to justify banning interposition, i.e., cutting ABI stability from the core.

Devirtualization, indirect inlining, and cross-file propagation are gated on whole-program visibility (LTO/IPA), and the negative scope shows boundary-crossing recovery is conditional and revertible — closed world makes these facts static instead of speculative. Also supplies the honest cost of the alternative: LTO/IPA costs compile time and memory.

The leading dispatch-generics candidate is guaranteed monomorphization; D001's negative scope flags unquantified cross-crate optimization limits — monomorphization structurally conflicts with separate compilation and a stable generic ABI, so the feature on trial fights the corpus's most-decided design candidate.

Negative scope: preserved runtime/reflection metadata 'may constrain closed-world assumptions unless restricted' — the dispatch debate used this to support restricting open-world machinery in a performance core, supporting closed world as the default.

Indirect calls through stable dispatch tables add lookup cost and block inlining — the same representational cost a stable internal ABI institutionalizes at every module boundary where the compiler must call through a frozen convention instead of specializing.

The ir-strategy kernel keeps source-level facts visible in a verified IR long enough to optimize them; an internal ABI boundary is exactly where such facts are cut short. The ir-strategy verdict explicitly names each added boundary 'a fact-loss and miscompile surface' — an internal module ABI adds a permanent boundary class.

Emitted no-alias contracts are UB-bearing and sound only if frontend obligations are true and metadata survives — facts crossing a separately-compiled boundary must be trusted from interface summaries rather than re-derived, enlarging the trusted-assertion surface a fallible AI writer feeds.

Return-noalias requires lifetime/provenance/escape rules discharged before emission; whole-program visibility gives the verifier every call site to discharge them against, while separate compilation forces discharge against frozen summaries.

Negative scope flags restrict-like contracts that 'depend on programmer truthfulness' as the dangerous class; module interface summaries under separate compilation are exactly such trusted contracts, and the writer here is an AI whose unchecked promises the ground rules say cannot be trusted at scale.

Establishes the corpus-wide asymmetry the dispatch judge relied on: conditional, analysis-dependent recovery of performance is categorically weaker than source/build-level guarantees — separate compilation demotes cross-module optimization from guaranteed to conditional recovery.

Documents the guard/invalidation machinery required when facts are not statically closed — the price a closed-world compiler avoids; an open internal world (dynamic loading, interposable symbols) would reimport speculation-and-invalidation complexity into an AOT core.

machineWriterCosts: Compile time in the AI iteration loop is the feature's strongest admissible defense, and it fails on the evidence: D004 shows the compile-speed benefit of separate units is purchased by inhibiting optimization, so module-granular builds only iterate fast in a de-optimized mode — which a whole-program compiler can equally provide via a fast unoptimized single-unit mode or compiler-internal incremental caching (D004 shows unit boundaries are build artifacts, not semantics; the dispatch critic made the same point). Incrementality belongs in the compiler implementation, not the core language.

Token/context cost cuts against the feature, not for it: separate compilation with a stable ABI requires interface declarations, export annotations, and ABI-stability markers that double-represent every boundary signature — tokens the AI writer must generate, keep synchronized, and re-read. Closed world has one definition per symbol and zero interface duplicates.

Consistency drift favors the alternative: an interface/implementation pair is a near-duplicate the AI writer can let drift (hallucinated signature skew); under whole-program compilation there is a single source of truth and the compiler checks every use against the one definition, converting a drift class into a compile error. [EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED as an AI-behavior claim]

Machine-readable diagnostics degrade at opaque boundaries: a separately-compiled module forces 'unknown callee / may-alias / may-escape' conservatism into diagnostics at every seam (D007/D008 class), whereas a whole-program compiler can emit precise cross-procedure facts (exact callee, exact alias verdict, exact instantiation) — higher-quality signal for the AI's repair loop. [EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED]

Costs of the alternative, honestly priced: whole-program compilation has documented compile-time/memory costs (D007 negative scope: LTO/IPA cost compile time, memory, code size) and the corpus contains zero quantification of compile time anywhere (caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify compile-time cost), so the AI-loop latency of whole-program builds at target scale is an open liability that must be measured, and mitigated by compiler-internal (non-semantic) incrementality if severe.

The AI writer's need for context-window-sized code units is a navigation/tooling problem, not an ABI problem: namespacing and file organization survive the cut as surface conventions, and compiler-generated interface digests can be emitted as tooling output without any language-level ABI stability commitment. [EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED]
consistencyRisks: The FFI fact-attenuation mechanism is a soundness precondition, not an afterthought: the dispatch-generics verdict explicitly requires designing how optimizer-visible facts are weakened across the FFI/dynamic-linking boundary (D008 class) BEFORE adopting closed-world/no-interposition semantics. Cutting internal boundaries concentrates all attenuation at one FFI seam — better than N implicit seams, but that one seam is currently undesigned and the entire ffi_abi_runtime area is missing_primary_source.

Closed world forecloses dynamic loading/plugins in the core (D005 negative scope territory); if the product later needs hot-loading or runtime extension, it must live outside the FFI boundary or force a core re-litigation.

If the dispatch-generics 'fourth model' (Swift-style IR-level generics with witness tables and resilience domains, implied by F009 and queued in the backlog) later beats guaranteed monomorphization, ABI-stability machinery could regain a use case; that dossier does not exist yet and could shift evidence.

Whole-program compile time at AI-relevant program scale is unmeasured (no compile-time quantification anywhere in the corpus); if it proves prohibitive in the iteration loop, the promised compiler-internal incrementality layer is real engineering whose cost is also unmeasured — the alternative's load-bearing mitigation is currently an IOU.

Build parallelism interacts with the ir-strategy decision (bespoke IR vs MLIR, division of optimization labor is expressly undecided); a whole-program pipeline concentrates memory and pass time in one process, and the ir-strategy verdict warns costs there are entirely unevidenced.

The verdict framework itself (decision-gates.md) says no feature becomes a core default merely because it is optimizer-attractive; the closed-world alternative must itself pass gates — the prosecution's claim is only that the module/ABI feature has zero supporting cards while the alternative has adverse-cost cards against the feature, not that closed world is proven optimal.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence: that interface/implementation duplication induces hallucination drift; that whole-program single-source-of-truth reduces it; that precise closed-world diagnostics improve AI repair loops; that AI writers do not need module-granular compilation units for context management.
Whole-program compile time and memory at realistic program scale: D007 attests LTO/IPA costs exist but nothing quantifies them; the corpus has zero compile-time numbers for any strategy (caveats.md).

Whether compiler-internal incremental recompilation (query/caching-based, no language-level modules) can deliver AI-loop latency comparable to separate compilation — asserted feasible by analogy to rustc incremental (D004's units are build artifacts), never measured.

The magnitude of cross-module optimization loss under separate compilation vs closed world: D004/D007/D008 attest the mechanism and direction, not the size; no card quantifies performance deltas for any boundary regime.

Monomorphization code-size/compile-time blowup under whole-program instantiation closure — flagged unquantified by D001/D009 negative scopes and by the dispatch verdict as its decisive missing measurement.

Fact-survival rates across serialized module summaries vs in-memory whole-program IR: the ir-strategy pass-consumer matrix is untested self-authored scaffolding; the claim that internal ABI boundaries lose F001/F003-class facts follows the confirmed conditionality in F003's negative scope but has no measured survival data.

Whether the single explicit FFI boundary can soundly attenuate every fact class (ownership, effects, noalias, layout) — the attenuation design does not exist and the underlying research area is missing primary sources.

result.stance: CUT. Automatic memory management (GC/RC/ARC, implicit drop) should be excluded from the core language. Explicit, compiler-checked region/arena/ownership annotations on every allocation and free carry the load. The null hypothesis (not in core) stands: the corpus contains zero cards in which automatic memory management supplies an optimizer fact, and multiple cards in which its absence of static facts must be recovered conditionally at runtime.
result.coreArgument: Three convictions. (1) REDUNDANCY: the round-1 aliasing verdict — the corpus's only design_candidate_later on memory semantics — already names checked ownership/exclusive-mutation in the type system as the core non-interference mechanism, and its critic phase found that "every sound road runs through ownership-like discharge" (F001, F006, C004 are the only adversarially-verified static discharge exemplars). So the language almost certainly pays for ownership/lifetime machinery anyway, for optimization reasons, before memory management is even discussed. Automatic memory management would then be a SECOND, parallel lifetime discipline whose facts the optimizer cannot consume: it duplicates the checker's job at runtime while erasing the source contracts the checker needs. Explicit regions/ownership are not an added cost of the cut — they are the same machinery doing triple duty: non-interference facts (F001/F002), data-race freedom (C004), and memory lifetimes. (2) CONDITIONAL VS GUARANTEED: the only automatic-management performance evidence in the corpus is F007/J004 — HotSpot escape analysis eliminates allocations and locks only conditionally, explicitly does NOT guarantee stack allocation, is inlining/profile/implementation-dependent, and carries design_generalization_confidence LOW. J001/J002/J005 document the price of runtime fact-recovery: profiling, guards, deoptimization machinery, warmup, code-cache memory. Per the corpus's own misleading-comparisons discipline I do not claim "GC is bad for performance"; I claim the carded asymmetry: managed runtimes recover some performance conditionally, unlike source-level guarantees — and an extreme-performance charter cannot rest its allocation story on conditional recovery. (3) VERIFICATION ASYMMETRY: the strongest allocation-shaped IR contract in the corpus, F004's noalias return ("acts like a system allocation function, disjoint from all caller-accessible storage"), is emittable only after lifetime/provenance/escape rules exist — exactly what explicit region/ownership annotations provide and what GC/RC structurally withholds; memory-object-model.md's emission rule forbids noalias/lifetime.start/end without a matching SOURCE contract and verifier obligation, and lists finalizers — a GC/RC-introduced construct — among fact-invalidators. With a fallible AI writer at scale, explicit annotations convert every allocation and free into a machine-checkable, machine-diagnosable obligation (F006: SPARK's checked ownership-transfer is a production exemplar); implicit management makes the same errors compile silently and fail only as runtime leaks or drift, invisible to the compile-loop diagnostics channel the writer depends on. F009 is the confession that seals it: the flagship ARC language had to build an entire second IR (SIL) and a dedicated family of ARC analyses/passes largely to make retain/release visible and then remove the overhead the feature itself introduced — compiler complexity purchased to claw back a self-inflicted cost, the precise opposite of a simple, verifiable, extreme-performance compiler. Finally, cutting is consistent with the round-1 dispatch verdict's design principle (guaranteed-static as the only implicit path; dynamic mechanisms explicit and restricted) and with A008's evidence that consumption/uniqueness typing lets even a pure array language avoid copies statically. Human ergonomics — the historical justification for GC — is inadmissible here by charter.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Language-level non-interference (Rust &mut exclusivity, Fortran dummy args, SPARK anti-aliasing) is the corpus's central verified optimizer enabler; ownership-style lifetime discipline is already the load-bearing mechanism, so explicit ownership for memory is machinery the language needs regardless — automatic management adds nothing the optimizer can consume.

The payoff of alias facts is proving non-interference around writes (register promotion, read/write elimination, reordering). Static lifetime/ownership annotations feed exactly these; a GC heap where any reference may keep anything alive feeds none of them at the source level.

The strongest known IR contract is allocation-shaped: noalias return models fresh, disjoint storage. Its negative scope says source languages need lifetime/provenance/escape rules BEFORE emitting it — explicit region/arena/ownership annotations are precisely those rules made checkable; GC/RC provides no per-site source contract to match.

SPARK is a production exemplar of the alternative: machine-checked ownership with transfer-on-assignment and banned mutable aliasing. Explicit, verified allocation discipline is attested; it is not a speculative invention.

The corpus's only performance evidence FOR automatic management is conditional: HotSpot escape analysis eliminates scalar-replaceable allocations only when analysis succeeds, explicitly does not guarantee stack allocation, and carries design_generalization_confidence LOW. Conditional recovery cannot anchor an extreme-performance core.

Primary-vendor sharpening of F007: runtime analysis yields conditional, not guaranteed, allocation optimizations. This is the prosecution's central asymmetry, stated in the corpus's own preferred wording (misleading-comparisons #3).

Swift/SIL shows the true compiler cost of ARC: an entire language-specific SSA IR plus dedicated ARC analyses and transformations exist substantially to expose retain/release and optimize away overhead the feature introduced. Keeping ARC means importing that pass-family and IR burden into a compiler whose charter is simplicity and verifiability.

Dynamic runtimes converge on recovering statically-expressible facts via profiling and guards — the information demand is identical, only the channel differs. GC is the same pattern applied to lifetimes: the writer could state the fact; the runtime rediscovers it at a price.

Speculative/runtime recovery requires invalidation machinery (deopt, de-specialization) — concrete mechanism costs a source-contract language avoids by making facts static. Documents the price of the runtime-recovery channel automatic management commits to.

Profile-driven runtime optimization has inherent warmup and memory costs (tiered compilation trades code-cache memory for startup). Runtime fact-recovery is never free; static lifetime contracts have no warmup.

Send/Sync plus exclusive borrowing gives type-level data-race freedom, letting the optimizer treat non-atomic memory as unshared unless types say otherwise. The ownership machinery the cut relies on pays a second, carded dividend that a global GC heap forfeits by making everything possibly-shared by default.

Possibly-shared memory forfeits classic optimizations (no introduced writes, no re-loading, no register caching across iterations). A design where sharing is the marked, typed case — the explicit-ownership alternative — keeps the unmarked case fully optimizable.

Futhark's uniqueness/consumption typing lets semantically pure array updates avoid copying when the old array is provably dead — carded evidence that explicit consumption/lifetime facts in types, not runtime management, is how an array-performance language avoids allocation and copy costs.

MLIR MemRef keeps allocation, shape, memory-space, and explicit no-alias/UB-bearing contracts optimizer-visible in IR — the modern optimizer-first infrastructure treats allocation as an explicit, contract-bearing operation, not an implicit runtime effect.

LLVM's noalias attributes and scoped metadata are the consumption vocabulary for lifetime/disjointness facts, with UB on violation — hence facts must be verified at source level (the checker the alternative mandates), and a management scheme that generates no such facts leaves this vocabulary unused.

Java final-field initialization safety shows even a GC language must carve narrow, escape-conditioned static contracts to regain optimizer-compatible guarantees — evidence that the guarantees come from static discipline, not from the collector.

machineWriterCosts: Annotation token volume: explicit region/arena/ownership annotations on every allocation and free increase generated tokens and consume context on every re-read of the code. This is a real, admissible cost of the cut; its magnitude relative to the (also verbose) defensive patterns AMM code needs is unmeasured. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Consistency drift across near-duplicates: repeated annotation patterns (region names, ownership transfers) are exactly the kind of near-duplicate text where an AI writer hallucinates divergent copies. Mitigating fact: under the explicit alternative such drift is a compile-time type/verifier error, not silent runtime corruption — drift is converted from a correctness hazard into a retry cost. Under GC, drifted lifetime assumptions compile silently. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED whether the retry cost is acceptable.

Checker compile-time in the AI iteration loop: round1-aliasing-model's evidence gaps explicitly note there are NO cards on borrow-checker/contract-verifier compile-time cost or implementation defect history. Ownership/region checking adds latency to every generate-compile-fix cycle; this is an unpriced cost of the alternative that the prosecution must concede and the corpus cannot currently bound.

Machine-readable diagnostics burden: ownership/region errors (borrow conflicts, region escapes, use-after-move) are structurally harder to report than 'missing free'. The alternative only works for a machine writer if the checker emits structured, actionable diagnostics; designing that channel is core-language work the cut makes mandatory rather than optional.

Retry-loop convergence risk: whether an AI writer converges to checker-clean ownership code in few iterations, or thrashes (the failure mode human Rust learners exhibit), is completely unevidenced — the corpus has no AI-codegen data at all. If convergence is poor, compile-loop cost could dominate. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, and the single biggest honest risk to the prosecution's case.

Frontier code cost: pointer-rich structures (graphs, intrusive lists, callbacks, FFI) push past the checked frontier into unsafe/manual territory of unmeasured size (round1-aliasing-model evidence gap: 'frontier size unmeasured'). That is where a fallible writer produces unchecked code — precisely where fallibility is most dangerous — and where AMM's forgiveness would have had the most value.
consistencyRisks: The proposed alternative's region/arena half belongs to the P3 mechanism family that round1-aliasing-model found has ZERO exemplar cards (no DPJ/Cyclone/Koka/MLIR-region coverage; caveats.md lists Halide/ISPC/Chapel/Mojo/MLIR as uncovered). The ownership half is carded (F001/F006/C004); the region/arena half is not. If regions prove unverifiable at acceptable cost, the fallback must be pure checked ownership — the verdict should not silently assume arenas work.

memory-object-model.md's prerequisites (object identity, provenance, interior-pointer stability across moves, uninitialized bytes, FFI/callback invalidation) block sound fact emission for the explicit alternative too; arenas add their own open questions (interior pointers into arenas, reset/reuse semantics, dangling-across-reset). Cutting AMM does not discharge these — it makes answering them mandatory for the core.

Coupling flagged by the round-1 judges: violation semantics, concurrency/race semantics, and aliasing guarantees cannot be finalized independently — the memory model is a prior decision. A cut-AMM verdict taken before race semantics (C002's UB-vs-bounded split) and violation semantics settle risks re-litigation.

Exclusivity/ownership-by-default everywhere is a one-way door reshaping every API and datatype, currently priced by zero benchmarks (round1-aliasing-model key tradeoffs; F002 negative scope: no quantified performance deltas for ANY contract regime). The prosecution's performance claim is payoff-in-kind, not payoff-in-magnitude.

If 'implicit drop' is cut entirely (not just GC/RC), even library-level RC needs explicit paired retain/release — reintroducing manual pairing drift at scale unless the checker verifies pairing (affine/linear typing).
