The verdict must specify that 'explicit and checkable' means checker-enforced frees, not merely visible frees, or the alternative degenerates into C-style manual management, which no card supports.

Consistency with other round-1 verdicts cuts both ways: cutting AMM aligns with dispatch-generics (guaranteed-static as the only implicit path) and ir-strategy's F009-route (self-owned IR carrying ownership facts), but if a later verdict admits a checked escape hatch with dynamic lifetimes (e.g., for JIT-style plugins or FFI-owned graphs), a library RC type with explicit, typed operations must be the sanctioned answer — the core cut must not forbid it.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence of any kind. Specifically: (a) that AI writers can produce correct region/ownership annotations at scale; (b) that they converge quickly on structured checker diagnostics rather than thrashing; (c) that annotation token overhead is affordable in generation and context re-reading; (d) that consistency drift across annotation copies is caught at acceptable retry cost; (e) that AI writers do not need GC's forgiveness for exploratory/scaffold code.

GC write-barrier, safepoint, and pause costs are background knowledge, NOT carded — per misleading-comparisons #3 the prosecution deliberately does not rely on 'GC is slow'; only the carded conditional-vs-guaranteed asymmetry (F007/J004) is in evidence.

The claim that RC retain/release traffic punctures shared-reference noalias facts (writes through shared paths, per F002's writes-are-the-hazard framing) is plausible but not directly carded; it parallels the uncarded interior-mutability tension round1-aliasing-model flagged as needing its own card.

No quantified performance delta exists for ANY memory/lifetime contract regime (F002 negative scope; caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude). The extreme-performance benefit of explicit annotations is attested in kind, never in magnitude; the recommended decisive artifact remains noalias-on/off benchmark deltas.

Borrow-checker/verifier compile-time cost and implementation-soundness history are uncarded (round1-aliasing-model evidence gaps) — the alternative's cost to the AI iteration loop cannot currently be bounded.

The size of the unchecked frontier (unsafe/raw-pointer density in performance-critical ownership-language code; SPARK contract-discharge rates) is unmeasured — the decisive variable for how much code escapes the checker under the alternative.

Whether emitted lifetime/noalias facts actually survive LLVM lowering and are consumed by passes at -O2/-O3 is uncarded (F003 negative scope; round1-aliasing-model: pipeline reliability unmeasured) — the payoff channel itself needs prototype validation.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP (honest concession on one of three sub-features): KEEP the module system as a language object (named interfaces carrying signatures + declared contracts, checked in both directions) and KEEP separate compilation in the frontend/verification sense (per-module parse/typecheck/contract-discharge/language-IR generation with content-hashed caching). CONCEDE stable internal ABI entirely: final codegen is closed-world, whole-program, LTO-style over the cached per-module IR, with no internal calling-convention, layout, or interposition guarantees. Module boundaries are verification and caching boundaries, never optimization barriers.
result.coreArgument: The proposed alternative conflates three separable design axes that the corpus treats as distinct: visibility (closed world), build granularity (compilation units), and ABI rigidity. I concede two of the alternative's premises — closed-world visibility is optimizer-positive (D007), and stable internal ABI is optimizer-negative (D008 directly cards that interposable/exported-symbol ABI semantics restrict IPA, inlining, and propagation; the round-1 dispatch verdict already carries no-interposition module semantics as an amendment). But the third premise — that a SINGLE compilation unit is required to get whole-program facts — is refuted in-corpus: D004 cards the exact composition (separate units + incremental compilation + LTO whole-program analysis at link time) in a production compiler with no stable internal ABI. So the optimizer loses NOTHING under my partial keep: every fact the single-unit model sees, the merged-IR closed-world backend sees. What the single-unit mandate does buy is a per-iteration cost structure that hits the admissible machine-writer costs squarely: (1) compile time in the AI loop — every iteration pays whole-program frontend + contract re-verification + monomorphization + LTO-grade optimization, the exact costs D004/D007/D001/D009 negative scopes card as real (though unquantified per caveats.md), where per-module caching makes cost scale with the change instead; (2) verification attribution — under the charter, contracts lowered to UB-bearing carriers (F003/F004) must have frontend obligations re-discharged whenever code changes; a frozen module-interface contract makes 'regenerated implementation M still satisfies the contract its verified clients assume' a stateable, checkable, locally-attributable obligation (F006 is the carded precedent for declared-specification-based checking; the granularity claim is my extrapolation), whereas the alternative can only check 'the new whole program verifies,' which conflates writer error in M with required changes elsewhere and yields globally-scoped, poorly-attributed diagnostics; (3) the alternative already needs interface machinery anyway — its own explicit FFI boundary is a module interface with fact attenuation (the D008-class soundness precondition the dispatch verdict says is undesigned), so keeping the same mechanism at internal granularity is marginal language complexity, not a new feature. Finally, the cached artifact should be the language-level fact-preserving IR (F009 direction), so separate compilation never strips facts before whole-program language-level passes run — this composition is uncarded and flagged below. The single-unit alternative deletes the only stable boundary objects an AI writer's toolchain, verifier, and diagnostics can anchor to, and gains no optimizer fact in exchange.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The pivotal card, and it cuts my way once ABI is conceded: it cards that more separate units speed compilation but may inhibit optimization, WHILE LTO recovers LLVM whole-program analysis at longer link time. This is an existence proof that separate compilation units and whole-program optimization compose in a production compiler (rustc) that has no stable internal ABI — so the alternative's single-unit mandate gains no optimizer fact over cached-units-plus-whole-program-merge, while its compile-time side of the trade is paid on every AI iteration.

Grounds my concession and its precise scope: ELF semantic interposition — i.e., a stable, replaceable internal ABI — restricts interprocedural analysis, inlining, and propagation even when source is statically known. Stable internal ABI is therefore indefensible on optimizer grounds and I drop it. The card's negative scope ('source-language design must separately choose module/ABI/interposition rules') is also my license to keep module structure while dropping ABI stability: they are separate axes, not a package.

Two-sided support: LTO/IPA mechanisms increase cross-file visibility (so closed-world merged codegen over per-module artifacts recovers the whole-program facts — no optimizer loss from keeping units), and its negative scope cards that LTO/IPA costs compile time, memory, and code size — the cost a mandatory single compilation unit converts from a final-build cost into an every-iteration cost in the AI loop.

Negative scope flags monomorphization compile-time/code-size tradeoffs and cross-crate instantiation limits as real and unquantified. Under a single-unit model, generic re-instantiation is redone whole-program on every iteration; per-module caching plus instantiation deduplication (the dispatch verdict's own follow-up, citing rustc share-generics against D004-class stress) is the carded mitigation direction.

Negative scope: over-specialization increases compile-time and cache pressure. Compounds the D001 point — specialization-heavy compilation (the direction the dispatch verdict leads toward) makes the per-iteration cost of whole-program recompilation grow with the very mechanism the language relies on for performance.

No-alias facts lower to UB-on-violation contracts that are sound only if frontend obligations are true. Every AI regeneration therefore triggers re-discharge of obligations — verification is per-change work at scale. A module interface freezes the assumable contract set at a boundary, so re-verification scope is the changed module against its frozen interface, with attributable failure; without the boundary object, obligation re-discharge and failure attribution are whole-program by default.

The corpus's carded precedent that a verification-oriented route works through declared specifications: ownership policies, anti-aliasing rules, and side-effect-free functions supporting 'mathematical treatment of functions in specifications' — i.e., checking code against declared contracts rather than against other code's bodies. My module interface is this mechanism placed at module granularity; the granularity/scaling claim itself is NOT in the card and is flagged as extrapolation.

Supports the form of the kept feature: the per-module cached artifact should be a semantics-preserving language-level IR (SIL direction) so that separate frontend compilation does not strip ownership/effect facts before whole-program language-level passes run. The card proves the IR direction; the serialization/merge composition with caching is uncarded and listed under empirical-untested.

Negative scope (reflection metadata may constrain closed-world assumptions unless restricted) supports my retained closed-world commitment: keeping modules must NOT reintroduce open-world features — no reflection over module boundaries, no dynamic loading in core, interfaces are compile-time objects only.

machineWriterCosts: Compile-time explosion in the AI iteration loop (admissible per charter): a mandatory single compilation unit makes every generate-compile-diagnose iteration pay whole-program frontend + contract re-verification + monomorphization + whole-program optimization; D004/D007 card these costs qualitatively (LTO/IPA compile time, memory, code size) and D001/D009 card the specialization compile-time component; per-module content-hashed caching makes iteration cost scale with the change instead. Magnitude is unquantified (caveats.md) — direction is carded, size is not.

Token/context blowup (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED): without interface objects, an AI writer's knowledge of a dependency comes from bodies or from ad hoc tool-synthesized summaries that no compiler checks; a module interface is the minimal, compiler-verified, machine-readable summary and the natural context-retrieval unit. The alternative deletes the language object and forces the toolchain to reinvent it uncheched.

Consistency drift at regeneration boundaries (mechanism carded-adjacent, AI behavior EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED): with a frozen interface checked in both directions, a regenerated module that silently changes its de facto contract is a boundary-local compile/verification error; under the alternative, drift is caught only if it breaks global compilation or a global proof — a weaker, later, poorly-attributed signal that conflates writer error with legitimate interface evolution.

Diagnostics attribution (admissible as machine-readable-diagnostics need): whole-program proof/type failures have no stable boundary to attribute to; module-interface violations give diagnostics a named contract, a module, and a direction (implementation-side vs client-side), which is what a machine consumer of diagnostics needs to route the fix. Partially speculative — flagged.

Cost of the KEPT feature, priced honestly: interface files are additional generated artifacts the AI must keep consistent with implementations (a new drift surface, mitigated by two-directional checking); per-module IR caching requires serializing the fact vocabulary without loss (untested, pass-consumer matrix is all TBD); and if frontend monomorphization wins the dispatch decision, cross-module instantiation may defeat much of the caching win (D001 negative scope, dispatch verdict tradeoff).
consistencyRisks: Dispatch-generics verdict compatibility: the round-1 leading candidate carries A2's no-interposition/finality closed-world module semantics. My partial keep is compatible ONLY under the discipline that module boundaries are visibility/verification/caching boundaries with zero optimization-barrier or ABI semantics — any future proposal to make module boundaries optimization-opaque or ABI-stable would collide with that verdict and with D008.

Undecided prior — where specialization lives (frontend monomorphization vs F009-style IR-level generics, dispatch verdict tradeoff): this 'affects separate compilation, generic ABI, and code-size control, and is entirely unexamined.' If frontend monomorphization is chosen, per-module caching of generic code weakens and my compile-time argument partially erodes; if IR-level generics are chosen, cached per-module IR strengthens.

IR-strategy verdict coupling — division of optimization labor is the hidden prior: if major restructuring happens in whole-program language-level passes above LLVM, cached per-module IR must serialize and merge without fact loss; the pass-consumer matrix is untested scaffolding and no card covers fact survival through a cache/merge boundary. My F009 composition claim inherits this gap.

Violation-semantics/concurrency coupling (cross-topic observation in round1-index): safe-code non-interference proofs quantify over race-free executions and the memory model is an undecided prior; if module-boundary contracts cannot fully capture memory-model assumptions, modular re-verification needs conservative global invalidation in some cases, eroding (not eliminating) the incremental-verification argument.

Evidence-base caveat: D004/D007/D008 are provisional direct-extraction D-cards, not adversarially verified like the F-series (the ir-strategy ruling weighed only F/A cards and flagged the D-series as unweighed there); my pivotal composition claim (D004) should be adversarially verified and version-pinned before this partial keep hardens into a decision.
caveats.md standing rule: no design decision should be finalized from this argument; the partial keep is a debate position with named unproven claims, not a settled architecture.
empiricalUntested: ALL claims about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence: that interface files reduce token/context cost; that AI writers drift between near-duplicate or regenerated code absent boundary checks; that AI iteration frequency makes compile latency the binding loop cost; that machine consumers of diagnostics benefit from boundary-attributed errors.

Compile-time magnitude: no quantified compile-time, memory, or code-size data exists for any compilation strategy in the corpus (caveats.md explicitly lists these as what documentation evidence does not show); the whole-program-per-iteration 'explosion' is directionally carded (D004/D007 negative scopes) but unmeasured for this language.

Fact survival through per-module IR serialization + closed-world merge: untested; the pass-consumer matrix is all-TBD self-authored scaffolding, and no card covers a cache/merge boundary as a fact-loss surface (each added boundary is a preservation hazard per the ir-strategy critique).

Soundness of modular contract re-verification under the not-yet-chosen memory model: whether interface contracts can be complete at module boundaries when proofs quantify over race-free executions is open (C002/C004 territory, undecided).

Whether per-module caching actually wins under frontend monomorphization (cross-module instantiation traffic), and the effectiveness of dedup/share-generics mitigations — named as a follow-up in the dispatch verdict, uncarded today.

The two-directional interface-check mechanism (implementation-satisfies-interface AND clients-use-only-interface) as a drift detector: mechanically straightforward but unprototyped; its false-positive burden on a machine writer (forced interface churn) is unmeasured.

result.optimizerRole: In the kept (deliberately minimal) form — monomorphization-only parametric generics, compiler-side guaranteed static specialization as the core's only implicit path (round-1 leading candidate, one of only two topics to reach design_candidate_later) — the feature's optimizer role is: (1) every instantiation reaches the optimizer as fully concrete code (D001: runtime behavior like concrete type-specific code), so per-instantiation F001/F002/F003 non-interference facts attach directly, with NO shared/erased body and therefore no open F003 alias-obligation discharge question — the prosecutor's strongest cards (F003, J001, J002, D001) indict sharing/erasure/speculation, not parametric templates, and compiler-side expansion reaches the same concrete-code endpoint J001 documents through a cheaper, guard-free, deterministic channel than either JIT speculation or generative AI rewriting; (2) the compiler retains cross-instantiation identity — the fact that N expanded bodies are one scheme — which textual AI-written near-duplicate copies destroy; this identity is what enables cross-codegen-unit instantiation deduplication (D004 context; rustc share-generics is a named round-1 follow-up) and is the precondition for the measurement-contingent fallbacks round 1 explicitly held open (D005 bounded sharing, F009 Swift-style annotated specialization) if code-size/i-cache costs prove severe; (3) expansion is fixed pre-IR at the frontend/AST, so the trusted optimization core never sees generic bodies at all — identical trust surface to the alternative, minus the copies — and the F009 IR-level-generics 'fourth model' stays a separately-arbitrated research question rather than a core commitment.
result.verificationRole: Verification is where the cut fails structurally, on the charter's own terms (verification matters MORE because the writer is fallible at scale). The defender's dilemma is sound and non-empirical: for the alternative's CHECK to block consistency drift it must verify copy == template[T], which requires the language to contain a template sub-language with type parameters, a computable substitution relation, and a comparison engine. A compiler that can compute template[T] to check a copy can emit template[T] directly — compiler-side expansion is a strict subset of the checker's own required work. Full substitution-equality checking therefore makes every AI-written copy a deterministic, compiler-recomputable artifact (pure redundancy: tokens plus drift surface, zero new optimizer information per D001); any weaker 'intent' check institutionalizes behavioral divergence inside its blind spots — silent, unverifiable drift. The prosecutor's own consistency-risk list concedes the escape hatches: legitimate per-type divergence (SIMD widths, layout-specific loop shapes) forces declared exceptions, and every exception is a region of unchecked near-duplicate code where hallucination drift returns; stale-copy windows during multi-step edits and cross-module duplicate templates add further drift surfaces that a single compiler-expanded definition never has. Under the keep: one definition, checked once against declared bounds; instantiation is deterministic substitution whose output is then fully re-checked as concrete code (the substitution engine is post-validated, not trusted — this neutralizes the prosecutor's trust-path argument, since both designs contain the same substitution engine and both post-check its subject with the concrete verifier); F003-class discharge arguments established at the template transfer per-instantiation by substitution. Consistency across specializations holds by construction rather than by post-hoc policing with holes.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: All claims in this section about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence (both cases concede this). On the charter's four admissible machine-writer cost axes the direction, though not magnitude, favors the keep by construction: (1) token/context — one template is O(1) in instantiation count vs O(transitive instantiation closure) generated, stored, and re-read source; (2) consistency drift — the alternative creates the near-duplicate drift surface and then polices it (with escape-hatch holes); the keep eliminates it; (3) compile time in the AI loop — the alternative parses N copies plus runs N conformance checks plus re-runs verification per copy, a strict superset of compiler-side instantiation of the same instance set, and every template edit triggers a regeneration cascade (each rewrite a fresh hallucination opportunity) before the build can pass; (4) machine-readable diagnostics — one template-level defect surfaces once at template+instantiation coordinates instead of fanning out as N near-duplicate per-copy divergence reports the AI must triage. Two design consequences for the machine writer: the kept generics must require explicit instantiation type arguments at use sites (no inference, no bound solving beyond substitution checking) — verbosity is admissible and this keeps the engine exactly as simple as the alternative's checker; and the whole-program instantiation-closure fixpoint (generic-calls-generic demand discovery, D001/D005/D010 precedents) stays inside the compiler's on-demand collector rather than being shifted onto the writer at generation time, where undershoot is a late build error and overshoot is dead expanded code. The alternative's only conceivable writer-side upside — hand-tuned per-copy divergence — is exactly what its own CHECK forbids.
whatMustBeTrue: The kept feature is the narrow round-1 skeleton, not maximal generics: monomorphization-only templates checked against declared bounds, compiler-side expansion guaranteed and the ONLY implicit path; no inference or bound solving beyond substitution-checkable explicit instantiation; polymorphic recursion and higher-rank parameters excluded so the instantiation closure is decidable and compile time bounded (the defense's conceded partial keep).

Expansion is fixed pre-IR (frontend/AST): the trusted optimizer core ingests only concrete code; every substitution-engine output is fully re-typechecked and re-verified as concrete code, so a substitution bug cannot silently miscompile any more than the alternative's checker bug could; the F009 IR-level-generics fourth model remains an explicitly separate, unadopted research track.

Dynamic dispatch remains a separate, explicit, restricted (D003-style dyn-compatible) construct per D002, with generic dispatchable methods excluded; the heterogeneous-generic composition hole (the critic's hit on Advocate 1) is closed by the round-1 design-spike follow-up as a designed construct, not left to hand-rolled void*/fn-pointer erasure the optimizer handles worse.

The D001/D009 negative-scope costs (code bloat, compile time, i-cache/cache pressure from over-specialization) are actually measured via round 1's pre-committed-threshold measurement program (mono vs shared-body vs dictionary, including rustc share-generics dedup data); if bloat proves severe, the remedy is a compiler-strategy shift toward bounded sharing or a Swift-style hybrid — contingent on the F003 shared-body discharge check — an option that exists only because expansion is compiler-side and identity is preserved.

Legitimate per-type divergence (type-specific SIMD widths, layout-specific loop shapes, per-type constants) gets an explicit, declared, concretely-checked specialization-override mechanism; this need exists under either design and must not grow into inference or coherence-dependent specialization (the Rust-specialization soundness trap).

The load-bearing dispatch cards D001, D002, D003, D005, D007 are adversarially verified per the caveats.md standing rule before this verdict hardens into final design; they are currently provisional direct extractions.

At least one AI-codegen experiment (see followUps) fails to overturn the directional token/drift/compile-loop analysis — if AI writers empirically maintain N checked copies more reliably and cheaply than single templates, the machine-writer side of this ruling must be reopened (the optimizer/verification side would still stand).
result.rationale: The prosecution loses on a structural point its own case concedes: the proposed alternative cannot instantiate the null hypothesis. To meet the charter's verification bar, its CHECK must verify copy == template[T], which requires a template sub-language, type parameters, and a computable substitution relation in the core — that is, the core of parametric generics relocated into a checker. Once the compiler can compute template[T], emitting it is a strict subset of checking it; the AI-written copies are then provably redundant artifacts contributing tokens, a drift surface, per-copy verification re-runs, and regeneration cascades, and zero optimizer information beyond what compiler expansion yields (D001). The prosecutor's dilemma-escape ('substitution matching only, no inference') fails because the defense's partial keep accepts exactly the same constitution for the kept feature — explicit instantiation, no inference, no solving — so the claimed complexity deletion nets to approximately zero while the added machine-writer costs are real and admissible. The prosecutor's strongest evidence (F003 discharge, J001/J002 concrete-type endpoint, D005's damaged sharing benefit, F009's IR metastasis risk) uniformly indicts SHARED/ERASED/IR-LEVEL generics, not compiler-side monomorphization; the kept minimal form takes those points as constraints, not objections. The trust-path argument (checker bug yields detected drift, generics bug yields miscompilation) fails symmetrically: both designs contain the same substitution engine, and in the keep its output is post-checked by the full concrete verifier, while in the alternative a checker bug plus drift ships divergent semantics just as silently. Two further asymmetries favor keeping: irreversibility — round 1 explicitly held the bounded-sharing/Swift-hybrid contingency open pending measurement, and source-level pre-expansion forecloses it with no compiler knob to un-expand; and consistency-by-construction versus consistency-by-policing-with-escape-hatches, which is precisely the axis the charter says matters more when the writer is fallible at scale. Honest limits: no quantitative card anywhere measures mono vs sharing (round-1 gap #1); all load-bearing D-cards are provisional; every AI-writer behavioral claim on both sides is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED. But those gaps arbitrate instantiation STRATEGY (how much to share, when to dedup), not existence — and the existence question is settled by the dilemma, which is logical, not empirical. Verdict: keep, in the monomorphization-only form, with sharing/IR-generics extensions held open exactly as round 1 left them.
followUps: Run the first AI-codegen experiment (would convert both sides' EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED flags into cards): have AI writers implement matched workloads three ways — (a) single generic template, (b) N written-out specializations with a mandatory substitution-equality checker, (c) N specializations unchecked — and measure divergence/defect rates, token cost of generation plus re-reading plus repair, edit-propagation reliability after template-level changes, and wall-clock AI-loop iteration time; pre-commit thresholds under which the machine-writer portion of this verdict would be reopened.
Adversarially verify D001, D002, D003, D005, D007 per the caveats.md standing rule before the kept design hardens (they are provisional direct extractions).

Execute the round-1 measurement program with pre-committed decision thresholds: monomorphization vs shared-body vs dictionary-passing on representative workloads (runtime, code size, i-cache, compile time), including rustc share-generics/dedup data — this arbitrates the held-open bounded-sharing contingency, not the keep/cut decision.

Run the decisive construction-level check from round 1: whether a shared/erased generic body can soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations and at what precision loss; record as a new evidence card (gates any future shift toward D005/F009 hybrids).

Design spike on the D003 composition hole: heterogeneous generic registries, generic methods at dyn/erased types — produce a sanctioned construct or document the limitation, so the cut's predicted hand-rolled erasure never becomes the de facto path.

Build the Swift generics ABI dossier (witness tables, @_specialize, resilience domains) and formally evaluate the F009 fourth model as a bounded extension — explicitly outside the kept core until measured.

Specify the explicit-instantiation surface for machine writers: instantiation-closure reporting tooling, template+instantiation-coordinate diagnostics (machine-readable), and the declared, concretely-checked per-type specialization-override mechanism for legitimate divergence.

Resolve the antecedent charter question round 1 flagged: whether performance transparency means a predictable cost model or a guaranteed-optimization floor (e.g., may the compiler silently devirtualize a provably-unique explicit dyn call) — it constrains how the guaranteed-specialization promise is worded.

result.stance: CANNOT HONESTLY DEFEND the full feature (general exceptions, catchable panics at arbitrary frames, frame-by-frame unwinding, drop-during-unwind) on admissible grounds — the corpus's own EH evidence is net-against it. I defend the STRONGEST PARTIAL KEEP: Result values remain the sole recoverable error channel and no frame-level unwinding exists anywhere, but traps must be (a) defined trap-with-report, not bare aborts, and (b) scoped to explicitly declared fault domains torn down by region reclamation (no user code, no drops), surfacing as an ordinary Result at the domain boundary — instead of the alternative's whole-process abort.
result.coreArgument: Concession first, per evidence discipline. Every relevant corpus artifact cuts against core unwinding: F008 (the only confirmed EH card; design-generalization confidence LOW) shows unwind machinery constrains optimization (funclets forbid register allocation for values live into/out of them); the two classic pro-unwinding claims — 'zero-cost EH keeps machinery off the normal path' and 'invoke-not-call' — were REFUTED 0-3 in refuted-and-unverified.jsonl, so the standard performance defense of exceptions is unusable here; F009's design lesson (performance-critical semantics visible to passes, not opaque runtime effects) and F006 (SPARK's side-effect-free expressions as the verification-oriented precedent) both favor explicit Result dataflow over implicit unwind edges; the unsound-optimization threat model's finalizers/destructors row prescribes 'explicit effects / no hidden finalizers in core', which condemns drop-during-unwind specifically; and the feature-evidence-coverage matrix shows exceptions/effects has exactly one card and 'needs design/implementation/prototype gating' — per decision-gates the feature cannot pass prototype_required, so the null hypothesis stands. Note also the static-vs-profile debate's use of F008: exit-path machinery taxes surrounding code even when rarely executed (J002 deopt analogy). Verification necessity also inverts: pervasive unwind edges multiply the invariant states a verifier must certify (every call becomes an exit point), which is exactly wrong when the writer is fallible at scale; Result-in-signature fallibility is the checkable form. BUT the alternative overshoots in one clause: 'traps abort' (process-wide). The round1-violation-semantics leading candidate (graduated obligations) requires trapped/checked tiers with an observability story, and its follow-ups explicitly demand a post-deployment trap-with-report design — C002's Go detect-and-halt is the only carded precedent. In an all-AI codebase, checked contracts WILL fire at scale; whole-process abort caps observation at one violation per process, taxes the AI test/fuzz loop with process-spawn per observed trap, and — the decisive machine-writer failure mode — pressures the writer to launder trap-class contract violations into Result-class recoverable values to preserve availability, forfeiting the check-then-assume structure (A005 pattern: a passed guard legitimizes the contract on dominated paths) and corrupting the violation-semantics tier discipline itself. The partial keep costs the optimizer nothing relative to the alternative: every function remains nounwind, traps remain noreturn, no invoke/landingpad/funclet vocabulary is ever emitted (the F008 coupling that the numeric-semantics debate warned observable traps would import is avoided by construction, because no frame is ever unwound — the domain's stack is discarded and its region reclaimed). Drop-free teardown is made sound by the corpus's best-attested machinery: ownership/region typing (F001/F006/C004; aliasing-model already design_candidate_later) type-confines domain-owned resources to the region, satisfying the threat model's no-hidden-finalizers remedy while restoring a containment boundary. Gating honesty: the boundary mechanism (stack-discard cost, external-handle tracking, trap-ordering precision under recovery) is uncarded and at best prototype_required; C002/A005/J002 carry no adversarial verification votes; and misleading-comparisons #4 bars blanket cost rhetoric in both directions — the deciding artifact is a benchmark of Result-threading vs process-abort vs domain-teardown codegen plus observability yield, which does not exist.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Only confirmed EH card: funclet-style unwind machinery constrains register allocation and EH representation choice affects optimization. Supports CUTTING frame-level unwinding from the core (conceded against my own feature), and motivates the partial keep's design constraint that no landingpad/funclet vocabulary may be emitted. Low design-generalization confidence respected: no stronger claim made.

The two classic pro-exception claims ('zero-cost model keeps EH off the normal path', 'invoke-not-call for throwing calls') were refuted 0-3 as worded. The defense refuses to rely on them, which is the main reason full exceptions are indefensible on this corpus; caveats.md forbids silently reintroducing them.

Bars the blanket 'exceptions are always slow' conviction: EH cost depends on representation, ABI, and hot/cold paths and 'needs precise evidence'. Establishes that the final cut-vs-keep of any boundary mechanism must be settled by benchmark, not rhetoric — the corpus quantifies neither side.

Go's race-detection halts are the corpus's only carded trap-with-report precedent; the round1-violation-semantics verdict names it the sole violation-observability story. My partial keep instantiates exactly this (defined halt + machine-readable report) plus containment. PROVISIONAL: C-cards carry no adversarial verification votes.

Runtime-check-plus-versioning is the carded middle mechanism where a passed guard legitimizes emitting the contract on dominated paths. Supports the laundering argument: if abort-pressure pushes violations out of the trap channel into Result values, the check-then-assume structure downstream of guards is forfeited. PROVISIONAL: unverified direct extraction.

Deopt/invalidation machinery documented as a cost of speculation; the static-vs-profile debate paired it with F008 as the corpus analogue for exit-path machinery pessimizing surrounding code even when rarely used. Supports removing pervasive unwind paths (concession side). PROVISIONAL card.

Design lesson that performance-critical semantics should be visible to optimizer passes rather than buried in opaque runtime effects. Implicit unwind edges and implicit drop-during-unwind effects are exactly such opaque effects; explicit Result dataflow is the visible form. Cited against my own feature, honestly.

SPARK requires side-effect-free expressions partly to support mathematical treatment of functions in specifications — the verification-oriented precedent. Unwinding from arbitrary expressions destroys this property; supports cutting general exceptions in a verification-first language.

Guard/trap semantics already pin speculation (no hoisting a udiv above its zero-guard). Establishes that the code-motion cost of ANY trapping tier is shared by the alternative and the partial keep alike, so it cannot be charged specifically against domain-scoped traps.

Finalizers/destructors break effects/reentrancy/lifetime facts, compiler verification is only 'partial', and the listed remedy is 'explicit effects / no hidden finalizers in core'. Directly supports abandoning drop-during-unwind and choosing drop-free region teardown for domain termination.

Ownership/exclusive-mutation typing is the corpus's best-attested area and the round-1 candidate closest to decidable. It is the mechanism that makes drop-free domain teardown sound: domain-owned resources are type-confined to the region, so no cleanup code needs to run at teardown.

Corpus's own ledger: exceptions/effects rests on F008 alone with 'needs design/implementation/prototype gating'. Per decision-gates ('a feature cannot become a language default merely because it is optimizer-attractive'), the full feature cannot currently pass any gate — the null hypothesis stands by the corpus's own rules.

The graduated-obligations candidate requires trapped/debug-checked tiers, and the follow-ups demand a post-deployment observability design (trap-with-report, canary builds, attributing miscompiles). Whole-process abort satisfies 'report' but minimizes observation yield; the partial keep is the minimal semantics that serves this carded requirement without unwinding.

machineWriterCosts: Violation laundering under process-abort: with abort-only traps, the AI writer is pressured to reclassify trap-class contract violations (bounds, checked overflow, broken invariants) as Result-class recoverable errors to preserve service availability; this forfeits A005-style check-then-assume optimization on dominated paths and corrupts the graduated-obligations tier discipline that round1-violation-semantics named as leading candidate. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (no AI-codegen evidence in corpus).

Iteration-loop throughput cap: abort-only limits observation to one contract violation per process run, so AI test/fuzz harnesses pay process-spawn cost per observed trap and lose in-process aggregation of machine-readable trap reports; domain-scoped traps allow many reports per execution. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED magnitude.

Ad-hoc isolation scaffolding: without a language-level containment boundary, supervision/restart/IPC-isolation logic gets re-implemented at many sites across an all-AI codebase — near-duplicate scaffolding is prime consistency-drift surface and recurring token cost (regenerated and re-read on every touch). EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Honest non-claim: Result-threading token blowup is largely neutralized by a propagation operator and is NOT load-bearing for this defense; signature rippling when fallibility changes is a token cost but arguably a verification benefit (forced re-audit of every caller by the type checker), which the design context weights higher.
consistencyRisks: [Alternative] Trap-vs-Result classification drift: the same failure condition (e.g. out-of-range index) classified as trap in one module and Result in a near-duplicate module; both versions type-check, so no compiler diagnostic catches the misclassification — a silent AI-hallucination-across-near-duplicates surface.
[Alternative] Drift across hand-rolled supervisor/restart/retry scaffolding replicated at many sites in the absence of a language-level fault boundary.

[Partial keep] Domain-boundary placement drift/metastasis: the writer may wrap everything in tiny fault domains (boundary machinery cost everywhere) or use one giant domain (no containment) — the analog of the marking-metastasis risk flagged in round1-violation-semantics; needs a machine-checkable placement policy before adoption.

[Partial keep] Inconsistent consumption of boundary Results carrying trap reports (retry vs log vs propagate outward) — same drift class as trap-vs-Result classification but confined to declared boundaries, a much smaller and auditable surface.

[Partial keep] Trap ordering/precision demands rise under recovery: if execution continues elsewhere after a domain trap, which trap fires first becomes programmatically observable; the numeric-semantics debate flagged relaxed trap ordering as reintroducing behavior divergence across optimization levels. Under pure abort this pressure is weaker — a real cost of the keep, unquantified.
empiricalUntested: ALL claims about AI writer behavior in this defense — laundering pressure, classification-drift rates, boundary-placement quality, scaffolding duplication, iteration-loop speedup from in-process trap aggregation — are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence.

No quantified error-channel cost data exists anywhere in the corpus: the 'zero-cost EH' claim is refuted as worded (0-3), and Result-threading vs process-abort vs domain-teardown codegen has never been benchmarked (misleading-comparisons #4 explicitly demands precise evidence). This benchmark is the deciding artifact for the residual keep-vs-cut.

The payoff of nounwind-everywhere is unmeasured: no card shows which F002-class optimizations actually fire when every call is provably non-unwinding, so even the alternative's headline optimizer benefit is structural conjecture.

Domain-teardown mechanics are uncarded: stack-discard cost, region/arena reclamation, per-domain external-handle (fd/socket) tracking, trap-report channel format, and interaction with the undecided memory model (a trapped domain sharing memory with live domains re-imports C002/C003 questions). Per decision-gates: prototype_required at best.

C002 detect-and-halt production cost is unmeasured (flagged in round1-concurrency follow-ups), and C/A/J cards used here carry no adversarial verification votes — provisional per agenda.md ground rules; formal verification of those cards should precede any decision that leans on them.

result.optimizerRole: Dynamic dispatch is a carded direct cost: D002 attests a dyn call is an indirect lookup that prevents inlining of the method body, blocking downstream optimization — and inlining is the gateway to the corpus's best-attested optimization class, the F001/F002/F003 non-interference machinery. The only carded recovery is D007-class devirtualization: conditional, speculative, revertible, option-dependent, paid in LTO/IPA compile time (D004) and voidable by linking semantics (D008); LLVM WPD has no card at all. This puts naive dyn on the wrong side of the corpus's one established asymmetry (guarantee-by-construction vs conditional recovery; F007, misleading-comparisons #3). BUT the explicit restricted construct is also a semantic carrier: it preserves the fact 'this call dispatches over the checked impls of interface I' into the compiler, which per F009 (workflow-confirmed, the corpus's highest-confidence class) is where lowering decisions belong — under closed-world semantics (D008/A2 amendment) the compiler can derive the switch or direct-call lowering from a dyn site, while nothing can derive dyn's facts back out of a hand-written table. The named alternative splits: its switch half is direct calls with per-arm inlining and per-arm alias facts (the favorable side of the asymmetry, consistent with D001's guarantee philosophy); its fn-pointer-table half is literally the corpus's documented defeat mode (D009: Function-typed fields hide call targets even in fully closed programs) with void*-erased payloads that block F003 fact emission. Net optimizer verdict: switch wins for closed sets on in-hand qualitative evidence; for open/erased sets the cut buys no speed (same indirect call) and loses compiler facts. No quantitative comparison of vtable vs switch vs table on runtime, code size, i-cache, or compile time exists anywhere in the corpus (caveats.md).
result.verificationRole: This is where the trial actually turns, because the charter weights verification above human-language norms. The restricted dyn construct packages three compiler-checked invariants: (a) dyn-compatibility so the runtime representation is coherent (D003 — precedented); (b) coherence — the type-to-table binding is compiler-generated from exactly one checked impl, so wrong-slot binding is impossible by construction; (c) the (data-pointer, table) pairing is established at a checked coercion. The named alternative preserves checking only on its switch half: exhaustiveness yields one crisp machine-readable diagnostic (missing-variant list) at every site, converting set membership into a compile-checked obligation — genuinely good under a fallible writer. But its table half demotes coherence to convention: a slot swap between same-signature functions type-checks and misdispatches silently, table-layout consistency across construction sites is unchecked, and payload/table provenance is locally unverifiable. The prosecutor concedes this in its own machineWriterCosts and consistencyRisks (predicting pressure to reintroduce 'a checked record-of-fn-pointers construct — dyn by another name'). Neither mechanism checks semantic correctness of arm/impl bodies. Analytic sub-ruling available now: bare, unchecked fn-pointer tables are disqualified as the sanctioned open-set path under this charter regardless of the final verdict — the writer cannot be trusted to keep unchecked invariants at scale, and silent misdispatch is the exact failure class the charter forbids. The remaining question is whether the core needs any erased-dispatch construct at all, or can forbid erased dispatch and force whole-program enumeration.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: Every claim in this section is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence (both parties correctly flag this). Costs borne by the alternative: O(sites × variants) token/context inflation generated and re-read on every pass vs O(impls) + O(call sites) for dyn; variant addition becomes a whole-program fan-out edit touching every switch site (each edit a hallucination opportunity) vs one impl block checked once; near-duplicate switch arms are the canonical consistency-drift surface, and exhaustiveness catches missing arms but never wrong arm bodies; the global enum definition is replicated knowledge across context windows with divergence risk; recompilation cascades per variant edit (D004 cost class) land inside the AI iteration loop; and freezing switch-vs-table-vs-guarded-call into source makes the writer the optimizer's actuator — re-optimization means regeneration, not recompilation (against F009 and the static-vs-profile round-1 direction). Costs borne by keeping dyn: the D003 object-safety sub-language is a prompt/knowledge burden with a multi-cause diagnostic family; the composition hole (D003 excludes generic dispatchable methods) pushes heterogeneous generic APIs back to hand-rolled erasure, meaning two dispatch mechanisms; if devirtualization is ever relied on, the cost model becomes opaque to the writer (D007: conditional, revertible) and LTO/IPA compile time taxes every iteration (D004) — mitigated only by the round-1 candidate's honest-indirect-call cost model. Diagnostics asymmetry (one impl-site error vs N scattered site errors, and which an AI repairs more convergently) is plausible in dyn's favor but unmeasured in either direction.
whatMustBeTrue: For the restricted KEEP to harden: open-set/erased/heterogeneous dispatch must be load-bearing in real AI-written performance code even under closed-world module semantics, where every variant set is in principle enumerable at whole-program compile time — a frequency claim with zero evidence (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

For the KEEP: the construct's stated cost model must remain an honest indirect call with no reliance on devirtualization (D007 negative scope; LLVM WPD uncarded), and the antecedent charter question — may the compiler silently lower a provably-closed dyn call to a switch/direct call — must be resolved first (round-1 flagged it as antecedent).

For the KEEP: F001/F003 alias facts must be shown to survive, or degrade soundly and predictably, at erased dyn boundaries — the construction-level check the round-1 critic identified as decisive, still unrun.

For the KEEP: the D003 composition hole (no generic dispatchable methods) must be closed by design spike or accepted with a sanctioned, checked fallback — otherwise keeping dyn still forces hand-rolled erasure for heterogeneous generic APIs and the feature fails its own use case.

For the CUT to be safe: AI writers must demonstrably maintain N-sites × M-variants exhaustive dispatch within token, drift, and compile-time budgets across realistic edit sequences (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; this is the decisive missing measurement class).

For the CUT: the language must provide consistency-checking of repeated dispatch code and a coherence-checked construction form for any grouped fn-pointer table — and that machinery must be priced honestly, because it converges toward dyn's own checked-impl machinery (the 'dyn by another name' convergence both parties acknowledge).
For either ruling: the load-bearing cards D001, D002, D003, D007, D008, D009 must survive adversarial verification per the caveats.md standing rule — all are provisional direct extractions today.

For either ruling: quantitative dispatch measurements must exist; in particular the cut is unsafe if exhaustive switches lose to indirect calls in high-cardinality or branch-predictor-hostile regimes, which no card addresses (branch-predictor vs indirect-branch-predictor behavior is unquantified).
result.rationale: Three sub-rulings are decidable on in-hand evidence and I make them now. (1) Implicit dynamic dispatch stays out of the core: D002's carded cost plus the D007/F007 conditional-recovery asymmetry settle it, and both parties agree. (2) Exhaustiveness-checked switch-over-variants belongs in the core as the sanctioned closed-set dispatch path: it is guarantee-by-construction on the favorable side of the corpus's one established asymmetry, feeds the best-attested F001–F003 machinery per arm, and its diagnostic is exactly the machine-readable obligation a fallible writer needs; the defender does not even contest this. (3) Bare, unchecked function-pointer tables are disqualified as the sanctioned open-set alternative: they are the corpus's documented optimizer-defeat mode (D009), they block F003 fact emission at erased payloads, and — analytically, no benchmark needed — same-signature slot swaps type-check and misdispatch silently, which violates the charter's requirement that writer-maintained invariants be checked. The prosecutor's own filings concede all three legs of this. What remains is exactly one bit: does the core include a checked erased-dispatch construct (which, per D003, carries most of restricted dyn's spec surface whatever it is called), or does it forbid erased dispatch entirely and force whole-program enumeration through switches plus single fn values? Neither side can win that bit on this corpus. The prosecutor's cut reduces to an unmeasured claim that AI writers can maintain O(sites × variants) dispatch within token/drift/compile budgets and that the open-set residue is rare; the defender's keep reduces to an unmeasured claim that the residue is load-bearing and that the unrun F003-discharge check will favor the construct. The corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence, zero quantitative dispatch measurements (caveats.md), every load-bearing D-card is provisional, and the round-1 judge already held this exact territory at design_candidate_later rather than deciding. cut_from_core is affirmatively wrong as stated because the named alternative fails the charter's verification test on its table half; keep_essential is unproven because necessity hinges on unmeasured open-set frequency and the unrun decisive check; replace_with_alternative would require me to specify a coherence-checked table construct that reconstructs the feature under another name — dishonest labeling the orchestrator would act on. The honest ruling is needs_evidence, with the restricted-dyn round-1 candidate remaining the leading design, not to be hardened or reopened until the named experiments run. The null hypothesis is not overcome, but neither is the alternative shown to carry the load — and the verdict schema exists precisely to say so.
followUps: Run the missing evidence class first — an AI-writer dispatch-maintenance experiment: same program in two representations (impl-based restricted dyn vs exhaustive switches + fn-pointer tables), scripted sequence of variant additions/modifications/removals; measure token/context cost, edit fan-out, drift rate (wrong-arm bodies, slot swaps, divergent replicated enum definitions), diagnostic-driven repair convergence, and iterations-to-green, with pre-committed decision thresholds. This is the single experiment this verdict most hinges on.

Quantitative dispatch benchmark with pre-committed thresholds: vtable indirect call vs exhaustive switch vs hand-rolled fn-pointer table across variant cardinalities and branch-predictability regimes, measuring runtime, code size, i-cache behavior, and compile time (extends round-1 follow-up; the corpus currently has zero such numbers).

Run the construction-level F003-discharge check the round-1 critic named decisive: determine against LLVM semantics whether an erased dyn boundary can soundly carry noalias/alias.scope/dereferenceable facts, quantify precision loss vs per-variant switch arms, and record the result as a new evidence card.

Build the LLVM whole-program devirtualization dossier (!type metadata, -fwhole-program-vtables, virtual-function elimination) with version-dated sources — the uncarded mechanism behind every dyn-to-direct-call lowering claim; D007 is GCC-only.

Resolve the antecedent charter question before re-judging: does performance transparency mean a predictable cost model or a guaranteed-optimization floor — specifically, may the compiler silently convert a provably-closed explicit dyn call into a switch (and conversely densify a switch into a table)? Both parties' cost-transparency arguments assume opposite answers.
Adversarially verify the load-bearing provisional cards D001, D002, D003, D007, D008, D009 per the caveats.md standing rule before any further weight is placed on them.

Design spike to test the 'dyn by another name' convergence claim: specify a minimal coherence-checked table construct (compiler-generated from a unique checked impl, no receiver magic, no method-call syntax) and measure its actual spec/type-checker surface against full D003-restricted dyn; if the delta is small, the cut saves nothing and the keep should be re-judged on that basis.

Close or price the D003 composition hole (generic dispatchable methods excluded from dyn-compatible interfaces): design spike on heterogeneous generic registries and instantiation-at-erased-types, since the hole undermines the keep and the fallback undermines the cut symmetrically; include measurement of enum max-variant-size padding vs pointer+table representation for heterogeneous containers (currently uncarded arithmetic).

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP (honest defense). The feature on trial bundles three separable mechanisms and only two are defensible: (1) implicit drop — KEEP in core; (2) checked RC/ARC — KEEP as a bounded, typed, optimizer-visible fallback tier at the frontier where static ownership/region discharge fails; (3) tracing GC — CONCEDE from core; on this corpus and this charter it cannot meet the standing rule. This defense aligns with, not against, the round1 aliasing-model leading candidate (checked ownership as the core discharge mechanism).
result.coreArgument: Disaggregate the feature; the null hypothesis wins against tracing GC, loses against implicit drop, and splits on ARC. (1) Implicit drop, optimizer-necessity + verification: the alternative demands every free be "visible and checkable" — but to CHECK an explicit free, the compiler must independently derive path-sensitive ownership-liveness and drop order, i.e., compute exactly what implicit drop computes. The writer-written free token therefore adds zero information the optimizer needs; its only degrees of freedom are disagreements with the derivation, making it a pure error-and-drift surface plus permanent token overhead on every generation and re-read. The explicitness the optimizer genuinely requires belongs in the IR, not source text: F009 is a production existence proof (Swift SIL) that compiler-INSERTED lifetime-end operations can be made explicit, SSA-visible, and optimized by dedicated passes before LLVM lowering — automatic-at-source does not mean opaque-to-optimizer. Machine-writer failure mode of the alternative: cleanup code multiplies as O(exit paths × live owned values), is drop-order-sensitive, and must be regenerated consistently on every control-flow edit including unwind/error paths (F008 shows EH paths are already an optimization-sensitive surface); near-duplicate cleanup blocks are the canonical substrate for cross-copy consistency drift (rate EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the combinatorics are not). A single OPTIONAL early-drop operator retains the one optimizer-relevant fact a source-level drop can add — live-range shortening feeding F002's payoff classes — without mandating frees anywhere. (2) Checked ARC, verification necessity: the corpus's own aliasing debate records every sound position conceding a frontier where static ownership/region annotation cannot discharge lifetimes (frontier size unmeasured — round1-aliasing-model evidence gaps), and C004's safe shared-state concurrency ecosystem is Arc-shaped precisely because dynamically-shared objects have no statically witnessable last user; "no use-after-free" there is not a checkable static invariant, and a writer that is fallible at scale cannot be trusted to assert it. Under the alternative, that frontier's only residues are (a) unchecked manual free — which, per memory-object-model.md's emission rule (no lifetime.start/end, dereferenceable, or noalias-return without a matching source contract AND verifier obligation, cf. F004's negative scope), forfeits sound emission of lifetime facts and poisons the optimizer's fact channel exactly where code is hardest — or (b) ad-hoc library refcounting invisible to the optimizer, losing the F009 retain/release-elimination channel. Checked ARC converts the uncheckable invariant into a by-construction one while keeping the operations language-known and optimizable. Its costs are priced honestly: atomic RC traffic on shared objects constrains optimization (C003) and punctures noalias facts at use sites, so the tier must be typed, marked, and bounded — a fallback, not a default. (3) Tracing GC, conceded: F007 and J004 establish that runtime recovery of allocation/lock costs is conditional (scalar replacement when analysis succeeds), explicitly NOT guaranteed stack allocation; J005 adds warmup/memory costs; no card supports GC meeting an extreme-performance bar, and the standing rule forbids proposing what cannot be cited. Misleading-comparisons #3 only bars the unqualified claim "GC is bad" — it supplies no affirmative case. Net proposal: ownership/region-explicit core (per round1), compiler-derived implicit drop with optional early-drop, and a checked ARC escape tier — because the alternative's real-world residue at its frontier is unchecked manual memory, the single worst verification regime for a fallible machine writer.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Production existence proof (Swift SIL) that automatic reference counting coexists with optimizer-first design: retain/release are explicit SSA-visible IR operations optimized by dedicated ARC passes before LLVM. Establishes that the optimizer-facing explicitness the alternative demands belongs in the IR, not in writer-generated source text, and that dropping language-level RC loses this optimization channel.

Grounds the concession: HotSpot escape analysis yields conditional allocation elimination/scalar replacement, never guaranteed stack allocation — so no defense of tracing GC via 'the runtime recovers it' is admissible, and static lifetime facts must live in the core language.

Primary-vendor sharpening of F007: runtime analysis gives conditional, not guaranteed, allocation optimizations. Blocks GC-by-default and simultaneously supports keeping lifetime knowledge compiler-derived rather than runtime-recovered.
Profile-driven runtimes carry inherent warmup and code-cache/memory costs — additional carded cost against putting a managed runtime in the core of an extreme-performance language.

The only carded static route to data-race freedom (Send/Sync + exclusivity) has an ecosystem whose safe shared-state pattern is Arc-based: dynamically-shared objects with no static last-user are a real frontier where region/ownership annotation cannot discharge the no-use-after-free obligation — the verification-necessity core of the ARC-fallback defense.

Prices the ARC fallback honestly: atomic operations and possibly-shared memory forbid caching/reordering freedoms, so atomic RC traffic is an optimizer cost — justifying that the ARC tier be typed, marked, and bounded rather than default.

Its negative scope states allocation-like noalias-return contracts require lifetime/provenance/escape rules FIRST; combined with memory-object-model.md's rule (no lifetime/dereferenceable metadata without a matching source contract and verifier obligation), writer-asserted manual frees at the alternative's unchecked frontier provide no verifier obligation, so the alternative forfeits sound emission of the strongest allocation facts exactly where it falls back to manual memory.

Names the payoff classes (register promotion, redundant-read/dead-write elimination, reordering) that sound lifetime/non-interference facts feed — the facts that stay sound only under checked regimes, and that an optional early-drop operator can strengthen via live-range shortening.

Exception/unwind representation is already an optimization-sensitive surface; the alternative forces explicit cleanup code on every unwind/error edge, multiplying the writer-generated near-duplicate cleanup blocks that implicit drop eliminates by construction.

Checked ownership/exclusivity is the corpus's verified non-interference exemplar; the partial keep is built AROUND this core (implicit drop is derived from ownership, ARC is the checked fallback beyond it), keeping the defense consistent with the round1 aliasing verdict rather than competing with it.

SPARK shows facts become assumable only via checking machinery between declaration and emission; by symmetry, explicit frees without full ownership checking are programmer-truthfulness contracts (the inadmissible unsound form), and WITH full checking the explicit free text is informationally redundant.

Cards compile-time as a real, tradeable cost axis (codegen units vs optimization); admissible support for weighing checker-error repair cycles and recompilation in the AI iteration loop, while noting the corpus prices neither regime's checking cost.

machineWriterCosts: Cleanup-path multiplication under the alternative: explicit frees scale O(exit paths x live owned values), are drop-order-sensitive, and include unwind/error edges (F008 surface) — direct token blowup in generation and in every re-read of the function. The combinatorics are structural fact; the resulting AI error rate is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Consistency drift across near-duplicates: per-path cleanup blocks are near-identical code copies, the exact substrate where cross-copy hallucination/drift is hypothesized to concentrate; a single divergent copy is a leak or double-free. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED as a rate claim; the review/verification burden it creates is not.

Edit amplification and iteration-loop tax: any control-flow edit (new early return, new error propagation) obligates consistent regeneration of frees on all affected paths; each omission is a checker error triggering a repair-recompile cycle. Compile-time in the AI loop is an admissible cost; relative convergence rates vs implicit drop are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Redundant encoding as pure error surface: if explicit frees are checkable, the checker independently derives drop points, so the free tokens can only agree (dead weight in the context window) or disagree (error); they carry no information the optimizer lacks — the worst ratio of token cost to fact content in the alternative.

Signature/type infection: region and ownership parameterization propagates transitively through signatures and data types (Rust-lifetime-like), so local edits require reloading and consistently rewriting non-local annotation context; magnitude of the context-window cost for AI writers is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Frontier failure mode: for dynamically-shared, dynamic-last-user structures the writer cannot produce ANY checkable static annotation; the alternative degenerates to unchecked manual free — UB on every writer error, at scale, with no machine-readable diagnostic at the fault site (use-after-free manifests far from its cause) — or to library RC invisible to the optimizer, losing the F009 channel.
consistencyRisks: Must not dislodge the round1 aliasing-model leading candidate (checked ownership core): this defense is architected around it, but the ARC fallback reintroduces shared-mutable/interior-mutability patterns that puncture shared-reference noalias facts (misleading-comparisons #2; round1 'double dividend' critique). ARC-tier use sites are fact-destroying and must be typed and marked; if the tier leaks into hot paths, it conflicts with the aliasing verdict's payoff story.

Misleading-comparisons #3 cuts both ways: the GC concession may not be argued as 'GC is slow' (uncarded); it rests solely on absence of guaranteed facts and absence of any supporting card under the standing rule. If future cards quantify modern GC (barriers, safepoints uncarded today), the concession must be re-weighed.

F009 generalization risk: Swift SIL/ARC evidence is implementation-specific (OSSA/non-OSSA phase distinctions, stale-doc caveats per caveats.md); 'ARC is optimizable' beyond Swift's implementation is medium-confidence extrapolation, and no card quantifies residual retain/release overhead after ARC optimization.

memory-object-model.md lists lifetime boundaries for stack/heap/arena/GC/RC as OPEN prerequisites blocking sound emission for every position; per the standing rule this defense names a direction, not a decision — nothing here is emission-ready.

Symmetric unmeasured compile-time: the iteration-tax argument against explicit frees also applies to the heavier ownership/region checking my own core assumes (round1 gap: checker compile-time cost uncarded); the alternative could win this axis if its checking is cheaper.

The attacked alternative is itself under-evidenced: zero corpus cards on region/effect languages (Cyclone/DPJ/Koka/MLIR regions — round1 gap, backlog item). The frontier-size argument (decisive for the ARC tier's scope) is unmeasured in BOTH directions; if region-language cards arrive showing wide static coverage, the ARC tier shrinks toward vestigial.

Concurrency-model and violation-semantics verdicts are prior/coupled decisions (round1-index cross-topic observation): the ARC tier's atomicity requirements and the semantics of a refcount-related violation cannot be finalized before the memory model and violation-semantics questions settle.
empiricalUntested: Rate at which AI writers drift or hallucinate across near-duplicate cleanup blocks and repeated region/free annotations — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence; the drift-substrate argument is structural, not measured.
Whether the token/context overhead of explicit region+free annotations materially degrades AI generation cost, latency, or output quality relative to implicit-drop code.

Whether AI repair loops converge faster on ownership/region checker diagnostics (explicit-free omission errors) than on implicit-drop borrow-checker diagnostics — both directions of the iteration-tax claim are unmeasured.

What fraction of AI-generated performance-critical code falls beyond the static ownership/region frontier and would actually need the ARC tier (frontier size is unmeasured even for human-written code — round1 evidence gap).
Whether an AI writer can reliably select the correct tier (region vs ARC) when both are available, or whether tier-choice itself becomes a new consistency-drift axis.

Whether machine-readable diagnostics from lifetime checkers are more automatically repairable by AI than the runtime manifestations of manual-free errors (use-after-free crashes far from the fault site) — assumed throughout this defense, never tested.

Whether compiler-inserted drop points ever destroy optimization opportunities an AI writer would have expressed via earlier explicit frees at scale — i.e., whether the optional early-drop operator is actually sufficient.

result.optimizerRole: For the static path, interfaces per se give the optimizer nothing: the only carded benefit mechanism is monomorphization (D001), which makes the abstraction disappear, and what passes actually consume are facts about concrete code — non-interference around writes (F001/F002), noalias contracts (F003), iterator/reduction/independence structure (A002, A005, A010). The defender concedes per-copy specialized functions carry the same local facts. Interfaces earn optimizer relevance only through three residual channels: (1) instantiation identity, which enables compiler-side dedup/sharing and codegen-unit strategies (D004, D005) that N independent hand copies forfeit; (2) structured dispatch facts, if any dynamic dispatch exists — a sanctioned dyn construct has a coherent optimizer-visible representation (D002) with D003-style containment, versus hand-rolled fn-pointer erasure which is the documented defeat class (D009), and D007's negative scope shows backend devirtualization recovery is conditional, speculative, and revertible, so structure lost at source is not reliably reconstructed; (3) type-level capability facts (C004-class Send/Sync), which the round1 index confirms are the soundness precondition for the flagship non-interference fact pipeline. Channels (1) and (2) are qualitative and rest on provisional cards; channel (3) is real but expressible as type-level fact annotation without the interface abstraction apparatus. Whether closed sum types with exhaustive dispatch match or beat restricted dyn on inlining/devirtualization is uncarded in both directions.
result.verificationRole: This is the strongest surviving argument for a trait-shaped construct, and it is structural rather than empirical: one named contract plus N mechanically generated per-impl discharge obligations, versus types-times-operations independent writer-asserted annotations with no shared identity. Under the alternative as proposed, nothing makes merge_i32 and merge_f64 answerable to the same associativity statement, so per-copy law drift is undetectable by construction — and because law/alias annotations are UB-if-false optimizer contracts (F003 class), a drifted 'pure' or 'associative' is a miscompilation vector, not merely a bug, in a charter where verification matters more than in human languages. Interfaces also move 'type lacks operation X' errors to declaration time rather than later call-site diagnostics, reducing iteration-loop round trips only if the replacement diagnostics are imprecise. But the verification benefit does not require interfaces-as-abstraction specifically: a named law-contract mechanism with binding identity (fact annotation plus identity, no dispatch, no reuse) plausibly restores it, and the keep introduces its own verification surface — instance resolution, coherence checking, and law conformance of every impl are new soundness-critical subsystems for a fallible writer to trip. Both routes are gated on the unresolved violation-semantics topic (round1: research_needed — compiler-proven contracts assumable, asserted ones quarantined) and on the corpus's explicitly missing purity-check evidence.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: Every load-bearing claim about AI writer behavior, on both sides, is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence whatsoever, and both parties honestly flag this. Against the cut: N-copy token/context inflation on every generation and re-read; consistency drift across near-duplicates whose blast radius is miscompilation because the drifting text includes UB-if-false law annotations; regeneration burden on every semantic change with silent-fork risk for missed copies; N duplicate diagnostics per root cause with no shared identity for machine-readable repair; over-emission risk (a demand-driven monomorphizer instantiates only used types per D001, a fallible writer may emit more, paying D009-negative-scope compile-time/cache pressure). Against the keep: trait solving and coherence checking add frontend time to the AI iteration loop on top of unquantified monomorphization code-size/compile-time costs (D001/D004/D009 negative scopes); impl-binding indirection forces the writer to re-read more context to know which code a call reaches. The magnitudes on both sides are unmeasured, and the cut's mitigation stack (deterministic expanders, generated-from provenance, per-copy law re-checks, N006-style refinement checking of near-duplicates) is unbuilt and untested at scale. Note the asymmetry in failure modes: the keep's unmeasured cost is compile time and code size; the cut's unmeasured cost includes silent miscompilation via annotation drift.
whatMustBeTrue: For the CUT to be safe: writer-side expansion must be demonstrated viable at scale — deterministic expander tooling, machine-readable generated-from provenance identifying the copy set, per-copy law re-verification, and N006/Alive2-style equivalence checking must actually catch drift across writer-emitted near-duplicates (currently zero evidence; this is the load-bearing untested premise).

For the CUT to be safe: closed sum types with exhaustive dispatch must cover machine-needed heterogeneity with optimizer behavior at least as good as a D003-restricted dyn construct, and the open-extension/FFI periphery must have some story other than hand-rolled fn-pointer erasure, which is the carded defeat class (D009).

For the KEEP to be justified: the trait layer must be the restricted form only — guaranteed static specialization as the sole implicit path (round1 leading candidate), strict one-impl-per-type-per-interface coherence, at most one explicit D003-restricted dyn construct; and if any body-sharing path exists, shared/erased bodies must be shown to soundly discharge F003 noalias obligations (round1's decisive unrun check).

EITHER WAY: type-level capability facts (C004-class Send/Sync markers) must have an expression vehicle in the core — the leading concurrency candidate and the soundness precondition for the F001–F003 fact pipeline depend on them; this survives as type-level fact annotation regardless of the interfaces verdict.

EITHER WAY: algebraic/effect law annotations must carry shared contract identity (one named associativity statement that all conforming concrete functions bind to) and must be compiler-checked or quarantined per the violation-semantics resolution — writer-asserted UB-if-false facts with no identity are unacceptable under this charter in both designs.

EITHER WAY: inheritance, default method bodies, blanket/overlapping impls, and structural conformance stay out of the core — neither party defends them and their only justification is reuse ergonomics, inadmissible here.

EITHER WAY: diagnostics for missing-operation/type-mismatch failures must be machine-readable and precise enough that losing declaration-time bound errors (if cut) does not multiply AI iteration cycles.
result.rationale: Neither side meets its burden on the corpus as it stands. The defender concedes the static optimizer case: monomorphization (D001) makes the abstraction vanish and every attested fact channel is about concrete code (F002/F003, A002/A005/A010), so keep_essential fails — its remaining pillars (instantiation identity via D004/D005, dyn structure via D002/D007, capabilities via C004) are qualitative, rest on provisional direct-extraction cards (caveats.md standing rule), and the capability pillar is expressible without interface abstraction. But the prosecutor's alternative as named — bare law annotations on concrete functions — demonstrably cannot carry the load either: it has no contract identity (drift across copies undetectable by construction, and drifted laws are F003-class miscompilation vectors, decisive in a charter where verification matters more), no vehicle for type-level capability facts that the round1 concurrency candidate requires, and no dispatch story beyond an uncarded sum-type inference, with the carded fallback being the D009 defeat class. So plain cut_from_core fails. What actually separates the strengthened cut (law contracts with identity + capability annotations + provenance/equivalence tooling + closed sum types) from the restricted keep (round1's guaranteed-static-specialization candidate) is exactly the set of questions the corpus cards nothing on: N-copy token/drift/regeneration economics versus trait-solving/monomorphization compile-time (zero quantitative cards anywhere — caveats.md; round1 verdict), AI writer drift rates (zero AI-codegen evidence), sum-type versus dyn optimizer behavior (uncarded), and F003 discharge by shared bodies (round1's own decisive unrun check). Both parties flag these as EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, and round1 already ordered the measurements without them being run. Ruling cut would overturn round1's design_candidate_later leading candidate on no new evidence; ruling keep would violate the caveats discipline against unquantified design decisions. The decisive experiments are cheap to name, and several invariants can be locked now regardless of outcome (see whatMustBeTrue). Verdict: needs_evidence.
followUps: E1 (the decisive AI-writer experiment, first AI-codegen evidence for the corpus): have an AI writer maintain N concrete type-specializations of a suite of generic algorithms through a scripted series of semantic changes, versus maintaining one generic definition; measure drift rate, missed-copy rate, false-law-annotation rate, and token/context cost. This directly tests the load-bearing premise of both cases.

E2 (already ordered by round1, unrun): quantitative comparison of full compiler specialization vs shared/erased bodies vs writer-emitted hand copies on runtime, code size, i-cache pressure, and compile time — the A1-vs-A3 measurement; no quantitative card exists on this axis.
