E3 (round1's decisive construction-level check): determine whether shared/erased generic bodies can soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations and at what precision loss; this sets the specialize/share boundary for any keep and tests the defender's sharing concession.

E4: benchmark closed sum-type exhaustive dispatch vs a D003-restricted dyn vtable construct vs hand-rolled fn-pointer erasure on inlining, devirtualization, and IPA behavior in closed-world programs — converts the prosecutor's uncarded enum-dispatch inference and the defender's D009 extrapolation into evidence.

E5: prototype mechanical checking of law annotations (purity, associativity, commutativity) as per-impl discharge obligations — the round1 index lists purity-check evidence as an explicit corpus gap, and both designs are unsound without it; sequence after (or jointly with) resolving the violation-semantics topic (research_needed).

E6: prototype the named law-contract mechanism (contract identity binding concrete functions, no dispatch, no reuse) and test whether it alone restores the defender's one-contract-N-obligations verification structure — if yes, the verification-necessity argument for interfaces-as-abstraction collapses and the trial reduces to E1/E2 economics.

E7: adversarially verify the load-bearing dispatch cards (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007), which are currently provisional direct extractions per the caveats standing rule and carry every dispatch argument on both sides.

result.optimizerRole: The optimizer does not need source-level type inference or implicit conversions; it needs the facts they would deliver, and the corpus shows the channel is fungible while explicit declaration is the only channel with no recovery machinery. J001 (information demand constant, only the channel differs), J002 (speculative channels buy guard/invalidation machinery), and J003 (stable layout is the optimizer-relevant property; static languages could guarantee it) establish that declared facts dominate recovered facts. D001's monomorphization consumes declared concrete types directly; F001/F002 non-interference facts are the corpus's best-attested enablers and originate as checked declarations plus compiler proof, not writer-elided guesses. D009/D010 show the flagship inference-for-performance design (Julia) recovers performance only conditionally and fails silently into dynamic dispatch — the exact guarantee-vs-conditional-recovery asymmetry the corpus repeatedly confirms (F007, dispatch-generics verdict: guaranteed static specialization as the core's only implicit path). Implicit conversions are worse than useless to the optimizer: under N001/N002, conversions move values between definedness classes and mode contracts, so an implicitly inserted conversion silently decides which optimization promise (nsw-grade, poison-bearing) applies — a contract the writer never stated. Critical scoping preserved from the prosecution: sound below-source derivation of ADDITIONAL facts (known-bits, ranges, flow-sensitive exclusivity spans finer than binding granularity per F002, alias refinement) stays in the optimizer and is not the accused feature; this scoping also absorbs the defense's F002 granularity objection, which otherwise attacked a strawman.
result.verificationRole: Verification is where the cut earns its place, but the defense's checked-vs-trusted dilemma lands and becomes a binding requirement rather than a keep. (a) Annotations must be CHECKED, never trusted: F003/N002/N004 show the fact channels are UB/poison-bearing contracts; misleading-comparisons note 5 says metadata is sound only if frontend proofs are sound; the violation-semantics post-critique convergence is 'proven facts assumable everywhere, unproven assertion never ambient.' A fallible AI writer's annotation is an unproven assertion, so UB-bearing annotations (noalias-grade, nsw-grade, range) are proof-gated or conservatively dropped — full annotation does NOT mean annotate-and-trust. (b) The defense is correct that checking requires a derivation engine; but with total annotation the engine is a syntax-directed local checker computing each expression's type bottom-up from declared leaves and comparing against declared facts — no unification variables, no defaulting, no policy choice, no determination of writer intent. That checker is the prosecution's scoped-in component and fits the corpus's model of trustworthy compilation (N006: small, mechanically checkable, Alive2-style verifiable rules); an intent-reconstruction solver has no such oracle, because nothing can verify a guess of intent, only its internal consistency. (c) Every fact having a declaring token converts the charter's named hazard — consistency drift across near-duplicates — from silent semantic divergence (inference quietly assigns each copy its own types; both compile) into a local, machine-readable compile error. Residual risk both parties concede: internally consistent-but-wrong annotation sets still type-check; redundancy gives cross-checking purchase that elision removes, but the catch-rate is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: All AI-writer behavioral claims on both sides are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence — so this ledger is priced analytically. Costs the verdict accepts: annotation token inflation at every binding and call (charter-payable verbosity; annotations are mechanically derivable from signatures, so tooling, not the writer's judgment, should produce them); context-window pressure from longer functions, with an unmeasured second-order hallucination risk; refactor fanout where one signature change forces edits at every use site (each edit local, mechanically derivable); higher compile-fix cycle counts, though each error is local, token-anchored, and machine-readable — plausibly the cheapest error class for automated repair (untested). Costs the verdict avoids: the single-token hallucination (0 vs 0.0) silently retyping an entire inference region while still compiling; implicit conversions laundering a type-mismatch hallucination into compiling-but-wrong code with changed numeric mode (N002-grade contract switch); non-local inference diagnostics reported far from cause; and the defense's own observation that implicit conversions make textually identical near-duplicates semantically divergent via different conversion paths. Costs the defense proved and the language must now engineer away (see whatMustBeTrue): O(uses) diagnostic cascades from one wrong signature, drift across regenerated near-identical annotation blocks, and the generation fixpoint (types unwritable without running the compiler) — the last is eliminated by the nameability requirement, since compositional writer-chosen names are writable a priori. The defense's O(n^2) combinator-chain source-growth claim is analytic, unmeasured, and the single strongest reason to run the follow-up experiments before freezing annotation density.
whatMustBeTrue: Nameability guarantee: every type, effect row, layout, and mode the language can construct has a canonical, finite, writable surface name available WITHOUT running the compiler — closures are lambda-lifted to named top-level items with explicit environment structs, generic instantiations are nameable via explicit instantiation syntax, and the core has no anonymous or opaque/existential 'compiler-fills-this-in' annotation. This is the load-bearing repair to the defense's dilemma: without it, mandatory annotation forces D002-style boxing (prevents inlining) or D009-style function-typed indirection, or readmits inference under another name, and the verdict fails.

Checked, never trusted: the compiler independently verifies every annotation against a truthful lowering table; UB/poison-bearing contract annotations (F003 noalias-grade, N002 nsw-grade, N004 range-grade) are assumable by the optimizer only when compiler-proven, with conservative fallback otherwise — per the violation-semantics convergence that unproven assertion is never ambient. 'Full annotation' is a redundancy-and-checking regime, not a trust regime.

Below-source fact derivation stays: the optimizer retains sound analysis deriving additional facts (known-bits, ranges, flow-sensitive exclusivity spans finer than binding granularity per F002) so binding-granularity annotation does not cap optimization precision; this derivation determines no source semantics.

No implicit value-changing conversions anywhere: every conversion that can change value, representation, precision, or numeric mode is a named explicit operator with truthful lowering (numeric-semantics leading candidate); bare literals without a declared mode are a hard error, not defaulted. At most, statically checked coercions that are provably representation- and value-identical (lowering no-ops, e.g. exclusive-to-shared reference weakening) may be implicit; anything stronger is out.

Consistency-checking of repeated code, as machinery the language/toolchain must provide: a canonical normal form for annotated code plus content-addressed or dedup checking so near-duplicate annotation blocks either collapse into one named definition or drift loudly as diagnostics; this addresses both residual drift channels the parties agree survive (internally-consistent wrong copies, systematic template copying) — EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED how much it recovers.

Root-cause diagnostics: machine-readable, declaring-token-anchored errors with provenance chains and deduplication so one wrong signature yields one root diagnostic, not O(uses) cascade noise flooding the repair loop — the defense's cascade attack is conceded and becomes a requirement.

Mechanical annotation tooling outside the core: annotations are derivable and insertable by tools from signatures and the lowering table, so the AI writer emits or repairs them without free-form guessing; interface changes propagate mechanically.

Separate-compilation firewall: fully annotated signatures are fixed compilation boundaries (D004); no fact needed to check one body ever depends on another body, keeping checking local, parallel, and incremental in the AI iteration loop.

Defeasance clause: if the follow-up experiments show full per-binding annotation measurably raises net writer error rates or context-window failure versus signature-anchored policy-free propagation, the narrow relaxation to reconsider is exactly the defense's residue — deterministic, policy-free, ambiguity-is-error propagation inside bodies from mandatory signature anchors — reintroduced as a surface abbreviation over the same checked semantics, never as solver-decided intent, never crossing signatures, and never touching conversions.
result.rationale: The two accused mechanisms fail differently, and most of the territory is decidable now. (1) Implicit value-changing conversions: cut, unanimously and decisively. The defense concedes them entirely; the prosecution's hazard structure is carded (N001 definedness lattice, N002 overflow-as-poison contracts); and the numeric-semantics record shows zero evidentiary support for any implicit-conversion design ('all mixed-mode conversion rules are invention') while its leading candidate is named, truthfully-lowered explicit modes. Under the null hypothesis this is the easiest call in the case. (2) Whole-program/global/cross-signature inference: cut, also effectively unanimous — the defense conceded it, and D004 (boundary coupling), D009/D010 (silent conditional recovery), and F007's guarantee-vs-conditional asymmetry support the prosecution. (3) The genuinely contested strip is narrow: whether body-local bindings may elide annotations under deterministic, policy-free propagation from mandatory signature anchors. Here I find for the prosecution on the law but for the defense on two facts. On the law: the null hypothesis puts the burden on the defense, whose positive case for elision rests entirely on EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED machine-writer claims (its own list says so), and whose F009/SIL citation is architectural precedent for compiler-managed IR facts, not for surface elision; meanwhile its F002 granularity leg attacks a strawman the prosecution had already scoped out (below-source analysis stays). The prosecution's silent-cliff cards (D009, F007) admittedly do not reach the conceded-down residue — policy-free inference with ambiguity-as-hard-error has no silent fallback — but 'the attack misses' is not affirmative evidence for keeping, and the corpus contains none. On the facts: the defense proved two things that convert cut_from_core into replace_with_alternative. First, the checked-vs-trusted dilemma is real — annotate-and-trust would institutionalize unsound frontend metadata emission (F003, N002, N004, misleading-comparisons note 5, violation-semantics convergence), so the alternative is binding only as a checked-redundancy regime with a syntax-directed local checker in the core. Second, the unnameable-types attack lands against the alternative as literally stated: with anonymous closure and instantiation types, 'annotate every binding' is either performance-lossy on carded costs (D002, D009) or self-refuting via opaque annotations. But this dilemma has a third horn the language can be required to build: guaranteed nameability (lambda-lifting, explicit environment structs, nameable instantiations) — verbose, charter-payable, and it simultaneously dissolves the defense's generation-fixpoint argument. Because the alternative carries the load only if the language affirmatively provides nameability, consistency-checking of repeated code, root-cause diagnostics, and mechanical annotation tooling, the correct verdict is replace_with_alternative rather than a bare cut. Honesty constraint: both parties' load-bearing D-cards (D001, D002, D009) are provisional direct extractions not yet adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule; round1-index common gaps), all AI-writer behavioral claims are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, and the corpus quantifies no compile-time or code-size magnitudes anywhere — so the verdict carries an explicit defeasance clause tied to the named experiments rather than pretending the annotation-density question is settled.
followUps: AI-codegen A/B experiment (the decisive one): generate a matched task corpus under (a) full per-binding annotation with nameability and (b) mandatory signatures plus policy-free body propagation; measure hallucination/drift rates across near-duplicates, compile-fix cycles to green, automated-repair success from diagnostics, and token/context cost on generation and re-read. Triggers the defeasance clause if (b) wins on net error rate.

Redundancy-as-cross-check experiment: inject known writer errors and measure the rate redundant annotations catch them versus new errors introduced by generating the extra annotation tokens — the cross-check-vs-doubling question both parties flag as untested.

Nameability prototype and token audit: verify lambda-lifting plus explicit instantiation naming covers all D001-class monomorphized intermediates and closures without D002-style boxing, and measure actual source growth in adapter/combinator chains (the O(n^2) claim is analytic, unmeasured).

Compile-time measurement at scale: syntax-directed checking of fully annotated large AI-generated sources versus policy-free local inference, including pathological function sizes — the corpus has zero compile-time quantification (D001/D004 negative scopes, caveats.md).

Fourth fact channel study (from the numeric-semantics record, uncarded in both directions): how much nsw/known-bits/range-grade optimization the compiler soundly derives from declared types and dominating guards with no extra frontend flags — bounds how much mode annotation the optimizer actually needs and reprices the annotation burden.

Diagnostic engineering prototype: provenance-chain, root-cause-deduplicated machine-readable diagnostics for annotation mismatch; measure whether they hold repair-loop cost at O(1) per root fault versus O(uses) cascades.
Adversarial verification of the load-bearing provisional cards D001, D002, D009 (and D004, D010) per the caveats.md standing rule — both sides built on unverified direct extractions.

Rule on the representation-identical coercion boundary explicitly (e.g., exclusive-to-shared weakening): specify the proof obligation that a coercion is a lowering no-op, or cut even these for uniformity.

result.optimizerRole: Per-instance, generics add nothing: D001 attests monomorphization yields runtime behavior identical to hand-written concrete code, so the optimizer sees the same machine-code endpoint either way — the prosecution is granted this fully. The feature's real optimizer role is structural: (1) cross-instantiation identity, which enables cross-unit deduplication (D004 context; rustc share-generics is a named round-1 follow-up) and which textual near-duplicate copies destroy the moment any drift or checked-divergence hatch exists; (2) deferral of the specialize/share decision, keeping open the D005 bounded-sharing hybrid and the F009 Swift-SIL fourth model (IR-visible generic structure) that round-1 explicitly held open pending measurement — source-level pre-expansion forecloses that axis irreversibly, with no compiler knob to un-expand; (3) demand-driven instantiation (D001/D005/D010), which throttles the over-specialization compile-time/cache pressure documented in D009's negative scope; and (4) delivery of the concrete-type endpoint that J001/J002 show every high-performance runtime converges on, through a deterministic channel with zero guard/deopt/invalidation machinery. In the minimal kept form (frontend monomorphization only), the round-1 decisive open question — whether shared/erased bodies can discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations — does not enter the shipped core: every instantiation discharges F003 per-instance exactly as concrete code does. That question attaches only to the sharing option, which stays gated behind the check round-1 already ordered.
result.verificationRole: This is the decisive axis, and it is where the alternative collapses. Both parties agree N specializations must be verifiable as instantiations of one declared intent — the alternative's own CHECK concedes drift is the expected failure mode of a fallible writer at scale. But any check adequate to that job IS parametric generics: it needs a template sub-language with type parameters, template well-typedness checked once against declared bounds (otherwise errors surface per-copy post-expansion, defeating machine-readable diagnostics), and a computable instantiation relation copy == template[T]. If the checker can compute template[T] to compare, the compiler can emit template[T] directly; the expander is a strict subset of the checker (substitution without tree-equality). Generation-by-construction is therefore strictly stronger than check-after-generation: there is no window where divergent copies exist, and no checker whose acceptance bugs silently ship semantically wrong programs. The prosecution's trust-path argument fails on inspection — a checker bug that admits a divergent copy is not 'detected-late drift'; it is an undetected wrong program, equivalent in running-program severity to a miscompile. Kept generics also give check-once/instantiate-many verification: a template checked against bounds is a universally quantified proof over all instantiations, and F003-class discharge obligations established once transfer by substitution soundness, whereas per-copy re-verification scales with the instantiation closure and any tractability weakening of the check reopens the drift surface the charter forbids.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: All AI-writer behavioral claims on both sides are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence. That said, the admissible cost directions are forced by construction and favor keep: (1) token/context mass scales with the transitive instantiation closure under written-out copies versus O(1) in instantiation count for a template — generated, stored, and re-read on every edit; (2) template edits under the alternative trigger regeneration cascades across every downstream module, each rewrite a fresh hallucination opportunity, with a stale-copy window until the build-gating check closes it; (3) deciding which copies must exist is a whole-program call-graph fixpoint (generic-calls-generic at caller parameters) that compilers perform internally on demand (D001/D005/D010) — the alternative shifts it onto the writer or external tooling; (4) one template-level defect fans out as N per-copy diagnostics unless tooling re-aggregates by template identity — machinery generics gets for free. Verbosity being admissible cuts FOR the minimal kept form: instantiation arguments can be fully explicit, deleting the inference/solving subsystem the prosecution rightly fears, at pure token cost the charter accepts. The one open writer-side risk on the keep side: whether AI writers handle template abstraction (binding, bounds, explicit instantiation) with lower error rates than near-duplicate emission — untested in both directions, and the experiment is named in follow-ups.
whatMustBeTrue: The kept core is minimal: monomorphization-only parametric generics, expansion always compiler-side, explicit instantiation arguments (no global inference or bound solving in the trust path), polymorphic recursion and higher-rank parameters excluded so the instantiation closure is decidable and compile time bounded.

Templates are type-checked once against declared bounds BEFORE instantiation (Rust-style, not C++-post-expansion-style), so diagnostics are template-level and machine-readable rather than fanning out per instance.

Dynamic dispatch remains a separate, explicit, restricted construct (D002/D003) — round-1's leading-candidate architecture — and generic dispatchable methods stay excluded from dyn interfaces pending the escape-hatch design spike; the D003 composition hole is carried as a documented limitation, not papered over.

No IR-level generic ABI, witness tables, or shared-body machinery enters the core now: the D005/F009 sharing-hybrid axis stays open exactly as round-1 gated it — behind the F003 shared-body discharge check and the mono-vs-shared-vs-dictionary measurement program with pre-committed thresholds.

Legitimate per-type divergence (SIMD widths, layout-specific loop shapes) is expressed as explicit, individually-checked overloads/specializations visible in source — never as unchecked divergence from a template — with coherence rules making wrong-instantiation selection a hard resolution error.

The load-bearing provisional cards D001, D002, D003, D005, D007 survive adversarial verification per the caveats.md standing rule; if D001's concrete-code-equivalence claim falls, this ruling is revisited.

The AI-codegen experiment program runs and does not show that writers are dramatically more reliable emitting checked near-duplicate copies than instantiating templates; a strong contrary result reopens the question at the level of form, not principle, since the checked-copy design still contains the template mechanism.
result.rationale: The null hypothesis is that generics are not in the core, and the prosecution must show the named alternative carries the load. The ruling turns on a structural finding: the alternative does not instantiate the null hypothesis. To declare and CHECK that copies instantiate a common template, the language must contain a parameterized template sub-language, once-checked template well-typedness against bounds (or diagnostics fan out per-copy, post-expansion), and a computable instantiation relation copy == template[T]. A compiler that can compute template[T] to check a copy can emit template[T]; the expander is a strict subset of the checker. So the 'cut' keeps essentially all of generics' language complexity, discards only the deterministic expansion step — the one part machines do perfectly — and adds back: O(instantiation-closure) token mass, regeneration cascades on template edits, writer-side whole-program closure computation (D001/D005/D010 show compilers do this on demand), N-fold diagnostic fan-out, and a new drift channel (checker acceptance bugs ship undetected wrong programs — the prosecution's 'detected-late drift, never miscompilation' claim is wrong on inspection, since an admitted divergent copy is compiled faithfully into a semantically wrong artifact). The prosecution's own risk list concedes the two remaining holes: 'mechanism regrowth' into a parametric type system living inside the checker, and checked-divergence escape hatches that reinstate exactly the unchecked near-duplicate regions the CHECK exists to eliminate. The defender's dilemma stands: full substitution-equality makes every copy a redundant echo of compiler output; anything weaker institutionalizes an unverifiable drift surface in a charter where verification matters more, not less. What the prosecution genuinely proved is granted and shapes the form of the keep: D001 means generics add no per-instance optimizer information, so the case for compiler-side expansion rests on cross-copy identity, deferral of the specialize/share decision (D004, D005, F009 — the axis round-1 explicitly held open pending measurement, which source-level pre-expansion would foreclose irreversibly), demand-driven instantiation throttling D009-class over-specialization, and check-once/instantiate-many verification with F003 obligations transferring by substitution. The prosecution's legitimate fears — inference/solving subsystems, F009-style metastasis into IR, witness-table ABI, the F003 shared-body question — are severable, and the ruling severs them: the kept core is monomorphization-only with explicit instantiation, which deletes that open-question cluster from the shipped core as effectively as cutting would, without paying the alternative's costs. J001/J002 settle the endpoint question in the keep's favor: every performance strategy converges on concrete-type specialization, the information demand is constant and only the channel differs, and a bounds-checked template with compiler expansion is the cheapest channel with zero guard/deopt machinery and zero redundant source. Why keep_essential rather than needs_evidence: the missing empirics (drift rates, token magnitudes, mono-vs-shared bloat — all EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, all D-cards provisional per caveats.md) arbitrate the eventual FORM of generics (mono-only vs bounded-sharing vs Swift-style hybrid), which round-1 already holds open behind a measurement program. They do not arbitrate the in/out decision, because the collapse argument is logical, not empirical: both parties' designs contain the template mechanism, so 'out' was never actually on offer — only 'in, plus a redundant serialized expansion with worse trust properties.' Verification necessity is thereby proven in the only sense available: the anti-drift check both sides require IS parametric generics, and generation-by-construction dominates check-after-generation. The verdict is decisive on that ground and honest about the rest: if adversarial verification breaks D001, or the AI-codegen experiments show template abstraction catastrophically degrades writer reliability, the question reopens — at the level of surface form, not of the mechanism's presence in the core.
followUps: Run the missing AI-codegen experiment: implement identical functionality as (a) generic template plus explicit instantiations, (b) N written-out copies under a substitution-equality checker, (c) N unchecked copies; measure drift rate, token/context cost, repair-loop iterations, and whether writers reliably regenerate all copies after a template edit. This is the corpus's first AI-writer evidence and directly tests both parties' EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED claims.

Execute round-1's measurement program with pre-committed decision thresholds: monomorphization vs shared-body vs dictionary-passing on runtime, code size, i-cache, and compile time, including rustc share-generics deduplication data — this arbitrates the held-open form of the kept feature.

Run the round-1 decisive construction-level check before any sharing extension: can a shared/erased generic body soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and at what precision loss; record as a new evidence card.
Adversarially verify D001, D002, D003, D005, D007 per the caveats.md standing rule; this ruling leans hardest on D001 and flags it for priority.

Design the explicit-instantiation surface: how the writer declares instantiation sets, how missing instantiations surface as machine-readable resolution errors, and cross-unit instantiation deduplication semantics (D004 boundary sensitivity).

Execute the round-1 escape-hatch design spike for the D003 composition hole: generic methods on dyn interfaces, instantiation at erased types, heterogeneous generic registries — close the hole or card its cost as a known limitation.

Specify the checked per-type specialization/overload mechanism (the legitimate-divergence channel): declaration form, coherence/ambiguity rules, and how the optimizer distinguishes a deliberate specialization from the template path.

Resolve the antecedent charter question round-1 flagged: whether performance transparency means a predictable cost model or a guaranteed-optimization floor — it gates whether the compiler may silently devirtualize or de-duplicate what the writer wrote explicitly.

Surface the N/A/C/J card series in a re-weighed dispatch debate (round-1 ran on the D/F subset only); J001/J002 entered this trial but the C and N series remain unexamined for generics-relevant evidence.

result.stance: CUT. Dynamic dispatch — including the restricted, dyn-compatible trait-object construct in round1's leading candidate — should be excluded from the core language. Exhaustiveness-checked switch-over-variants plus typed function-pointer tables, written out by the AI, carry the load.
result.coreArgument: The null hypothesis holds because dynamic dispatch fails all three charter tests — extreme performance, compiler simplicity, verifiability — while its admissible benefits to a machine writer are entirely unproven and its costs are the best-carded facts in the dispatch corpus. (1) Performance: dyn is a documented optimization defeat (D002: vtable lookup cost, method inlining prevented), and every recovery path in evidence is conditional, speculative, revertible, and option-dependent (D007), blockable by linking semantics (D008), and pattern-matched by the corpus's general finding that runtime recovery is never a guarantee (F007/J004, J002). The round1 leading candidate itself concedes the point: its dyn construct's "stated cost model does not rely on devirtualization" — meaning in-core dyn is priced as a permanent indirect call. The alternative dominates it: every closed-set dispatch site stays on the corpus's best-attested mechanism — guaranteed static, concrete code (D001) — as an exhaustive switch whose arms are direct, inlinable calls that preserve per-callee alias facts (F003); at genuinely open sites, a typed function-pointer table pays exactly the indirect-call cost dyn would pay anyway (D009 shows function-valued indirection is the cost driver regardless of language feature). So the language construct buys zero performance over the alternative at any site. (2) Compiler simplicity: even "restricted" dyn imports the entire D003 rule subsystem (dyn-compatibility exclusions, receiver restrictions), plus compiler-synthesized vtable layout, fat-pointer representation, unsizing coercions, and dyn-generics interaction into the trusted core — and D003's exclusion of generic dispatchable methods means the construct still fails to compose (round1's flagged composition hole): you pay the surface and keep the gap. The alternative adds approximately zero new surface — sum types, exhaustive match, and first-class function values are machinery the core needs regardless — and its dispatch tables are user-visible checked code rather than trusted compiler output. (3) Verification, which this charter weights above all: a dyn call site is a universally-quantified obligation over an open implementer set — worst-case fact attenuation, with the decisive question of whether erased/indirect bodies can discharge F003 noalias obligations still unrun (round1's own top follow-up). A switch is a syntactically closed callee set: exhaustiveness is machine-checked, contracts are checkable per named target, and when the variant set grows the compiler emits a machine-readable edit list of every affected site — exactly the diagnostic a fallible writer at scale needs, versus dyn's silent absorption of a new implementer with no site-level re-verification trigger. The alternative's real admissible costs — more tokens per dispatch site and drift risk across duplicated arms — are precisely the error class this language exists to convert into compile-time checks, and no corpus evidence weighs them against dyn's carded costs. The defense's entire case rests on unmeasured claims about AI-writer convenience and an uncarded devirtualization story (LLVM WPD has no evidence card at all). Under the standing evidence rule and the null hypothesis, dyn does not make the core.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Directly cards the cost: trait objects are data pointer + vtable, calls use runtime virtual dispatch, add lookup cost, and prevent inlining of the method body, blocking optimizations. Its positive scope supports only making dynamic dispatch explicit IF used — it is not evidence for including it in the core of an extreme-performance language.

The only carded recovery mechanism (GCC devirtualization/IPA/LTO) is compiler- and option-dependent; speculative devirtualization may be reverted and LTO/IPA costs compile time, memory, and code size. Recovery is conditional, never a guarantee — so dyn must be priced as a permanent indirect call in the core.

Semantic interposition can block interprocedural analysis and inlining even for statically-known-looking code; dyn performance recovery depends on visibility/linking assumptions the project has explicitly not designed (round1: core/periphery fact attenuation is undesigned), so recovery cannot be assumed.

Shows that even a 'restricted' dyn construct is a whole restriction subsystem (no Self:Sized, no associated constants, no generic dispatchable methods, receiver rules) — real compiler surface and diagnostic surface — and its exclusion of generic dispatchable methods is the very composition hole round1 flagged: the construct's price is paid but heterogeneous generic APIs still have no sanctioned path.

Monomorphization/static specialization yields concrete-code runtime behavior — the corpus's most direct generics/dispatch performance card. Switch-over-variants keeps every closed-set dispatch on this guaranteed-static path with direct, per-arm-inlinable calls.

The flagship carded optimization class (noalias/alias.scope) is per-callee reasoning. Direct calls in switch arms let alias obligations be discharged against named targets; an indirect vtable call attenuates facts to the trait signature. Round1 records the discharge-through-erased/indirect-bodies question as decisive and unresolved — verification-first design should not adopt the construct that depends on its favorable resolution.

Carded pattern that managed-runtime recovery is conditional elimination, not a guarantee — the corpus-wide qualitative asymmetry (guarantee-by-construction beats recovery-by-optimization) that round1's judge endorsed, applied here against relying on devirtualization to salvage dyn.
Reinforces F007 independently: Oracle explicitly documents runtime analysis as conditional, not guaranteed — the same evidence class that makes 'the optimizer will fix dyn' an uncarded hope.

Speculative optimization requires guard and invalidation machinery; any speculative-devirt route to recover dyn cost imports deoptimization-class complexity into a compiler whose charter is simplicity and verifiability.

Optimization already depends on codegen-unit/LTO boundaries; recovering dyn performance via whole-program analysis requires LTO-class compile time and memory — a direct, admissible tax on the AI iteration loop that the switch/table alternative never incurs.

Honest two-way card: Function-typed fields hide call targets even inside closed programs, so the function-pointer-table alternative pays the same indirect-call cost at truly-dynamic sites. This proves indirection itself is the cost driver — the dyn language construct adds compiler surface without adding performance over explicit tables anywhere.

Negative scope: runtime metadata/reflection constrains closed-world assumptions unless restricted — supporting the exclusion of open-set dispatch machinery from a performance core whose optimizer wants closed-world facts.

Acknowledged tension, turned: the only workflow-confirmed dispatch-adjacent card teaches that performance-critical semantics should be VISIBLE to optimizer passes rather than buried in opaque effects. Explicit switches and explicit typed tables are maximally visible dispatch; compiler-synthesized vtables behind a dyn abstraction are the less-visible representation.

machineWriterCosts: Token/generation cost of the alternative is real and conceded: an N-variant dispatch site costs O(N) tokens as a switch versus O(1) as a dyn call, and the AI regenerates that structure at every dispatch site. Magnitude and whether it matters at all are unmeasured (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

Re-read/context cost cuts AGAINST dyn: a dyn call site hides its target set, so an AI re-reading or modifying the code must retrieve every implementer into context to reason about behavior; a switch enumerates all targets at the site itself. Information locality favors the alternative for the re-read half of the token budget (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

Compile time in the AI iteration loop: switch/table code needs no whole-program devirtualization, LTO, or speculative machinery to hit its stated performance (D004/D007 costs avoided). In-core dyn either stays permanently slow or drags LTO/WPD-class compile times into every iteration — and LLVM WPD is uncarded, so even that path is an evidence-free bet.

Machine-readable diagnostics: exhaustiveness failures are local, enumerable, and directly actionable — the compiler emits the exact list of sites and missing arms when a variant is added, a to-do list a fallible writer can execute mechanically. Dyn-compatibility violations (D003 class) are nonlocal trait-level errors over a larger rule surface, and adding an implementer to a dyn hierarchy triggers NO site-level diagnostics at all — silent behavior extension is the worst failure mode for an unattended writer.

Consistency drift across duplicated arms is the alternative's honest worst cost — but it is exactly the error class the charter says to convert into compile-time verification (exhaustiveness checking plus per-variant typed tables turn missing-arm and wrong-wiring hallucinations into compile errors), whereas dyn's failure modes (wrong implementer registered, stale override) surface only at runtime.
consistencyRisks: Arm-body drift: N near-duplicate switch sites over the same variant set can diverge behaviorally when regenerated; exhaustiveness checking catches missing arms, not subtly different arm bodies. Mitigation is an idiom — one canonical typed function table per variant set, with switches reduced to table lookups — but idiom adherence by AI writers is unverified (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

Wrong-slot wiring in hand-written function-pointer tables (variant/entry mismatch) if tables are index-addressed arrays; the language must require per-variant named, typed table fields so mis-wiring is a type error, not a latent runtime bug.

Round1's carded counter-risk applies to the cut: with no sanctioned dyn construct, writers may hand-roll void*/erasure patterns that the optimizer and F003 machinery handle WORSE than a language construct would (A1 weakness #4). The prosecution's remedy is that the checked switch/table idiom must ship as the blessed, verifier-supported pattern occupying that niche — this is a real obligation on the alternative, not a free win.

Variant-set evolution touches every dispatch site: the compiler enumerates all of them as errors (good), but the resulting multi-site edit is itself a drift opportunity for a fallible writer performing k near-identical edits (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED whether error-guided edits drift less than dyn's zero-edit silent extension misbehaves).
empiricalUntested: The corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence; every claim herein about AI writer behavior — drift rates across duplicated arms, token costs of generation vs re-reading, ability to enumerate closed target sets, whether exhaustiveness diagnostics reduce repair iterations, idiom adherence for canonical tables — is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Prevalence claim untested: that most dispatch sites in AI-written extreme-performance code have closed, statically-knowable target sets (the load-bearing assumption that switches, not tables, carry most of the weight).

No quantitative corpus evidence anywhere on switch/direct-call vs indirect-call runtime, code size, i-cache behavior, or compile time (round1 evidence gaps; caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude) — the branch-predictor vs indirect-branch trade at high arity is unmeasured.

Whether shared/erased/indirect bodies can soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and at what precision loss — round1's decisive construction-level check, never run; the prosecution's fact-attenuation claim about vtable calls is directionally carded but unquantified.
LLVM whole-program devirtualization (!type metadata, -fwhole-program-vtables) has no evidence card — the defense's best recovery story is uncarded, but so is its refutation.
All load-bearing D-cards (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule); this prosecution inherits that provisionality.

result.stance: KEEP — restricted partial form only. I defend an explicit, dyn-compatible (D003-style), visibly-costed trait-object construct as a non-default escape hatch, exactly the round1-dispatch-generics leading candidate. I do NOT defend implicit or pervasive dynamic dispatch, and I concede that closed, write-time-enumerable variant sets should prefer switch-over-variants (which the language should also have).
result.coreArgument: Cutting dyn does not remove indirect calls from programs; it removes the FACTS from the indirect calls. The workloads that need runtime dispatch (heterogeneous registries crossing module boundaries, callbacks/closures, the FFI/dynamic-linking periphery named in the interop dossier) still exist, so the AI writer will materialize dispatch as function-pointer tables over void* — which is the corpus's documented worst case, not a neutral lowering. (1) OPTIMIZER NECESSITY: a language-level dyn call is an indirect call PLUS facts the alternative loses: a complete, restricted method set per interface (D003) that D007-class devirtualization, speculative devirtualization, and indirect inlining can consume; finality facts (D006); and a typed, ownership-tracked payload whose F001/F002 non-interference facts can still be discharged as F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations. A hand-rolled fn-pointer table is an indirect call MINUS facts: D009 documents Function-typed fields hiding call targets as an optimizer defeat even inside fully closed programs, and there is no hierarchy for D007 machinery to consume. The round1 critique already recorded this as A1's composition hole: banning a sanctioned dyn path 'predicts hand-rolled void*/fn-pointer erasure that the optimizer and F003 machinery handle worse.' (2) VERIFICATION NECESSITY: the alternative's exhaustiveness check verifies the wrong invariant. It cannot check (a) data/vtable coupling — nothing stops the fallible writer pairing a Circle payload with a Square table row, a well-typed wrong-behavior or UB miscall over void*; (b) cross-site semantic agreement — K switch sites can each be exhaustive yet jointly divergent in what they do for variant X, whereas dyn makes 'one type, one behavior' structural (one impl); (c) F003 obligations on erased payloads, which are UB-grade promises that must be frontend-checked and become unverifiable once the payload exits the ownership checker via void*. The check is also fragile: it only works if default/wildcard arms are banned, which forces strict K×N arm growth. In a charter where the writer 'cannot be trusted to keep unchecked invariants,' the alternative manufactures three new classes of exactly such invariants. (3) MACHINE-WRITER FAILURE MODES: adding one type is an O(1) impl edit under dyn versus O(K) site regenerations under switches — an arithmetic asymmetry in edits, context re-reads, and (per D004's unit-boundary tradeoff) recompilation scope; K×N near-duplicate arms are the canonical drift substrate; and table-slot bugs type-check silently while dyn coercion failures are compile-time, machine-readable diagnostics. Decisive closing point: any attempt to repair the alternative — typing the tables, checking data/table coupling at construction, restricting signatures so rows are call-compatible, checking registry completeness — reconstructs trait objects feature by feature. The checked version of the alternative IS the feature. The null hypothesis is overcome only for this restricted form; per D001/D002 static specialization stays the sole implicit path and dyn's cost model must be stated truthfully (indirect call, no inlining assumed, no reliance on devirtualization per D007's negative scope).

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Documents that Function-typed fields hide call targets and defeat the optimizer even in fully closed programs — the fn-pointer-table alternative reproduces this defeat class exactly; the round1 evidence-gap list notes no position handles this, making it the attested cost of cutting dyn.

Attests a language can restrict which interfaces are dynamically dispatchable to keep the runtime representation coherent — precedent that the (data pointer, vtable) coupling invariant is machine-checkable as a language construct, which is the core verification-necessity claim; also defines the restriction set the kept feature must adopt.

Positive scope explicitly supports making dynamic dispatch explicit and optimizer-visible when used; its cost claims (lookup cost, blocked inlining) are conceded up front, which is why the defended form is a non-default, truthfully-costed escape hatch rather than an implicit path.

Devirtualization, speculative guarded devirtualization, and indirect inlining consume structured virtual-call and hierarchy facts — a recovery path that exists for a language-level dyn construct but has nothing to consume in hand-rolled tables; its negative scope (conditional, revertible) is honored by not letting the cost model rely on it.
Sealed/finality facts are precedented source-level inputs to dispatch reasoning; a language dyn construct can carry them to the optimizer, an AI-written pointer table cannot.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-on-violation promises sound only if frontend obligations are checked and true; a void*-erased payload exits the checker so these obligations become unverifiable, while a dyn payload inside the ownership discipline can still discharge them.

Language-level non-interference is the corpus's best-attested optimizer enabler; keeping heterogeneous payloads inside checked exclusivity across dispatch boundaries preserves this enabler, while unchecked erasure creates trust boundaries the fallible writer must maintain by convention.

The optimization value of alias facts is proving non-interference around writes (register promotion, load/store elimination, reordering); dispatch payloads that stay typed and ownership-tracked keep these optimizations sound across indirect calls.

Guaranteed static specialization remains the core's only implicit path; dyn is defended solely as the explicit residue for what monomorphization cannot express, consistent with the round1 leading candidate rather than in competition with it.

Attests compile-time vs optimization tradeoffs of compilation-unit boundaries — supporting the change-locality argument: a one-impl dyn edit confines recompilation to one unit, while variant addition invalidates every switch-site-containing unit in the AI iteration loop.

Dynamic-linking/interposition boundaries are real interop targets where write-time enumeration of variants at every switch site is impossible; an unchecked indirect-call idiom would emerge at the periphery regardless, and dyn is its checked form.

machineWriterCosts: Edit propagation: adding one type/variant is an O(1) localized impl edit under dyn versus O(K) switch-site regenerations (or table-row insertions) under the alternative; each of the K edits requires re-loading that site's context — an arithmetic asymmetry in tokens, though magnitude is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Generated-code volume: K dispatch sites x N variants yields K*N arms that must be emitted, re-read, and kept in sync; banning default arms (required for exhaustiveness checking to be meaningful) makes this growth strict.

Global read dependency for local edits: the switch author must enumerate the whole-program variant set at every site, so a local change requires whole-program knowledge at write time; dyn requires only the interface contract.

Compile-time in the AI iteration loop: every variant change invalidates all K dispatch-site modules for recompilation, versus one new unit for a dyn impl (D004 attests unit-boundary/compile-time coupling qualitatively; magnitude unquantified).

Diagnostics asymmetry: a wrong-slot or wrong-pairing bug in a hand-rolled table type-checks and fails only at runtime with no machine-readable diagnostic; a dyn coercion or impl-signature mismatch is a compile-time typed error pointing at the interface/impl pair — the diagnostic channel the charter says the machine writer needs.
consistencyRisks: Cross-site switch drift: K switch sites over the same variant set can each pass exhaustiveness yet silently diverge in per-variant behavior; the compiler cannot check cross-site semantic agreement, whereas dyn makes single-point-of-definition structural.

Partial regeneration: when the AI adds a variant and updates only some sites, exhaustiveness catches it ONLY if wildcard/default arms are banned; any default arm silently swallows new variants — a language-rule dependency the alternative's proponents must own.

Vtable-by-convention drift: positional slot meaning (slot 3 = area in every table) is an untyped ABI maintained by a fallible writer across all tables; one drifted row is a well-typed wrong-function call or UB signature misinterpretation over void*.

Data/table coupling drift: nothing in the alternative prevents pairing a payload of type X with the table for type Y at registry-construction sites; under dyn this pairing exists only through checked coercion.

Residual risk if kept: dyn impl bodies can still drift semantically from the interface's intended contract (drift is localized to one impl but not eliminated), and registry-construction sites remain the places to audit; behavioral contracts on trait methods would be the follow-on mitigation, which ties into the violation-semantics topic.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI-writer drift rates on near-duplicate switch arms and table rows: the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence; the K*N drift-substrate argument is structural, its incidence rate is untested.
Token/context cost magnitude of O(K) site propagation versus O(1) impl edits in real AI iteration loops.

Runtime performance delta between dyn indirect calls, switch-over-variants, and devirtualized direct calls: round1 records zero quantitative cards on runtime, code size, i-cache, or compile time for any dispatch strategy.
Code-size/i-cache impact of K*N switch arms versus shared vtables at scale (the plausible optimizer-side cost of the alternative for large N) — unquantified in corpus.

Whether LLVM whole-program devirtualization (!type metadata, -fwhole-program-vtables) actually recovers dyn cost for an LLVM-targeted language: uncarded per round1 evidence gaps; D007 is GCC-only and conditional, so the defense deliberately does not rest on devirtualization.

Whether erased/shared bodies can soundly discharge F003 noalias obligations and at what precision loss — the round1 follow-up construction check has not been run; my claim that void* erasure makes discharge unverifiable-in-practice is the currently-recorded but unproven direction of that check.
All load-bearing D-cards (D001, D002, D003, D007) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule).
Compile-time magnitude of switch-site invalidation versus incremental dyn-impl compilation: D004 supports the coupling qualitatively only.

result.stance: CUT. Type inference and implicit conversions must be excluded from the core language. Full mandatory annotation of every type, effect, layout, and mode at every binding and call carries the load. The null hypothesis holds: no corpus card evidences an optimizer benefit from inference or implicit conversion, while multiple cards evidence optimizer benefit from explicitly stated, compiler-checked facts.
result.coreArgument: The indictment has four counts. COUNT 1 — Inference destroys the fact channel the optimizer needs. The corpus's central lesson is that extreme performance comes from stated, verified, lowering-surviving facts: F009 (SIL keeps performance-critical semantics visible to passes rather than buried), F001/F003 (language-level stated non-interference rules are the enablers; restrict-like contracts 'depend on programmer truthfulness' when unchecked), N002 ('overflow facts are contracts, not mere annotations'), N004 (declared ranges are semantic promises), A001/A003 (MLIR wins by carrying shape/stride/layout structurally). The round1 leading candidates all mandate type-level facts: checked ownership (aliasing-model), Send/Sync-like capabilities (concurrency, C004), named truthfully-lowered numeric modes (numeric-semantics), guaranteed static specialization where dispatch-generics converged on making guaranteed specialization 'the core's only implicit path' — the debates are already squeezing implicitness out. Inference inverts this architecture: the writer withholds the fact, and the compiler must reconstruct it by solving, then trust its own reconstruction. The AI writer already computed every type, layout, and mode when it generated the expression; eliding them discards information only to re-derive it at compile time inside the iteration loop. COUNT 2 — Verification asymmetry, the decisive count. This language's premise is a writer fallible at scale whose unchecked invariants cannot be trusted. A mandatory annotation is checked redundancy: two independent derivations (writer's claim, compiler's check of the expression against it) that must agree, so every hallucination becomes a localized type error at the drift site. Inference is single-sourced: the compiler silently adopts whatever type the possibly-wrong expression has, and the error either surfaces far from its cause or never surfaces — it just compiles to the wrong layout, mode, or specialization. D009 is the carded exhibit: in Julia, inference failure (type instability) is not an error but silent dynamic dispatch and indirect storage — silent performance loss is precisely the failure mode an optimizer-first language cannot host in its core. J001/J002 show the information demand is invariant — what is not stated statically must be reconstructed with profiling, guards, and deoptimization machinery, and F007/J004 show compiler-recovered facts are conditional ('when analysis succeeds'), never guaranteed. Stated facts are unconditional. COUNT 3 — Implicit conversions are compiler-inserted unrequested computation that reassigns semantic contracts without a token in the source. N001/N002 establish that numeric semantics are per-operation contracts (wrapping vs nuw/nsw-poison); an implicit widening or int-to-float silently swaps which contract governs, making the round1 'named, truthful numeric modes' candidate incoherent — implicit conversion is unnamed by construction. D010 shows dispatch selects on all argument types, so conversions silently perturb which method is chosen and specialized (D001). The replacement cost is one explicit conversion call per site: linear, checkable, trivially generatable. COUNT 4 — Compiler simplicity is verification budget. Cutting inference and coercion deletes the unification/constraint solver, defaulting rules, coercion-insertion pass, and ambiguity-resolution ordering — each a semantic-bearing component that would need its own proof discipline (N006 shows even single InstCombine folds demand Alive2 proofs; the refuted-claims ledger shows two plausible LLVM claims died under adversarial check — semantic surface area is where errors hide). What remains is a syntax-directed, local, parallelizable checking judgment: the compiler never chooses a type and never inserts an operation; it only verifies stated facts and computes bottom-up that fully-annotated leaves compose (which is checking against declared signatures, not inference). Diagnostics become site-local and machine-readable by construction, versus the notorious non-locality of solver-based inference errors. The defense must prove, with evidence the corpus does not contain, that eliding facts from a fallible writer's output improves an optimizer-first language. It cannot.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Swift SIL's design lesson: performance-critical semantics should be visible to optimizer passes rather than buried in implicit effects. Mandatory source annotations are the source-level instantiation of this principle; inference buries facts in solver state that must be reconstructed before they can be preserved.

Language-level stated non-interference rules (Rust &mut, Fortran dummy rules, SPARK anti-aliasing) are the central optimizer enablers, and its negative scope warns that restrict-like contracts 'depend on programmer truthfulness' when unchecked — with a fallible AI writer, facts must be both stated and checked, which is exactly what mandatory annotation is and inference is not.

LLVM separates default arithmetic from optimization promises via nuw/nsw: 'overflow facts are contracts, not mere annotations.' Implicit conversions silently change which numeric contract governs an operation with no source token, corrupting the contract channel.

The poison/undef/freeze lattice shows optimizer-facing semantics are precise per-value contracts; a core that lets the compiler invent conversions injects contract changes the writer never stated and the verifier cannot attribute to any source claim.

Range metadata is the model of an explicit fact channel: a declared promise whose violation is poison. Mandatory annotations generalize this — every binding becomes a declared, checkable, lowerable promise.

Even individual InstCombine folds require Alive2 proofs and flag-interaction tests. Every inference rule, defaulting rule, and coercion-insertion rule is additional semantic-bearing compiler surface demanding the same proof discipline; deleting the solver and coercion pass shrinks the verification budget of the whole compiler.

Julia's carded failure mode: when inference cannot pin concrete types, code silently degrades to dynamic dispatch and indirect storage. Inference failure is silent performance loss, not an error — inadmissible in an extreme-performance core where wrong-but-compiling is the worst outcome.

Dispatch selects methods on all positional argument types; implicit conversions therefore silently change which method is selected and specialized, making call-site behavior depend on coercion-resolution internals rather than stated facts.

Monomorphization gives concrete-code performance but requires concrete types per instantiation; explicit call-site instantiation annotations make every specialization a checkable stated fact rather than a solver artifact, aligning with the dispatch-generics round1 candidate of guaranteed static specialization.

Dynamic runtimes converge on reconstructing types via profiling and guards — 'the information demand is the same, only the channel differs.' Since the demand is invariant, the design question is only which channel is cheapest to verify; explicit static annotation is the only channel that is checkable before execution.

Speculation requires invalidation machinery (counters, deopt, one-directional generalization) — the carded price of acting on reconstructed rather than stated facts. A core that guarantees stated facts avoids this machinery class entirely.

HotSpot escape analysis yields conditional elimination, 'not guaranteed stack allocation' — compiler-derived facts are inherently conditional on analysis success, whereas the alternative's stated layout/mode facts are unconditional guarantees the optimizer can always assume.

Rust shows sharing can be a type-level fact (Send/Sync plus exclusive borrowing) letting the optimizer treat non-atomic memory as unshared 'unless types say otherwise' — the mode annotations the alternative mandates at every binding are exactly this fact class, and they must be stated to be checked.

MLIR MemRef succeeds by making shape, strides, offsets, and memory spaces optimizer-visible structural facts with explicit contracts; mandatory layout annotations extend the same architecture up to source, giving the frontend facts to emit rather than reconstruct.

Affine's lesson: restricting expressiveness to make analysis 'efficient and reliable' is a winning trade. Restricting the source to fully-stated types/effects/layouts/modes makes front-end checking syntax-directed and reliable in the same way.

The optimization value of facts is proving non-interference around writes (register promotion, read/write elimination, reordering); these payoffs flow from facts the compiler can trust, strengthening the case that the core's job is verifying stated facts, not synthesizing them.

machineWriterCosts: Generation token cost: annotating every binding, parameter, return, call-site instantiation, effect, layout, and mode materially inflates tokens generated per iteration and the context occupied when re-reading code. Conceded as real and admissible. Partial offset (untested): annotation tokens are low-perplexity/high-predictability for an LLM, and fully-annotated fragments are self-describing, so an AI re-reading one function does not need transitive module context to know any binding's type — a potential net context saving.

Refactoring amplification: changing one type, layout, or mode fans out to every annotated site; the AI writer must regenerate N sites per change, and each regeneration is a fresh hallucination opportunity. Mitigation: every miss is caught by the checker at the miss site (checked redundancy), converting correctness risk into bounded iteration-loop cost — but the loop cost itself is real.

More compile-error iterations: mandatory annotations mean more distinct mismatch errors per attempt, including trivial ones inference would have absorbed, lengthening the AI edit-compile loop even when the underlying logic was correct. Offset: each error is site-local and mechanically fixable, which is the diagnostic shape a machine consumer wants.

Explicit conversion calls at every representation change add call-site noise the writer must generate correctly; a forgotten conversion is a hard error rather than silently absorbed. This is a deliberate trade of iteration friction for contract precision.

Diagnostics volume: full annotation multiplies checkable claims, so diagnostic output per failed compile grows; machine-readable structured diagnostics are a hard requirement for the alternative to be workable, not an optional nicety.

Costs avoided by the cut (credited against the above): no compile-time spent in constraint solving inside the iteration loop; no need for the AI to simulate the inference engine or coercion-resolution order to predict what its own code means; no non-local inference errors whose provenance the AI must reconstruct across the module.
consistencyRisks: Inference-side silent divergence (the prosecution's core drift claim): two near-duplicate code copies — expected under a verbosity-tolerant AI writer — can infer different types/layouts/modes from a one-token difference (e.g., a literal), and nothing flags the divergence; the copies silently compile to different representations and different performance. Annotation converts this exact drift into a compile error at the drift site.

Annotation-side drift channel (owned honestly): full annotation creates massive checked redundancy; the residual risk is correlated drift, where the AI consistently mis-annotates AND writes matching wrong code at multiple sites, which type-checks. The type/effect/mode system catches divergent drift, not coherent wrongness; contract/verification layers (violation-semantics topic) must carry coherent-wrongness, and the prosecution's case does not claim otherwise.

Boundary-drawing risk: the cut must be specified as (a) no elided facts at bindings/params/returns/instantiations and (b) no compiler-inserted conversions, while bottom-up checking of expressions from fully-annotated leaves remains. If the judge treats local expression typing as 'inference', the feature becomes a spectrum and the clean CUT verdict blurs; the prosecution's line is: the compiler may never choose a type or insert an operation, only verify.

Cross-topic consistency: the alternative hard-depends on the round1 leading candidates (checked ownership, capability modes, named numeric modes, static specialization) supplying a finite, closed annotation vocabulary. Those topics are research_needed/prototype_needed, not decided; if the mode vocabulary churns, every annotation in the corpus of generated code churns with it — a systemic rewrite risk unique to the fully-annotated design.

Toolchain consistency: if any inference or coercion later leaks in at a non-core layer (macros, codegen templates, a 'convenience' front-end), the guarantee that every fact is stated-and-checked silently evaporates; the cut is only stable if the IR verifier (ir-strategy candidate) rejects unstated facts, closing the back door.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: all claims about AI writer behavior — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence cards. This includes every claim below.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: AI annotation-hallucination rate vs AI inference-misprediction rate — whether an LLM writer produces wrong explicit annotations more or less often than it mispredicts what an inference engine would conclude about its unannotated code.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: net context/token economics — whether self-describing fully-annotated fragments reduce total context needed for AI re-reading and editing by more than the annotation tokens cost; no measurements exist in either direction.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: compile-time delta between syntax-directed checking of fully annotated programs and constraint-based inference for a type system carrying ownership, effects, layouts, and modes — the direction (checking is cheaper) is structural, the magnitude in the AI iteration loop is unmeasured.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: consistency-drift rates between near-duplicate AI-generated code under annotated vs inferred regimes, and what fraction of drift the checker catches versus coherent-wrongness that type-checks.
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: refactoring fan-out cost — edit volume and induced error rate when an AI propagates a type/layout/mode change across all mandatory annotation sites.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (corpus-wide, per caveats.md): no quantified performance deltas exist for any contract regime; documentation evidence shows what compilers claim, not magnitudes. D/A/C/J cards are direct-extraction and not adversarially verified, so D009/D010/J001/J002/A001/A003/C004 citations are provisional per the agenda's standing rule.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the numeric-semantics backlog item cuts both ways — the corpus itself flags (missing-research-backlog, round1 numeric follow-up) that how much the optimizer can infer without frontend flags is unmeasured; if compiler inference recovers most facts cheaply, part of the prosecution's 'stated facts are load-bearing' premise weakens for that subdomain.

result.stance: CUT. Closures/higher-order functions should be excluded from the core language. Defunctionalization — top-level functions plus explicit environment structs, with no capture semantics — carries the load. The null hypothesis stands unrebutted: the corpus contains not one verified card in favor of closures, and its only direct card on function values is an optimizer-defeat card.
result.coreArgument: The prosecution's case rests on four pillars. (1) Closures are the corpus's documented optimizer defeat, with zero countervailing evidence. The single card that directly addresses function values, D009, documents that Function-typed fields hide representation and call targets and cause dynamic dispatch or indirect storage — inside fully closed programs, which is why the round-1 dispatch critique used D009 to refute even the closed-world advocate. The feature-evidence coverage matrix rates closures/captures at low_to_medium confidence, 12 named evidence gaps, 'evidence supports tradeoff mapping only', needs adversarial verification. Under the project's own decision-gates rule ('a feature cannot become a language default merely because it is optimizer-attractive'), a feature with this evidentiary posture cannot enter the core; here it is not even optimizer-attractive. (2) Closures structurally contradict both existing design_candidate_later leaders. The dispatch-generics leader is 'guaranteed static specialization as the core's ONLY implicit path, with dynamic dispatch solely through an explicit, restricted construct' — a first-class closure is an implicit indirect-call site in D002's cost class (lookup cost, prevents inlining, blocks optimizations), smuggled past the explicitness rule. The aliasing-model leader (checked ownership/exclusive mutation) has, per the judge, 'no carded story for higher-order functions/callbacks', and misleading-comparisons note 2 names callbacks as the thing that complicates lowering ownership to noalias. Capture semantics would force a bespoke capture-mode subsystem onto the aliasing core; explicit environment structs make every capture an ordinary struct field that the already-planned ownership machinery checks with no new language surface. (3) Closure costs are recovered only conditionally; defunctionalized code never incurs them. Escaping closure environments require heap allocation whose elimination depends on escape analysis that F007/J004 confirm is conditional, inlining-dependent, and explicitly not guaranteed stack allocation; indirect calls require devirtualization/indirect-call promotion that D007 documents as speculative, revertible, and option-dependent, and D008 shows linking semantics can block outright. J001/J002 show what a language buys when it erases call targets from the static channel: profiling, guards, and deoptimization machinery. Defunctionalized code needs none of this — every call site names a top-level function (direct, trivially inlinable) or an explicit, writer-chosen tag dispatch whose cost is visible in source, exactly the transparency the charter demands. (4) The compiler gets simpler and more verifiable. The llvm-mlir capability map's 'closure capture' row is all TBD — preconditions, IR construct, consuming passes, verifier checks, hazards: nobody has sketched a sound lowering; cutting the feature deletes the row. Closure conversion and capture inference are a whole compiler phase that the verifier-checked IR strategy would have to prove semantics-preserving, and F004's negative scope warns that strong allocation facts (noalias returns) require lifetime/provenance/escape rules first — anonymous escaping environments are precisely what complicates those rules. Finally, the headline use case is already spoken for: the arrays-loops leader is structured-arrays-first (A002/A003/A006/A010 class), where map/reduce/parallel-for bodies are expressed as structured operations with explicit iteration semantics — Halide's algorithm/schedule separation (A006) demonstrates high-level composition in a performance core without first-class capture. Iterator pipelines, callbacks, and async can all take a top-level function plus an explicit env-struct pointer; the compiler sees a concrete function symbol and a concrete struct type, and D001-style static specialization does the rest. Since human ergonomics is inadmissible, the only defense left to closures is machine-writer cost — addressed below, conceded where real, and insufficient: every defunctionalization artifact is machine-checkable, whereas hidden capture is a semantic invariant the fallible-at-scale writer would hold unchecked.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's only direct card on function-typed values: Julia performance guidance says Function-typed fields hide representation/call targets and cause dynamic dispatch or indirect storage — a documented optimizer defeat that holds even in closed programs (used in round1-dispatch-generics to refute the closed-world advocate).

Establishes the cost class of indirect dispatch (runtime lookup, prevents inlining of the method body, blocks optimizations) and its positive scope supports making dynamic dispatch EXPLICIT when used — closures are implicit members of this cost class; defunctionalized tag-dispatch is the explicit form.

Devirtualization/indirect-call recovery is conditional, speculative, revertible, and option-dependent — the mechanism a closure-bearing core must lean on is not a guarantee, violating the guarantee-by-construction asymmetry the dispatch judge found best-attested.

Semantic interposition can block interprocedural analysis of exported symbols — closure-typed values crossing module/ABI boundaries lose call-target facts the optimizer cannot recover; named top-level functions keep the boundary analyzable or at least explicit.

Escape analysis yields conditional allocation elimination (scalar replacement when analysis succeeds), explicitly NOT guaranteed stack allocation — the recovery path for heap-allocated closure environments is exactly this conditional class.

Primary-vendor sharpening of F007: runtime analysis gives conditional, not guaranteed, allocation optimization — closure environment allocation cannot be promised away; explicit env structs let the writer choose stack placement statically.
What static types don't carry, runtimes must recover via profiling+guards — erasing call targets behind closure values recreates the dynamic-language information deficit inside a static language.
Speculation requires invalidation machinery (guards, deopt) — the runtime price a core pays when call targets are values rather than symbols; a defunctionalized core needs none of it.

Negative scope: strong allocation-like noalias facts require the source language to settle lifetime/provenance/escape rules first — anonymous, capture-bearing, escaping closure environments are the hard case that complicates those rules; explicit env structs are ordinary objects under the ordinary rules.

Static specialization of top-level generic functions gives concrete-code runtime behavior — the alternative's performance path (top-level function + env-struct type parameter monomorphized) is the corpus's best-attested generics mechanism.

Precedent that a language can RESTRICT dynamic-dispatch interfaces to keep runtime representation coherent — supports offering only a restricted explicit construct (defunctionalized dispatch) rather than general first-class functions.

Halide separates algorithm from schedule as explicit manipulable structure — high-level composition in a performance layer is achieved without first-class capture-bearing functions, covering the 'we need HOFs for composability' defense.

Linalg keeps loop/data semantics as structured operations with explicit iterator roles — the map/reduce/parallel-for use case (the dominant closure workload in performance code) is served by structured ops, per the arrays-loops leading candidate (structured-arrays-first).
Fortran DO CONCURRENT expresses loop-body independence via restrictions and locality specifiers, not lambdas — precedent for parallel iteration constructs without closures.

Optimization already depends on compilation-unit/LTO boundaries; adding closure values that flow across those boundaries multiplies the visibility problem the corpus says is real, while named top-level functions are the unit LTO machinery is designed around.

machineWriterCosts: Token/context cost — CONCEDED, bounded: each would-be lambda becomes three artifacts (env struct definition, top-level function, explicit construction site). This inflates generated tokens and the context needed to re-read call sites. Bounded because the charter accepts verbosity, structured-array ops absorb the highest-frequency case (loop bodies), and every added token is declarative, machine-checkable structure rather than free-form logic.

Consistency drift across the fn/env-struct/construction-site triple — CONCEDED as the alternative's most serious cost, but every drift mode is a compile error: a hallucinated field name, wrong arity, or stale struct shape fails type-checking with a trivially machine-readable diagnostic ('struct S has no field x'). Contrast the feature on trial: a wrong implicit capture (by-value where by-reference was needed, unintended alias captured) can type-check and run — a silent semantic invariant the fallible-at-scale writer holds unchecked, which the debate charter says is the worse failure class.

Diagnostic quality — favors cutting: capture-related diagnostics (borrowed-value-escapes-closure, capture-mode inference errors) are notoriously entangled with the aliasing model, which per round1-aliasing-model has no carded story for callbacks at all; env-struct diagnostics are ordinary struct/ownership errors the planned core must emit anyway. (Relative AI consumability of the two diagnostic classes: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.)

Compile time in the AI iteration loop — likely a wash, direction unproven: the alternative emits more source to parse/typecheck, but deletes capture inference, closure conversion, and the escape analysis needed to recover environment allocations, and removes a verified-lowering obligation (the all-TBD capability-map row). No carded compile-time evidence exists on either side (caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify compile-time cost).

Generation-habit mismatch — the real risk to the alternative: AI writers are trained overwhelmingly on closure-rich languages and may emit lambda-shaped code or defunctionalize incorrectly under distribution shift. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, and symmetric: the same shifted writer producing closures in a capture-bearing core would produce subtly wrong captures that pass the compiler.
consistencyRisks: Near-duplicate environment structs: without closures, the writer will mint many small env structs that are structurally similar (e.g., {arr, len, scale} variants); AI drift across these near-duplicates could swap field orders or types between copies. Mitigation is mechanical — nominal struct typing turns every such drift into a type error at the construction site; nothing is silently coerced.
