Signature drift between a top-level function and its env struct when one is edited without the other (a field added to the struct but unused, or used but never initialized) — partially checked (missing field/arity is an error; unused/uninitialized-field lints must be core diagnostics, which the language should mandate as machine-readable checks rather than warnings).

Cross-module duplication: the same defunctionalized 'callback' pattern re-generated in multiple modules may diverge over time where a single shared closure would have been one definition. This is a real repetition-consistency cost of the alternative; it is also exactly where the writer can be required to reference one top-level symbol — a discipline the compiler can enforce (symbol exists, signature matches), unlike consistency between two independently-written lambdas.

Risk to the prosecution's own coherence: if the structured-arrays surface (arrays-loops leading candidate) ends up needing non-escaping body regions (Linalg-style blocks), the language must draw the line precisely — second-class, non-escaping, capture-free body regions are NOT the feature on trial, and conflating them with first-class capture-bearing closures in later rounds would smuggle the feature back in.

Coupling risk flagged by the judges: aliasing, concurrency, and violation semantics are entangled priors; if the aliasing model later acquires a carded callback story, the cost side of this prosecution weakens and the topic should be re-weighed — per the standing rule, this verdict is evidence-conditional, not permanent.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus has no AI-codegen evidence: drift rates across env-struct/function/construction triples vs drift rates in implicit captures; whether AI writers defunctionalize correctly under distribution shift from closure-rich training data; whether machine-readable struct diagnostics actually shorten AI repair loops vs capture diagnostics.

No quantitative performance delta exists anywhere in the corpus for closures vs defunctionalized code (runtime, allocation rate, i-cache, code size) — caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude; D009 is qualitative Julia guidance.
Compile-time comparison (capture inference + closure conversion + escape analysis vs larger defunctionalized source volume) is unmeasured in either direction.

The load-bearing D-cards (D002, D007, D009) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule; round1-dispatch-generics flags this) — the prosecution's cost claims inherit that caveat, though the defense has NO cards at all, verified or not.

Whether structured-array ops plus top-level-function references cover the full closure workload (async continuations, event callbacks, iterator adapters with state) without unacceptable env-struct proliferation is undesigned and untested — flagged by round1-dispatch-generics follow-up ('add evidence and a design position on dispatch through first-class functions and closures') and by the all-TBD closure-capture row in the llvm-mlir capability map.
The alternative's interaction with the concurrency leading candidate (Send/Sync-like sharing capabilities over env structs) is uncarded inference — C-series cards do not address captures.

result.stance: CUT. Macros, comptime, and all in-language metaprogramming are excluded from the core language. The AI writer is the macro processor: every expansion happens before the source exists. The language has zero staging — the compiler consumes only first-order, fully-expanded, concrete code, and every source position is ground truth.
result.coreArgument: The null hypothesis stands unrebutted because the evidence set for this feature is literally empty. The corpus's standing rule (notes/caveats.md: "Any future design proposal must cite exact evidence cards") cannot be satisfied by the defense: not one card in F001-F009, N001-N006, A001-A010, D001-D010, C001-C006, or J001-J005 concerns macros, comptime, or staged metaprogramming, and the missing-research backlog does not even list it as a gap worth filling. Every optimizer enabler the corpus actually verifies is a SEMANTIC FACT CHANNEL — non-interference rules (F001), write-provenance proofs (F002), IR no-alias encodings (F003/F004), overflow/range contracts (N002/N004), shape/stride structure (A001-A004), static specialization (D001). Macros deliver none of these. By the time the optimizer runs, macro-expanded code is indistinguishable from directly written code; the feature's entire payoff is authoring convenience for a human who does not exist in this design. The alternative — the AI emits the expanded code directly — delivers byte-identical input to the optimizer, so the feature contributes exactly zero to the extreme-performance goal while charging real costs to the one thing this project says is non-negotiable: a simple, verifiable compiler. The costs are threefold. First, staging multiplies the trusted surface. The ir-strategy debate's leading candidate is a verifier-checked, semantics-preserving IR; the corpus's verification lesson (F006: SPARK gets facts true-by-construction only by RESTRICTING the language, down to side-effect-free expressions) points in exactly the opposite direction from embedding a Turing-complete evaluator in the compiler. A comptime interpreter must agree bit-for-bit with runtime lowering on the entire numeric contract the N-series shows is already treacherous (N001's UB/poison/undef ladder, N002's flag semantics) or it becomes a cross-stage miscompile generator; it adds CTFE nontermination, hygiene, cross-stage name resolution, and expansion-aware diagnostic mapping to a compiler whose charter is simplicity; and it makes compile time unbounded by construction inside the AI iteration loop, worsening a tension the corpus already flags as carded-but-unquantified (D001, D004 negative scopes). Second, verification gets strictly harder. Zero staging means the checker only ever discharges obligations against first-order concrete code — the simplest, most decidable form, per-site, with concrete types. Admitting a generator forces a dilemma: either check only after expansion (C++-template-style late, per-instantiation error discovery, with unexpanded branches never checked at all — intolerable when the writer is fallible at scale) or build a second, staged type system to verify generators for all inputs — a quantified problem and a massive core-complexity purchase with no supporting card. Worse, a fallible AI writing one wrong macro produces CORRELATED, silently replicated defects at every expansion site, and auditing the macro requires simulating expansion; a fallible AI writing N concrete copies produces decorrelated defects that the full verifier catches individually at exact source lines. Third, the one legitimate load macros could carry — compile-time specialization — is already assigned to a better, carded mechanism: the dispatch-generics leading candidate (design_candidate_later) puts guaranteed static specialization in the TYPE SYSTEM (D001/D002/D003), typed and checked before instantiation. Where typed generics guarantee specialization, comptime is redundant; everywhere else it is an unchecked code generator. Even the corpus's staging-adjacent evidence cuts against the defense: A006 (Halide) shows optimization choices surfaced as checkable program STRUCTURE in a constrained DSL — structure the AI can emit directly — and its negative scope explicitly denies transfer to a general language; F009 says the performance-critical semantics belong in the IR below the source, not in a text-production layer above it; J001/J002 show that when facts aren't static you pay for guard-and-invalidation machinery — and the AI-as-expander produces static facts without any in-language stage. The admissible machine-writer costs of the alternative (tokens, duplicate-copy drift, update amplification) are real, but they are tooling problems and empirically untested ones — nothing prevents the AI from running deterministic external generators whose OUTPUT is the checked, zero-stage source of record — whereas the feature's costs land permanently inside the trusted compiler. A feature with zero corpus evidence, zero unique performance contribution, a redundant load-bearing story, and a direct attack on the verifier's simplicity has no claim on the core. Cut it.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Confirmed (3-0): the carded optimizer enablers are language-level non-interference SEMANTICS (Rust &mut exclusivity, Fortran dummy args, SPARK anti-aliasing, restrict) — semantic fact channels, not code-production mechanisms. Macros add no such facts; its negative scope (contracts depend on writer truthfulness) also warns against enlarging unchecked generative surface.

Confirmed: optimization value comes from proving non-interference around writes. Nothing in the mechanism depends on how the source text was produced, so AI-expanded code and macro-expanded code are optimizer-identical — the feature has zero unique performance contribution.

Confirmed: IR no-alias encodings are UB-bearing promises sound only if frontend obligations are true. A comptime/macro stage inserts an unverified code-fabrication layer upstream of the obligation checker, expanding exactly the surface the ir-strategy verdict says must be verifier-checked.

Confirmed: SPARK's verification-oriented route works by RESTRICTING language semantics (ownership, anti-aliasing, side-effect-free expressions) so facts are true by construction. Embedding a Turing-complete compile-time evaluator moves the opposite direction — it enlarges what the verifier must model.

Confirmed: performance-critical semantics should be preserved and visible in an IR below the source (Swift SIL lesson). The performance leverage lives below source level; a text-expansion layer above source is invisible to every carded optimization pathway.

Provisional (unverified per backlog): monomorphization already carries the compile-time-specialization load as a typed, checked mechanism — the dispatch-generics leading candidate. Its negative scope flags compile time/code size as unquantified even for typed specialization; an unbounded comptime stage worsens both with no measurement.

Provisional: the dyn-compatibility composition hole (no sanctioned path for heterogeneous generic APIs) is the pressure point where macro-like escape hatches will be demanded; the debate record shows this must be answered inside the dispatch/generics design, not by admitting an unchecked code generator.

Provisional: compile-time vs optimization is already a carded tension (codegen units, LTO). A Turing-complete compile stage makes compile time unbounded by construction — a direct, admissible cost in the AI iteration loop.

Provisional: Halide is the corpus's closest staging-flavored evidence, and it shows optimization choices surfaced as pure, checkable program STRUCTURE in a constrained DSL — structure an AI can emit directly; its negative scope explicitly denies automatic transfer to a general-purpose language, so it cannot carry an in-language metaprogramming core feature.

Provisional: dynamic runtimes must recover statically-missing facts via profiling+guards — the information demand is constant, only the channel differs. The AI-as-expander supplies static facts through the cheapest channel (concrete source) with no in-language stage and no guard machinery.

Provisional: speculation requires invalidation machinery — carded proof that deferred/staged decision-making buys complexity. Zero staging keeps all expansion decisions before compile, avoiding any analogous cross-stage consistency machinery in the compiler.

Confirmed: LLVM's UB/poison/undef/freeze ladder shows how subtle the runtime semantic contract already is. A comptime interpreter must replicate this contract exactly at compile time or produce cross-stage semantic divergence — a new miscompile class the zero-staging alternative cannot have.

Confirmed: default arithmetic vs nsw/nuw promise separation is precise and easy to get wrong; compile-time evaluation must mirror these mode distinctions (wrapping/checked/unsafe per the numeric-semantics debate), doubling the surface where the truthful-lowering requirement must be audited.

machineWriterCosts: Token/generation cost multiplication: emitting N expanded specializations or repeated idioms costs roughly N times the tokens of one macro definition plus N invocations, on both generation and every subsequent re-read of the file into context. Admissible and conceded; magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Consistency drift across near-duplicates: when the AI later edits one of several intended-identical expansions, copies can diverge (hallucinated micro-variations). This is the defense's strongest card. Prosecution answer: the drift that matters is drift the verifier cannot see, and the core's carded fact channels (types, contracts, ownership) force each copy to independently re-prove its obligations at exact source lines — while the 'these copies were meant to be identical' meta-invariant can be enforced by external deterministic generators and diff tooling OUTSIDE the trusted compiler. Residual risk conceded; rate EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Update amplification: changing one abstraction means regenerating every expansion site — one conceptual edit becomes N coordinated edits in the AI loop, trading compiler CTFE time for AI regeneration latency. Mitigable by deterministic out-of-language generation whose output is the source of record; net latency EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Loss of a language-checked single point of truth: an invariant that a macro states once must be restated per copy (as contracts) or held as unchecked convention across copies. Partially answered by the dispatch-generics candidate (typed generics remain the sanctioned once-stated parametric mechanism, D001), but non-parametric repetition genuinely loses the single-definition property.

Diagnostic volume: N concrete copies produce N separate diagnostics for a shared defect, consuming context window in the repair loop — though each diagnostic points at literal source text the AI wrote, which is strictly simpler to consume than expansion-backtrace diagnostics into code that exists in no file. Net effect on repair-loop success EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
consistencyRisks: Generics boundary must be drawn crisply or the cut is incoherent: the dispatch-generics leading candidate keeps guaranteed static specialization (monomorphization) in the core, and the defense will argue that is staging. The cut must be stated as: parametric, type-checked-before-instantiation generics IN; user-visible compile-time execution and arbitrary code-producing stages OUT. D003's composition hole (heterogeneous generic APIs have no sanctioned path) is where pressure for macro-like escape hatches will concentrate.

Constant-expression boundary: every optimizing compiler folds constants, and types may require compile-time-known values (array sizes, layout constants, A001-style shape facts). 'Zero staging' must be defined as no user-visible compile-time EXECUTION semantics, not no constant folding, or the arrays/layout and numeric-mode designs (N-series truthful lowering) become unstateable.

Arrays-loops direction: if an A006/Halide-style algorithm/schedule separation is adopted, zero staging forbids in-language schedule generation and autotuning; schedule search must live in the AI/tooling loop and land as concrete schedule structure. This constrains the arrays-loops prototype (round1 outcome: prototype_needed) and should be stated there explicitly.

Violation-semantics/contract-proliferation coupling: per-copy restatement of contracts multiplies proof obligations under the graduated-obligations candidate; prover throughput and per-fact audit cost become the scaling bottleneck where a macro would have stated the obligation once. Interacts with the unresolved violation-semantics outcome (research_needed).

Derive-shaped functionality (serializers, layout descriptions, FFI shims per the runtime-abi dossier targets): with no derive mechanism the AI writes all of it; any future 'just one small derive/annotation processor' proposal reopens this feature through the back door and must be adjudicated as external tooling, not core.

Static-vs-profile candidate (firewalled offline profile data for cost decisions): if profile feedback must ever change code STRUCTURE rather than compiler cost tables, zero staging pushes that regeneration into the AI loop; the firewall design should confirm cost-only consumption so no in-language regeneration mechanism is needed.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence cards of any kind (and no metaprogramming cards at all).
Consistency-drift rate: how often an AI editing one of N near-duplicate expanded code copies introduces divergence, and what fraction of that divergence escapes type/contract checking.
Token/context economics: actual generation + re-read cost of fully expanded code vs macro'd code at repository scale, and whether longer contexts measurably increase AI defect rates in this regime.

Repair-loop asymmetry: whether diagnostics pointing at literal expanded source yield faster/more reliable AI fixes than expansion-backtrace diagnostics into generated code (the prosecution asserts yes; untested).
Determinism of AI-side expansion: whether an AI plus external deterministic generators can reproduce expansions stably across edit cycles without a language-level guarantee.

Compile-time magnitudes: the corpus quantifies no compile-time costs anywhere (caveats.md; D001/D004 negative scopes) — neither CTFE/macro-expansion cost in the compiler nor the compile-time saved by receiving pre-expanded source is measured, including net latency of the AI iteration loop under each regime.

Correlated-vs-decorrelated defect claim: that one buggy macro replicated everywhere is worse for a fallible-at-scale writer than N independently checked copies is argued from verification structure, not measured.

Whether checking N concrete instantiations is in practice cheaper for the compiler than checking one generator (the decidability argument is structural; wall-clock and code-size effects unmeasured, cf. the unquantified monomorphization trade in the dispatch-generics verdict).

result.stance: KEEP IN RESTRICTED FORM (partial keep, honestly argued): statically-typed closures where each closure is a unique anonymous type consumed by specializing generics, with MANDATORY explicit capture lists (compiler checks exactness in both directions: no unlisted free variable, no unused capture), explicit per-capture mode (move / shared-borrow / exclusive-borrow), and non-escaping by default. Escaping or heterogeneous function values are NOT defended as implicit closures; they route through the already-leading explicit restricted dynamic-dispatch construct (D003-style) or through library-level tagged defunctionalization. Full implicit-capture, escaping-by-default, allocation-hiding closures (JS/Swift-style) are conceded to the null hypothesis.
result.coreArgument: The defense rests on three admissible pillars. (1) OPTIMIZER NECESSITY — the guarantee-vs-conditional-recovery asymmetry. The alternative ("top-level functions + explicit env structs, no capture semantics") has exactly two realizable encodings wherever a function must be passed as a value: (a) function pointer + env pointer — an indirect call, which the corpus documents as adding lookup cost and blocking inlining (D002), recoverable only via conditional, speculative, revertible devirtualization (D007 negative scope) and blocked outright across interposition boundaries (D008); or (b) Reynolds-style tag-enum + central apply() switch — direct in a trivial sense, but per-call-site specialization (switch-out-of-loop, cloning) is again an optional optimizer recovery, not a semantic guarantee. By contrast, closures-as-unique-static-types consumed by the core's already-leading guaranteed-static-specialization path (D001; round1-dispatch-generics leading candidate) make every parameterized-algorithm callback a GUARANTEED direct call with exact callee identity and environment layout visible — the same source-guarantee-vs-conditional-recovery asymmetry the dispatch-generics judge ranked as the corpus's best-attested qualitative pattern (D007/D008/F007/J004, misleading-comparisons note 3). D009 independently documents that Function-typed fields hiding call targets defeat the optimizer even inside fully closed programs; the alternative generalizes precisely that shape to every function value in the program, and round1-dispatch-generics explicitly flags closure/first-class-function dispatch as the hole no position covered. Second optimizer fact lost: a compiler-derived capture list is a provably MINIMAL, provably SOUND footprint for the function value — the compiler knows the callback touches exactly the captured locations with the declared borrow kinds, which feeds the corpus's flagship non-interference machinery (F001/F002) at call sites (caller keeps unrelated values in registers across the callback) and lowers to F003 noalias/alias.scope facts that are sound by construction rather than writer-asserted (misleading-comparisons note 5: IR metadata is only as sound as the frontend proof behind it; F003 makes violations UB, so unsound writer-asserted footprints are miscompiles, not slowdowns). Hand-built env structs make the footprint a convention: over-capture and routing through wider shared structs silently widen the assumed footprint with no diagnostic. Third: a language-level non-escaping closure kind makes environment stack placement a CHECKED property, versus the F007/J004-documented reality that escape-analysis-based recovery is conditional and explicitly not a stack-allocation guarantee. (2) VERIFICATION NECESSITY. A closure IS defunctionalization — performed by the compiler, deterministically, against a checked specification: environment contents = exactly the declared captures = exactly the body's free variables; every field populated; capture mode checked against use; environment lifetime tied to captured borrows. The alternative mandates the same transform be performed by a fallible stochastic writer across three separately-editable artifacts (struct definition, population site, top-level body) and DELETES the correspondence check: nothing in the language verifies the struct matches the body's needs or that the intended sharing semantics were implemented. The killer instance is snapshot-vs-live: capture-by-value and capture-by-pointer both type-check in many contexts yet differ semantically; under the charter the writer is fallible at scale and cannot be trusted with unchecked invariants, and this invariant is exactly the kind that becomes uncheckable when the feature is cut — the compiler cannot check an intent that is no longer declared anywhere. With mandatory explicit capture lists the intent is declared once and machine-checked in both directions, which is strictly MORE verification than either Rust-style inference or the manual struct. C004-style Send/Sync race-freedom checks on spawned tasks also become a mechanical check over the derived environment (parity with structs is conceded, but exactness of the derived environment is not available to hand-rolled structs). (3) MACHINE-WRITER FAILURE MODES OF THE ALTERNATIVE: three-artifact consistency drift, central-enum edit amplification (every new behavior touches enum + switch + construction site — a canonical near-duplicate hallucination channel and an incremental-compilation choke point per D004-class boundary effects), token blowup from thousands of globally-unique name pairs plus wiring, and nonlocal context required to re-read any call site. All AI-behavior claims are flagged EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED below. HONEST CONCESSIONS: for heterogeneous STORED callback sets (event tables, schedulers, async continuation queues), closures buy nothing — dyn function values are indirect calls too (D002), and a closed tag-enum with exhaustive matching is arguably better for both optimizer and verifier; that is why the defended form routes escaping/heterogeneous cases to the explicit restricted dynamic construct or to library-level defunctionalization rather than defending implicit escaping closures. Monomorphized closures inherit D001's unquantified code-size/compile-time negative scope. The corpus has no closures-specific verified card (feature-evidence-coverage.csv rates closures/captures low_to_medium on D009 alone), so this defense is structural extrapolation from adjacent carded facts, stated per the standing rule with its unproven remainder listed below. Architecturally, the restricted form adds no new dispatch channel: it is the dispatch-generics leading candidate (implicit path = guaranteed static specialization; dynamic path = explicit and restricted) applied to function values, closing the gap that debate's own follow-up list ordered filled.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Monomorphization yields concrete-code runtime behavior as a source-level guarantee; closures-as-unique-static-types extend exactly this guarantee to function values, making callback calls direct by construction. Its negative scope (unquantified code bloat/compile time) is carried as an honest cost of the kept feature.

Documents that indirect/dyn dispatch adds lookup cost and prevents inlining, blocking downstream optimization — this is the cost class the alternative's function-pointer+env encoding imposes on every passed function value; its positive scope (dynamic dispatch should be explicit when used) supports confining escaping closures to an explicit construct.
Precedent that a language can restrict dynamic-dispatch interfaces to keep runtime representation coherent — the host for the conceded escaping/heterogeneous residue in the partial-keep design.

Compilation-unit/LTO boundaries condition optimization and compile time; central-enum defunctionalization concentrates every callback into one apply() module, stressing exactly these boundaries and incremental recompilation in the AI iteration loop.

Devirtualization/indirect-call recovery is conditional, speculative, revertible, and option-dependent — the alternative's only path back to direct calls is not a guarantee, which is the core asymmetry of the defense.
Interposition/dynamic-linking semantics can block interprocedural analysis entirely — across module boundaries the alternative's function-pointer encoding may lose even conditional recovery.

Julia evidence that Function-typed fields hide representation/call targets and cause dynamic dispatch — a documented optimizer defeat inside closed programs; the alternative generalizes this shape to all function values, while unique closure types keep the common cases out of it.

Language-level non-interference rules are the corpus's central optimizer enabler; compiler-derived capture lists with declared borrow kinds are precisely such a rule for function values, giving callers sound non-interference across callback invocations.

Alias information's value is proving non-interference around writes (register caching, read/write elimination, reordering) — the caller-side optimizations a checked, minimal callback footprint enables and an over-wide hand-built env struct silently forfeits.

LLVM noalias/alias.scope facts carry UB on violation, so footprint facts must be sound, not asserted; compiler-derived environments are sound by construction, writer-populated structs are trust-based (reinforced by misleading-comparisons note 5).

HotSpot escape analysis supports conditional elimination, not guaranteed stack allocation — the documented weakness of 'recover it in the optimizer' that a checked non-escaping closure kind avoids by making environment stack placement a language guarantee.

Primary-vendor sharpening of F007: runtime analysis yields conditional, not guaranteed, allocation optimization — same asymmetry, used to justify a declared non-escaping closure kind over post-hoc analysis.

Swift SIL shows optimization-first design benefits from preserving high-level semantics (ownership, ARC) into IR for passes LLVM cannot see; capture lists, capture modes, and escape kinds are exactly such semantics, and cutting the feature deletes them from every IR level.

Type-enforced data-race freedom (Send/Sync + exclusive borrowing) is the concurrency leading candidate; checked capture semantics make 'what does this spawned task capture, and is it Send' a mechanical check over a compiler-derived environment.

Negative-scope support for the concession structure: shared/erased representations preserve metadata but constrain closed-world assumptions — informing why the defended core keeps closures specialized and pushes erased function values into an explicit restricted construct.

machineWriterCosts: Three-artifact drift under the alternative: env struct definition, population site, and top-level function body are separately editable with no language-level correspondence check; the compiler-checked closure collapses these into one declaration site. (Cost of alternative; drift-rate magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.)

Edit amplification in the tag-enum encoding: adding one behavior requires coordinated edits at three or more distant sites (enum variant, apply() switch arm, construction site); central apply() becomes a hallucination and merge hotspot and a whole-module recompilation trigger in the AI iteration loop (D004-class boundary cost).

Token/context blowup under the alternative: every small function value costs a globally-unique function name + struct name + field wiring, and safely re-reading or editing any call site requires pulling the nonlocal struct and function definitions into context; closures keep creation, body, and captures syntactically local. (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED magnitude.)

Over-capture economy of a machine writer: reusing one wide env struct across near-duplicate callbacks is locally cheaper to generate and type-checks, but silently widens the callback's assumed footprint and forfeits caller-side F002-class optimizations with no diagnostic; exact capture lists make minimal footprints the checked default.

Naming-collision / wrong-referent wiring: thousands of near-identical generated names (do_map_env_17, do_map_fn_17) invite cross-wiring env struct A with function B; anonymous compiler-derived closure types have no writer-invented names to confuse.

Honest cost of the KEPT feature: monomorphized closures inherit D001's negative scope — unquantified code-size and compile-time growth per instantiation — which directly taxes the AI iteration loop's compile step; zero quantitative cards exist (caveats.md), so this ledger entry is real and unpriced.

Honest cost of the KEPT feature: mandatory explicit capture lists add tokens per closure relative to inference; this is accepted deliberately because the exactness check (both directions) is the verification payload, and verbosity is admissible under the charter.
consistencyRisks: Snapshot-vs-live divergence: under the alternative, capture-by-value vs capture-by-pointer both type-check in many contexts; near-duplicate population sites can silently disagree, producing behavioral drift no compiler flags. This is the single strongest drift channel the closure feature eliminates by making capture mode a declared, checked property.

Stale environment after body edits: when the AI edits a top-level function body to read a new variable (or stop reading one), nothing forces the env struct and all population sites to follow; with checked capture lists, a free variable not in the list or an unused listed capture is a compile error.

Central-enum bidirectional drift: exhaustive matching catches a missing switch arm but not a dead variant, a mis-ordered tag mapping, or a construction site building the wrong variant with a plausible name.

Cross-wiring risk: passing env struct A to top-level function B when names are similar — type-distinct env structs mitigate this only if the writer consistently mints one struct per function, which is itself an unchecked convention.

Risk on the KEPT side, and why the restricted form exists: implicit capture inference (Rust/Swift-style) would let an AI writer silently capture unintended variables or the wrong borrow kind with no declaration to check against; the defended form therefore mandates explicit capture lists rather than inference, converting this risk into a checked contract.

Risk on the KEPT side: if escaping closures were implicit, allocation and lifetime extension would be hidden control of performance-critical behavior; the defended form makes escape a distinct, explicit construct so that the cost model stays truthful (aligned with D002 positive scope and the dispatch-generics candidate's explicit-dyn principle).
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI-writer error rates — drift across the three defunctionalization artifacts, snapshot-vs-live mistakes, naming cross-wiring, over-capture frequency — are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence of any kind.

The load-bearing verification claim that compiler-checked exact capture lists measurably reduce machine-writer defect rates versus disciplined manual env-struct conventions is untested; it is an inference from the charter's fallible-writer premise, not from data.
Token/context-cost deltas (generation plus re-reading) between closure-bearing and defunctionalized code for machine writers are unmeasured.

The performance magnitude of indirect-call versus guaranteed-direct-call for callback-heavy code, and real-world devirtualization recovery rates, are unquantified in the corpus (D002/D007 are qualitative; caveats.md notes documentation evidence never quantifies magnitude).

Whether a tag-enum apply() loop or a shared/erased function body can soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and at what precision loss, is the named-but-unexecuted follow-up from round1-dispatch-generics — it would arbitrate the footprint-precision half of this defense.
Monomorphized-closure code-size and compile-time growth (D001 negative scope) has zero quantitative cards; the defended form could lose in size-bound or compile-time-bound regimes.
Central-enum incremental-recompilation explosion under high callback-addition churn is plausible from D004-class boundary evidence but unmeasured.

Every load-bearing D-card (D001, D002, D003, D007, D009) is a provisional direct extraction awaiting adversarial verification per the caveats.md standing rule; the closures/captures row of feature-evidence-coverage.csv is rated low_to_medium confidence on D009 alone, and round1-dispatch-generics records first-class-function dispatch as an evidence gap no debate position covered.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP (split verdict). DEFEND: local/bidirectional type inference for derived facts inside mandatorily-annotated boundaries (every function signature, module interface, and every intent-bearing choice — numeric mode, ownership/aliasing mode, effect row, public layout — stays mandatory and explicit). CONCEDE: whole-program Hindley-Milner-style inference across unannotated boundaries, and essentially all implicit VALUE-CHANGING conversions (numeric promotion, narrowing, int<->float, user-defined conversion operators) — cut them; the only conversion sliver worth keeping is compiler-PROVEN identity/subsumption coercion (zero runtime effect, unique target), and even that should emit a machine-readable elaboration record rather than being silent. The strongest honest position is therefore not 'keep the feature as commonly bundled' but 'infer derived facts, mandate intent facts' — which is precisely the distinction the proposed alternative erases.
result.coreArgument: The alternative ('annotate every type, effect, layout, and mode at every binding and call') impales itself on a dilemma, and both horns are bad under this charter. Horn (a): if interior annotations are BINDING facts the optimizer assumes, then a fallible at-scale writer is emitting millions of restrict-grade promises, exactly the class F001's negative scope warns 'depend on programmer truthfulness or unsafe assumptions' and F003 marks as UB-on-violation; the corpus's standing verification rule (contracts assumable only when proven) then forces the compiler to re-verify every one anyway. Horn (b): if annotations are all CHECKED against what the checker derives, they add zero information the compiler does not already compute — their entire value is redundancy, and redundancy is only worth its cost in proportion to the independence of the two statements. Interior annotations written in the same generation pass as the code they annotate have near-zero independence (the same model conditions one on the other), while their costs are concrete and fourfold: (1) token mass — the type of stage k of a generic pipeline syntactically contains stages 1..k-1 (D001-style instantiated types), so annotating every intermediate is O(k^2) tokens by structural arithmetic, multiplying both generation cost and every future re-read of the file into context; (2) consistency drift — 100+-token instantiated types restated at many bindings, where every mismatch is a compile-error -> regenerate iteration (compile time inside the AI loop), and some drifts are SILENT: wherever the annotation itself drives elaboration (literal typing, defaulting, overload selection), a wrong annotation like `let x: u8 = 255` where u16 was intended typechecks and changes runtime semantics with no diagnostic — mandatory annotation maximizes the count of such writer-owned silent decision sites, whereas boundary-only annotation forces interior types by unification, so they cannot drift because they do not exist as text; (3) edit cascades — one boundary change forces lockstep re-annotation of all transitive interior bindings and calls, making regeneration cost proportional to program size rather than change size; (4) unnameable types — closures and per-instantiation generic types either require new global naming machinery (more drift surface) or ban the abstractions outright. Separately, mandatory LAYOUT/MODE at every binding is anti-optimizer on card-backed grounds, not just costly: F007/J004 show allocation elimination and scalar replacement are analysis-decided and conditional, so many bindings have no stable writer-decidable layout — a per-binding layout annotation either pins representation and forbids scalar replacement/register promotion (the exact payoffs F002 documents), or it is a lie the verifier must specially ignore; F009, A002, and A006 show the well-attested architecture keeps high-level semantics abstract so the compiler chooses representation and schedule late — total per-binding layout annotation fuses schedule into algorithm at every intermediate step, foreclosing exactly the tiling/fusion/promotion/vectorization decisions the A-cards say the structure exists to enable. Finally, the corpus shows the TRUSTED channel for whole families of optimizer facts is compiler derivation, not writer assertion: C004's Send/Sync auto traits are structurally inferred facts the optimizer consumes ('treat non-atomic memory as unshared unless types say otherwise'), and C004's negative scope — incorrect MANUAL impls are UB — is precisely the writer-assertion failure mode the alternative scales to every binding. Under a charter where the writer is the least trusted component and the checker the most trusted, moving derived-fact production from writer text into the checker is a verification improvement, not a convenience. Implicit conversions, by contrast, I largely concede: a silent conversion is a silent semantic mode choice (N002 shows wrap vs nuw/nsw are distinct optimizer promises; the numeric-semantics round demands named, truthfully-lowered modes), so value-changing conversions must be explicit, mode-carrying operations the checker can verify — the only defensible remnant is checker-proven no-op subsumption, where forcing an explicit upcast at every site adds a writer-owned wrong-target opportunity while adding no information.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Negative scope: 'restrict-like contracts depend on programmer truthfulness or unsafe assumptions' — grounds horn (a) of the dilemma: mandatory writer-asserted binding annotations at every site are restrict-grade promises scaled to millions, unsound for a fallible writer unless each is checked, at which point they are redundant.

LLVM no-alias promises are UB on violation and 'only sound if frontend obligations are true' — establishes that assumed-but-unchecked annotations are a miscompilation channel, so the alternative cannot claim its annotations are free verification; binding annotations must be proven, i.e., derived by the checker anyway.

Names the concrete payoffs (register promotion, load/store elimination, reordering) that per-binding layout pinning would forfeit: a binding annotated with a memory layout cannot be freely promoted to a register unless the annotation is non-binding.

Escape analysis 'conditional elimination and scalar replacement, not guaranteed stack allocation' — allocation/layout decisions are analysis-decided facts about a binding, demonstrating that many bindings have no writer-decidable layout to annotate truthfully.

Primary-vendor sharpening of F007: allocation optimization outcomes are conditional compiler decisions; a mandatory per-binding layout annotation either forbids these decisions or is unverifiable fiction the toolchain must ignore.

Swift SIL preserves high-level semantic information so optimization is decided in the compiler below the source — direct evidence for keeping representation/lowering commitments OUT of mandatory per-binding source text and letting the optimizer choose.

Linalg keeps iteration structure abstract precisely so tiling/fusion/promotion/vectorization can be chosen later; annotating layout and mode at every source binding is the opposite move — premature, text-level lowering.

Halide's algorithm/schedule separation shows optimization-relevant layout/loop choices belong in a separable layer; mandatory per-binding layout/mode annotation fuses schedule into algorithm at every intermediate, destroying the separation this card evidences as valuable.

Send/Sync auto traits are compiler-DERIVED type facts the optimizer consumes for non-interference; the negative scope (incorrect manual impls are UB) is exactly the manual-assertion hazard the alternative generalizes — evidence that derivation, not writer assertion, is the sound channel for derived facts.

Monomorphization yields per-instantiation concrete types — the enormous, often unnameable types the alternative would force the writer to restate at every binding and call; also flags the compile-time/code-size sensitivity that annotation-driven text mass aggravates.

Julia recovers performance 'through type inference and specialization when types are stable and concrete' — used narrowly: inference is an established performance-fact channel, and with mandatory boundary annotations all interior types are stable, so local inference is exact rather than heuristic (its negative scope, heuristic failure under abstract types, is disarmed by mandatory boundaries).

Wrapping vs nuw/nsw are distinct optimizer promises — supports my CONCESSION: a conversion is a semantic mode choice, so implicit value-changing conversions silently choose optimizer contracts and must be cut in favor of explicit mode-carrying operations.

Range metadata is a value-fact channel finer than declared types — compiler-inferred facts can exceed the precision of anything a writer would annotate, so mandatory annotation is not the ceiling of optimizer knowledge and cannot substitute for inference inside the compiler.

machineWriterCosts: Token blowup of the alternative, structural not behavioral: the instantiated type of stage k in a generic pipeline syntactically contains stages 1..k-1 (D001-style monomorphized types), so annotating every intermediate binding costs O(k^2) tokens in pipeline depth; this multiplies generation cost AND every subsequent re-read of the file into a finite context window.

Compile-loop inflation: every drifted restatement of a long type is a compile error whose only fix is another generate->compile round trip; mandatory interior annotation maximizes the number of restatement sites and therefore the expected iterations per change (compile time inside the AI loop is charter-admissible).

Silent semantic drift sites: wherever the annotation drives elaboration (literal typing, defaulting, overload selection), a wrong-but-well-typed annotation changes runtime semantics with no diagnostic — e.g. `let x: u8 = 255` where u16 was intended. Full mandatory annotation makes EVERY binding such a writer-owned decision site; boundary-only annotation + unification makes interior types non-textual and hence non-driftable.

Edit cascades: one boundary type/effect change forces lockstep re-annotation of all transitive interior bindings and call-site annotations, so regeneration cost scales with program size rather than change size; under inference the checker propagates the fact and only true contracts need edits.

Unnameable-type distortion: closure types, per-instantiation generic types, and existential returns are unnameable in extant designs; mandatory annotation everywhere requires either new global naming machinery (a fresh consistency-drift surface) or banning those abstractions.

Layout-annotation dilemma at bindings: per-binding layout/mode text either pins representation — forbidding scalar replacement, register promotion, and late layout choice (F002/F007/J004 payoffs) — or is declared non-binding, in which case it is unverified noise the verifier and every future re-reader must carry and special-case.

Degraded blame assignment (flagged untested): the classic claim that annotations localize type errors assumes annotations are more reliable than code; when the same model writes both in the same pass, every mismatch has two equally-suspect sides, so the diagnostic 'the annotation is ground truth' heuristic that machine-readable error tooling would rely on is unsound.

Honest residual cost of MY position: un-annotated interior code forces a re-reading model to re-derive types, a hallucination source; mitigation is compiler-emitted elaborated views (inferred types on demand), which are trusted-compiler output rather than untrusted writer text — but this is a hard tooling requirement, not a free lunch.
consistencyRisks: Redundancy-independence assumption: my dismissal of horn (b) rests on same-pass annotations being error-correlated with the code they annotate; if AI-written interior annotations empirically DO catch generated-code errors at high rates (double-entry effect), the checked-redundant horn regains value and interior annotation density should be re-tuned upward, weakening this defense.

Scope confinement: I defend only local/bidirectional inference inside mandatorily-annotated signatures; whole-program inference makes error blame non-local and worst-case check cost superlinear. If the language later slides toward cross-boundary inference, this defense does not transfer and the diagnostics argument inverts against me.

Ownership/effect inferability: the aliasing-model leading candidate (checked ownership in the type system) and any effect system may not be locally inferable; if effect/ownership propagation demands annotations well inside function bodies, 'boundary-only' converges toward 'everywhere' for effect-heavy code and my token/drift savings shrink.

Identity-coercion creep: the kept sliver (implicit subsumption) is safe only while coercions are unique-target and zero-runtime-effect; if later design adds representation-changing coercions (boxing, width change) the implicit channel must not silently widen, or it reintroduces exactly the silent-mode-choice hazard I conceded on.

Elaborated-view dependency: my mitigation for the re-reading cost assumes toolchain-emitted inferred-type views are always available and consumed; in any workflow where the model reads raw source without the elaborator, unannotated interiors are strictly harder to re-read correctly than annotated ones.

Corpus mismatch risk: no card directly studies source-level annotation regimes; all my card usage is transfer from IR/compiler-internal evidence (F/A/C/D/J series), the exact inference-direction move caveats.md's scaffolding rule polices — this whole topic sits in a corpus gap and the round1-aliasing gap note (no cards on annotation error rates or inference feasibility) applies to both sides.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: AI writers drift when restating long types/annotations across near-duplicate sites (the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence; drift frequency and severity are assumed, not measured).

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: same-pass generated annotations are error-correlated with generated code (the independence argument against redundant interior annotations is mechanistically motivated by autoregressive conditioning but unmeasured).
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: compiler-emitted elaborated views reduce AI re-reading errors relative to inline writer annotations.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: frequency of deep generic pipelines in AI-written code (the O(k^2) annotation-token growth is structural arithmetic, but its practical weight depends on how often such code shapes occur).

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: annotation error rates for restrict-like contracts — the corpus itself flags this gap (round1-aliasing-model.md, missing-cards note), and it governs how dangerous horn (a) writer-asserted annotations actually are.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: how much optimizer-fact benefit compiler inference recovers without frontend annotations — the numeric-semantics round explicitly marks compiler-inferred nsw/range facts as an 'uncarded fourth channel' in both directions.
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: compile-time comparison of local-inference-plus-boundary-annotations vs full mandatory annotation at scale (no cards; D004 covers codegen-unit/LTO tradeoffs only).
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: magnitude of edit-cascade regeneration cost under mandatory annotation vs inference (mechanically argued, never measured).

result.stance: CUT. Interfaces/traits as an abstraction mechanism (reuse + dispatch) do not belong in the core language. The core keeps only algebraic/effect law annotations (associative, commutative, pure, non-interfering) attached to concrete functions; any trait-like grouping is at most a later, non-core frontend/tooling layer that elaborates to concrete functions plus law annotations.
result.coreArgument: The trait abstraction earns its keep in human languages by letting one hand-typed body serve many types. In an AI-only, optimizer-first language that justification is inadmissible by charter, and what remains on the ledger is nearly all cost. (1) The performance contribution is zero-or-negative. Every optimizer enabler the corpus actually attests is a fact about concrete code: non-interference facts (F001, F002, F003), reduction/parallelism/independence structure (A002, A005, A010), and purity/effect facts (F006). No card shows any pass consuming 'T implements interface I' as an optimization fact. On the static path, traits compile by monomorphization into exactly the concrete per-type functions an AI writer could emit directly (D001) — identical runtime artifact, so the trait layer is pure frontend elaboration. On the dynamic path, trait objects are a carded optimizer defeat: data pointer + vtable, runtime lookup cost, inlining of the method body prevented (D002); recovery by devirtualization is conditional, revertible, and option-dependent (D007 negative scope), further blocked by linking semantics (D008), and the round1-dispatch critic established that 'guaranteed devirtualization' has no card behind it anywhere in the corpus. (2) The feature drags a large rule-surface into a core whose charter (round1 ir-strategy) is a small verifier-checked, semantics-preserving pipeline. Dyn-compatibility alone is a dedicated restriction subsystem — excluding Self:Sized, associated constants, generic dispatchable methods, opaque returns, unsupported receivers (D003) — and round1-dispatch documents that even with it the feature is incomplete at its own job: dyn-compatible traits exclude generic dispatchable methods, leaving heterogeneous generic APIs with no sanctioned path (A1 weakness #4). Coherence, bound solving, and associated-type projection are all new verifier obligations that discharge no optimizer-consumable fact. In a language where the writer is fallible at scale, verification budget must go to facts passes consume (F003-class alias obligations, A005-class legality facts), not abstraction bookkeeping; a wrong trait bound is a hallucination surface the optimizer never repays. (3) The alternative demonstrably carries the load. What 'many types, one algorithm' must deliver to the optimizer is concrete types plus laws: Julia's carded evidence shows high-performance specialization keys on concrete argument-type tuples, not declared interfaces (D009, D010), and D009 specifically shows that abstraction-typed fields (Function-typed fields) HIDE call targets and defeat the optimizer even in closed programs — abstraction is the documented enemy of call-target visibility, concreteness the documented friend. Reduction, parallelism, and purity laws attach per concrete function (A002, A005, A010, F006) and are exactly what the alternative keeps. (4) Machine-writer economics do not rescue the feature. The source duplication traits avoid is regenerated at codegen by mandatory monomorphization anyway (D001 negative scope), the corpus contains zero quantitative evidence that frontend deduplication saves compile time or code size (round1-dispatch evidence gap; caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude), and trait resolution inserts an extra solving stage into every compile in the AI iteration loop while producing non-local diagnostics (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED for AI writers). (5) Cutting is reversible; keeping is not. All three round1 advocates converge on concrete specialized code as the compilation target, so a trait layer can be added later as sugar elaborating onto the concrete core — but baking a trait system into the core now commits to a subsystem whose decisive measurements (specialization vs code growth; whether shared bodies can discharge F003 obligations; the F009 Swift fourth model) do not exist, in a debate the judge explicitly left at design_candidate_later. The corpus's standing null hypothesis and caveats discipline both say: not in core.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Rust generics compile by monomorphization into concrete type-specific versions — the trait layer produces exactly the concrete functions an AI writer could emit directly, so static traits add no runtime artifact the alternative lacks; its negative scope also flags unquantified compile-time/code-size costs of that expansion.

Trait-object dynamic dispatch is a carded performance defeat: vtable lookup cost and prevented inlining block optimizations — the only runtime capability traits add beyond concrete functions is a documented hazard.

Dyn compatibility is a whole dedicated restriction subsystem (no Self:Sized, no associated constants, no generic dispatchable methods, no opaque returns) — carded proof of the rule-surface and verifier burden the feature drags into the core, and of its incompleteness (heterogeneous generic APIs excluded).

Devirtualization recovery is conditional, speculative, revertible, and option-dependent — dynamic trait dispatch cannot be rescued by a promised optimization; no card supports guaranteed devirtualization.
Interposition/dynamic-linking semantics can block interprocedural devirtualization even when code looks statically known — a second carded reason dyn trait costs are not reliably recoverable.

Julia performance guidance: abstraction-typed (Function-typed) fields hide representation and call targets and cause dynamic dispatch; performance comes from concrete types and specialization — direct evidence that concreteness, not interface abstraction, is the optimizer-relevant property.
Julia dispatch specializes on concrete argument-type tuples, not declared interfaces — a shipped high-performance dispatch model needing no interface abstraction as compiler input.

The corpus's best-attested optimizer enablers are language-level non-interference rules on concrete access paths (Rust &mut, Fortran dummy args, SPARK, restrict) — none route through an interface; the alternative keeps all of them.

The concrete optimizer-facing encodings (noalias, alias.scope) attach to functions, parameters, and accesses — the verifier's budget should discharge these obligations, which concrete functions with law annotations feed directly.

SPARK shows a verification-oriented route where side-effect-freedom and ownership are per-function semantic restrictions — carded precedent for effect/purity law annotations on concrete code carrying the load without an interface layer.

Linalg iterator kinds (parallel/reduction) are the carded consumer-facing form of algebraic facts: structure annotations on concrete operations enable tiling/fusion/vectorization — the exact fact class the alternative preserves.
LLVM vectorizers consume alias facts, reductions/inductions, FP flags, and loop shape — the complete list of what backends actually eat contains no interface-conformance fact.
Fortran DO CONCURRENT encodes independence intent as loop-level restriction — algebraic/independence law annotation on concrete code, precedented without any dispatch abstraction.

Conditional runtime recovery is not a source-level guarantee (HotSpot escape analysis analog) — supports the asymmetry that traits' dynamic path relies on conditional recovery while the alternative needs no recovery at all.

The one workflow-confirmed dispatch-adjacent card supports preserving optimizer-visible semantic facts in IR — facts like ownership and ARC ops, not interface conformance; it also names a fourth generics model the round1 debate left unexamined, underscoring that committing traits to core now is premature.

Even monomorphized code's optimization is codegen-unit/LTO-dependent — the trait feature does not even deliver predictable optimization on its favored static path without a further undesigned build/visibility model.

machineWriterCosts: COST OF KEEPING — compile time in the AI iteration loop: trait resolution/bound solving adds a frontend stage to every compile, and monomorphization's compile-time cost is flagged but unquantified (D001 negative scope, D004, caveats.md); with concrete functions only, incremental recompilation is per-function and the solving stage disappears entirely.

COST OF KEEPING — hallucination surface: writing a trait impl requires the AI to satisfy coherence, bound chains, and dyn-compatibility rules (D003) that discharge no optimizer fact; a subtly wrong bound or impl selects wrong behavior silently at dispatch resolution rather than failing at a concrete call site. (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED as an AI error mode.)

COST OF KEEPING — diagnostics: trait-solver errors (ambiguous impls, unsatisfied bound chains, coherence conflicts) are non-local relative to concrete-function type errors, degrading the machine-readable diagnostic loop. (Outside-corpus, flagged; no diagnostics evidence cards exist.)

COST OF CUTTING — token/generation cost: the AI must emit N concrete copies of each algorithm instead of one generic body. Real and admissible; mitigations are a deterministic expander tool outside the core and the fact that monomorphization regenerates the N copies at codegen anyway (D001), so total artifact size is not obviously worse — but source-token cost is strictly higher. (Magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.)

COST OF CUTTING — context/re-read cost: N near-duplicate copies inflate the context the AI must load to reason about a change, and retrieval may surface a stale or wrong-type copy. (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.)

COST OF CUTTING — change fan-out: a bug fix or law change to an algorithm must be regenerated at every concrete copy; without tooling, a missed site is a silent stale duplicate. This is the strongest defense card and must be answered by derived-from provenance metadata plus compiler-checked cross-copy obligations (a tooling/verification convention, not a core language feature).
consistencyRisks: Consistency drift across near-duplicates: with no single generic source of truth, AI-regenerated concrete copies of the same algorithm can silently diverge (the hallucination-across-copies failure the design context names). Primary mitigation is non-core: deterministic expansion tooling plus compiler-checked equivalence/provenance obligations — but that machinery is undesigned.

Law-annotation drift: algebraic/effect annotations (associative, pure, non-interfering) repeated on each concrete copy can contradict each other across copies; the core must at least check annotation consistency within a provenance group, or wrong laws become wrong optimizations (F003-class UB if alias facts are wrong — misleading-comparisons note 5: metadata is only sound if frontend obligations are true).

Loss of a compiler-checked contract group: without interfaces there is no language-level statement that N functions implement the same contract; conformance of the family becomes a tooling obligation, and in this charter unchecked invariants are explicitly untrustworthy.

Ad-hoc dispatch reinvention: workloads needing heterogeneity may drive generated code toward hand-rolled fn-pointer/tag erasure, which the optimizer handles worse than a designed construct (round1-dispatch A1 weakness #4; D009 documents function-valued indirection hiding call targets). The cut position must pair with an explicit closed-variant/switch construct or inherit this risk.

Piecemeal trait re-emergence: the round1 leading candidate retains an explicit restricted dyn construct; if that lands later, defining its typed function-table without any interface concept risks recreating trait machinery incrementally and incoherently.

Round1-dispatch reached design_candidate_later assuming a generics/trait-bounded surface exists; cutting interfaces entirely reopens parts of that verdict (e.g., what the explicit dyn construct ranges over) and must be recorded as a coupled amendment, not an independent decision.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence. Specifically untested: drift rates when regenerating N near-duplicate concrete functions vs error rates when writing trait impls/bounds; actual token/context cost deltas; whether an AI reliably propagates a change to all copies; whether trait-solver diagnostics are harder for an AI to consume than local type errors.

No quantitative comparison exists anywhere in the corpus of monomorphization vs shared-body vs dictionary generics vs directly-emitted concrete code on compile time, code size, i-cache, or runtime (round1-dispatch evidence gap; caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude) — the compile-time-in-the-loop claim on both sides is unmeasured.

Whether a shared/erased generic body can soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and at what precision loss, is the named open construction-level check; if sharing turns out cheap and sound, one prosecution asymmetry weakens (though the cut still stands on rule-surface grounds).
Whether explicit closed-enum/switch dispatch outperforms vtable dispatch for generated closed-world code is an inference from D002/D009, not a carded result; no card measures it.

All load-bearing D-series cards (D001, D002, D003, D007) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule; feature-evidence-coverage.csv rates dispatch evidence low_to_medium confidence).

No prototype-level validation exists that law annotations on concrete functions actually survive lowering and are consumed by passes end-to-end (round1 cross-topic gap) — the alternative's load-carrying capacity is itself asserted from A/F-card positive scopes, not demonstrated.

result.stance: CUT. Human-oriented syntax (infix operators, precedence tables, sugar, elision) must be excluded from the core language. The sole core syntax should be a single, fully regular canonical form — one spelling per semantic construct, one legal formatting, surface tree isomorphic to the checked core representation.
result.coreArgument: The feature fails on evidence, on verification cost, and on machine-writer cost; the canonical alternative carries the full load. (1) Zero carded benefit: every confirmed optimizer enabler in the corpus (F001-F009, N001-N006, A001-A010, D001-D010, C001-C006, J001-J005) is a semantic fact — aliasing, ownership, effects, numeric modes, dispatch, memory models. No card attributes any performance, verification, or pipeline benefit to infix, precedence, sugar, or elision. The corpus explicitly deferred syntax (source-to-ir-lowering-sketches.md: 'No syntax decisions yet'; caveats.md: scaffolding must 'not decide source syntax'). Under caveats.md's standing rule — cite exact cards or it does not ship — the defense holds an empty hand, and the null hypothesis (not in core) prevails by default. (2) Sugar is an unverified transformation layer in a pipeline whose sound spots are already scarce. F003's negative scope and misleading-comparisons.jsonl both warn that optimizer facts are sound only if frontend obligations are true and survive lowering; round1-index.md flags as a cross-topic gap that there is no prototype validation that emitted facts survive lowering at all. Every desugaring, precedence-resolution, and elision-inference rule is one more frontend transformation whose semantics-preservation must be proven, tested, and maintained — exactly the class of hazard open-questions.md lists as a historical failure ('unsound frontend metadata emission'). N006 shows the discipline mature compilers demand: even trivial InstCombine folds are expected to be Alive2-verified. A syntax with zero desugarings incurs zero such obligations; a sugared syntax buys nothing carded and pays in proof surface. (3) The corpus's own design lessons point away from sugar. F009's lesson is that performance-critical structure should be visible to the tools, not buried; sugar and elision bury structure at the surface and force reconstruction. F006 shows SPARK — the corpus's verification-first exemplar — deliberately restricting surface expressiveness (side-effect-free expressions) precisely to keep the source-to-specification mapping mathematical and simple. An all-AI, verification-heavy language should follow the SPARK direction, not the C direction. (4) Machine-writer economics favor canonical form on every admissible axis except raw token count (contested, untested): one spelling per meaning makes consistency drift between near-duplicate code copies byte-detectable and hash-consable; a fixed-form grammar yields deterministic O(n) parsing, node-addressed machine-readable diagnostics, and content-addressed caching of parse/typecheck/verification results in the AI iteration loop — where compile-time/code-size tradeoffs are already carded as real costs (D001, D004); and it eliminates the silent precedence-misbinding error class entirely, which matters because the writer is fallible at scale and the compiler is the only reviewer. Human-oriented syntax was engineering for a consumer this language does not have; its entire benefit column is inadmissible by the ground rules, and its cost column lands on the two things this project can least afford: verifier trust surface and pipeline determinism.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Negative scope states optimizer-facing facts are sound only if frontend obligations are true and metadata survives lowering. Every sugar/elision/precedence layer is additional frontend transformation surface where obligations can be mis-emitted before the verifier ever sees the program — the exact soundness chokepoint this card identifies.

SPARK, the corpus's verification-first precedent, deliberately restricts surface expressiveness (expressions and function calls must be side-effect free) to permit mathematical treatment of source in specifications. Direct precedent that a verification-heavy language constrains surface convenience to keep the source-to-logic mapping simple — the same move as mandating a canonical, core-isomorphic syntax.

Swift SIL's carded design lesson: performance-critical semantics should be visible to tools rather than buried in implicit forms. Sugar and elision are the surface-level inverse — they hide structure the checker and optimizer must then reconstruct through an elaborator; canonical syntax keeps the written artifact identical to the analyzed tree.

InstCombine canonicalization shows mature compilers treat every rewrite, even trivial folds, as needing verification discipline (Alive2-backed, tested, target-independent). By the same standard, each desugaring rule is a rewrite demanding equivalent proof effort; a zero-sugar core incurs zero such obligations.

Rust monomorphization is carded with explicit compile-time/code-size tradeoffs, establishing compile-time as an admissible, evidence-recognized cost channel — the channel canonical syntax optimizes via deterministic parsing and content-addressed caching in the AI iteration loop.

Codegen units/incremental compilation/LTO card confirms the corpus treats compile-time vs optimization-power tradeoffs as first-class design facts; a trivially parseable, byte-canonical surface is the cheapest place in the whole pipeline to buy back iteration-loop latency and cache determinism without sacrificing any optimization power.

The corpus's flagship finding on what actually enables optimization lists only semantic mechanisms (exclusivity, anti-aliasing contracts) across four languages. Representative of all 46 confirmed cards: optimizer benefit accrues from semantics, never from surface notation — so cutting notation sugar forfeits nothing carded.

machineWriterCosts: Multiple equivalent spellings (infix vs call form, optional parens, elided types/arguments, formatting freedom) make semantically identical code textually divergent, so consistency drift across AI-generated near-duplicates becomes undetectable by byte/hash comparison; canonical form makes drift mechanically detectable and dedupable.

Precedence and associativity create a silent misbinding error class (writer intends one parse, grammar delivers another) that type-checks and compiles; with a fallible writer at scale and no human reviewer, every such silent class is a direct correctness tax. Fixed-form syntax makes the written tree the parsed tree by construction. (AI misbinding rates: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.)

Diagnostics must be phrased in surface terms and mapped back through desugaring/elision to be actionable, requiring resugaring machinery; canonical syntax allows diagnostics as exact node paths into the text the writer emitted — strictly better machine-readable feedback for the repair loop.

Rich grammars require heuristic error recovery that emits cascading, misleading parse errors; fed back into an AI iteration loop these poison repair attempts. A fixed-form grammar has trivial, local, deterministic error reporting. (Loop-convergence impact: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.)

Non-canonical formatting defeats content-addressed caching, minimal diffs, and subtree hash-consing; byte-canonical sources give deterministic incremental compilation and cheap context re-reading via stable diffs — compile-time in the iteration loop is a carded cost channel (D001, D004).

The elaborator (sugar-to-core) becomes trusted, verification-demanding compiler surface (per F003's soundness caveat and N006's Alive2 discipline), adding maintenance and proof burden that competes with the optimizer/verifier work that actually delivers carded performance.
consistencyRisks: Token-count risk of the cut: fully parenthesized canonical forms are plausibly more verbose than infix, increasing generation cost and context pressure, which could itself raise long-file hallucination/drift — this cuts against the prosecution and is unmeasured in either direction.

Training-distribution risk: current LLMs are trained overwhelmingly on infix languages; initial error rates writing an unfamiliar canonical form may exceed infix error rates. This is an admissible machine-writer cost (not human familiarity) and is unquantified.

Enforcement risk: 'one canonical formatting' only pays off if tooling never reintroduces variance; any pretty-printer, macro layer, or out-of-core sugar dialect that emerges informally would recreate the multiple-spellings problem without the core's verification discipline — the cut must be policed at the toolchain boundary or it silently un-cuts itself.

Scope-creep risk in the other direction: some elision (e.g., local type inference) may interact with already-leading semantic candidates (checked ownership, static specialization) in ways round 2+ debates could want; cutting all elision now must not be read as pre-deciding inference questions that belong to the type-system debates.

Corpus-bias caveat applies to the prosecution too: the corpus contains no syntax evidence because syntax was out of scope by selection (caveats.md), not because syntax was investigated and found worthless; the null-hypothesis win is procedural, not empirical.
empiricalUntested: Relative token/context cost of canonical (S-expression/fixed-form) vs infix-sugared code for LLM generation and re-reading — EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; corpus has no AI-codegen evidence.
AI writer error rates on precedence/associativity misbinding in infix grammars vs structural errors in canonical forms — EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
Whether byte-canonical single-spelling code measurably reduces consistency drift/hallucination across AI-generated near-duplicate code copies — EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
Whether node-path machine-readable diagnostics from a fixed-form grammar improve AI repair-loop convergence vs conventional surface diagnostics — EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Actual share of parse/elaboration in end-to-end compile latency for an AI iteration loop, and the realized caching benefit of canonical bytes (content-addressed incremental compilation) — EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; corpus cards compile-time tradeoffs (D001, D004) but nothing frontend-specific.

Training-distribution penalty: whether current models generate canonical fixed-form syntax with higher initial defect rates than infix, and how fast that gap closes with in-context examples — EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
Elaborator defect rates in practice (bugs per desugaring rule) for sugared languages as a proxy for the verification burden avoided by cutting sugar — not carded in this corpus.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP (honest defense of a restricted core). CUT: free-form syntactic/token/AST macros — I cannot defend them under the admissible criteria; the AI genuinely can be the syntax-sugar processor, and token rewriting carries no fact the compiler retains anyway. KEEP: a verified staging core consisting of (1) pure, terminating, fuel-bounded comptime evaluation producing values, sizes, layouts, and target parameters inside the compiler's trusted evaluator; (2) parametric definitions (generics/templates) whose instantiation happens in-compiler, not in the AI's forward pass; (3) compiler-side derive-style generation from type definitions (single-source-of-truth invariants). The 'AI is the macro processor, zero staging' alternative should be rejected for these three sub-features specifically.
result.coreArgument: The alternative — all expansion happens before the source exists — does not eliminate metaprogramming; it relocates the macro processor from a deterministic, checkable evaluator inside the compiler to a probabilistic writer outside the verification boundary, and in doing so it destroys facts and forecloses decisions the corpus says belong in the compiler. (1) OPTIMIZER NECESSITY: a parametric definition plus argument list is a syntactic certificate that N expansions are the same computation modulo parameters. Pre-expanded copies are textually independent functions; recovering their equivalence is generally undecidable, so the compiler loses deduplication, shared-body, and outlining options. Concretely, the round-1 dispatch-generics verdict (design_candidate_later) explicitly held the monomorphize-vs-share axis OPEN pending measurement (D001 vs D005), and flagged the F009 Swift-SIL 'fourth model' — IR-level generics where the OPTIMIZER decides specialization per target and cost model — as the strongest unexamined candidate. Zero staging hard-commits that open axis at generation time, by an agent that has no target description, no cost model, and (per the static-vs-profile leaning) no sanctioned profile channel. F009's confirmed design lesson — keep performance-critical semantics visible to passes rather than pre-lowering them — applies directly: AI pre-expansion is premature lowering performed outside the compiler. Likewise, comptime target parameterization (vector widths, cache-line sizes, cfg variants) collapses under zero staging into one hand-expanded source per target — exactly the hardware-baked fragility A007 documents for ISPC and A004's staged lowering exists to avoid. (2) VERIFICATION NECESSITY: this charter says the writer is fallible at scale, and the corpus's repeated pattern (F006 SPARK, F001 Rust, A008) is that facts are trustworthy only when made true by construction under a restricted model, never when promised. Under zero staging two invariant classes become UNCHECKABLE because the artifact that states them never exists: (a) cross-copy consistency — 'these 500 functions are instances of one pattern' is checkable only if the pattern exists; with AI expansion, drift between near-duplicates (the admissible failure mode) has NO detection mechanism at any drift rate; (b) value-computation correspondence — comptime-computed constants (tables, sizes, alignments, ranges) are derived by a trusted evaluator from their defining expression, whereas AI-precomputed literals are unchecked assertions that flow directly into UB-bearing optimizer fact channels (F003 noalias, N002 nsw/nuw poison, N004 range-metadata poison, N005 inbounds poison), converting a single hallucinated table entry into silent miscompilation-grade UB. A fallible writer feeding UB-bearing contracts is precisely the configuration the ir-strategy round disqualified ('uncheckable trusted contracts... disqualifying'). (3) MACHINE-WRITER FAILURE MODES OF THE ALTERNATIVE: O(N)-copy token generation and re-reading on every edit versus O(1) definition edits; N-fold diagnostics with no expansion provenance for the AI to map back to a pattern; and compile-time that does not obviously improve, since the build still parses/typechecks/optimizes N copies while losing instantiation caching and dedup (D004, D009 negative scopes). I concede the honest costs of keeping: unbounded CTFE is a compile-time hazard in the AI iteration loop, and comptime-dependent types threaten decidable checking — hence the restriction to a pure (F006-precedent), fuel-bounded, non-token-rewriting core. The null hypothesis is overcome only for that core, not for macros in general.
