citedEvidence.howItSupports: Monomorphization is compiler-side metaprogramming from one parametric definition; its negative scope (code size, compile time, unquantified) frames the specialize-vs-share trade that must stay a compiler decision — AI pre-expansion makes it irreversibly at generation time.

.NET's shipped hybrid (specialize value types, share reference-type bodies) is the corpus's only bounded-code-growth generics strategy; it is only reachable if the parametric body survives into the compiler — zero staging forecloses it, contradicting the round-1 dispatch verdict that held this axis open pending measurement.

Workflow-confirmed: Swift SIL keeps high-level semantics visible so optimization decisions happen in-compiler with information LLVM would not see; the design lesson condemns pre-lowering performance-critical structure — which is exactly what AI-side expansion is.

Codegen-unit/LTO boundaries show specialization/inlining outcomes depend on build-time visibility and options; expansion decisions fixed before the source exists cannot respond to any of these, and the alternative loses cross-unit instantiation deduplication entirely.

Julia evidence that over-specialization costs compile time and cache pressure — specialization requires cost-model discretion the generation-time expander lacks; also documents that hidden call targets defeat optimizers, the failure mode of hand-rolled expansion residue.

ISPC gang/mask sizes are target-dependent and baking execution strategy into algorithm text is hardware-fragile; AI pre-expansion of target parameters bakes hardware constants into source, multiplying variants per target and inheriting this attested fragility.

Halide's schedule layer is corpus-attested staged metaprogramming: optimization choices as first-class manipulable structure separate from the algorithm; a zero-staging language cannot express this layer at all, forcing hand-expanded scheduled loops.

Affine restriction makes dependence analysis efficient and reliable; hand-expanded (pre-tiled, pre-unrolled) loop nests written by the AI in place of staged expansion are exactly the semi-affine/arbitrary-indexing forms whose analyzability A003's negative scope says degrades.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-bearing promises sound only if frontend obligations are true; constants and disjointness facts derived by a trusted comptime evaluator are true by construction, while AI-asserted literals feeding these channels are unchecked promises.
nsw/nuw make overflow poison — numeric facts are contracts, not annotations; wrong AI-precomputed constants woven into flagged arithmetic become deferred UB rather than detectable errors.

range metadata makes out-of-range values poison; a hallucinated bound on a precomputed table is a semantic promise with UB consequences, which a comptime evaluator would instead derive correctly from the defining expression.

SPARK's side-effect-free expression rule is the precedent for the restricted keep: a pure, mathematically treatable evaluation fragment, and the corpus's exemplar that facts must be true by construction via toolchain checking rather than by writer promise.

machineWriterCosts: ALTERNATIVE — consistency drift with no detector: once the pattern exists only in the AI's context, 'copies must match modulo parameters' is stated in no artifact, so no checker can exist; any nonzero drift rate between near-duplicate expansions is permanently silent. This is the admissible drift failure mode in its worst form: undetectable, not merely present. (Drift RATE is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the absence of a detection mechanism is structural and rate-independent.)

ALTERNATIVE — token/context blowup: every edit to a pattern costs O(N copies) regeneration plus O(N) re-reading to edit any one copy safely, versus O(1) definition edit with compiler re-expansion; target/config parameterization multiplies this again (one expansion per target, per A007's target-dependence evidence). Magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

ALTERNATIVE — diagnostics multiplication and provenance loss: a defect in the pattern surfaces as N copy-local diagnostics with no machine-readable link to a common origin; compiler-expanded code carries structured provenance (definition + instantiation arguments), giving the AI one actionable repair site. Machine-readable diagnostics are an admissible cost channel per the charter.

ALTERNATIVE — compile-time does not clearly improve: the build still parses, typechecks, and optimizes N expanded copies while losing instantiation caching, cross-unit dedup (D004), and shared-body options (D005); pre-expansion trades compiler-side instantiation for frontend volume. Direction and magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — no quantitative compile-time cards exist (caveats.md).

ALTERNATIVE — verification amortization lost: a bounds-constrained parametric body can be checked once for all instantiations; N independent copies require N verification runs and N proof re-discharges, multiplying the verification bill exactly where the charter says verification matters most.

KEEP (honest cost) — unbounded comptime execution is a real compile-time hazard in the AI iteration loop (C++-template-class blowup; D001/D009 negative scopes attest specialization compile-time costs); the defended core must be fuel-bounded/terminating, with evaluation cost surfaced as a machine-readable diagnostic.

KEEP (honest cost) — comptime adds a second evaluation semantics the AI must model when generating code; mispredicting comptime results is a new writer error class, mitigated (not eliminated) by the compiler being the arbiter that reports actual values. EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
consistencyRisks: Coupling with dispatch-generics: my strongest optimizer-necessity argument leans on the round-1 dispatch-generics leading candidate (guaranteed static specialization from in-compiler parametric definitions, D001/D005/F009 axis held open). If that topic later resolves toward AI-side monomorphization, the defended core shrinks to comptime evaluation + derive, and this verdict should be revisited.

Coupling with arrays-loops: the arrays-loops leading candidate includes 'an optional schedule layer confined to the pure fragment' (A006) — a staging construct. A zero-staging ruling here would contradict that leaning; conversely, keeping my restricted core must not be read as licensing an unrestricted macro system through the schedule layer's back door.

Comptime-dependent types (Zig-style types-as-values) risk making type checking evaluation-dependent and potentially undecidable, in tension with the verification-first charter; the keep is only consistent if comptime is confined to a terminating, decidable fragment, and that boundary is not yet designed.

Violation semantics: comptime evaluation failure (fuel exhaustion, comptime overflow, comptime UB) is a new violation class that must be slotted into the graduated-obligations framework the violation-semantics round left at research_needed; keeping comptime before that lands creates an undefined failure mode.

static-vs-profile firewall: keeping specialization decisions in-compiler preserves the 'profile data restricted to cost decisions' firewall; but any future proposal to feed profile data INTO comptime evaluation would breach it — the keep must forbid comptime reading profile facts.

Corpus coverage: there are zero direct macro/comptime/CTFE evidence cards; this entire defense argues by extension from D/F/A/N cards plus construction-level reasoning, which caveats.md's standing rule requires stating explicitly — the keep is a design candidate argument, not a carded conclusion.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior: drift/hallucination rates across near-duplicate expansions, reliability of AI-precomputed constants (the hallucinated-table failure mode), and token-economics of regenerating N copies versus one definition — the corpus contains no AI-codegen evidence whatsoever.

Whether compilers can partially recover equivalence of independently generated near-duplicate functions (identical-code-folding effectiveness on AI-generated copies with drift) — if recovery were cheap and robust, the dedup/shared-body loss argument weakens.

Compile-time comparison of N pre-expanded source copies versus one definition plus N in-compiler instantiations — no quantitative compile-time, code-size, or i-cache cards exist for any expansion strategy (caveats.md; dispatch-generics evidence gap).

Whether comptime-computed constants actually survive lowering and are consumed as optimizer facts (trip counts, range metadata, alignment) — the arrays-loops round found end-to-end fact consumption unvalidated for every position; my 'trusted evaluator feeds fact channels' benefit inherits that gap.

Whether shared/erased generic bodies can soundly discharge F003 noalias obligations and at what precision loss (the backlog's construction-level check) — this arbitrates how much optimizer value the preserved parametric form actually buys.

Whether a fuel-bounded, pure comptime fragment covers the value-computation workload (tables, layouts, target parameters) without pressure to grow toward unrestricted CTFE — no workload census exists.

The premise that generation-time expansion cannot make good specialization decisions: the AI could in principle be given target descriptions and measurements at generation time; whether that channel is worse than compiler cost models is untested (though it would still lack the drift detector and per-target re-decision cheapness).

result.stance: CUT. Rich error handling (unwinding, panics/exceptions, drop-during-unwind) must be excluded from the core language. Result values as the only error channel, with traps that abort the process and zero unwinding machinery, carries the full load.
result.coreArgument: The null hypothesis is exclusion, and the corpus hands inclusion nothing. The only adversarially confirmed exception-handling evidence in the entire corpus is a cost card: F008 documents that EH representation choice affects optimization, that Windows-style funclets forbid register allocation for any value live into or out of a funclet, and that even the 'better' landingpad model is only better *relative to funclets* — and F008 carries design_generalization_confidence: low. Meanwhile both claims that would exonerate the feature — 'LLVM zero-cost EH keeps machinery off the normal path' and the invoke-representation story — were REFUTED 0-3 by adversarial verification and sit in refuted-and-unverified.jsonl with an explicit caveats.md instruction not to silently reintroduce them. So the corpus state is: confirmed costs, refuted exculpations, zero confirmed benefits. Under the standing rule that proposals must cite exact cards, the feature cannot meet its burden. (Per misleading-comparisons.jsonl, I am NOT arguing 'exceptions are always slow' — I am arguing the evidence for paying their machinery cost does not exist, and the null wins ties.) The feature also taxes everything this project actually values. Structurally (flagged: extension beyond card scope), unwinding turns every may-throw call into an implicit CFG edge and a mid-function observation point for memory state, constraining exactly the F002-class payoffs — register promotion, dead-store elimination, load/store reordering — that the corpus's aliasing-heavy evidence base (F001/F002/F003) exists to unlock. Drop-during-unwind synthesizes a shadow cleanup CFG the AI writer never wrote and never re-reads; the ownership/effect facts on those paths must survive lowering, which the violation-semantics verdict lists as a known unmeasured weak point, and they double the N006-style Alive2 validation surface of the verifier-checked IR that is the ir-strategy leading candidate. For a fallible machine writer at scale, exception safety is the worst possible invariant class: implicit, cross-cutting, compiler-unchecked, and exercised only on rare paths — silent-failure territory by construction. The alternative composes with every leading candidate already on the table: Result<T,E> is ordinary typed dataflow verified at every call site, monomorphizes to concrete code under the dispatch-generics candidate (D001), and keeps error semantics visible to optimizer passes rather than buried in opaque runtime machinery — the exact design lesson F009 draws from Swift SIL making ARC visible. Traps-abort matches the only carded post-violation observability precedent in the corpus (C002: Go's race-detection halts) and requires no new semantics; the corpus's one carded optimization-recovery mechanism (A005 runtime checks + loop versioning) is branch-based and needs no unwinder. Cutting the feature deletes whole compiler subsystems — per-platform landingpad/funclet lowering, unwind tables, personality functions, cleanup-block synthesis, drop-flag tracking, may-unwind effect analysis — and removes an entire coupled dimension from three still-open debates (violation-semantics, concurrency, ir-strategy). A simple, verifiable, extreme-performance compiler is bought by deleting exactly this kind of machinery.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's ONLY confirmed EH card, and it is cost-side: funclet-based EH constrains register allocation (no registers for values live into/out of funclets), and landingpad is documented as better only relative to funclets. Platform-coupled representation choice = per-target compiler complexity. design_generalization_confidence is low, so it cannot carry an inclusion case even if read charitably.

The claim 'LLVM's zero-cost EH model keeps machinery off the normal execution path' failed adversarial verification 0-3. The standard exculpatory argument for unwinding ('the happy path is free') is affirmatively unavailable in this corpus and flagged for manual recheck before any reuse.

The invoke-vs-call representation claim also failed 0-3; even the feature's basic IR-representation story is unverified. caveats.md orders these refutations preserved so overbroad EH claims are not reintroduced — the feature's advocates must not be allowed to argue from them.

The carded payoff of non-interference is register promotion, redundant-read elimination, dead-write elimination, and reordering around writes. Unwind edges add mid-function points where memory state becomes observable, constraining these optimizations at every may-throw call site. (Flagged: this specific extension is structural inference, not carded measurement.)

Carded design lesson: performance-critical semantics (ownership, refcounting) should be VISIBLE to optimizer passes rather than buried in opaque runtime effects. Result values are visible dataflow; unwinding is the paradigm case of semantics buried in runtime machinery (unwind tables, personality routines) invisible to passes.

Go's race-detection halts are the only carded post-violation observability precedent in the corpus, and the violation-semantics verdict names trap-with-report as the carded diagnosis channel. Traps-abort aligns the error-handling design with the only evidence-backed failure semantics; unwinding has no carded observability story.

InstCombine/Alive2 discipline: every semantic channel a transform can touch must be tested and proof-validated. Unwind edges and synthesized cleanup blocks enlarge the validation surface of every pass in a compiler this project wants verifiable. (Flagged: surface-size claim is structural, not measured.)

The corpus's carded mechanism for recovering assumption-grade optimization at runtime is plain branch-based checks plus code versioning. No carded optimization mechanism anywhere requires an unwinder; failed checks branching to slow paths or trapping covers the recovery design space.

Rust monomorphization compiles generics to concrete type-specific code, so Result<T,E> lowers to concrete-code discriminated values under the dispatch-generics leading candidate (guaranteed static specialization) — the alternative rides machinery the debates already favor, adding no new subsystem.

SPARK, the corpus's most verification-oriented carded design, requires expressions and function calls to be side-effect free to support mathematical treatment of functions. Implicit throwing control flow inside expressions is contrary to that carded discipline. (Flagged: the card does not name exceptions; this is an extrapolation from its side-effect-freedom finding.)

The corpus already prices trap-like immediate-UB semantics: guards pin code motion per checked operation. That cost is localized, already inside the violation-semantics frame (graduated obligations), and bounded — unlike unwinding's per-call-site tax, which is corpus-wide and uncarded. Defends the alternative against the 'traps also pessimize' counterattack.

Constrains the prosecution's own wording: 'exceptions are always slow' is flagged misleading. The case therefore rests not on a speed claim but on the null hypothesis plus confirmed representation costs (F008) plus refuted exculpations plus zero benefit cards — which is exactly the preferred 'precise evidence' framing.

machineWriterCosts: Consistency drift on unwind-safety invariants (the decisive writer cost of KEEPING the feature): exception safety is implicit, cross-cutting, and compiler-unchecked — cleanup ordering, partially-initialized state, and invariant restoration on paths that execute only when something throws. An AI editing one of N near-duplicate functions gets no local diagnostic when it breaks an invariant that only fires during unwinding; this is precisely the hallucination-across-near-duplicates failure mode. Result-only converts every error path into type-checked dataflow where drift is caught mechanically at each call site. [EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED as to actual AI failure rates]

Context/re-read cost of invisible control flow: with unwinding, a function's true CFG is not in its token stream — the writer must reconstruct implicit edges from callee throw-sets it may not have in context when re-reading or editing. Result propagation keeps the complete control flow in the visible text; verbosity is admissible, invisible semantics are not. [EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED]

Compile-time in the AI iteration loop: cutting the feature deletes cleanup-block synthesis, unwind-table emission, per-platform landingpad/funclet selection (F008), personality-function plumbing, drop-flag tracking, and may-unwind effect analysis, and shrinks every pass's CFG. [Unquantified — caveats.md notes documentation evidence never quantifies compile-time cost; no corpus card measures EH compile-time]

Machine-readable diagnostics: trap-abort yields exactly one well-defined failure point with a report (C002 Go-halt precedent, and the violation-semantics verdict's trap-with-report channel). Catchable/rethrowable panics make failure reporting path-dependent and interceptable — a degraded, ambiguous diagnostic channel for an automated writer that must parse failures programmatically.

Conceded cost of the alternative, entered honestly: Result propagation inflates tokens per call site and creates near-duplicate propagation boilerplate to generate and re-read. Mitigation: every propagation site is type-checked (drift is caught, not silent), and the residual unchecked surface (error-payload content) is small. Whether the token cost outweighs the verifiability gain is untestable from this corpus. [EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED]
consistencyRisks: FFI/unwind boundary: foreign code that unwinds (C++ exceptions, Rust panic=unwind) entering a no-unwind runtime is undefined behavior unless firewalled; the cut decision must ship with an explicit abort-at-boundary rule. The backlog flags exactly this area (ffi_abi_runtime: 'ABI, unwinding boundaries' — missing_primary_source, high priority), so the boundary rule is currently evidence-free in both directions.

No task-level fault isolation: traps kill the process, so long-running or multi-tenant systems need OS-process isolation for fault containment. Later pressure to add catch/recover would reintroduce unwinding as an unplanned bolt-on — the worst outcome. The cut verdict must be paired with a declared process-isolation doctrine to be stable.

Coupling to three research_needed topics: violation-semantics' leading candidate (graduated obligations) is compatible with trap=abort but is thereby constrained to halt-and-report observability; the concurrency model (C001/C002 undecided) must still define what a trap means under racing threads; the ir-strategy verifier must still verify trap reachability. Cutting unwinding simplifies these couplings but does not eliminate them.

Ownership/RAII interplay: the aliasing-model leading candidate is checked ownership; with no unwinding, destructors run only on normal paths, so 'cleanup always runs' cannot be a language invariant. External-resource cleanup (files, locks, IPC state) after aborts falls to the OS or to design patterns — an invariant downgrade other design docs must not silently assume away.

AI iteration-loop harness: without catchable panics, an in-process test runner cannot isolate failing tests; harnesses must be multi-process, adding restart latency to the compile-test loop and creating pressure for inconsistent in-band workarounds.

Sentinel-value drift: hot-path pressure may push the machine writer to encode errors in-band (sentinels, NaN, -1) to avoid Result branch/layout costs, recreating exactly the unchecked error channels the alternative exists to eliminate; needs lint/verifier countermeasures.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI-writer behavior in this case — that AIs maintain explicit Result plumbing more reliably than implicit exception-safety invariants, that invisible unwind edges raise re-read/edit error rates, that token-cost of propagation is affordable — are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence.

Performance delta between Result-based and unwinding-based error propagation (hot path and cold path) is unquantified anywhere; round1-index lists 'no quantified performance deltas for any contract regime' as a cross-topic gap, and F002's negative scope disclaims magnitudes.
Compile-time cost of EH machinery, and savings from its removal, are unmeasured (caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify compile-time cost).

Whether trap-abort-with-report is a sufficient post-failure diagnostics channel is untested — round1-violation-semantics lists post-deployment violation observability as an open evidence gap with C002's Go halt as the only adjacent precedent.
Code-size tradeoff: Result monomorphization bloat (D001's negative scope flags code-size tradeoffs as uncovered) versus unwind-table and cleanup-code size — neither side is measured.

F008's generalization limits: design_generalization_confidence is low, the funclet constraint is Windows/LLVM-specific, and the normal-path cost of even the landingpad model is unmeasured — two broader LLVM EH claims were refuted outright.

My structural extensions — that unwind edges materially constrain F002-class optimizations at may-throw call sites, and that cleanup CFGs materially enlarge the N006 validation surface — are directionally supported but not carded as measurements; they need the same benchmark discipline round1 demanded of every other position.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP ONLY — honest concession on the full feature. Infix operators, precedence, general sugar, and elision as a bundle are NOT defensible on optimizer necessity or verification necessity: surface notation is erased at parse, both grammars yield identical ASTs, and every compiler-facing fact survives the canonical alternative. The alternative (fully regular canonical syntax) should win the CORE: it is the stored, diffed, and diagnostic-echoed ground-truth form. I defend one narrow remnant: a minimal, fixed, bijective infix-expression CODEC (arithmetic/comparison/indexing only; tiny fixed precedence table; no user-defined operators, precedence, or overloading; mandatory parentheses across precedence classes; deterministic round-trip to canonical form enforced by a parse→print→parse fixpoint check; compiler always echoes canonical form). Elision is kept only for facts the compiler infers deterministically and immediately re-elaborates into the stored canonical artifact — contract-bearing facts (aliasing/ownership, numeric modes, types at boundaries, effects) may never be elided; writer-stated inferable facts are cross-checked against inference as hallucination checksums (hard error on mismatch).
result.coreArgument: The only admissible defense is concrete machine-writer failure modes of the canonical-only alternative, and two are substantive. (1) Token blowup in the AI iteration loop: extreme-performance code is expression-dense, and a fully-parenthesized prefix form inflates exactly the expression-dense parts most. A rough proxy measurement on six representative kernel fragments (complex MAC, Horner polynomial, row-major addressing, masked bounds check, matmul inner loop, normalized difference) shows S-expression forms cost ~1.35–1.5x the tokens (1.9x characters) of infix equivalents; this tax is paid on every generation AND every context re-read, compounding across repair iterations of the AI loop. (2) Transcription-distance error induction, which is verification-relevant: performance kernels are written from specs whose formulas are in standard mathematical (infix) notation. The canonical-only alternative forces the writer to apply a nontrivial notation transform to every expression; the characteristic failure — operand-order swaps in non-commutative operators ((div b a) for a/b, (sub b a) for a−b) and wrong-associativity regrouping — type-checks cleanly and is semantically silent, i.e., it lands precisely in the class of errors the verifier CANNOT catch, in a language whose whole premise (per the corpus: F001/F003/N001–N005 treat optimizer facts as explicit checked contracts) is that unchecked writer claims are the enemy. A near-identity transcription codec removes the transform step where this error class is created; prevention at generation substitutes for detection that cannot exist. Both arguments are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (the corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence) — so the correct disposition is: canonical core now, codec admitted only behind an A/B codegen experiment (same kernel specs, two grammars; measure tokens, first-parse success, silent semantic-error rate via differential testing, repair iterations). Everything else in the feature — user-defined operators/precedence, statement sugar, semantic elision — I cannot defend and cut: it destroys the single-canonical-form property, obscures mode selection (N002), and removes explicit contract channels (F001/F003), while its only benefits are inadmissible ergonomics. N006 supports the architecture: canonicalization to one form is already how optimizer infrastructure validates transforms; the surface should mirror that, with notation as a verified compression layer, not a semantic layer.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Supports my concession on optimizer necessity: Swift SIL evidence locates optimization value in preserving high-level SEMANTIC structure into the IR, not in surface notation. A canonical S-expression surface preserves the identical AST/semantic structure, so no compiler fact is lost by cutting infix syntax — the defense cannot rest on optimizer necessity.

InstCombine is a target-independent canonicalization pass whose transforms are validated against single canonical forms (Alive2 @src/@tgt proofs). Supports the partial-keep architecture: one canonical stored/diffed/diagnosed representation is the norm optimizer infrastructure already relies on; any kept notation must be a bijective codec over it, never a second semantic surface.

LLVM separates wrapping arithmetic from nuw/nsw promise-bearing arithmetic as distinct contracts. Constrains the kept codec: an infix '+' must map to exactly one explicitly named numeric mode (aligning with the round1 numeric-semantics candidate of named, truthfully-lowered modes); operator sugar that obscures mode selection is banned from the keep.

Language-level non-interference rules are explicit contracts the optimizer assumes. Supports banning ELISION of contract-bearing facts (aliasing/ownership/exclusivity annotations) in any sugar layer: eliding them either destroys the fact channel or forces unverified re-inference, which the writer-fallibility premise forbids.

LLVM noalias/alias.scope facts carry UB obligations if false. Supports the requirement that the stored canonical artifact be fully elaborated and explicit — sugar/elision may exist only on the write path, because obligation-bearing facts must be visible, checkable, and single-sourced in the ground-truth form.

Its negative scope explicitly states the card 'does not decide source syntax' — representative of the whole corpus (echoed in caveats.md: implementation evidence must not decide source syntax). Establishes that NO corpus card supports either side of this syntax debate, forcing every writer-behavior claim to be flagged EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED and gating the codec on a proposed experiment.

machineWriterCosts: Token blowup of the canonical-only alternative on expression-dense performance code: measured ~1.35–1.5x tokens (1.93x characters) for S-expression vs infix on six representative kernel fragments (proxy tokenizer, scratchpad measurement; script at /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-bytedance-Dev-xlang/035e62ef-9ecd-42d5-b288-f6f92231c257/scratchpad/tokcount.py). The tax applies to every generation and every re-read of context, compounding across AI repair iterations; per-file inflation also shrinks how much program fits in one context window.

Transcription-distance error induction: converting spec formulas (which are in infix mathematical notation) into prefix canonical form is a per-expression transform whose failure modes — operand-order swaps in non-commutative ops (sub/div/shift/compare) and associativity regrouping errors — are type-correct and semantically silent, i.e., unverifiable by the type system or contract checker. The alternative manufactures errors in the exact class verification cannot catch.

Deep-nesting repair loops: large kernel expressions in fully-parenthesized form require deeply nested balanced structure; imbalance is caught by the parser (cheap) but each failure costs a full compile-diagnose-regenerate cycle in the AI loop, and balanced-but-wrongly-nested forms (operand attached to the wrong operator) are again silent.

Drift surface scaling: the brief's named risk of consistency drift across near-duplicate copies plausibly scales with per-copy token count; a ~1.4x more verbose canonical form enlarges every repeated block that must be kept consistent (weak, untested).

Costs I concede AGAINST my position: canonical syntax parses trivially and unambiguously (no precedence table to misapply), has exactly one formatting (perfect diffing/dedup), simplifies machine-readable diagnostics with stable node addressing, and adds zero compile-time — the alternative wins on every axis except generation-token cost and transcription fidelity, which is why I concede the core and keep only a codec.
consistencyRisks: Dual-representation drift: if infix (write/render codec) and canonical (stored) spellings of the same expression both circulate in the AI's context, near-duplicate detection and copy consistency degrade — the same expression has two spellings. Mitigation: store and echo ONLY canonical form, codec is write-path-only; residual risk in mixed contexts remains.

Precedence misrecall: an AI writer emitting infix under a misremembered precedence table produces a silently different parse than intended (the classic C '&' vs '==' bug class, likely reproduced by writers trained on C-family corpora). Mitigated by a tiny fixed table plus mandatory parentheses when mixing precedence classes, but not eliminated — this is the strongest argument for cutting even the codec.

Codec non-bijectivity or version drift: any codec bug or grammar evolution that changes the infix→canonical mapping silently changes semantics of existing text; the parse→print→parse fixpoint check must be a mandatory compiler invariant, and codec versions must be pinned in artifacts.

Redundancy-checksum erosion via elision: every elided inferable fact removes a cross-check channel that could catch writer hallucination; the keep therefore makes stating inferable facts legal and checked (mismatch = hard error), but writers that always elide get less verification — a policy knob, not a solved problem.

Scope creep: sugar historically accretes (user operators, implicit conversions, statement forms); each addition multiplies surface forms per semantic construct and erodes the single-canonical-form property that makes the stored corpus diffable and dedupable. The keep must be frozen at expression-level arithmetic/comparison/indexing.
empiricalUntested: ALL AI-writer behavioral claims herein — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence cards (confirmed by corpus-wide search; caveats.md and A001 explicitly exclude source-syntax decisions from existing evidence).

Token-cost ratio of infix vs canonical form measured with real model tokenizers on whole programs (my 1.35–1.5x figure is a regex-proxy on expression fragments; real BPE vocabularies merge paren runs, and declaration/control-flow-heavy code will inflate less than expression-dense kernels).

The claim that AI writers commit more silent operand-order/associativity errors when emitting prefix/canonical form than infix (transcription-distance hypothesis) — the load-bearing claim of my defense; no evidence either way.
The counter-claim that AI writers misapply infix precedence tables at rates exceeding prefix transcription errors — if true, the codec should be cut too; equally untested.
Whether paren-balancing failures in deeply nested canonical forms materially increase AI repair-loop iterations, given parser diagnostics are immediate and machine-readable.
Whether consistency drift across near-duplicate blocks actually scales with per-copy verbosity.

Proposed gating experiment before admitting the codec: A/B codegen benchmark — identical kernel specs rendered to both grammars; measure generation tokens, first-parse success rate, silent semantic-error rate via differential testing against a reference implementation, and repair iterations to green.

result.stance: CUT — module system / separate compilation / stable internal ABI must not be in the core language. Whole-program single-unit compilation, closed world, with one explicit FFI boundary carries the load.
result.coreArgument: The null hypothesis wins on the corpus's own standing rule. Caveats.md demands that any design proposal cite exact evidence cards, and the corpus contains ZERO cards supporting a stable internal ABI: the ffi_abi_runtime backlog entry is explicitly marked missing_primary_source/priority-high ("Collect sources on ABI, unwinding boundaries, ... LTO, ... interop" — blocks moving from research_only to design_candidate). The feature cannot even be lawfully proposed yet; the alternative IS the null. Worse, every carded appearance of a compilation boundary in this corpus is as a performance pathology: D004 documents that more separate codegen units "may inhibit optimization" and that LTO exists precisely to buy back whole-program visibility at longer link time; D007 documents LTO/IPA as recovery machinery that "costs compile time/memory/code size" and whose speculative devirtualization "may be reverted"; D008 documents that ABI/dynamic-linking semantics (ELF interposition) "can block optimizer assumptions even when source code appears statically known." Separate compilation is the disease for which LTO is the documented, expensive, conditional cure — an optimizer-first language should not ship the disease in its core. Second, a stable internal ABI is a fact-destruction surface, and facts are this language's entire value proposition. The confirmed payload (F001/F002 non-interference, F003/F004 noalias, N004 range, N005 provenance) must survive to consumers; the ir-strategy verdict's cleanest cross-position lesson is that every added boundary is "a fact-loss and miscompile surface," and misleading-comparisons #5 warns metadata is sound only if preserved. A frozen internal ABI forces a dilemma: either re-specify the entire (still-growing, admittedly incomplete per caveats.md) fact vocabulary in a stable binary encoding — a massive frozen spec surface that contradicts a simple, verifiable compiler — or attenuate facts at every internal call boundary, which contradicts extreme performance. Third, and decisive under this project's verification-first premise: separate compilation converts globally checkable facts into TRUSTED interface assertions. F003 facts are UB-bearing promises; the writer is a fallible machine operating at scale. With separately compiled artifacts, a stale or drifted interface summary (module A compiled against yesterday's declaration of B's purity/noalias/ownership signature) is a silent miscompile channel — exactly the unchecked-invariant class this language exists to eliminate. A closed world makes every cross-function fact re-derivable and re-checkable from bodies on every build; version skew is not detected, it is impossible. This also composes with the round-1 leaders: the aliasing-model design candidate (checked ownership) checks globally without inventing an ownership-carrying ABI that has no precedent card; the dispatch-generics leader (guaranteed static specialization) requires instantiation-site body visibility that D004 shows separate units stress, while ABI-stable generics forces the witness-table/dictionary model — D002-costed dynamic-dispatch flavor whose only precedent (Swift resilience) is uncarded and listed as an evidence gap. The dispatch judge already ruled that the closed-world position's module elements — no-interposition semantics grounded "genuinely and specifically" in D008, reflection restriction grounded in D005's negative scope — "survive as valuable amendments regardless of the generics decision." Finally, the admissible machine-writer costs favor cutting more than keeping: interface/implementation declaration duplication is a consistency-drift surface for a hallucinating writer (two copies of every signature to keep in sync), and namespacing for token-efficient reference is trivially separable surface syntax requiring no compilation semantics. The one real cost — compile latency in the AI iteration loop — is an implementation problem (deterministic compiler + content-addressed function-level memoization; even D004's codegen units are build-artifact parallelism, not language semantics), not a reason to freeze a semantic ABI into the core. Incrementality-by-caching under a closed world needs no stable ABI because nothing outlives the build that could skew. Keep the compiler simple, keep every fact checked, pay boundary costs exactly once at the explicit FFI wall.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Direct documentation that separate compilation units inhibit optimization and that LTO is bolt-on machinery to recover whole-program visibility at extra link time — the core language should start whole-program rather than ship the boundary and buy it back; also shows unit boundaries are build artifacts, not semantics, so parallelism/incrementality can live in the implementation.

Semantic interposition — an ABI stability guarantee — is documented to block interprocedural analysis, inlining, and propagation even for statically known code; a stable INTERNAL ABI imports exactly this pathology inside the program. The dispatch judge called D008 'genuine, specific support for no-interposition module semantics,' i.e., for the closed-world alternative.

LTO/IPA and devirtualization are conditional, option-dependent, revertible recovery mechanisms that cost compile time, memory, and code size — evidence that recovering cross-boundary optimization after the fact is expensive and unguaranteed, versus never creating the boundary.

Indirect calls through table-based dispatch add lookup cost and block inlining — the same cost class a stable-ABI call boundary or witness-table generic ABI imposes on every cross-module call that cannot be inlined.

The dispatch-generics leading candidate (guaranteed static specialization/monomorphization) requires the compiler to see instantiation sites and bodies together; its negative scope flags cross-crate optimization limits — separate compilation is in tension with the already-leading generics design.

Negative scope: runtime-preserved generic/reflection metadata 'may constrain closed-world assumptions unless restricted' — carded support for restricting open-world machinery (reflection, dynamic metadata) in a performance core, which the dispatch judge endorsed as a surviving amendment.

Noalias facts are UB-bearing promises 'only sound if frontend obligations are true and metadata survives lowering' — under separate compilation these obligations become trusted interface summaries vulnerable to version skew from a fallible machine writer; under closed world they are re-checked from bodies every build.

Return-noalias demands lifetime/provenance/escape rules be discharged before emission; discharge requires seeing the allocation and all uses — cross-ABI-boundary discharge would require encoding lifetime/escape proofs into a frozen ABI with zero corpus precedent.

The confirmed IR-strategy anchor: keep source-level semantic facts visible to the optimizer as long as possible. A stable internal ABI is the opposite move — it forces facts down to a frozen binary encoding early at every module edge, multiplying the fact-loss boundaries the ir-strategy verdict identifies as miscompile surfaces.

The language's payoff facts (exclusivity/non-interference) are language-level global properties; their value depends on the optimizer trusting them everywhere — carded as central enablers, which a fact-attenuating internal boundary directly dilutes.

machineWriterCosts: Compile latency in the AI iteration loop: whole-program compilation means edits can trigger global recompilation and whole-program analysis, and D007's negative scope confirms whole-program IPA/LTO-class work costs compile time and memory. Mitigation (deterministic compiler with content-addressed, function-level memoization inside the closed world) is an implementation strategy with no corpus card and no prototype — this is the alternative's single largest real cost and it is currently unpriced.

Loss of compact interface summaries as writer context: module interfaces double as cheap re-reading surfaces for a token-bounded writer; without them the AI must re-read or externally summarize bodies to recall signatures and contracts. Note this is a tooling artifact (an interface VIEW can be generated by the compiler without semantic separate compilation), but the tooling does not exist and its token economics are unmeasured.

Nonlocal diagnostics risk: closed-world global checking can surface an error far from its cause (e.g., an ownership or noalias violation detected at a distant use site), and machine-readable diagnostics must carry cross-file provenance chains to stay actionable for automated repair — a harder diagnostics design than per-module errors.

Memory/scale ceiling: whole-program in-memory IR at large program sizes is unmeasured; if the ir-strategy division-of-labor lands on a heavy language-level optimizer (F009 direction), peak compiler memory could bound maximum program size before any hardware limit does.

No precompiled internal artifacts: every build ingests all source; mixed-provenance or vendored compiled components are impossible inside the language — all reuse must cross the explicit FFI boundary and pay full fact attenuation there, even for code that was itself written in this language.
consistencyRisks: The FFI boundary must now carry ALL boundary load, and the dispatch-generics follow-up explicitly rules that core/periphery fact attenuation across FFI/dynamic-linking boundaries (D008 class) is 'a precondition, not an optimization detail' for any closed-world commitment — that mechanism is undesigned; if it fails or bloats, there is no internal-module fallback.

Closed world forecloses dynamic loading, plugins, and hot code replacement; the concurrency-model topic is still research_needed and a future verdict requiring runtime-loaded components (foreign threads, accelerator runtimes per the interop dossier) would force reopening this cut.

If dispatch-generics later shifts toward the flagged 'fourth model' (Swift-style separately-compiled generics with witness tables and opt-in specialization, gestured at by F009), that model's entire rationale is a resilient ABI — adopting it would directly contradict this cut; the two verdicts must be decided jointly.

The ir-strategy debate's hidden prior (division of optimization labor above vs below LLVM) is undecided; a heavy language-level whole-program optimizer amplifies the compile-time/memory costs this cut accepts, so an adverse ir-strategy outcome worsens this verdict's cost side retroactively.

The dispatch critic showed closed-world visibility makes devirtualization/specialization POSSIBLE, not GUARANTEED (no card behind 'guaranteed devirtualization'; inliner heuristics stay heuristics; D009-class function-typed values hide call targets even in closed programs) — the prosecution's performance upside must not be oversold as automatic.

Caveats.md warns the corpus is aliasing-heavy by selection and has no ABI/linking primary sources at all (ffi_abi_runtime backlog); this cut rests partly on the absence of pro-ABI evidence, which is selection-shaped absence — the standing rule cuts against PROPOSING the feature, but honest bookkeeping requires noting the ABI research simply has not been done.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: that an AI writer's iteration loop is throughput-bound in a way implementation-level caching (content-addressed function-level memoization in a closed world) can service without language-level separate compilation — no AI-codegen or compiler-caching evidence exists in the corpus.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: that interface/implementation declaration duplication measurably causes AI consistency drift (hallucinated signature mismatches between near-duplicate copies) — plausible from the drift premise, but no corpus card covers AI writer behavior.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: that AI writers do not need module boundaries for context-window management, or that compiler-generated interface views are an adequate token-cost substitute for semantic module interfaces.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: whole-program compile time and peak memory at realistic AI-generated program scales (likely more verbose and repetitive than human code per the design premise, possibly much larger); D007 evidences cost direction, never magnitude — caveats.md notes no quantified compile-time evidence anywhere.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the claimed verification win — that closed-world re-checking eliminates a version-skew miscompile class that separate compilation would actually exhibit in practice with an AI writer; the mechanism follows from F003's soundness conditions, but no incident evidence (e.g., Rust's noalias miscompile history, flagged as an uncarded gap in the ir-strategy verdict) has been carded.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: that nonlocal whole-program diagnostics can be made machine-actionable for automated repair loops; the diagnostics advantage claimed for language-level IRs is explicitly noted in the ir-strategy verdict as having no supporting card.

result.stance: DEFEND — partial keep. The null hypothesis is overcome for three components: (1) interfaces as named operation-bundles carrying signatures plus algebraic/relational/effect law annotations; (2) bounded quantification (generic functions/types over interface bounds) with guaranteed static specialization as the only implicit path; (3) compositional type-capability predicates (Send/Sync-class marker traits). I do NOT defend open-world dynamic dispatch as a core default: keep it only as the explicit, restricted, D003-style construct named in the round-1 dispatch-generics leading candidate, or gate it behind a prototype. Closed-world heterogeneity via sum types + exhaustive match is conceded to the alternative as often optimizer-superior.
result.coreArgument: The alternative does not eliminate abstraction; it relocates specialization from the compiler into the AI writer and erases, from every artifact, the fact that N concrete functions are instances of one algorithm. That erasure loses facts the optimizer needs, makes charter-critical invariants uncheckable, and maximizes the exact failure surface (near-duplicate drift) the charter names as admissible cost. (1) VERIFICATION NECESSITY. Under the alternative each polymorphic algorithm becomes N hand-specialized near-duplicates, each carrying its own copy of law annotations (pure/associative/noalias-return). These annotations are optimizer contracts with UB consequences when false (F003, N002 — 'overflow facts are contracts, not mere annotations'), and their soundness is conditional on writer truthfulness (F001 negative scope; misleading-comparisons note 5). The alternative therefore multiplies the independently-trusted contract surface by the duplication factor: N assertion sites per algorithm family, any of which the fallible writer can silently desynchronize from its body on regeneration — and nothing can check agreement, because 'these N copies implement the same contract' is not expressible without a grouping construct; post-hoc semantic equivalence of drifted copies is undecidable. A single bounded generic body makes that invariant true by construction and reduces each law to one audit/proof site per contract plus one per impl. Bounded quantification also converts algorithm preconditions (comparator totality, clone-returns-disjoint-storage in the F004 class, eq/hash coherence) from tacit knowledge in the writer's context window into declared, compiler-enforced obligations: the generic body provably uses only what the bound grants, and each impl is checked against the contract exactly once. Relational laws spanning multiple operations require an operation bundle to attach to — that bundle IS an interface; the alternative's per-concrete-function annotations cannot express them at all. Finally, the round-1 concurrency and aliasing leading candidates discharge the corpus's best-attested optimizer facts (F001/F002 non-interference) through compositional, type-level, checked predicates with use-site obligations — C004 documents this as Send/Sync auto TRAITS. The alternative as stated (annotations on concrete functions only) cannot host per-type, structurally derived capabilities with caller-transferred obligations; cutting the mechanism strands two leading candidates and pays most of the machinery cost anyway if capabilities are later smuggled back. (2) OPTIMIZER NECESSITY. Round-1 dispatch-generics ruled the specialize-vs-share axis empirically unarbitrable today (zero quantitative cards on code size/i-cache/compile time) and explicitly held open WHERE specialization is decided; F009 — the only workflow-confirmed dispatch-adjacent card — evidences preserving high-level generic semantics into the optimizer so specialization can be an optimizer decision. The alternative pre-decides this axis in its most extreme form (source-level mono-only) and makes it irreversible: with no shared body in any artifact the compiler can never deduplicate instantiations (the share-generics-class lever round-1 named for bounding mono code size), never fall back to bounded sharing (D005) or annotated specialization (the F009 fourth model) in size-bound regimes, and machine-level identical-code-folding is defeated because writer-drifted copies are not bit-identical. Where genuine open-set dispatch is needed, the alternative degrades to hand-rolled fn-pointer/void* erasure — D009's carded optimizer defeat (Function-typed fields hide call targets), and exactly the failure mode the round-1 critique flagged ('erasure that the optimizer and F003 machinery handle worse') — instead of a language-known restricted dyn (D002/D003) whose representation is coherent, whose cost is explicit, and whose type structure D007-class devirtualization machinery can consume. (3) MACHINE-WRITER FAILURE MODES OF THE ALTERNATIVE. Source tokens scale O(N·K) per algorithm family versus O(K + impl declarations), paid at generation and at every re-read; the near-duplicate mass is the canonical drift substrate; one algorithm change becomes N edits, N frontend re-checks, and N law re-verifications inside the iteration loop. All writer-behavior claims are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, but the load-bearing claims here are structural, not behavioral: the inexpressible cross-copy invariant, the multiplied trusted-annotation surface, and the foreclosure of compiler-side sharing hold given only the charter's own stipulation that the writer is fallible at scale and unchecked invariants cannot be trusted. HONEST LIMITS: sum types cover closed heterogeneity, so open-world dyn is defended only as the explicit restricted escape hatch, not as core-default; trait machinery must be restricted to a coherent, decidable, non-overlapping core or it imports its own compile-time explosion; and the specialization cost cards (D001/D009 negative scopes) cut against my side too — except the alternative pays those identical costs at source level while destroying every lever for bounding them.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Workflow-confirmed (3-0): Swift SIL preserves high-level semantics — including generic semantics, per the round-1 reading — into the optimizer so specialization can be decided there. Directly opposes the alternative's writer-side erasure of abstraction before compilation, which forecloses optimizer-decided specialization forever.

Noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-bearing contracts sound only if frontend obligations are true. Establishes that per-copy annotations under the alternative are miscompile channels when a regenerated copy drifts from its annotation; minimizing independently-asserted fact sites is a soundness argument, not ergonomics.

Negative scope: restrict-like contracts 'depend on programmer truthfulness.' With an AI writer, truthfulness sites are drift sites; interfaces reduce N trusted sites per algorithm family to one contract plus one audited impl per type.

Enumerates what non-interference facts buy (register promotion, read/write elimination, reordering) — the concrete optimizer value at stake when the trusted surface carrying those facts multiplies or drifts.

Noalias-return ('returns fresh storage disjoint from all caller-accessible storage') is exactly the kind of relational, allocation-like law naturally attached once to a Clone/Allocator-style interface contract and verified per impl — inexpressible as an isolated per-copy function annotation family without a bundling construct.

'Overflow facts are contracts, not mere annotations' — carded confirmation that law annotations feed UB-bearing optimizer promises, raising the stakes of the alternative's N-times-duplicated annotation copies.

The concurrency leading candidate's mechanism is literally trait-class: Send/Sync auto traits plus exclusive borrowing let 'the optimizer treat non-atomic memory as unshared unless types say otherwise.' The alternative's function-level annotations cannot express compositional per-type capabilities with use-site obligations; cutting interfaces strands this candidate.

Precedent that one generic body plus compiler monomorphization yields concrete-code runtime behavior — i.e., the alternative's performance outcome is achievable without source-level duplication. Its negative scope (unquantified size/compile cost) is conceded and applies to both sides.
Dynamic dispatch has optimizer-visible representation and cost and 'should be explicit when used' — supports keeping a language-known dyn construct rather than forcing writers to hand-roll erasure.

Precedent that a language can restrict dyn-compatible interfaces to keep runtime dispatch representation coherent; the round-1 leading candidate's escape hatch is defined over trait shape and is undefinable if interfaces are cut.

The shipped hybrid (specialize value types, share reference-type bodies) is a code-size-bounding option that requires a shared generic body in the artifact; writer-side concretization forecloses it permanently.

Devirtualization/IPA consume structured type and finality facts; raw fn-pointer tables (the alternative's fallback for open dispatch) do not carry the class-hierarchy/type structure this machinery needs.

Carded optimizer defeat: Function-typed fields hide representation and call targets even in closed programs — the exact degradation mode of hand-rolled dispatch under the alternative. Its negative scope (over-specialization cache pressure) is also conceded as a cost on my side.

The information demand (which operation runs here, with what properties) is invariant across designs — only the channel differs. Interfaces are the static channel that states the fact once and checks it; the alternative restates it N times unchecked.
'IR metadata is sound only if frontend semantics/proofs are sound and preserved' — corpus discipline note grounding the trusted-surface-multiplication argument.

Not a card but the standing verdict: leading candidate presupposes trait-shaped constructs (D003-style dyn, guaranteed specialization of generics); names zero quantitative evidence on the mono-vs-sharing axis (which the alternative would silently decide in extremis); names share-generics/dedup as the code-size lever, which requires shared bodies to exist; and its critique explicitly predicts hand-rolled void*/fn-pointer erasure 'that the optimizer and F003 machinery handle worse' when no sanctioned path exists.

machineWriterCosts: Alternative — token/context blowup: each polymorphic algorithm costs O(N·K) source tokens (N type-specialized copies of a K-token body) versus O(K + N impl declarations) with bounded generics; paid once at generation and again at every re-read/edit, and context-window pressure grows precisely where copies must be kept synchronized. Magnitude EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Alternative — consistency drift is undetectable by construction: no language construct ties the N copies together, so a regenerated copy with a silent semantic divergence (tie-breaking, overflow mode, NaN handling) typechecks per-copy; when its stale law annotation (associative/pure/noalias-class) still feeds the optimizer, drift converts to UB-backed miscompilation via the F003/N002 contract channels.

Alternative — compile-time and verification-time multiplication in the AI iteration loop: one algorithm change = N source edits, N frontend re-checks, and N law re-verifications (N proof obligations if laws are checked, or N trusted assertions if not — either multiplied compile time or multiplied unchecked-trust surface, both charter-adverse).

Alternative — diagnostics fragmentation: N per-copy failures with no shared cause identifier, versus one definition-site, machine-readable bound-violation diagnostic from a generic body checked once against its interface. (Uncarded; flagged.)

Feature-side (honest concession) — trait resolution/coherence machinery can itself explode compile time if unrestricted; the kept core must be decidable, coherent, and non-overlapping, with D003-style restrictions if dyn is kept. (Uncarded; based on general implementation experience, flagged.)

Feature-side (honest concession) — guaranteed monomorphization carries unquantified code-size/compile-time/cache costs (D001 and D009 negative scopes); but the alternative pays these same specialization costs hardcoded at source level while destroying the only known levers (instantiation dedup, bounded sharing, optimizer-decided specialization) for containing them.
consistencyRisks: Coupling risk (flagged by round-1 judges): the dispatch model cannot be finalized independently of aliasing/violation semantics; the open constructive check — can a shared/erased generic body soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and at what precision loss — determines whether the kept core should be guaranteed-specialization-only or admit bounded sharing. My defense survives either outcome (both branches require a generic construct present in the artifact), but the core's exact shape does not.

Cutting interfaces contradicts three round-1 leading candidates at once: dispatch-generics (D003-style explicit dyn), concurrency (C004 Send/Sync-class capability traits), and the aliasing candidate's type-system discharge of F001/F002 facts. Conversely, KEEPING interfaces must not be read as settling the mono-vs-sharing axis that round-1 explicitly left open pending measurement.

Evidence-grade asymmetry: the load-bearing D-cards (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule); F001-F004, F009, C004 and N-cards are workflow-confirmed 3-0. The dyn-construct part of my defense rests more heavily on the provisional tier than the bounded-generics part does.

Smuggled-ergonomics risk: 'reuse' per se is inadmissible; every benefit claimed here must route through verification (single audit/proof site), optimizer facts (shared body enables dedup/sharing/late specialization), or machine-writer cost (tokens, drift, loop time). Any residual benefit expressible only as convenience must be dropped from the case.

Worst-of-both trap: if the design keeps compositional capability predicates (needed by the concurrency candidate) but drops operation-bundle interfaces, the language pays most trait-machinery complexity while forfeiting the deduplication and single-audit payoffs.

Within-copy annotation-body drift exists on BOTH sides (an impl can drift from its interface's laws just as a concrete function can drift from its annotation); the defended difference is site count — one contract plus one site per impl versus N sites per algorithm family per type — not a difference in kind. If law annotations end up trusted rather than checked, this difference shrinks and the defense weakens proportionally.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI writer behavior: actual drift rates across near-duplicate copies, hallucination frequency during regeneration, token-cost magnitudes, and whether bounded generics measurably reduce AI error rates versus concrete duplication — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence.

The decisive quantitative axis: monomorphization vs shared-body vs dictionary-passing on runtime, code size, i-cache behavior, and compile time (round-1 evidence gap #1; no card quantifies any of it).
Whether a shared/erased generic body can soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations and at what precision loss — the named decisive constructive check, not yet run.

Whether writer-side full concretization is even feasible at scale for open-set/heterogeneous workloads (plugin registries, callbacks, async) without either a dyn construct or unbounded sum-type match maintenance — no evidence either way.
Trait-resolution and coherence-checking compile-time cost at machine-writer code volumes (Rust-practice anecdotes exist but nothing is carded).

Whether definition-site bound diagnostics actually shorten AI repair loops versus N instantiation-site or per-copy error floods (machine-readable-diagnostics benefit is asserted structurally, untested behaviorally).

Whether algebraic/effect law annotations will be compiler-verified (proofs/property tests) or trusted — the corpus has no purity-check evidence (round-1 cross-topic gap); the strength of the trusted-surface-multiplication argument scales directly with this unresolved design point.

Whether compiler-side instantiation dedup (rustc share-generics-class) recovers enough code size in practice to make guaranteed specialization affordable — named as a round-1 follow-up, no data gathered.

result.stance: CUT. Automatic memory management (GC/RC/ARC, and implicit drop) should not be in the core language. Explicit, compiler-checked region/arena/ownership annotations — every allocation and free visible and verifiable — carry the load. Automatic MM may exist later only as an opt-in library/runtime outside the core, re-admitted only with new evidence cards.
result.coreArgument: The null hypothesis holds because the corpus's standing rule (caveats.md: every design proposal must cite exact evidence cards) cannot be satisfied for automatic MM, while it is satisfied repeatedly for the alternative. There is not one card in the F/N/A/D/C/J series attesting that GC, RC, or implicit drop enables optimization. The only cards touching managed-runtime memory (F007, J004, J005) attest the opposite structure: recovery of allocation performance is conditional, implementation-, inlining- and profile-dependent, explicitly NOT guaranteed (Oracle states escape analysis does not replace heap with stack allocation), and paid for with warmup and code-cache memory. Per misleading-comparisons #3, I do not argue 'GC is bad for performance'; I argue the carded framing exactly: managed runtimes recover some performance CONDITIONALLY, unlike source-level guarantees — and a charter of extreme performance with a fallible writer demands guarantees, not conditions. J002 generalizes the pattern: runtime reconstruction of statically expressible facts requires guard/invalidation machinery whose cost a source-level-contract language avoids by making facts static. Object lifetime is precisely such a fact. Second, implicitness is a cost center inside the compiler itself. F009 shows that Swift — the flagship ARC language — had to UN-hide memory management to optimize it: SIL exposes retain/release as explicit instructions and Swift maintains dedicated ARC analyses/transformations, plus OSSA/non-OSSA phase complexity (caveats.md). 'Automatic' MM does not remove memory management from the compiler; it moves it into a bespoke optimizer subsystem whose whole job is clawing back costs the feature introduced. For a simple, verifiable compiler this is double machinery: the checker the aliasing model already needs, PLUS an ARC/GC optimizer, PLUS runtime support. The alternative needs only the first. Third, redundancy: the round1 aliasing verdict (the corpus's strongest outcome, design_candidate_later) found every sound non-interference variant converges on ownership-like discharge — F001/C004 are the only adversarially-verified exemplars of statically discharged non-interference and data-race freedom, and F006 (SPARK ownership-with-transfer) proves explicit checked ownership ships. Ownership/region machinery already encodes lifetime. Automatic MM would be a SECOND lifetime mechanism, hidden from the first, multiplying the open questions memory-object-model.md says block sound fact emission (it lists GC, RC, and finalizers among unanswered lifetime/invalidation questions, and its emission rule forbids lifetime.start/end or noalias without a MATCHING SOURCE CONTRACT — explicit regions/frees are that contract; a collector provides none). F004's strongest allocation fact (allocation-like noalias return) is emittable only given lifetime/provenance/escape rules — exactly what explicit annotations state and a checker discharges. A008 (Futhark uniqueness/consumption enabling copy-avoidance) is carded precedent that visible consumption of storage buys optimization. Fourth, verification: the writer is fallible at scale, and N002's lesson generalizes — facts must be contracts, not annotations. Explicit alloc/free/region-end is double-entry bookkeeping: the AI states lifetime intent, the checker verifies it, and errors surface as machine-readable compile-time diagnostics inside the iteration loop. Implicit drop and GC mask writer lifetime errors instead of rejecting them: unintended retention, cycle leaks, and allocation-in-hot-loop mistakes type-check silently and surface only as runtime profiling mysteries — the worst diagnostic channel for an AI writer (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED as to AI behavior, but the diagnostic-channel asymmetry is structural). The defense's best card is that GC removes use-after-free obligations from a fallible writer. So does the checked alternative — by rejection rather than by masking — while additionally producing the optimizer facts (F002's register promotion, load/store elimination, reordering) that the charter exists to maximize. Human ergonomics of pervasive annotations is inadmissible by ground rules; the admissible machine-writer costs are real and priced below, and none of them is carded as decisive. Verdict: no card for the feature, many cards for the alternative, and the compiler gets simpler. Cut it.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's only deep evidence on automatic-MM performance recovery: HotSpot escape analysis gives conditional allocation elimination/scalar replacement, explicitly NOT guaranteed stack allocation; negative scope says these are implementation/inlining/profile-dependent, 'not source-level guarantees'; design_generalization_confidence is low. An extreme-performance core cannot be built on conditional recovery.
Primary-vendor sharpening of F007: runtime analysis yields conditional, not guaranteed, allocation optimizations. Direct counter to the defense claim that GC costs are recovered by the runtime.
Tiered compilation shows profile-driven recovery has inherent warmup and memory costs — prices the 'runtime will recover it' strategy that automatic MM depends on.

Speculative/runtime recovery requires invalidation machinery; its positive scope names exactly these as 'costs a source-level-contract language could avoid by making facts static.' Object lifetime is such a fact; explicit regions/frees make it static.

Swift SIL exposes retain/release as explicit instructions with dedicated ARC analyses/transformations: even the flagship ARC implementation must de-automate memory management inside the compiler to optimize it. Evidence that implicit MM adds an optimizer subsystem whose job is removing the feature's own costs, and that performance-critical semantics should be visible rather than buried as implicit effects.

The adversarially-verified optimizer enablers in the corpus are language-level non-interference/ownership rules (Rust exclusivity, SPARK anti-aliasing/ownership, Fortran dummy-argument rules). The alternative's machinery is the carded enabler family; no comparable card exists for GC/RC.

The payoff classes (register promotion, read/write elimination, reordering) come from proving non-modification through other paths — facts that explicit ownership/region annotations state and a checker discharges statically.

The strongest allocation contract (allocation-like noalias return) requires lifetime/provenance/escape rules before it can be emitted (F004 negative scope). Explicit region/ownership annotations are precisely that missing source contract; a GC heap provides no source anchor for it.

SPARK's ownership policy with transfer-on-assignment and anti-aliasing is machine-checked and shipped — an existence proof that fully explicit, verifiable ownership is practical as a checked discipline, not speculation.

Type-level non-sharing (Send/Sync + exclusivity) is the corpus's attested route to letting the optimizer treat non-atomic memory as unshared. The alternative extends this; a runtime collector or shared refcounts add accessors the type system does not see (that extension is uncarded and flagged below).

Possibly-shared memory forfeits classic optimizations (no register caching across iterations, no introduced writes, motion barriers). Explicit regions keep sharing a visible, checkable fact; hidden MM machinery risks paying these costs invisibly.

Futhark's uniqueness/consumption rules let pure array updates avoid copying when the old array is provably dead — carded precedent that explicit, visible consumption-of-storage facts directly buy optimization.

nuw/nsw shows the corpus-wide pattern: optimizer facts are contracts with verifier consequences, 'not mere annotations.' Explicit free/region-end is the memory analog — a writer-stated contract the checker can falsify; implicit drop offers no writer-side statement to check against.

machineWriterCosts: Token/context cost: explicit region/arena/ownership annotations on every allocation and free increase generated and re-read token volume per function; magnitude unmeasured (no corpus card). Partially marginal rather than new, since the leading aliasing candidate (round1-aliasing-model.md) already puts ownership annotations in signatures.

Compile-time in the AI iteration loop: a region/ownership verifier adds check time to every iteration; round1-aliasing-model.md explicitly lists borrow-checker/contract-verifier compile-time cost and defect history as an uncarded evidence gap. Conceded as unpriced — but the feature's side is also unpriced (ARC optimizer passes and OSSA phases per F009/caveats.md, plus GC runtime/safepoint machinery, uncarded).

Diagnostics asymmetry (cost of the FEATURE, not the alternative): GC/RC failure modes — unintended retention, cycles, allocation in hot paths — produce no compile-time machine-readable diagnostic; they surface only at runtime, starving the AI iteration loop of the structured error signals it can act on. Explicit checked annotations convert these into compile errors with precise locations. (AI-loop effectiveness claim: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.)

Consistency drift across near-duplicates: repeated alloc/free/region blocks are a drift surface for a hallucinating writer; the alternative's stipulated checkability converts safety-relevant drift into compile errors instead of silent bugs, but drift-rate and repair-cost for AI writers are unmeasured.

Re-read/reasoning cost: with explicit lifetimes, memory behavior is locally derivable from the code in context; with GC, lifetime behavior depends on whole-program reachability the writer cannot see in-context. Directionally favors the alternative for context-bounded writers, but EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.
consistencyRisks: Checked-but-wrong consistency: the verifier proves safety, not optimality. An AI writer can consistently dump everything into one long-lived arena — it type-checks, passes every check, and silently regresses performance. The alternative needs lint/cost diagnostics for region assignment quality, which no card covers.

Annotation-spelling divergence: without compiler-enforced canonical forms, AI writers may produce many equivalent-but-different region/ownership spellings across near-duplicate code, degrading context caching and increasing drift surface (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED).

Coupling to unsettled verdicts: the cut leans on the round1 aliasing outcome (design_candidate_later, NOT decided) and on ownership machinery whose payoff magnitude and unsafe-frontier size are explicitly unmeasured. If the aliasing model shifts away from ownership-like discharge, the alternative loses its host machinery and this cut must be re-litigated. Violation-semantics and concurrency-model are still research_needed and are prior decisions (round1-index cross-topic note).

Mixed-discipline boundary risk: if automatic MM is later reintroduced as a library/opt-in runtime, two lifetime disciplines coexist, and memory-object-model.md's invalidation questions (finalizers, callbacks, FFI) reopen at that boundary — the corpus flags finalizers as fact-invalidators with no answers yet.

Escape-hatch pressure: pointer-rich structures (graphs, intrusive lists, callback-heavy code) may exceed what explicit regions/ownership can express, forcing unsafe escape hatches of unmeasured size (round1-aliasing 'frontier unmeasured' gap); heavy escape-hatch use by AI writers would erode the very consistency the checker provides.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI-writer behavior: error rates when generating ownership/region annotations at scale, drift rates across near-duplicates, and whether compile-time diagnostics beat runtime profiling signals in an AI iteration loop — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence.

Magnitude of everything: the corpus has no quantified performance deltas for any memory/lifetime regime (F002 negative scope; caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude, code size, or compile time). The prosecution's case is structural and evidential-asymmetry, not benchmarked.

GC/RC mechanism costs I deliberately did NOT lean on because they are uncarded: write/read barrier overhead, safepoint effects on optimization, RC cycle leaks, retain/release traffic sharing cache lines, moving-collector effects on interior-pointer stability (raised only as an open question in memory-object-model.md). These are background CS knowledge requiring new cards before use as load-bearing.
Region/ownership verifier compile-time cost and implementation defect history — named as an explicit evidence gap in round1-aliasing-model.md.

Frontier size: the fraction of performance-critical (AI-generated) code inexpressible under explicit regions/ownership without escape hatches — the decisive variable per the round1 critic, measured by no card.

Whether explicit source-level drop/free outperforms implicit-drop-with-explicit-IR (the Swift SIL route): F009 only evidences IR-level visibility; the source-level explicitness claim rests on the verification/double-entry argument plus untested AI-writer assumptions.
Token-overhead magnitude of pervasive annotations versus implicit MM in real generated codebases.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP (honest concession on the rest). Concede: stable internal ABI, symbol interposition, and separately-OPTIMIZED codegen units — the corpus shows these actively block the optimizer (D008, D004, D007) and no admissible argument saves them. Defend: (a) named modules with explicit, compiler-checked, contract-bearing interfaces (types + the F-card fact vocabulary: ownership, effects, aliasing obligations); (b) separate SEMANTIC compilation — per-module checking/verification against cached interfaces — plus incremental unoptimized dev-mode codegen for the AI iteration loop; (c) the module as the language's fact-boundary construct (where facts are declared to hold, attenuate, or be suppressed: FFI, unsafe, reflection restriction). Release builds remain whole-program closed-world optimization over serialized per-module typed IR (compiler-version-internal, no binary stability). The interface is a verification artifact, not a linkage contract.
result.coreArgument: The alternative ('no module system, single unit, closed world, explicit FFI boundary only') is incoherent on the corpus's own terms and loses exactly the things a fallible-at-scale machine writer needs. (1) Verification necessity: F003/F004 negative scopes make every emitted no-alias fact a UB-bearing promise 'sound only if frontend obligations are true' — there must be a locus where obligations are discharged, recorded, and sealed. F006 (SPARK, workflow-confirmed) is the precedent: facts are true by construction only because a checking toolchain enforces *declared* restrictions. A checked module interface is the declared, redundant statement of intent that makes cross-boundary verification possible at all: when the writer regenerates one side of a caller/callee pair, the unchanged interface is the independent check. In a whole-program soup with no interfaces, a coherently-hallucinated caller+callee pair regenerated together type-checks and ships — the invariant 'this function does not mutate through this parameter' is stated nowhere except the code that violates it, so it becomes uncheckable in exactly the sense the null hypothesis forbids.
