(2) The alternative smuggles the feature back in: the closed-world guarantee is itself a module-semantics invariant — the dispatch-generics verdict's leading candidate explicitly carries 'A2's no-interposition/finality MODULE semantics' as an amendment, D008's negative scope says the language 'must separately choose module/ABI/interposition rules' to license closed-world IPA, D005's negative scope says reflection metadata breaks closed-world assumptions 'unless restricted' (a per-unit visibility declaration), and the dispatch debate ruled core/periphery fact attenuation at the FFI boundary an *undesigned soundness precondition* for any closed-world commitment. The alternative's 'explicit FFI boundary' IS the hardest module machinery, unnamed. (3) Machine-writer failure modes: D004 documents that codegen units/incremental compilation exist precisely to cut compile time while whole-program LTO trades it away; D007 prices LTO/IPA in compile time and memory; D001/D009 price monomorphization/over-specialization in compile time — and the leading dispatch candidate (guaranteed static specialization) sits in the expensive regime. Single-unit whole-program compilation puts that full documented cost inside every write-check iteration of the AI loop with no incremental reuse boundary, and forces the writer's correctness-relevant context to be the whole program (or unchecked prose summaries that drift). What the optimizer needs — whole-program visibility at release — is fully preserved by my partial keep, because interfaces are serialized fact-carriers into a whole-program backend (F009 pattern: semantics preserved in IR above LLVM), not opaque ABI walls. The optimizer loses nothing; the verifier and the writer's loop gain a boundary.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Grounds my concession (semantic interposition restricts IPA/inlining — stable internal ABI must die) AND my keep: its negative scope states the source language must 'separately choose module/ABI/interposition rules', i.e., a no-interposition closed world is a module-semantics declaration, not the absence of one.

The direct tradeoff card: more separate units speed compilation but inhibit optimization; LTO enables whole-program analysis at longer link time. Proves the alternative forfeits the documented compile-time side of the tradeoff — admissible cost in the AI iteration loop — and simultaneously grounds my concession that release codegen should be whole-program.

Devirtualization/IPA/LTO gain cross-file visibility (supports closed-world release optimization) but its negative scope prices LTO/IPA in compile time, memory, and code size — the cost the alternative pays on every AI iteration instead of once at release.

Negative scope: runtime metadata/reflection 'may constrain closed-world assumptions unless restricted' — the restriction is a per-module visibility/capability declaration, requiring a module construct to state it on.

Emitted noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-bearing promises 'only sound if frontend obligations are true' — the obligation ledger needs a discharge-and-seal locus; a checked module interface is the proposed carrier.

Return-noalias demands lifetime/provenance/escape rules be discharged before emission — per-unit verification against a declared interface is where that discharge is recorded and not silently reopened by regeneration elsewhere.

Workflow-confirmed precedent that optimizer-facing facts become true by construction only via a checking toolchain enforcing declared restrictions (ownership, anti-aliasing, side-effect-free expressions) — the model for contract-bearing interfaces checked at boundaries.

Swift SIL preserves source semantics in an IR above LLVM so language-level passes see what LLVM would not — supports module interfaces as serialized typed-IR fact carriers into a whole-program backend (compiler-internal, version-locked), replacing binary ABI stability without losing the boundary.

Monomorphization has compile-time/code-size tradeoffs; the leading dispatch candidate (guaranteed static specialization) puts the language in the compile-time-expensive regime, compounding the alternative's per-iteration whole-program compile cost.

Over-specialization increases compile-time/cache pressure — same cost class, corroborating that specialization-heavy compilation without incremental unit reuse is the worst-case iteration-loop configuration.

machineWriterCosts: Compile-time explosion in the AI iteration loop: the alternative pays documented whole-program LTO/IPA compile-time and memory costs (D004, D007) compounded by guaranteed-specialization compile costs (D001, D009) on EVERY write-check cycle, with no incremental reuse boundary; the AI writer's throughput is gated by rebuild latency, and memory-heavy whole-program builds limit parallel writer agents.

Token/context blowup: with no checked interface artifact, the correctness-relevant context for regenerating any function is in principle the whole program; the writer must either re-read arbitrary amounts of code per edit or rely on unchecked summaries (comments, prose docs) that nothing keeps true. A checked interface is compression that the compiler enforces.

Silent consistency drift with unbounded blast radius: whole-program regeneration lets a coherently-hallucinated caller/callee (or N near-duplicate specializations) pass all checks because the violated invariant is stated nowhere independent of the code; module interfaces convert this to a localized boundary error.

Degraded machine-readable diagnostics: without boundaries, cross-cutting errors surface as whole-program type/verification failures pointing into a soup; boundary-anchored contract mismatches ('module X violates declared effect/aliasing contract of interface Y at obligation Z') are mechanically actionable repair targets for the writer.

Verification re-run cost: with no sealed per-unit discharge of F003/F004-class obligations, every regeneration reopens the whole obligation set; verification cost scales with program size per edit rather than with changed-module size.
consistencyRisks: Of the alternative: caller/callee (or trait-impl/use-site) pairs regenerated together can drift coherently — both sides consistent with each other and inconsistent with the rest of the system — with no second statement of intent to check against; this is the highest-severity drift mode because it is invisible to type checking.

Of the alternative: under monomorphization-only dispatch (the leading candidate), near-duplicate specialized code paths regenerated at different times can diverge semantically with no boundary at which the divergence is diagnosed.

Of my partial keep (honest): interfaces are a duplicated statement of declarations — a new drift surface and a token cost. Drift is converted into a compile error (the point of the feature), BUT a writer that mechanically 'fixes' the interface to match a hallucinated body launders drift through the checker; interface edits must therefore be a privileged, separately-gated operation, which is a policy the language must design, not get for free.

Of my partial keep: cached per-module verification results are sound only if cache keys are exact semantic fingerprints; because there is deliberately no stable internal ABI, any weaker cache key (timestamps, names) silently reuses stale discharge of F003/F004 obligations — a miscompile vector, not an ergonomics bug.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: all claims about AI writer behavior — that checked interfaces reduce required context, that boundary-localized diagnostics are more mechanically repairable by an AI than whole-program errors, that interface-as-second-statement actually catches coherent hallucination pairs at useful rates, and that interface-laundering (writer edits the interface to match a wrong body) can be policy-controlled. The corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: magnitude of the compile-time delta between whole-program single-unit builds and incremental modular checking + dev codegen — caveats.md explicitly notes documentation evidence never quantifies compile-time cost; D004/D007/D001/D009 establish direction only.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: whether module interfaces can actually carry the full fact vocabulary (ownership, effects, aliasing, shape) — the ir-strategy fit-gap matrix is entirely TBD, and no card describes a contract-bearing interface format; Swift resilience-domain/witness-table evidence is an explicitly open dossier item.

EXTRAPOLATION FLAG: F006 attests SPARK's declared restrictions + checking toolchain, but does not explicitly attest per-unit modular proof discharge; my verification-locality claim (obligations discharged once per module, re-verified only on change) extends the card beyond its text.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: whether unoptimized incremental dev builds suffice for the AI loop — if the loop requires release-quality performance feedback per iteration (plausible for an optimizer-first language), the compile-time defense weakens substantially and the whole-program alternative's cost gap narrows.
EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: net token economics — interface generation/maintenance cost vs tokens saved by not re-reading implementation bodies; unmeasured in both directions.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP (narrowed) — full rich error handling is not honestly defensible on this corpus. I concede catchable source-level exceptions as general control flow: the null hypothesis holds for them. The strongest defensible keep is a three-layer decomposition the alternative's blanket wording ('no unwinding machinery anywhere') over-cuts: (1) ALWAYS KEEP unwind metadata (tables/CFI) — required by the machine-readable-diagnostics requirement and the offline-profile channel, independent of error-handling semantics; (2) KEEP unwind execution + drop-during-unwind ONLY in checked/verification builds, as trap containment at test-harness isolation boundaries — release builds compile trap=abort with universal nounwind, so the alternative's release-mode optimizer facts are fully preserved; (3) CUT catchable exceptions from source semantics entirely — Result values remain the only source-level error channel; panics/traps are reserved for checker-inserted contract violations (bounds, overflow, asserted-contract checks) and are never catchable in release semantics, only reportable (trap-with-report, then abort).
result.coreArgument: The defense rests on verification necessity and machine-writer failure modes of the literal alternative, not on performance — because the corpus deliberately destroys BOTH classic performance arguments: the pro-exceptions claim ('zero-cost EH keeps machinery off the normal path') was adversarially REFUTED 0-3 (refuted-and-unverified.jsonl rows 1-2, needs_manual_recheck), and the anti-exceptions claim ('exceptions are always slow') is flagged as a misleading comparison (misleading-comparisons.jsonl #4: 'exception representation affects optimization and needs precise evidence'). Argument A (verification necessity): this language's premise is a writer that is fallible at scale, so checker-inserted traps firing during test/fuzz runs are the COMMON case in the iteration loop, not the rare case. Under trap=abort-everywhere, every failed check kills the process: the verification loop degrades to one machine-readable failure report per process execution, batch in-process testing and warm fuzzer state are impossible, and — critically — the failure-path cleanup of EXTERNAL state (files, shared memory, cross-process locks) becomes an unexercisable, hence unverifiable, code path; drop-during-unwind in checked builds is what makes 'cleanup runs on every exit path' a testable invariant rather than an unchecked one, and the brief says unchecked invariants are exactly what this writer cannot be trusted with. The round1 violation-semantics verdict already establishes that the ONLY carded observability story for contract violation is trap-with-report (C002, Go race-detection halts) and that its leading candidate (graduated obligations) REQUIRES a debug-checked tier — a tier whose diagnostics-per-run economics depend on containment, and whose mechanics the corpus marks uncarded; cutting all unwinding machinery now would silently decide that open question. Argument B (diagnostics/profile machinery): trap-with-report needs stack attribution and the static-vs-profile leading candidate firewalls offline profile data for cost decisions — both consume stack-walking metadata, so the alternative either re-imports unwind tables (conceding layer 1) or pays a permanent frame-pointer register cost in a language whose sole goal is extreme performance (unquantified; no card either way). Argument C (machine-writer failure mode of Result-only diagnostics): panic/unwind context (backtrace + cause chain) is runtime-generated — zero generated tokens per site, zero drift surface; Result-only context must be constructed by the AI at every propagation site, and context-dropping or context-mislabeling variants TYPE-CHECK, making this the canonical silent near-duplicate drift class the brief names admissible (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED). Argument D (representation, conditional): if unwinding exists in any build mode, F008 mandates the landingpad-style representation over funclets, and F009's SIL lesson mandates unwind edges be explicit IR structure, not hidden runtime effects; and N003 shows the speculation barrier attaches to the guarded check itself regardless of whether failure aborts or unwinds — so the marginal checked-build optimizer cost of unwinding over aborting is confined to CFG/regalloc effects (F008), while release mode pays nothing by construction. Finally, process discipline: the corpus's own effects-exceptions file (implementation/effects-exceptions-control-flow.md) instructs comparison of result types, zero-cost exceptions, funclets, and panic/abort 'without selecting a winner', and missing-research-backlog marks 'unwinding boundaries' (ffi_abi_runtime) as a high-priority missing primary source that BLOCKS design_candidate status — including for FFI, where 'no unwinding machinery anywhere' makes any foreign C++/Rust throw crossing our frames uncontainable UB with no carded mitigation. A permanent cut of all unwinding machinery therefore outruns the cards exactly as much as a permanent keep would; the narrowed keep above is the largest portion of the feature the evidence supports, and the rest should be decided by the named research items, not by fiat.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: The corpus's only verified EH card: EH representation choice is optimizer-relevant (landingpad 'strictly better for optimization' than funclets; funclets forbid register allocation of values live into/out of them). Supports (a) that unwinding cost is a representation variable, not a constant, so the alternative's implicit premise 'unwinding = fixed optimizer tax' is uncarded; (b) the conditional design rule: if unwinding exists in checked builds, mandate landingpad-style form; (c) honestly, it also supports confining unwind execution out of release builds — which the narrowed keep does. Note its design_generalization_confidence is LOW.

Both broad LLVM EH claims ('zero-cost model keeps machinery off normal path'; 'invoke not call for throwing calls') were refuted 0-3 with needs_manual_recheck. This removes the classic pro-exceptions performance defense from my side — forcing the defense onto verification/diagnostics grounds — AND caveats.md requires these refutations be preserved so overbroad claims are not reintroduced; the prosecution may not assume the negation either.

'Exceptions are always slow' is a flagged misleading claim; preferred wording is 'exception representation affects optimization and needs precise evidence.' This is the corpus directly forbidding the prosecution's strongest rhetorical move and marking the performance question as an evidence hole, not a settled loss for the feature.

Go's race-detection halts are the only carded precedent for trap-with-report — the observability mechanism the round1 violation-semantics verdict calls the only post-violation diagnosis story in the corpus. Machine-readable trap reports require stack attribution, i.e., unwind metadata (layer 1 of the keep), even under trap=abort semantics.

Guarded potentially-failing operations (div-by-zero example) block speculation/hoisting regardless of failure semantics. Neutralizes the anti-unwind code-motion argument: the speculation barrier is a property of having runtime checks at all — shared by both designs — so the marginal optimizer cost of checked-build unwinding over checked-build aborting is limited to the F008 CFG/regalloc effects, and release-mode cost is zero under the narrowed keep (universal nounwind).

SIL lesson: performance-critical semantics must be visible IR structure, not hidden runtime effects. Conditional design constraint for the kept layer: checked-build unwind paths must be explicit, verifier-checkable edges in the ir-strategy candidate IR — and it argues against any design where cleanup-on-failure happens through an opaque runtime mechanism the optimizer and verifier cannot see.

'ABI, unwinding boundaries, GC statepoints, stack maps...' is marked missing_primary_source, priority high, and explicitly 'blocks moving this area from research_only to design_candidate.' The corpus's own gate says a permanent 'no unwinding machinery anywhere' decision cannot be made before this card exists — foreign unwinds crossing our frames (C++/Rust panic=unwind libraries) have no carded containment story under the alternative.

The corpus's own scoping note: seed evidence confirms ONLY that EH representation affects optimization plus the two refutations, and instructs comparing result types, zero-cost exceptions, funclets, panic/abort 'without selecting a winner.' Supports research_needed for the layers I do not concede, and blocks the prosecution from claiming the corpus has decided for Result-only.

The leading violation-semantics candidate (graduated obligations) requires a debug-checked tier with dynamic checks and names trap-with-report as the only observability story; its evidence gaps list 'checked-tier mechanics uncarded' and 'post-deployment observability' as open. Cutting all unwinding machinery now would pre-decide those open questions against the leading candidate's checked tier; the narrowed keep is the containment mechanism that tier needs to produce more than one diagnostic per process death.

Leading candidate firewalls offline profile data (sample-based PGO/AutoFDO/BOLT named in follow-ups) for cost decisions. Sample-based profiling consumes stack-walking machinery; the alternative must either keep unwind tables (conceding layer 1) or pay universal frame pointers — a real register cost in an extreme-performance language. Flagged: the 'sampling needs unwind tables or frame pointers' link is a well-known implementation fact but is itself uncarded in this corpus.

machineWriterCosts: COST OF ALTERNATIVE — single-diagnostic-per-execution verification loop: under trap=abort everywhere, the first failed checker-inserted trap kills the test/fuzz process; the AI iteration loop gets one machine-readable failure per run, loses in-process test batching and warm fuzzer/corpus state, and pays process-respawn latency per defect. With a fallible-at-scale writer, trap firing is the common case, so this cost multiplies across the whole loop. (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED magnitude; mechanism concrete — cf. existing toolchains where panic=abort disables multi-failure test reporting, uncarded.)

COST OF ALTERNATIVE — per-site diagnostic context generation and drift: Result-only error context (what failed, where, cause chain) must be constructed by the AI at every propagation site; context-dropping/mislabeling variants type-check, making them silent near-duplicate drift — exactly the hallucination-across-copies failure mode the brief names admissible. Runtime-carried panic context (backtrace + cause chain) is by-construction: zero generated tokens per site, zero drift surface. (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED rates.)

COST OF ALTERNATIVE — hidden re-import of unwind machinery: machine-readable trap reports (required) and sample-based profile collection (static-vs-profile leading candidate) both need stack attribution; the alternative either keeps unwind tables anyway — conceding the machinery it claims to delete — or pays a permanent frame-pointer register cost in release code (unquantified, no card).

COST OF ALTERNATIVE — unverifiable failure-path cleanup: abort-on-trap makes the failure-path cleanup of external state (files, shared segments, cross-process locks) an unexercisable code path; the invariant 'cleanup runs on every exit path' becomes unchecked, which the brief says is intolerable for this writer. Drop-during-unwind in checked builds makes it a testable obligation.

COST OF KEPT FEATURE (conceded) — dual-mode semantics: checked-unwind vs release-abort is exactly the debug/release divergence risk round1-violation-semantics flags as real and unsized; the AI writer and the verifier must reason about two lowerings, and divergence-escape rates are unevidenced.

COST OF KEPT FEATURE (conceded) — compile-time and artifact size in the loop: building and testing two modes, plus unwind-table/CFI emission (object size, link time), are unquantified costs paid inside the AI iteration loop; no corpus card prices them.

COST OF KEPT FEATURE (conceded) — token/context cost of trap-reachability: with any unwinding tier, the reading model must know what can trap inside callees; mitigation is a statically enumerable per-function 'can-trap' effect bit in machine-readable interface metadata (checker-inserted traps only, since user exceptions are cut), but that metadata channel is itself uncarded.
consistencyRisks: Violation-semantics coupling (alignment, but fragile): the narrowed keep implements the leading candidate's debug-checked tier and its trap-with-report observability story — but that candidate is itself research_needed and its checked-tier lowering mechanics (trap intrinsics, check+flag co-emission soundness) are uncarded; if round 2 rejects graduated obligations, layer 2 of my keep loses its consumer.

Cross-mode semantic equivalence: transforms legal under release nounwind are illegal in checked builds with unwind edges; defects may reproduce in only one mode. No card measures divergence-escape rates; this is the strongest standing objection to my own position.

Concurrency-model coupling: any checked-build unwinding forces a panic-across-threads policy (lock poisoning or equivalent) that the C004-style sharing-capability candidate must specify; under release=abort the risk is confined to checked builds but does not vanish from the language definition.

Aliasing-model coupling (design_candidate_later): drop-during-unwind creates implicit use points; drop order under unwind must be shown to respect exclusive-mutation/ownership invariants, adding a proof obligation to the corpus's best-attested win (F001/F006) — uncarded.

IR-strategy coupling: the verifier-checked IR candidate must carry mode-conditional unwind edges structurally (F009 lesson); functions having different CFGs per build mode complicates 'facts survive lowering' validation, which round1-ir-strategy already lists as unvalidated end-to-end.

Target split: F008's funclet constraints mean checked-build unwinding is more expensive on Windows-style EH targets than Itanium-style ones — checked-tier economics become target-dependent, and the corpus has no non-LLVM/non-Windows EH evidence at all.

Trap-vs-Result boundary drift: with two failure channels (Result for expected errors, trap for contract violations), nothing in the type system forces the AI to classify the same condition consistently across modules; a language-enforced classification rule is needed and is uncarded.
empiricalUntested: All claims about AI-writer behavior herein are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence cards (confirmed: no such series exists in notes/ or synthesis/).

That checker-inserted traps fire frequently enough during AI-driven test/fuzz iteration for in-process containment (unwind-to-harness-boundary) to materially beat process-respawn on wall-clock per detected defect — the single most decision-capable missing measurement for this debate.

That AI-generated per-site Result context code drifts (type-correct context loss/mislabeling) at rates high enough to matter, versus runtime-carried panic context; needs a controlled generation study.
That dual-mode (checked-unwind / release-abort) semantics causes AI writers to ship mode-divergent defects at meaningful rates (the standing objection to my position is as untested as my position).

The refuted zero-cost-EH claims' underlying question — actual happy-path and code-size cost of unwind tables + landingpad-style EH vs Result-return propagation vs trap=abort on representative kernels — refuted-as-worded 0-3 with needs_manual_recheck; a three-way benchmark is required before any performance claim in either direction is admissible.

That sample-based profiling for the firewalled offline profile channel specifically requires unwind tables versus frame pointers on target platforms, and the register/performance cost of the frame-pointer alternative — well-known implementation folklore, uncarded here.

FFI unwind-boundary behavior (foreign throws crossing no-unwind frames, catch-all shims at boundaries, cost thereof) — flagged by missing-research-backlog ffi_abi_runtime as missing_primary_source and blocking design_candidate status.
Whether AI writers keep a two-channel failure taxonomy (Result vs trap) consistent across a large codebase without a language-enforced classification rule.

result.optimizerRole: Net negative-to-neutral, and therefore insufficient for core inclusion. The corpus's only direct card on function values is a defeat card: D009 documents Function-typed fields hiding representation/call targets and forcing dynamic dispatch even inside fully closed programs — the round1-dispatch-generics judge used it against the closed-world advocate, and line 52 of that verdict records first-class-function dispatch as a gap no position covered. General closures are implicit members of D002's indirect-call cost class (lookup cost, prevents inlining), recoverable only via D007's conditional/speculative/revertible devirtualization, blocked by D008 interposition, with environment allocation recoverable only via F007/J004's conditional (explicitly not guaranteed) escape analysis, and with J001/J002 showing the profiling/guard/deopt machinery a runtime needs once call targets are values. The defender's counter-asymmetry (guaranteed direct calls via closures-as-unique-static-types) is real but not distinctive: its 'exactly two realizable encodings' claim for the alternative is FALSE given the dispatch-generics leading candidate — an explicit env struct passed as a type parameter implementing a call interface, monomorphized per D001, yields the identical guaranteed-direct-call, exact-layout machine code. The restricted closure IS compiler-performed defunctionalization; the optimizer sees no difference. So closures add no optimizer capability the mandated alternative lacks, while unrestricted closures add D009-class defeats and an all-TBD verified-lowering obligation (llvm-mlir-capability-map closure-capture row). The defender's minimal-footprint argument (capture lists feeding F001/F002/F003 facts) is partially valid — exact capture lists guarantee footprint minimality where env structs make it a convention — but footprint SOUNDNESS is equal in both designs under the checked-ownership aliasing leader, minimality-payoff magnitude is uncarded, and misleading-comparisons #2 flags callback lowering to noalias as unresolved for every design. Per decision-gates.md, even optimizer-attractiveness would not suffice for a default; closures do not clear the lower bar.
result.verificationRole: This is the genuine contest, and closures' one real contribution must be reconstructed in the alternative rather than used to keep capture semantics. The defender's strongest point: mandatory explicit capture lists give a bidirectional exactness check (no unlisted free variable, no unused capture) plus by-name binding at the creation site, whereas the manual triple (struct definition, population site, function body) has no native correspondence check — a stale or over-wide env struct type-checks. But the defender's killer instance, snapshot-vs-live, is SYMMETRIC on inspection: under the ownership-based aliasing leader, an env-struct field's declared type (owned T vs &T vs &mut T) carries exactly the same declared, compiler-checked capture-mode information as an explicit capture-mode annotation; the wrong choice type-checks in both surfaces, so 'the intent is no longer declared anywhere' is overstated. The recoverable residue is therefore: (1) exactness — must become mandated core ERRORS over env structs (unused field, uninitialized field, dead dispatch variant, non-exhaustive dispatch), not lints, as the prosecutor conceded; (2) by-name binding — population-site wrong-referent wiring (.x = wrong_but_type-compatible_var) remains checkable only by type, not by name, in the alternative; this is the one verification property that cannot be fully reconstructed and is the strongest surviving argument for a later checked-desugaring layer, pending the named experiment. Cutting the feature also DELETES verification obligations: capture inference, closure conversion as a semantics-preservation proof burden in the verifier-checked IR strategy, and the entirely-TBD capability-map row; F004's negative scope (lifetime/provenance/escape rules precede strong allocation facts) says anonymous escaping environments are precisely the hard case the memory-object model would otherwise have to settle first. C004 Send/Sync checks apply to env structs as ordinary structs with no new machinery.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: All AI-writer behavioral claims on both sides are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence (caveats.md). Ledger as judged: (1) Token/context cost — the alternative loses: each function value becomes struct + top-level fn + construction wiring with globally unique names, and re-reading a call site requires pulling nonlocal definitions into context; conceded, admissible under the charter, magnitude unmeasured. (2) Consistency drift — the alternative's most serious cost, but the mandated diagnostics (see requirements) convert missing-field/arity/stale-shape drift into machine-readable compile errors; the residual silent channels are population-site wrong-referent (type-compatible but wrong variable) and snapshot-vs-live field-type choice — the latter symmetric with capture-mode choice under closures, the former genuinely worse without by-name binding. (3) Edit amplification — the defender's central-enum objection is valid against tag-enum defunctionalization but not against the mandated generic-parameter encoding, which is the required hot-path form; tag enums are confined to genuinely heterogeneous stored-callback sets, where the defender itself concedes closed enums beat dyn function values for optimizer and verifier alike. (4) Diagnostics — env-struct errors are ordinary struct/ownership errors the core must emit anyway; capture diagnostics would entangle with an aliasing model that has no carded callback story (round1-aliasing-model line 52). (5) Distribution shift — AI writers trained on closure-rich corpora may emit lambda-shaped code or defunctionalize incorrectly; symmetric risk (wrong captures that type-check in a capture-bearing core) and untested in both directions. Compile-time in the iteration loop: unmeasured, plausibly a wash (more source volume vs deleted capture-inference/closure-conversion/escape-analysis phases).
whatMustBeTrue: The language MUST provide the guaranteed-direct-call encoding: env structs passable as type parameters implementing a call interface, monomorphized under the dispatch-generics leading candidate (D001), so parameterized-algorithm callbacks are never forced through fn-pointer+env indirect calls; the dispatch leader must itself survive its verification program (F003-discharge check, code-size measurement).

The language MUST mandate machine-readable core ERRORS (not lints) reconstructing capture-list exactness over the manual artifacts: unused env-struct field, field used but never initialized, dead dispatch variant, non-exhaustive tag dispatch, signature mismatch between env struct and consuming function — with diagnostics that name all three artifacts (struct, function, construction site) so the AI repair loop is single-hop.

The language MUST make the snapshot-vs-live choice explicit and coercion-free: env-struct field types (owned vs shared-borrow vs exclusive-borrow) are the declared capture modes, checked by the ownership core (C004/F001 machinery), with no implicit copy/borrow coercion at population sites.

Escaping and heterogeneous function values route ONLY through the explicit, restricted D003-style dynamic construct (or closed tag-enums with exhaustiveness checking); Function-typed fields with hidden call targets (D009's defeat shape) must be inexpressible in the core.

Structured-array body regions (A002/A006/A010 surface) must be specified as second-class, non-escaping, capture-free-or-explicitly-parameterized regions, with the closure/region line drawn precisely so later rounds cannot smuggle first-class capture back in through loop bodies.

The toolchain must enforce single-definition discipline for repeated callback patterns (dedup detection of structurally identical env-struct/function pairs across modules) to contain cross-module duplication drift.

The aliasing model must acquire a carded story for borrows stored in env structs lowering to F003 noalias/alias.scope facts — misleading-comparisons #2 names callbacks as the fact-destroyer, and this verdict inherits that unresolved obligation.

The load-bearing provisional cards D001, D002, D007, D009 must survive adversarial verification (caveats.md standing rule); if D009 or the D007-conditionality asymmetry falls, the cost side of this ruling must be re-weighed.

The AI-writer drift experiment (see follow-ups) must not show a dominant silent-semantic-divergence rate for mandated-diagnostic defunctionalization versus checked capture-list closures; if it does, a capture-list desugaring layer that compiles to exactly the mandated encoding should be added — reversing the surface decision without touching this ruling's core semantics.
result.rationale: The null hypothesis stands, but only because the alternative is promoted to a language requirement. On the evidence: the closures/captures coverage row rests on a single card, D009, at low_to_medium confidence — and D009 is an optimizer-defeat card; the llvm-mlir capability map's closure-capture row is all TBD; no F/A/C/J card attests any benefit of capture semantics; and decision-gates.md bars admitting features on optimizer-attractiveness alone, a bar closures do not even reach. Both design_candidate_later leaders are structurally hostile: the dispatch leader admits exactly one implicit path (guaranteed static specialization) plus an explicit restricted dyn construct, and the aliasing leader has no carded callback story at all (round1-aliasing-model line 52; misleading-comparisons #2). The defender's case is the strongest possible for the feature and it still fails at its load-bearing joint: the claim that the alternative has only indirect encodings (D002/D007/D008 cost class) or tag-enum apply loops is refuted by composition with the corpus's own leading generics candidate — an explicit env struct passed as a monomorphized type parameter is machine-code-isomorphic to the defender's 'closures as unique anonymous types consumed by specializing generics.' The defended restricted form IS compiler-performed defunctionalization; since the optimizer cannot distinguish them, optimizer necessity is not proven, and the defense's real content is (a) requirements the alternative must satisfy (guaranteed direct calls via the generic encoding, checked capture-mode declaration, non-escaping guarantees by construction rather than F007/J004-conditional recovery) and (b) a verification residue (bidirectional exactness, by-name binding) plus EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED drift-rate claims that cannot overcome the null hypothesis but do define the reopening experiment. The defender's killer snapshot-vs-live instance is symmetric once env-field types are recognized as declared capture modes under the ownership core; the prosecutor's 'every drift mode is a compile error' is likewise overstated (population-site wrong-referent and field-type choice type-check), which is exactly why this is replace_with_alternative rather than cut_from_core: a bare cut leaving fn-pointer+env as the idiomatic encoding would reinstate D009's defeat shape everywhere, and a cut without mandated exactness diagnostics would hand a fallible-at-scale writer the three-artifact drift channels unchecked. Cutting deletes real obligations — capture inference, closure conversion as a verified-IR proof burden, the TBD lowering row, and the F004-flagged anonymous-escaping-environment complication to the memory-object model — while the escaping/heterogeneous residue lands in the already-planned explicit dynamic construct, where the defender itself concedes closed tag-enums serve optimizer and verifier better than erased function values. This verdict is evidence-conditional per the standing rule: every load-bearing D-card is a provisional direct extraction, and the named experiment can reopen the surface (not the semantics) as a checked desugaring layer.
followUps: Run the decisive AI-writer experiment (currently zero AI-codegen evidence in corpus): same task suite, same model, comparing (a) manual defunctionalization with the mandated exactness diagnostics vs (b) explicit-capture-list closures desugaring to identical IR; measure silent-semantic-divergence rate (especially snapshot-vs-live and wrong-referent wiring), fraction of drift caught at compile time, repair-loop length per diagnostic class, and tokens generated/re-read. Pre-commit a threshold at which the desugaring layer is added.

Execute round1-dispatch-generics follow-up line 68 as amended by this ruling: add a design position and evidence cards for function-value dispatch, now scoped to the mandated encoding (env-struct type parameters, explicit dyn residue, tag-enum heterogeneous sets).

Run the F003-discharge construction check for the explicit dynamic construct and tag-enum apply bodies (named-but-unexecuted follow-up from round1-dispatch-generics line 59) — it arbitrates the footprint-precision residue of the defender's case.
Adversarially verify D001, D002, D007, D009 per the caveats.md standing rule before further weight is placed on this verdict.

Produce the aliasing-model callback card: how ownership-checked borrows stored in env structs lower to noalias/alias.scope, resolving misleading-comparisons #2 for the mandated encoding; treat as a blocking prerequisite alongside memory-object-model.md's open questions.

Specify the non-escaping body-region construct for the structured-arrays surface (A002/A006/A010) and document the precise closure/region boundary as a spec artifact, so later rounds cannot reintroduce capture semantics through loop bodies.

Replace the llvm-mlir capability map's all-TBD 'closure capture' row with rows for the mandated encoding (env-struct generic specialization, explicit dyn construct, tag-enum dispatch), each with preconditions, IR constructs, consuming passes, and verifier checks.

Stress-test workload coverage: generate representative async-continuation, event-callback, and stateful-iterator-adapter code under the mandated encoding and measure env-struct proliferation and cross-module duplication, testing the prosecutor's untested claim that structured ops plus top-level references cover the closure workload.
Design the dedup/single-definition toolchain check for structurally identical env-struct/function pairs across modules, and measure its false-positive rate on generated code.

result.optimizerRole: Dynamic dispatch is a carded optimization defeat, not an enabler: D002 documents vtable lookup cost and blocked method inlining; every recovery path in evidence is conditional, revertible, and option-dependent (D007 negative scope, D008 interposition), matching the corpus-wide pattern that recovery-by-optimization is never a guarantee (F007, J004, J002). The round1 leading candidate concedes the point by stating a dyn cost model that does not rely on devirtualization — pricing dyn as a permanent indirect call. D009 establishes that function-valued indirection is the cost driver regardless of language packaging, so a dyn construct buys zero performance floor over a typed function-pointer table at genuinely dynamic sites, while the alternative keeps every closed-set site on the corpus's best-attested mechanism: D001 guaranteed-static, per-arm direct, inlinable calls that preserve F003 per-callee noalias discharge against named targets. Dyn's only residual optimizer value — carrying structured hierarchy/finality facts (D006) for D007-class devirtualization — is an upside the candidate's own cost model disclaims and LLVM WPD is uncarded; a feature cannot be kept on an optimization channel its own cost model refuses to price. The prosecutor's F009 turn is legitimate: explicit switches and explicit typed tables are the maximally optimizer-visible dispatch representation; compiler-synthesized vtables behind an abstraction are the less visible one.
result.verificationRole: This is where the case is actually decided, and it splits. Against dyn: a dyn call site is a universally quantified obligation over an open implementer set; adding an implementer triggers no site-level diagnostic — silent behavioral extension is the worst failure mode for an unattended fallible writer. A switch is a syntactically closed callee set: exhaustiveness is machine-checked, contracts are checkable per named target, and variant-set growth produces a machine-readable, site-enumerated edit list — exactly the diagnostic channel the charter demands. For the defense: the BARE alternative manufactures three unchecked-invariant classes the charter forbids — (a) data/table coupling (Circle payload with Square table row: well-typed wrong behavior or UB over void*), (b) cross-site semantic agreement (K exhaustive-but-divergent switch sites; dyn makes one-type-one-behavior structural via a single impl), (c) F003 noalias obligations become unverifiable once payloads exit the ownership checker via erasure — and the round1 critique itself carded hand-rolled void*/fn-pointer erasure as the predicted failure mode of an unmitigated ban (A1 weakness 4). Ruling: the defender's attacks defeat the bare cut, not the checked cut. Every hole is closed by requirements (named typed table fields, construction-checked coupling, payloads inside the ownership discipline, banned wildcard arms, single-point-of-definition tables), and the residual existential arises only at genuinely open implementer sets, which a closed-world core excludes by construction — sum types make sets closed; open sets belong to the undesigned periphery (D008 class), where the dyn question is remanded, not settled.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: All claims here are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence. Generation cost favors dyn: O(1) call and O(1) impl edit versus O(N) arms per site and O(K) site regenerations on variant addition; K×N near-duplicate arms are the canonical drift substrate. Re-read cost favors the alternative: a switch enumerates its full target set at the site, while a dyn site forces retrieval of every implementer into context to reason about behavior. Compile time in the iteration loop favors the alternative for stated performance (no LTO/WPD/speculative machinery needed; D004/D007 taxes avoided), though variant-set changes invalidate K units versus one (D004) — mitigated by canonical tables. Diagnostics decisively favor the alternative for a fallible writer: exhaustiveness failures are local, enumerable, mechanically executable to-do lists; dyn's failure modes (new implementer silently absorbed, wrong registration, stale override) produce no compile-time signal at all, and D003-class dyn-compatibility errors are nonlocal trait-level diagnostics over a larger rule surface. The drift risks are symmetric, not one-sided: the alternative risks arm-body divergence across sites (addressed by the required single-point-of-definition tables); dyn risks impl-vs-contract divergence and zero-edit silent extension. Since neither side's writer-behavior claims are measured and the risks roughly offset, the null hypothesis breaks the tie against the feature.
whatMustBeTrue: Sum types with compiler-checked exhaustive dispatch are core, and wildcard/default arms are banned (or require an explicit audited opt-out) in checked dispatch contexts, so variant addition fails closed at every site rather than being silently swallowed.

Typed operation tables are a blessed, verifier-supported language form: per-operation NAMED fields (index-addressed/positional tables prohibited), per-row signature checking, and table payloads that remain inside the ownership/F003 discipline — void*/untyped erasure has no sanctioned core form. This discharges the defender's coupling and F003-erasure attacks, which are valid against a bare cut.

Table/payload coupling is checked at construction: tables are built per variant through a checked constructor form (or as records of closures capturing typed payloads), so pairing payload X with table Y is a type error, never a latent runtime miscall.

Single-point-of-definition consistency checking of repeated code: one canonical table per (variant-set, operation) with a compiler-enforced one-definition-per-(variant, operation) rule; ad-hoc switch sites over the same variant set either lower to canonical-table lookups or are flagged. This converts the defender's strongest surviving structural point (cross-site arm-body drift is invisible to exhaustiveness checking) into a checked invariant instead of a reason to keep dyn.

On any variant-set change, the compiler emits a machine-readable, site-enumerated edit list (file, site, missing arm/row) as a mechanical repair plan — the diagnostic contract that makes O(K) propagation executable by a fallible writer.

First-class typed function values/closures remain core (both sides assume them; D009 makes their cost class explicit). It must be acknowledged that a closure is a degenerate single-method existential, so a record-of-closures is the sanctioned residual idiom for open-ish dispatch; its cost versus a fat-pointer trait object (N env+code pointers vs 1+1) is uncarded and must be measured, since this is the crack through which restricted dyn could legitimately re-enter.

The core is closed-world for dispatch: genuinely open implementer sets (dynamic linking, FFI callbacks, D008-class interposition boundaries) live in the periphery, whose fact-attenuation design round1 already lists as an undesigned precondition. The periphery design must revisit checked open-set dispatch and MAY reinstate a restricted, D003-style dyn construct there; this verdict cuts it from the core only.

The F003 noalias/alias.scope discharge-through-indirect-bodies construction check (round1's flagged decisive follow-up) is run against LLVM semantics; it prices indirection for tables and closures just as it would have for dyn, and its result becomes an evidence card constraining the table mechanism's alias-fact story.
result.rationale: The null hypothesis holds, but only because the alternative is promoted to a language requirement; a bare cut would fail the charter. (1) Optimizer necessity is not proven: D002 cards dyn as a documented optimization defeat; all recovery is conditional and revertible (D007/D008, pattern-matched by F007/J004/J002); the round1 leading candidate's own dyn cost model disclaims devirtualization, pricing dyn as a permanent indirect call; and D009 shows indirection itself is the cost driver, so the construct adds zero performance over checked tables at open sites while closed sites are strictly better served by D001-path exhaustive switches with direct, per-arm-inlinable calls preserving F003 per-callee discharge. LLVM WPD — the defense's only unpriced upside — has no evidence card. (2) Verification necessity half-succeeds and that half is absorbed as requirements: the defender correctly shows the unchecked alternative manufactures three invariant classes (payload/table coupling, cross-site arm agreement, F003 on erased payloads) that a fallible writer cannot be trusted to keep, and the round1 critique itself carded hand-rolled void* erasure as the predicted failure mode of an unmitigated ban. But every hole closes under the required checked forms, and the defender's convergence argument (the checked alternative IS the feature) only bites at genuinely open implementer sets — which a closed-world core excludes by construction via sum types, remanding open-set dispatch to the undesigned periphery (D008) where restricted dyn may yet be reinstated. Meanwhile the alternative wins the charter-weighted diagnostics contest outright: exhaustiveness violations and variant-addition edit lists are local, enumerable, machine-readable repair plans, whereas dyn silently absorbs new implementers with no site-level re-verification trigger — the worst failure mode for an unattended writer. (3) Compiler simplicity: cutting dyn removes the D003 rule subsystem, vtable synthesis, fat pointers, and unsizing coercions from the trusted core, and D003's exclusion of generic dispatchable methods means keeping dyn would not even close the heterogeneous-generic-API hole — sum-type dispatch closes it better, since switch arms call generic per-variant code freely. (4) Why not needs_evidence: the untested empirics (open-site prevalence, drift rates, O(K)-edit magnitude) do not arbitrate between the designs — the F003-discharge check prices indirection identically for both once erasure is banned, and the writer-drift risks are symmetric (K×N arm drift vs silent-extension/impl-contract drift) — so under the standing evidence rule ties break to the null. The verdict is decisive on today's cards and names its reopening conditions: the periphery design, the record-of-closures cost measurement, and first AI-codegen drift data.
followUps: Run the round1-flagged construction check: can shared/erased/indirect callee bodies soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations against LLVM semantics, and at what precision loss versus direct per-variant code; record the result as an evidence card (it prices indirection for tables, closures, and any future periphery dyn alike).

Produce the first AI-codegen evidence cards: measure writer drift and repair success on (a) compiler-edit-list-guided O(K) switch-site updates after variant addition versus (b) O(1) dyn-style single-impl edits, and (c) arm-body divergence rates across K duplicate switch sites with and without the canonical-table one-definition rule.

Measure prevalence: in representative AI-written extreme-performance codebases, what fraction of dispatch sites have closed, write-time-enumerable target sets — the load-bearing assumption that switches, not tables, carry most weight.

Build the LLVM whole-program devirtualization dossier (!type metadata, -fwhole-program-vtables, virtual-function elimination) with version-dated sources — the uncarded mechanism behind any future case for reinstating dyn.

Design the core/periphery fact-attenuation mechanism for FFI/dynamic-linking boundaries (D008 class) and, within it, the checked open-set dispatch construct; explicitly re-try the restricted D003-style dyn question at that boundary, where write-time enumeration is genuinely impossible.

Resolve the closure/first-class-function dispatch gap (D009; round1 lists it as unaddressed by every position): specify the record-of-closures idiom as the residual open-ish dispatch form and measure its cost (size, indirection count, i-cache) against a fat-pointer trait object — the honest test of the defender's convergence argument.

Quantitative measurement with pre-committed thresholds: exhaustive switch versus indirect table call versus (periphery) vtable call on runtime, code size, i-cache, and branch-predictor behavior at varying arity — the corpus has zero quantitative dispatch cards (caveats.md).

Adversarially verify the load-bearing provisional cards D001, D002, D003, D007, D009 per the caveats.md standing rule before this verdict hardens into a spec; this ruling inherits their provisionality.

result.optimizerRole: None carded. Every confirmed optimizer enabler in the corpus is semantic — aliasing/non-interference (F001, F002, F003), ownership (F006), numeric modes (N002), specialization/compile-time channels (D001, D004), memory/layout facts (A001) — and no card attributes any performance benefit to infix, precedence, sugar, or elision. Surface notation is erased at parse; both grammars yield the identical checked tree, so cutting notation forfeits zero optimization power. The corpus explicitly deferred syntax (source-to-ir-lowering-sketches.md: 'No syntax decisions yet'; caveats.md: scaffolding must not decide source syntax; A001 negative scope: 'Does not decide source syntax'). If anything, the corpus's practice points toward the alternative: N006 shows optimizer infrastructure already operates on single canonical forms, and F009's lesson is that performance-critical structure should be visible to tools rather than buried in implicit forms.
result.verificationRole: Negative for the feature, positive for the alternative — and verification is the decisive axis here. Every desugaring, precedence-resolution, and elision-inference rule is frontend transformation surface whose semantics-preservation must be proven and maintained; F003's negative scope (facts sound only if frontend obligations are true and survive lowering), the misleading-comparisons entry ('IR metadata solves source design' is misleading because metadata is sound only if frontend semantics are sound), open-questions.md's historical-failure list ('unsound frontend metadata emission'), and round1-index.md's cross-topic gap (no prototype validation that emitted facts survive lowering) all mark the frontend as the pipeline's soundness chokepoint. N006's Alive2 discipline implies each desugaring rewrite demands proof-grade validation; a zero-sugar core incurs zero such obligations. F006 (SPARK) is direct precedent for restricting surface expressiveness to keep the source-to-logic mapping simple. Additionally, elision of contract-bearing facts (aliasing/ownership per F001/F003, numeric modes per N002, effects, boundary types) would either destroy the explicit contract channel or force unverified re-inference — forbidden under the writer-fallibility premise. The canonical alternative makes the stored artifact identical to the checked tree and enables a checksum channel: writer-stated inferable facts cross-checked against inference, mismatch = hard error.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: All AI-writer behavioral claims on both sides are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence cards, and the null-hypothesis win on syntax is procedural (syntax was out of scope by selection), not empirical. Verified in this hearing: the defender's token-cost proxy reproduces (S-expression forms cost 1.35–1.48x proxy tokens and 1.93x characters vs infix on six expression-dense kernel fragments; script at /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-bytedance-Dev-xlang/035e62ef-9ecd-42d5-b288-f6f92231c257/scratchpad/tokcount.py) — but this is a regex proxy on fragments, not real BPE on whole programs, and declaration/control-flow-heavy code will inflate less. The two load-bearing untested hypotheses cut in opposite directions: (a) transcription-distance errors — converting infix spec formulas to prefix form induces silent operand-order/associativity errors that type-check and are invisible to the verifier; (b) precedence misbinding — writers trained on C-family corpora silently misparse their own infix under a misremembered precedence table, an error class canonical form eliminates by construction. Neither rate is measured; the codec question hinges on their difference. Uncontested machine-writer wins for canonical form: byte-detectable consistency drift across near-duplicates, deterministic O(n) parsing with local error reporting, node-path machine-readable diagnostics, content-addressed caching in the iteration loop (compile-time is a carded cost channel, D001/D004). Training-distribution penalty for an unfamiliar canonical form is an admissible, unquantified cost.
whatMustBeTrue: The language defines exactly one canonical surface form: one spelling per semantic construct, one legal byte-level formatting, grammar tree isomorphic to the checked core representation; non-canonical input is a hard compiler error, not auto-formatted.

Consistency-checking of repeated code is a first-class toolchain obligation, not a passive benefit: content-addressed subtree hashing, duplicate detection, and drift reports across near-identical AI-generated blocks must be emitted by the compiler, since byte-canonical form only pays off if the toolchain actually surfaces drift.

Contract-bearing facts are never elidable in the stored artifact: aliasing/ownership (F001/F003), numeric mode selection (N002 — each arithmetic node names exactly one mode), effects, and types at function/module boundaries are always explicit; any compiler-inferred fact is immediately re-elaborated into the stored canonical form.

Writer-stated inferable facts are legal and cross-checked against inference with mismatch as a hard error (redundancy as hallucination checksum), replacing the verification value that elision would have silently discarded.
Diagnostics are machine-readable node paths into the canonical artifact the writer emitted, with deterministic local parse-error recovery — no resugaring machinery, no heuristic cascade errors.

Parse/typecheck/verification results are content-addressed and cached on canonical bytes, converting the surface decision into iteration-loop latency wins (the carded compile-time channel, D001/D004) that offset the measured token-verbosity cost.

The canonical grammar is designed to minimize gratuitous verbosity without adding alternate spellings (short fixed operator atoms, flat n-ary nodes for provably associative same-mode operations), directly attacking the one measured cost of the cut.

The cut is policed at the toolchain boundary: no pretty-printer, macro layer, or out-of-core dialect may introduce a second stored spelling; any future write-path render codec must be bijective, version-pinned, enforce a parse-print-parse fixpoint invariant, echo only canonical form in all compiler output, and is admissible only after the gating A/B experiment succeeds.

Local type inference and similar elision questions that interact with the type-system debates (checked ownership, static specialization) are explicitly not pre-decided by this ruling; whatever those debates admit must still re-elaborate into the fully explicit stored artifact.
result.rationale: The prosecution wins on the core and the defense conceded it: under the corpus standing rule (caveats.md — cite exact cards or it does not ship) and the null hypothesis, human-oriented syntax holds an empty evidence hand. All confirmed enablers are semantic (F001–F009, N, A, D, C, J); syntax was explicitly deferred by the corpus (source-to-ir-lowering-sketches.md, caveats.md, A001 negative scope). On verification — the axis this project weights heaviest — sugar and elision are pure liability: each rule adds unverified frontend transformation surface at the exact soundness chokepoint the corpus flags (F003 negative scope, 'unsound frontend metadata emission' in open-questions.md, round1-index's lowering-survival gap), while N006's Alive2 discipline prices every rewrite at proof-grade cost and F006 (SPARK) is precedent that verification-first languages restrict surface expressiveness. This is replace_with_alternative rather than cut_from_core because the canonical form is not a passive default — it becomes a load-bearing requirement: the prosecution's benefit column (drift detection, node-path diagnostics, content-addressed caching, single-spelling dedup) only exists if the language mandates and polices those properties, so they are spelled out as obligations. Why not needs_evidence: the genuinely open empirical questions (token-cost ratio with real tokenizers — the 1.35–1.48x proxy reproduced in this hearing; transcription-distance vs precedence-misbinding error rates) govern only the defender's narrow write-path infix codec, and that codec is additive tooling over a fixed canonical core — a bijective renderer can be admitted later behind the defender's own A/B experiment without reopening the core decision. The core ruling is robust to either experimental outcome, since even the defender's proposal stores and echoes only canonical form. Deciding the core now is therefore safe and decisive; the codec stays out until evidence exists. All AI-writer behavioral claims remain EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED and are gated accordingly.
followUps: Run the gating A/B codegen experiment before admitting any infix write-path codec: identical kernel specs rendered to both grammars; measure generation tokens (real model tokenizers, whole programs, not the fragment proxy), first-parse success rate, silent semantic-error rate via differential testing against reference implementations (specifically operand-order/associativity swaps vs precedence misbindings), and repair iterations to green.

Prototype the canonical grammar with node-path diagnostics and subtree hashing; measure parse/elaboration share of end-to-end compile latency and the realized content-addressed caching benefit in a simulated AI repair loop (D001/D004 channel, currently frontend-unquantified).

Test the drift hypothesis: whether byte-canonical single-spelling code measurably reduces consistency drift across AI-generated near-duplicate blocks, and whether drift scales with per-copy verbosity.

Measure the training-distribution penalty: initial defect rates of current models emitting the canonical form vs infix, and how fast the gap closes with in-context examples — this bounds the transition cost of the ruling.
Open a syntax/AI-codegen evidence lane in the corpus (new card IDs) — every behavioral claim in this debate is currently uncardable, and the null-hypothesis win is procedural, not empirical.

Write the toolchain-boundary policy document: allowed render layers, mandatory parse-print-parse fixpoint invariant, codec version pinning, and canonical-only storage/echo rules, so the cut cannot silently un-cut itself.

Coordinate with the type-system debates (aliasing-model, dispatch-generics, numeric-semantics) on which elisions are actually inference questions owned by those topics, keeping the invariant that stored artifacts are fully elaborated.

result.stance: PARTIAL KEEP with honest concession. I concede the null hypothesis for pervasive tracing GC as the core memory model: on this corpus it is indefensible for an optimizer-first language. I defend two components of the feature: (1) implicit drop — compiler-inserted deallocation derived from the checked-ownership facts the aliasing debate already crowned — as the ONLY implicit reclamation path; and (2) RC/ARC as an explicit, restricted, optimizer-visible fallback TYPE for the statically-unprovable-lifetime residue, never the default. I reject the full alternative ('region/arena/ownership annotations everywhere; every allocation and free writer-written') on verification-necessity and machine-writer-failure grounds. This mirrors the corpus's own crowned architecture pattern (round1-dispatch-generics: guaranteed static path as the only implicit mechanism, dynamic mechanism available solely through an explicit restricted channel).
result.coreArgument: Three admissible lines. (1) VERIFICATION NECESSITY: the round1-aliasing verdict establishes that every sound memory discipline surveyed runs through ownership-like discharge (F001/F006) — so the alternative's 'checkable explicit frees' require the same ownership checker the language must build anyway. That creates a dichotomy fatal to explicit-frees-everywhere: where the checker CAN verify a writer-written free, it can compute the drop point itself (the free is a redundant assertion the checker recomputes — pure token/drift surface, zero new facts); where it CANNOT (graphs, shared caches, detached tasks — the frontier round1-aliasing flags as unmeasured), the explicit free becomes an unchecked trust point, which the project's soundness rule bans (F003: alias facts are UB-if-false; C004 negative scope: unsafe is UB). The alternative's practical escape — arena-plus-index restructuring — reintroduces use-after-free as well-typed index-into-recycled-slot bugs invisible to any checker: the no-use-after-free invariant becomes UNCHECKABLE exactly where the at-scale-fallible writer is most error-prone. A restricted RC tier keeps that residue inside machine-enforced semantics. (2) OPTIMIZER NECESSITY: cutting implicit drop gains the compiler nothing — the optimizer's fact channel is the ownership/non-interference types (F001/F002), not free statements. F009 is a production existence proof that automatic reclamation ops can be explicit, optimizer-visible IR instructions placed and optimized by language-level passes before LLVM: 'visible and checkable' does not entail 'writer-written'. Conversely, writer-placed frees are either compiler-movable (their placement is noise tokens) or fixed observable points (forfeiting drop sinking, batching, and elision — the ARC-optimization class F009 documents). F004's strongest-known contract (fresh-return) discharges naturally when the language owns allocation semantics; writer-chosen region placement makes freshness a per-annotation proof obligation. (3) MACHINE-WRITER FAILURE MODES of the alternative: viral region/lifetime parameters on every transitive signature, long-range alloc/free pairing obligations, and global frontier restructurings are structural drift amplifiers and token multipliers, converted by the checker into compile-iteration cost in the AI loop (rates EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; structure is arithmetic). Additionally, under annotations-everywhere, reclamation TIMELINESS becomes unverifiable — allocating into a too-long-lived region is well-typed, so 'no unbounded growth' drops out of the machine-checkable diagnostic channel; RC ties reclamation to last-reference by construction (modulo cycles — conceded, uncarded). My concession on GC uses the corpus's preferred wording (misleading-comparisons #3): managed runtimes recover performance only conditionally (F007/J004), unlike source-level guarantees, and every runtime-managed possibly-shared object forfeits classic optimizations (C003) — so automatic management must be the explicit checked exception, never the ambient default.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Swift SIL exposes retain/release as explicit IR instructions with dedicated ARC analyses/passes running before LLVM. Proves automatic reclamation can be compiler-placed AND optimizer-visible simultaneously — the alternative's core premise that only writer-written frees are 'visible and checkable' is false at the IR level, and drop placement is itself an optimization the compiler must own.

Checked ownership/exclusivity is the corpus's only adversarially-verified statically-discharged non-interference mechanism. The same facts that discharge noalias determine every last-use point, so the compiler must already derive each drop point; a writer-emitted free duplicates a recomputable fact — adding drift surface, no optimizer facts.

SPARK's ownership policy (single read-write owner, transfer on assignment) shows checked ownership implies compiler-known unique reclamation responsibility at every point — and that 'checkable' explicit boundary contracts already import ownership machinery, so explicitness buys no verification power the implicit form lacks.

The optimizer payoff (register promotion, read/write elimination, reordering) flows from proven non-interference around writes — carried by ownership types, not by free statements. Implicit drop preserves this fact channel identically; cutting it gains zero payoff-class coverage.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-if-false, so every writer-authored free or region annotation feeding them is a new trust point unless discharged by ownership machinery; once that machinery exists, the drop point is derivable and the explicit annotation is redundant.

Return-noalias models a fresh-allocation contract — the strongest known — but its negative scope demands lifetime/provenance/escape rules first; a language-owned allocation/drop semantics is the natural discharge point, versus per-annotation proof obligations under writer-chosen regions.

Grounds my concession: HotSpot escape analysis gives conditional elimination/scalar replacement, never guaranteed stack allocation — pervasive GC's performance recovery is conditional runtime analysis, not a source-level guarantee, so GC cannot be the optimizer-first default.

Primary-vendor sharpening of F007 (explicitly: no heap-to-stack replacement) — confines the defensible feature to statically-fact-backed automatic management (ownership-derived drop, elidable ARC), not tracing GC.

Type-level non-sharing gives data-race freedom in safe code, but its negative scope (unsafe code is UB) is exactly why the unprovable-lifetime frontier must not be pushed into unsafe manual freeing; a checked RC tier keeps the frontier inside sound semantics.

Possibly-shared memory forfeits register caching, reordering, and write elision — this prices my kept RC tier honestly and dictates its shape: an explicit restricted exception whose residue fraction must be kept small and diagnosable, never the ambient regime.

machineWriterCosts: Viral annotation propagation: region/lifetime parameters thread through every transitive caller's signature, so a local change to one allocation's storage class forces regeneration of many near-duplicate signatures — each a token multiplier on generation and context re-reading, and a near-duplicate-text drift site (the exact hallucination-across-copies failure mode). Structural property; AI-specific rates EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

Long-range paired obligations: every writer-written alloc/free pair spans arbitrary code distance; regenerating one side without the other is guaranteed rejection. The checker converts this drift into compile iterations rather than silent bugs — but compile-time in the AI iteration loop is an admissible cost, and the corpus has zero cards on ownership/region-verifier compile cost (round1-aliasing gap: 'checker as trusted computing base').

Frontier restructuring blowup: where lifetimes are statically inexpressible (graphs, dynamic dataflow, detached tasks), the writer must globally restructure to arena-plus-index designs; indices are checker-invisible pseudo-pointers, so slot-recycling use-after-free returns as a well-typed logical bug — precisely the unchecked-invariant class an at-scale-fallible writer cannot be trusted with. Prevalence uncarded (Rust-ecosystem background knowledge).

Redundant-assertion token cost: every allocation site gains a writer-emitted free that the checker must recompute anyway to verify; an always-recomputable assertion class is pure token cost whose only distinctive failure mode is inconsistency between the writer's copy and the checker's derivation.

Loss of machine-checkable reclamation timeliness: under regions-everywhere, allocating into a too-long-lived region is well-typed, so leak/growth diagnostics vanish from the machine-readable channel; the writer must solve a global region-granularity optimization problem on every edit with no checker feedback on wrong (leaky) choices. Region-inference leak pathologies are uncarded background (MLKit-era experience), EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED here.
consistencyRisks: Interior-mutability tension: an RC/ARC fallback tier with shared mutable interiors punctures shared-reference noalias facts — the exact uncarded tension round1-aliasing and misleading-comparisons #2 flag. The kept RC type must forbid or exclusivity-check interior mutation, or it erodes the crowned aliasing-model candidate this defense depends on.

Residue creep: per C003, every RC-shared object joins the possibly-shared class and forfeits classic optimizations. If generated code idiomatically overuses the 'restricted exception' tier, the design degrades toward the conditional managed-runtime regime F007/J004 warn about. Needs a guardrail (residue-fraction diagnostics); AI tier-selection habits are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

F009 is N=1: the optimizer-visible-ARC architecture rests on one implementation-specific exemplar with OSSA/non-OSSA phase caveats, stale-doc risk, and Apple-scale maintenance cost (caveats.md) — thin support for generalizing to a new language's pipeline.

Hidden control flow from implicit drop: compiler-inserted drops of effectful destructors add unwritten effect points at scope exits, interacting with the unsettled violation-semantics and effects/exceptions debates (F008 shows EH representation constrains optimization). Drop points must surface in machine-readable diagnostics or this defense conflicts with other candidates' no-hidden-effects direction.

memory-object-model.md prerequisites (object identity, provenance, interior-pointer stability across moves, FFI/callback invalidation) block sound lifetime emission for BOTH the feature and the alternative; per its emission rule, neither side can be finalized before those are answered.

Premise dependency: the 'drop is derivable' argument presumes the aliasing-model leading candidate (checked ownership/exclusive mutation) lands as decided. If round 2 displaces ownership with boundary contracts or a region family, implicit drop loses its derivation source and this defense must be re-run.
empiricalUntested: All AI-writer behavioral claims: drift rates on regenerated region-annotated signatures, alloc/free pairing error rates, token-cost multipliers from annotation plumbing, and tier-selection habits — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence; these arguments are structural in kind, unmeasured in magnitude.

Frontier size: what fraction of performance-critical code has statically unprovable lifetimes (round1-aliasing names this the decisive unmeasured variable) — determines how much weight the RC-fallback keep actually carries.

Region-verifier feasibility, discharge rates, and compile-time: zero exemplar cards exist for any region/effect language (Cyclone, DPJ, MLKit, Koka uncovered per caveats.md and round1-aliasing P3) — the ALTERNATIVE'S soundness mechanism is itself the uncarded family.

No quantified performance deltas anywhere: ARC retain/release overhead vs arena allocation vs implicit drop, drop-sinking/batching payoff, RC-elision rates under ownership facts — F002's negative scope and caveats.md (documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude) apply to both sides.

Background knowledge used as motivation but uncarded: Rust shipping Arc for detached concurrency despite ownership, index-arena use-after-free bug prevalence, MLKit region-inference leak pathologies, RC cycle leaks — all need evidence cards before this verdict hardens.

Whether compiler-placed drops measurably outperform writer-placed frees on real workloads — no benchmark exists; the optimizer-freedom argument is currently in-kind (F009 pass classes), not in-magnitude.

result.optimizerRole: None that is unique to the feature. By optimization time, macro-expanded and AI-pre-expanded code are indistinguishable inputs (F002: the mechanism depends on proven non-interference, not on how text was produced), so in-language expansion contributes zero to the extreme-performance goal per se. The one genuine optimizer-necessity argument the defense raised — preserving parametric structure so the specialize-vs-share decision stays in-compiler (D001 vs D005, and the F009/Swift-SIL fourth model the dispatch round flagged as unexamined) — is real and verified against round1-dispatch-generics.md, but it is carried by the typed-generics feature adjudicated in that topic, not by macros/comptime; the defender conceded this coupling explicitly. Comptime-derived constants feeding fact channels (N004 range, F003 noalias, N005 inbounds) is the residual optimizer claim, and it is answerable without staging: facts about literal constant data can be compiler-DERIVED by inspection, which is equally sound and requires no evaluator. A006 (Halide) cuts prosecution's way at card level: optimization choices as checkable program STRUCTURE the writer can emit directly, with a negative scope denying transfer of the staged-DSL machinery to a general language.
result.verificationRole: This is where the case was actually decided, and it cuts both ways. Against keeping: an in-language Turing-complete evaluator enlarges the trusted surface in exactly the direction the corpus's confirmed pattern forbids — F006 (SPARK) achieves true-by-construction facts by RESTRICTING semantics to a side-effect-free fragment; the ir-strategy verdict's non-negotiable soundness rule disqualifies uncheckable trusted contracts; a CTFE stage must bit-match the treacherous runtime numeric contract (N001 UB/poison ladder, N002 flag semantics) or become a cross-stage miscompile generator; and generator verification forces either late per-instantiation checking or a quantified staged type system with no supporting card. Zero staging keeps every obligation first-order, per-site, decidable. Against a bare cut: the defense proved two structural verification gaps in the naked alternative — (a) cross-copy consistency ('these N functions instantiate one pattern') is stated in no artifact, so no in-language checker can exist at ANY drift rate; (b) AI-precomputed constants asserted into UB-bearing channels are exactly the writer-trusted contracts the ir-strategy round disqualified. Both gaps are closable by requirements outside the trusted compiler (provenance-checked deterministic generation; compiler-derived-not-writer-asserted constant facts), which is why the verdict is replace_with_alternative rather than cut_from_core: the alternative carries the load only if those obligations are made mandatory.
result.aiWriterConsiderations: All admissible machine-writer claims on both sides are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED — the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence cards and zero metaprogramming cards of any kind (verified by full-corpus search). Conceded-real costs of the alternative: O(N)-copy token generation and re-read on every pattern edit vs O(1) definition edits; consistency drift across near-duplicates (the strongest defense card — and its structural point stands: without a pattern artifact, drift is undetectable in-language at any rate); N-fold diagnostics without expansion provenance; update amplification moving regeneration latency into the AI loop; per-copy contract restatement multiplying proof obligations.
